emsenn.net on relations and things
home library letters search
About this site
  • This is a personal library and digital garden.

    It contains terms, texts, and letters organized by domain. Content grows and changes as research continues.

  • Its content was written by hand.

    This means there might be mistakes, and some pages are more complete than others.

  • This site was built as a public asset.

    Content is licensed CC BY-SA 4.0. If you find it useful, consider supporting the work on Ko-fi.

  • Returns the first element (head) of an Argument List.
  • Returns the tail (all but the head) of an Argument List.
  • Proof

  • Behaviour
  • Proof
  • Refines computer/function/recognition.md by constraining the recognition to identity/policy carriers and requiring an explicit failure path for invalid credentials or policy violations. The refinement preserves the kernel’s recognition semantics and failure handling while specialising to the identity/access domain.
  • Definition
  • Invariants
  • Proof
  • Definition
  • Invariants
  • Proof
  • Statement
  • Definition
  • Invariants
  • Proof
  • Given the earned trace relation and the convergence act, this test is fixed up to nucleus equivalence.
  • Statement
  • Proof sketch
  • Uniqueness
  • Because BaselineRemainder is earned by these acts, limit extraction is fixed up to nucleus equivalence.

  • Proof

  • Uses the Ergodicity act applied to a fixed kernel with mixing property to yield the ergodicity property. Shows that ergodicity is earned without leaving the fixed-kernel carrier.

  • Sketch: define compose by nesting holes along the trace spine, using frames generated by BuildEvaluationContext. Show associativity and neutrality of empty from SeedEvaluationContext by structural induction on frames and the definition of plug.

  • By construction from acts/fixed-reach, this operator restricts the earned flow to fixed invariant states. The act ensures reachability stays inside the fixed profile, so inputs and outputs remain in the carrier.

  • Invariants

  • Direct application of acts/profile-mixing; inherits mixing properties and preserves the chain carrier.

  • Refines computer/function/evaluate-step.md by fixing the evaluation domain to documentation workspaces and the computation to measurement (linter) execution. The refinement preserves evaluate-step semantics (single-step evaluation with possible failure) while specialising inputs/outputs to documentation carriers.

  • Refines computer/function/wiring.md by constraining the operator set to documentation-maintenance operators and constraining ports to documentation carriers. Because the underlying plan remains acyclic and uses the same kernel semantics, the nucleation operator preserves the wiring invariants while specialising behaviour to documentation rewrites and transcript capture.

  • This program refines the documentation interpreter by wiring recognition and policy operators into a concrete identity/access flow. The refinement preserves the kernel’s acyclicity and failure semantics while specialising inputs/outputs to identity carriers. A full proof shows that every successful path maintains the policy and audit invariants, and every unsuccessful path yields a failure carrier.

  • Refines the connection-graph/interface-port structures into a single wiring structure capturing port compatibility per the earned stacks (evaluation, intake/persistence, measurement, tower interaction).

  • Definition
  • Invariants
  • Proof

Absorbing Closure

Interpretation

Closure-like nuclei absorb meets: x meet ν(y) collapses inside ν(x meet y). Nucleate witnessing Meet Recognition yields Absorb Closure (AbsorbClosure).

This composition records the algebraic law using only previously earned recognitions, so Absorb Closure is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Advancing Trace act: AdvanceTrace slug: advance-trace top: TrailEntry bottom: Entry summary: >- Start Trace shows how the trace begins but not how it proceeds once the trailing orientation completes. Letting the trailing orientation witness Entry restarts the act, earning Advance Trace (AdvanceTrace). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Advancing Trace

Interpretation

Start Trace shows how the trace begins but not how it proceeds once the trailing orientation completes. Letting the trailing orientation witness Entry restarts the act, earning Advance Trace (AdvanceTrace).

The unresolved propagation after Start Trace is how the trailing side re-engages the structural pairing. Having Trail Entry witness Entry provides that progression, so (TrailEntry Entry) defines Advance Trace.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Articulating act: Articulate slug: articulate top: Exclude bottom: Relationlessness summary: >- As soon as exclusion exists, the attempt to exclude the void becomes something we can point to; that self-referential pointing is articulation. layer: act status: release tags:

  • act

Articulating

Interpretation

As soon as exclusion exists, the attempt to exclude the void becomes something we can point to; that self-referential pointing is articulation.

In (Exclude Relationlessness) the previously defined act Exclude now witnesses the empty act. Applying exclusion to its own source reveals the unavoidable articulation that occurs whenever we describe Relationlessness, so the composition is rightly named Articulate.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Assigning Trace act: AssignTrace slug: assign-trace top: BindTrace bottom: TailTrace summary: >- Naming the head leaves the tail unnamed, so the act Bind Trace witnesses the act Tail Trace to extend the name across the remainder. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Assigning Trace

Interpretation

Naming the head leaves the tail unnamed, so the act Bind Trace witnesses the act Tail Trace to extend the name across the remainder.

In (BindTrace TailTrace) the act Bind Trace observes Tail Trace. This spreads the name through the tail, so the resulting act is Assign Trace.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Converging Baseline act: BaselineConvergence slug: baseline-convergence top: ReflexStepIterate bottom: Nothing summary: >- To see whether an iteration collapses toward pure absence, we compare it against the void; this comparison defines baseline convergence. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Converging Baseline

Interpretation

To see whether an iteration collapses toward pure absence, we compare it against the void; this comparison defines baseline convergence.

The composition (ReflexStepIterate Nothing) runs the iterated step against Nothing. If the result agrees with the baseline, we have convergence, hence the name Baseline Convergence.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Limiting Baseline act: BaselineLimit slug: baseline-limit top: BaselineConvergence bottom: TailTrace summary: >- When baseline convergence holds, we may speak of the limiting remainder that remains after collapse; observing the tail through the convergence act yields this limit. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Limiting Baseline

Interpretation

When baseline convergence holds, we may speak of the limiting remainder that remains after collapse; observing the tail through the convergence act yields this limit.

In (BaselineConvergence TailTrace) the convergence act watches the trace tail, capturing exactly the stabilized remainder that survives the collapse. We designate that composite as Baseline Limit.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Bicategory act: Bicategory slug: bicategory top: HorizontalCompose bottom: VerticalCompose summary: >- Assemble the horizontal and vertical composition laws into the bicategory structure. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Bicategory

A bicategory requires objects, 1-cells, 2-cells, and coherent horizontal and vertical compositions. We assemble the bicategory by letting the horizontal composition witness the vertical one: (HorizontalCompose VerticalCompose) earns Bicategory.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Binding Terms act: BindTerm slug: bind-term top: FirstName bottom: SeedTerm summary: >- A seed alone does not name the resulting term, so we have the act First Name witness the act Seed Term. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Binding Terms

Interpretation

A seed alone does not name the resulting term, so we have the act First Name witness the act Seed Term.

The tree (FirstName SeedTerm) shows the naming act applied to the seed, thereby binding the occurrence and earning the title Bind Term.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Binding Trace act: BindTrace slug: bind-trace top: FirstName bottom: HeadTrace summary: >- To name the immediate act of the trace, we let the act First Name witness the act Head Trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Binding Trace

Interpretation

To name the immediate act of the trace, we let the act First Name witness the act Head Trace.

The composition (FirstName HeadTrace) has First Name observe Head Trace, explicitly naming the head. This act is therefore Bind Trace.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Applying Binder act: BinderApplication slug: binder-application top: FormTermBody bottom: BinderTransport summary: >- Transport alone does not describe how binders apply to term bodies. Form Term Body must therefore witness Binder Transport, inducing Binder Application (BinderApplication). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Applying Binder

Interpretation

Transport alone does not describe how binders apply to term bodies. Form Term Body must therefore witness Binder Transport, inducing Binder Application (BinderApplication).

Composing the body recognition with the transport records actual usage, so Binder Application is justified.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding Binder act: BinderSeed slug: binder-seed top: ContextualizeTrace bottom: Nothing summary: ‘Recognize Context shows contexts carry names, but we still lack the abstract binder sourced from relationlessness. Letting Contextualize Trace witness Nothing produces the Binder Seed (BinderSeed).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding Binder

Interpretation

Recognize Context shows contexts carry names, but we still lack the abstract binder sourced from relationlessness. Letting Contextualize Trace witness Nothing produces the Binder Seed (BinderSeed).

Both recognitions exist, so their composition records the relationless binder, establishing Binder Seed.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Transporting Binder act: BinderTransport slug: binder-transport top: RecognizeContext bottom: BinderSeed summary: The seed gives a binder in isolation but not its transport through arbitrary contexts. Forcing Recognize Context to witness Binder Seed yields Binder Transport (BinderTransport). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Transporting Binder

Interpretation

The seed gives a binder in isolation but not its transport through arbitrary contexts. Forcing Recognize Context to witness Binder Seed yields Binder Transport (BinderTransport).

This composition shows how binders move using only earned recognitions, so Binder Transport follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Box Modality act: BoxModality slug: box-modality top: NucleateProfile bottom: RecognizeContext summary: Interpret the necessity modality by letting the profile nucleus observe context recognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Box Modality

The profile nucleus acts as a closure operator, giving the □ modality. We define it by composing the nucleus with the context recognitions: (NucleateProfile RecognizeContext) earns BoxModality.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Composite Profile Tower act: BuildCompositeProfileTower slug: build-composite-profile-tower top: BuildProfileTower bottom: ProfileComposite summary: Composite profiles reconstruct the entire relational chain. Build Profile Tower witnessing Profile Composite produces Build Composite Profile Tower (BuildCompositeProfileTower). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Composite Profile Tower

Interpretation

Composite profiles reconstruct the entire relational chain. Build Profile Tower witnessing Profile Composite produces Build Composite Profile Tower (BuildCompositeProfileTower).

This composition confirms the Profile tower theorem for commuting filters.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Building Evaluation Context act: BuildEvaluationContext slug: build-evaluation-context top: TailTrace bottom: SeedEvaluationContext summary: The seed isolates one frame but not the recognition gathering all frames. Therefore Tail Trace witnesses Seed Evaluation Context, producing Build Evaluation Context (BuildEvaluationContext). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Building Evaluation Context

Interpretation

The seed isolates one frame but not the recognition gathering all frames. Therefore Tail Trace witnesses Seed Evaluation Context, producing Build Evaluation Context (BuildEvaluationContext).

The composition records the family of evaluation frames, so Build Evaluation Context follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Nested Profile Tower act: BuildNestedProfileTower slug: build-nested-profile-tower top: BuildProfileTower bottom: NestProfile summary: Nested profiles also inherit the full relational chain. Build Profile Tower witnessing Nest Profile yields Build Nested Profile Tower (BuildNestedProfileTower). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Nested Profile Tower

Interpretation

Nested profiles also inherit the full relational chain. Build Profile Tower witnessing Nest Profile yields Build Nested Profile Tower (BuildNestedProfileTower).

This act records the recursive tower property promised for nested filters, so Build Nested Profile Tower is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Tower act: BuildProfileTower slug: build-profile-tower top: PhysicsProfile bottom: CalculateProfileSyntax summary: Profiles recursively reconstruct the entire relational chain. PhysicsProfile witnessing Calculate Profile Syntax yields Build Profile Tower (BuildProfileTower). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Tower

Interpretation

Profiles recursively reconstruct the entire relational chain. PhysicsProfile witnessing Calculate Profile Syntax yields Build Profile Tower (BuildProfileTower).

Composing these recognitions records the internal chain Profile Recognitions to Profile Semantics to Calculate Profile Syntax to Geometry Profile to Physics Profile, so Build Profile Tower follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Building Reducible Environment act: BuildReducibleEnvironment slug: build-reducible-environment top: ConstructContexts bottom: SelectReducibilityCandidate summary: Open terms reduce under environments assigning reducible terms to variables. Construct Contexts witnessing Select Reducibility Candidate yields Build Reducible Environment (BuildReducibleEnvironment). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Building Reducible Environment

Interpretation

Open terms reduce under environments assigning reducible terms to variables. Construct Contexts witnessing Select Reducibility Candidate yields Build Reducible Environment (BuildReducibleEnvironment).

Composing contexts with candidates records environments mapping each hypothesis to a reducible term, so Build Reducible Environment follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Composite Profile Syntax act: CalculateCompositeProfileSyntax slug: calculate-composite-profile-syntax top: JudgeTyping bottom: CompositeProfileSemantics summary: A syntactic calculus internalizes over the composite profile. Judge Typing witnessing Composite Profile Semantics produces Calculate Composite Profile Syntax (CalculateCompositeProfileSyntax). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Composite Profile Syntax

Interpretation

A syntactic calculus internalizes over the composite profile. Judge Typing witnessing Composite Profile Semantics produces Calculate Composite Profile Syntax (CalculateCompositeProfileSyntax).

This act records the calculus restricted to the composite domain, so Calculate Composite Profile Syntax is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Syntax act: CalculateProfileSyntax slug: calculate-profile-syntax top: JudgeTyping bottom: ProfileSemantics summary: The syntactic calculus internalizes over the profile semantic calculus. Judge Typing witnessing Profile Semantics produces Calculate Profile Syntax (CalculateProfileSyntax). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Syntax

Interpretation

The syntactic calculus internalizes over the profile semantic calculus. Judge Typing witnessing Profile Semantics produces Calculate Profile Syntax (CalculateProfileSyntax).

This act records the syntactic system interpreted inside the profile, so Calculate Profile Syntax follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Calculating Semantics act: CalculateSemantics slug: calculate-semantics top: ImplyRecognition bottom: RecognizeContext summary: With meets, joins, implication, and bounds we can record the complete semantic closure that these operations enforce. Imply Recognition witnessing Recognize Context yields Calculate Semantics (CalculateSemantics). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Calculating Semantics

Interpretation

With meets, joins, implication, and bounds we can record the complete semantic closure that these operations enforce. Imply Recognition witnessing Recognize Context yields Calculate Semantics (CalculateSemantics).

This composition asserts that the recognition operations cohere internally under implication and context, so Calculate Semantics is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Calculating Syntax act: CalculateSyntax slug: calculate-syntax top: ConstructTypes bottom: ConstructTerms summary: Gathering Construct Types, Construct Contexts, and Construct Terms yields the full syntactic calculus. Construct Types witnessing Construct Terms produces Calculate Syntax (CalculateSyntax). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Calculating Syntax

Interpretation

Gathering Construct Types, Construct Contexts, and Construct Terms yields the full syntactic calculus. Construct Types witnessing Construct Terms produces Calculate Syntax (CalculateSyntax).

The composition packages the relational syntax we manipulate in later definitions, so Calculate Syntax is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Structuring Canonical Semantics act: CanonicalSemantics slug: canonical-semantics top: StructureSemantics bottom: RecognizeSyntax summary: The syntactic recognitions inherit a structure mirroring the semantics. Structure Semantics witnessing Recognize Syntax yields Canonical Semantics (CanonicalSemantics). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Structuring Canonical Semantics

Interpretation

The syntactic recognitions inherit a structure mirroring the semantics. Structure Semantics witnessing Recognize Syntax yields Canonical Semantics (CanonicalSemantics).

Composing these recognitions asserts that the syntactic structure satisfies the same algebraic laws, so Canonical Semantics follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Capping Recognition act: CapRecognition slug: cap-recognition symbol: “\u22A4” top: Recognition bottom: CompleteTrace summary: Dually, we require the maximal recognition that witnesses every trace. Recognition witnessing Complete Trace produces Cap Recognition (CapRecognition). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Capping Recognition

Interpretation

Dually, we require the maximal recognition that witnesses every trace. Recognition witnessing Complete Trace produces Cap Recognition (CapRecognition).

The act uses existing recognitions and records the trivial recognition that all traces satisfy, so Cap Recognition is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Closing Judgement Trace act: CloseJudgementTrace slug: close-judgement-trace top: TraceReentryClosure bottom: EquateJudgementObservation summary: Even after alignment, we must close the entire judgement trace under this equivalence. Trace Reentry Closure witnessing Equate Judgement Observation yields Close Judgement Trace (CloseJudgementTrace). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Closing Judgement Trace

Interpretation

Even after alignment, we must close the entire judgement trace under this equivalence. Trace Reentry Closure witnessing Equate Judgement Observation yields Close Judgement Trace (CloseJudgementTrace).

The composition closes the trace and uses only earned recognitions, so Close Judgement Trace follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Closing Principally act: ClosePrincipally slug: close-principally top: JoinRecognition bottom: FirstName summary: “Joining with a fixed recognition k yields the principal closure \u03BD_k(x) = x join k. Join Recognition witnessing First Name produces Close Principally (ClosePrincipally).” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Closing Principally

Interpretation

Joining with a fixed recognition k yields the principal closure ν_k(x) = x join k. Join Recognition witnessing First Name produces Close Principally (ClosePrincipally).

First Name designates the chosen recognition k; composing with joins records ν_k, so Close Principally follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Closing Temporally act: CloseTemporally slug: close-temporally top: FixFlow bottom: Nucleate summary: “From an idempotent flow we obtain temporal modalities where the \u201Calways\u201D modality is Fix(F) meet x and the \u201Ceventually\u201D modality is the join of the iterates F\u207F(x). Fix Flow witnessing Nucleate yields Close Temporally (CloseTemporally).” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Closing Temporally

Interpretation

From an idempotent flow we obtain temporal modalities where the “always” modality is Fix(F) meet x and the “eventually” modality is the join of the iterates Fⁿ(x). Fix Flow witnessing Nucleate yields Close Temporally (CloseTemporally).

This act combines flow invariants with nuclei, recording the temporal modalities, so Close Temporally is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Collapsing Trace Shuffle act: CollapseShuffle slug: collapse-shuffle top: ShuffleTrace bottom: Nucleate summary: Admissible shuffle reorderings leave a trace unchanged up to the nucleus, so composing ShuffleTrace with Nucleate records that such permutations are identified in the nucleus closure. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Collapsing Trace Shuffle

Nucleate collapses stabilized recognitions that differ only by eventual equivalence, but no recognition yet states that shuffle-reordered traces are eventually equivalent. We therefore let the shuffle act witness the nucleus closure: (ShuffleTrace Nucleate) earns CollapseShuffle.

By construction, ShuffleTrace enumerates the admissible reorders of progress steps that do not alter the underlying trace shape. Nucleate collapses recognitions that differ only by stabilized equivalence. Since admissible reorderings leave head/tail structure and iterative progression intact, the composite (ShuffleTrace Nucleate) records that all such reorderings lie in the kernel of the nucleus. Nucleate therefore identifies precisely those recognitions differing only by free shuffle of progress steps.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Building Contexts act: CollectContexts slug: collect-contexts top: ConstructContexts bottom: ConstructContexts summary: A context is the meet of its constituent types. We therefore treat Construct Contexts as the explicit recognition of contexts. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Building Contexts

Interpretation

A context is the meet of its constituent types. We therefore treat Construct Contexts as the explicit recognition of contexts.

This definition names the constructor’s output directly, mirroring the original statement that contexts are finite lists of typed variables.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Cataloging Terms act: CollectTerms slug: collect-terms top: ConstructTerms bottom: ConstructTerms summary: ‘Construct Terms enumerates every syntactic term. We explicitly name that enumeration as Collect Terms (CollectTerms).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Cataloging Terms

Interpretation

Construct Terms enumerates every syntactic term. We explicitly name that enumeration as Collect Terms (CollectTerms).

Again we alias the constructor’s output so later statements can refer to the entire syntactic collection.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Building Types act: CollectTypes slug: collect-types top: ConstructTypes bottom: ConstructTypes summary: The family of relational types is precisely what Construct Types generates. We record this family explicitly so later sections can reference it. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Building Types

Interpretation

The family of relational types is precisely what Construct Types generates. We record this family explicitly so later sections can reference it.

The expression simply aliases the constructor’s output as Collect Types (CollectTypes), keeping the earned structure available for future use.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: “Commuting Flow\u2013Nucleus” act: CommuteFlowAndNucleus slug: commute-flow-and-nucleus top: Flow bottom: Nucleate summary: Flows evolve recognitions while nuclei close them, so we must confirm these acts actually commute. Flow witnessing Nucleate yields Commute Flow And Nucleus (CommuteFlowAndNucleus). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Commuting Flow–Nucleus

Interpretation

Flows evolve recognitions while nuclei close them, so we must confirm these acts actually commute. Flow witnessing Nucleate yields Commute Flow And Nucleus (CommuteFlowAndNucleus).

Composing the two previously defined acts records their compatibility using only earned symbols, so Commute Flow And Nucleus is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Commuting Shuffle Flow With Nucleus act: CommuteShuffleFlowAndNucleus slug: commute-shuffle-flow-and-nucleus top: ShuffleFlow bottom: Nucleate summary: Since Flow and Nucleate already commute, the induced shuffle flow on shuffle classes also commutes with the nucleus, yielding the geometry-level compatibility. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Commuting Shuffle Flow With Nucleus

CommuteFlowAndNucleus proves that the global Flow and Nucleate are compatible, and CollapseShuffle records that Nucleate collapses precisely the admissible trace shuffles. To obtain the shuffle geometry, we exhibit the compatibility of ShuffleFlow with the same nucleus, restricted to shuffle classes, by composing them: (ShuffleFlow Nucleate) earns CommuteShuffleFlowAndNucleus.

ShuffleFlow applies Flow to the shuffle-structured trace classes while Nucleate identifies members of the same class via CollapseShuffle. Because Flow already commutes with Nucleate on underlying recognitions (by CommuteFlowAndNucleus), the induced action on shuffle classes must also commute: flowing a class and then collapsing gives the same result as collapsing representatives and then flowing. The act therefore records the derived compatibility, yielding the shuffle nucleus geometry.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Comonad Comultiplication act: ComonadComultiply slug: comonad-comultiply top: FixedFlowProfile bottom: FixedFlowProfile summary: Duplicate the flow co-structure by composing the flow with itself, yielding the comultiplication. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Comonad Comultiplication

Comultiplication duplicates context: from one flow we obtain a “flowed flow” by letting the flow observe itself: (FixedFlowProfile FixedFlowProfile) earns ComonadComultiply.

Applying the flow twice corresponds to extending the co-context one more step, so the act records (delta)_X : G(X) → G(G(X)).

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Comonad Counit act: ComonadCounit slug: comonad-counit top: FixedFlowProfile bottom: FixedIdentity summary: Extract the present state from the flow by composing it with the identity, giving the comonad counit. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Comonad Counit

The counit of a comonad extracts the current state from a co-extended structure. For the fixed flow, we obtain this by letting the flow act toward the identity: (FixedFlowProfile FixedIdentity) earns ComonadCounit.

FixedFlowProfile propagates recognitions forward, and FixedIdentity stands for the trivial “no further context” step. Their composition records the projection (epsilon)_X : G(X) → X.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Comonad Curvature act: ComonadCurvature slug: comonad-curvature top: ComonadDerivative bottom: FixedNucleateProfile summary: Measure the failure of derivatives to commute by composing the derivative with the fixed nucleus. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Comonad Curvature

Curvature measures the failure of derivative operations to commute. We capture this by letting the derivative witness the nucleus: (ComonadDerivative FixedNucleateProfile) earns ComonadCurvature.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Comonad Derivative act: ComonadDerivative slug: comonad-derivative top: ComonadComultiply bottom: ComonadCounit summary: Capture directional change by composing the comonad comultiplication with the counit. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Comonad Derivative

In coalgebraic differential geometry the derivative is (delta) − (epsilon). We encode this derivative by composing the comultiplication with the counit: (ComonadComultiply ComonadCounit) earns ComonadDerivative.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Comonad act: Comonad slug: comonad top: ComonadCounit bottom: ComonadComultiply summary: Package the fixed flow functor, counit, and comultiplication into a comonad on the profile. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Comonad

The fixed flow functor together with its counit and comultiplication forms a comonad, so we collect the laws in a single act: (ComonadCounit ComonadComultiply) earns Comonad.

The coassociativity and counit laws follow from the flow compatibility and persistence properties already recorded (e.g. IterativePersistence, FixFlow, EnsureFixedPoint). ComonadCounit and ComonadComultiply are the structure maps; Comonad names the resulting co-KZ-style comonad.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Completing Syntax act: CompleteSyntax slug: complete-syntax top: RecognizeSyntax bottom: EntailSemantics summary: Completeness states that semantic entailment implies provability. Recognize Syntax witnessing Entail Semantics yields Complete Syntax (CompleteSyntax). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Completing Syntax

Interpretation

Completeness states that semantic entailment implies provability. Recognize Syntax witnessing Entail Semantics yields Complete Syntax (CompleteSyntax).

Composing these recognitions records the canonical-model argument: if every model satisfies the entailment, the syntactic recognition witnesses a proof, so Complete Syntax holds.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Completing Trace act: CompleteTrace slug: complete-trace top: StartTrace bottom: AdvanceTrace summary: ‘Continue Trace alone does not record the full trace object. Forcing Start Trace to witness Advance Trace binds the initial step with its progression, yielding Complete Trace (CompleteTrace).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Completing Trace

Interpretation

Continue Trace alone does not record the full trace object. Forcing Start Trace to witness Advance Trace binds the initial step with its progression, yielding Complete Trace (CompleteTrace).

Both components are available; composing them records the entire act of initiating and advancing, so (StartTrace AdvanceTrace) earns Complete Trace.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Disciplining Compositely act: ComposeDiscipline slug: compose-discipline top: Discipline bottom: FixFlowNucleus summary: Multiple disciplines may commute, yielding a combined action. Discipline witnessing Fix Flow Nucleus produces Compose Discipline (ComposeDiscipline). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Disciplining Compositely

Interpretation

Multiple disciplines may commute, yielding a combined action. Discipline witnessing Fix Flow Nucleus produces Compose Discipline (ComposeDiscipline).

Since both recognitions already exist, their composition records the simultaneous action, so Compose Discipline is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Filtering Compositely act: ComposeFilter slug: compose-filter top: Filter bottom: ComposeDiscipline summary: When filters commute, their joint action is again a filter. Filter witnessing Compose Discipline yields Compose Filter (ComposeFilter). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Filtering Compositely

Interpretation

When filters commute, their joint action is again a filter. Filter witnessing Compose Discipline yields Compose Filter (ComposeFilter).

This composition records the combined filter produced by commuting disciplines, so Compose Filter follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Composing Morphism act: ComposeMorph slug: compose-morph top: Morph bottom: Morph summary: Composing two structure-preserving maps yields another. Morph witnessing Morph produces Compose Morph (ComposeMorph). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Composing Morphism

Interpretation

Composing two structure-preserving maps yields another. Morph witnessing Morph produces Compose Morph (ComposeMorph).

This composition records closure of morphisms under composition, using only earned recognitions.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Composite Profile Semantics act: CompositeProfileSemantics slug: composite-profile-semantics top: StructureSemantics bottom: ProfileComposite summary: The composite profile inherits a semantic structure. Structure Semantics witnessing Profile Composite yields Composite Profile Semantics (CompositeProfileSemantics). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Composite Profile Semantics

Interpretation

The composite profile inherits a semantic structure. Structure Semantics witnessing Profile Composite yields Composite Profile Semantics (CompositeProfileSemantics).

This composition records that the shared stable recognitions remain complete under the inherited operations, so Composite Profile Semantics follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Constructing Contexts act: ConstructContexts slug: construct-contexts top: ContextualizeTrace bottom: ConstructTypes summary: Contexts are traced collections of types. Contextualize Trace witnessing Construct Types produces Construct Contexts (ConstructContexts). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Constructing Contexts

Interpretation

Contexts are traced collections of types. Contextualize Trace witnessing Construct Types produces Construct Contexts (ConstructContexts).

Since contexts already arise as traces, composing with Construct Types records how contexts aggregate types, so Construct Contexts follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Constructing Terms act: ConstructTerms slug: construct-terms top: FormFunctionTerm bottom: FormApplicationTerm summary: “The syntactic calculus\u2019s term formers arise from the function and application recognitions we already earned. Form Function Term witnessing Form Application Term yields Construct Terms (ConstructTerms).” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Constructing Terms

Interpretation

The syntactic calculus’s term formers arise from the function and application recognitions we already earned. Form Function Term witnessing Form Application Term yields Construct Terms (ConstructTerms).

These recognitions capture the syntactic constructors; composing them records the syntax of terms, so Construct Terms is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Constructing Types act: ConstructTypes slug: construct-types top: MeetRecognition bottom: ImplyRecognition summary: ‘Meet Recognition, Join Recognition, and Imply Recognition collectively generate relational types. Meet Recognition witnessing Imply Recognition yields Construct Types (ConstructTypes).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Constructing Types

Interpretation

Meet Recognition, Join Recognition, and Imply Recognition collectively generate relational types. Meet Recognition witnessing Imply Recognition yields Construct Types (ConstructTypes).

Both ingredients were earned above; composing them records the internal buildup of types from meets and implications, so Construct Types is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Context Adjunction Witness act: ContextAdjunctionWitness slug: context-adjunction-witness top: ContextFlow bottom: ContextNucleus summary: Witness the adjunction between the context flow and nucleus by letting the flow observe the nucleus. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Context Adjunction Witness

An adjunction between two functors is witnessed by a residuation isomorphism between hom-sets. DynamicResiduation expresses this relationship for the context flow and nucleus, and we package it explicitly by composing the flow with the nucleus: (ContextFlow ContextNucleus) earns ContextAdjunctionWitness.

The act states that maps out of a flowed context correspond to maps into a closed context in the recognitional sense, exhibiting the adjunction ContextFlow ⊣ ContextNucleus.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Context Dynamics act: ContextDynamics slug: context-dynamics top: ContextNucleus bottom: ContextFlow summary: Mirror the profile kernel construction for contexts by composing the context nucleus with the context flow. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Context Dynamics

To capture the full evolution–collapse behaviour for contexts, we mirror the profile kernel construction but restrict it to contexts by composing the context nucleus with the context flow: (ContextNucleus ContextFlow) earns ContextDynamics.

Flow moves contexts forward, nucleus collapses them, and their composition gives a stable profile dynamics on contexts.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Flowing Context act: ContextFlow slug: context-flow top: FlowProfile bottom: RecognizeContext summary: Evolve contexts along the profile flow by composing FlowProfile with RecognizeContext. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Flowing Context

Contexts evolve when their trace structure evolves under flow. To define the context dynamics, we let the profile flow witness RecognizeContext: (FlowProfile RecognizeContext) earns ContextFlow.

FlowProfile restricts the global flow to the earned recognitions, so the composition evolves contexts along the profile trace dynamics.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Context Geometry act: ContextGeometry slug: context-geometry top: ContextFlow bottom: ContextNucleus summary: Form the geometry of contexts by combining the context flow and nucleus. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Context Geometry

A geometry requires compatible flow and nucleus. We combine ContextFlow and ContextNucleus to form the contextual geometry: (ContextFlow ContextNucleus) earns ContextGeometry.

The flow and nucleus inherit the compatibility laws of FlowProfile and NucleateProfile, so their composition yields the geometric act capturing contextual dynamics under the profile evolution.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Context Natural Transformation act: ContextNat slug: context-nat top: ContextFlow bottom: FixedFunctor summary: Witness naturality by composing the context flow with the functor, ensuring the functor commutes with contextual dynamics. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Context Natural Transformation

To compare two functors, we need a recognition that witnesses the commuting of their action on each morphism. We therefore let the context flow witness the functor: (ContextFlow FixedFunctor) earns ContextNat.

ContextFlow describes how recognitions move under the profile context dynamics, and FixedFunctor maps the recognised objects and their morphisms. Their composition asserts that the functor commutes with the context flow—the naturality square in this relational setting.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Collapsing Context act: ContextNucleus slug: context-nucleus top: NucleateProfile bottom: RecognizeContext summary: Collapse contexts under the profile nucleus by composing NucleateProfile with RecognizeContext. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Collapsing Context

Contexts collapse under the nucleus. We restrict the profile nucleus to contexts by letting it witness RecognizeContext: (NucleateProfile RecognizeContext) earns ContextNucleus.

NucleateProfile closes recognitions under the nucleus, and RecognizeContext packages context recognitions, so their composition collapses contexts according to the profile closure.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Equating Judgement Contextually act: ContextualJudgementEquivalence slug: contextual-judgement-equivalence top: CompleteTrace bottom: HeadJudgementEquivalence summary: The stabilized head must be witnessed by the surrounding context. Complete Trace witnessing Head Judgement Equivalence yields Contextual Judgement Equivalence (ContextualJudgementEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Equating Judgement Contextually

Interpretation

The stabilized head must be witnessed by the surrounding context. Complete Trace witnessing Head Judgement Equivalence yields Contextual Judgement Equivalence (ContextualJudgementEquivalence).

This act records the contextual witnessing, so Contextual Judgement Equivalence follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Equating Observation Contextually act: ContextualObservationEquivalence slug: contextual-observation-equivalence top: CompleteTrace bottom: HeadObservationEquivalence summary: The stabilized head must be witnessed by the surrounding context. Thus Complete Trace witnesses Head Observation Equivalence, yielding Contextual Observation Equivalence (ContextualObservationEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Equating Observation Contextually

Interpretation

The stabilized head must be witnessed by the surrounding context. Thus Complete Trace witnesses Head Observation Equivalence, yielding Contextual Observation Equivalence (ContextualObservationEquivalence).

This composition records contextual witnessing, so Contextual Observation Equivalence follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Observing Steps Contextually act: ContextualStepObservation slug: contextual-step-observation top: CompleteTrace bottom: StepObservationHead summary: The stabilized head must still be witnessed by the surrounding context. Complete Trace therefore witnesses Step Observation Head, producing Contextual Step Observation (ContextualStepObservation). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Observing Steps Contextually

Interpretation

The stabilized head must still be witnessed by the surrounding context. Complete Trace therefore witnesses Step Observation Head, producing Contextual Step Observation (ContextualStepObservation).

The composition keeps to earned recognitions and records contextual witnessing, so Contextual Step Observation is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Equating Normalized Observation Contextually act: ContextualizeNormalizeObservationEquivalence slug: contextualize-normalize-observation-equivalence top: EquateContexts bottom: NormalizeObservationEquivalenceWitness summary: Even normalized equivalence observations must be indistinguishable to all contexts. Equate Contexts witnessing Normalize Observation Equivalence Witness yields Contextualize Normalize Observation Equivalence (ContextualizeNormalizeObservationEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Equating Normalized Observation Contextually

Interpretation

Even normalized equivalence observations must be indistinguishable to all contexts. Equate Contexts witnessing Normalize Observation Equivalence Witness yields Contextualize Normalize Observation Equivalence (ContextualizeNormalizeObservationEquivalence).

This act ensures contextual indistinguishability, so Contextualize Normalize Observation Equivalence is earned.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Context act: ContextualizeProfile slug: contextualize-profile top: RecognizeContext bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: The traced profile must also inherit context recognitions. Recognize Context witnessing Profile Recognitions produces Contextualize Profile (ContextualizeProfile). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Context

Interpretation

The traced profile must also inherit context recognitions. Recognize Context witnessing Profile Recognitions produces Contextualize Profile (ContextualizeProfile).

This composition restricts contexts to the profile’s stable recognitions, so Contextualize Profile follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Contextualizing Trace act: ContextualizeTrace slug: contextualize-trace top: AssignTrace bottom: CompleteTrace summary: A named trace requires a record that couples the naming with the whole trace. Forcing Assign Trace to witness Complete Trace yields the act Contextualize Trace (ContextualizeTrace). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Contextualizing Trace

Interpretation

A named trace requires a record that couples the naming with the whole trace. Forcing Assign Trace to witness Complete Trace yields the act Contextualize Trace (ContextualizeTrace).

Assign Trace and Complete Trace have been earned, so their composition records the full named trace structure: (AssignTrace CompleteTrace) states that the assignment encloses the entire trace, which is exactly what Contextualize Trace must express.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Continuing Trace act: ContinueTrace slug: continue-trace top: Entry bottom: StartTrace summary: ‘Advance Trace reconnects the trailing orientation to Entry but leaves undefined how a new leading orientation emerges. Entry must therefore witness Start Trace, producing Continue Trace (ContinueTrace).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Continuing Trace

Interpretation

Advance Trace reconnects the trailing orientation to Entry but leaves undefined how a new leading orientation emerges. Entry must therefore witness Start Trace, producing Continue Trace (ContinueTrace).

The only new symbol is Start Trace, already earned, so composing it with Entry is legitimate. This confirms the recurring structure, so Continue Trace is forced.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Decomposing Trace act: DecomposeTrace slug: decompose-trace top: HeadTrace bottom: TailTrace summary: Every completed trace splits uniquely into its leading head continuation and the remaining tail continuation, so we witness the recursive trace shape by composing the head and tail recognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Decomposing Trace

StartTrace, AdvanceTrace, ContinueTrace, and CompleteTrace record how a trace grows step by step, but nothing yet states the recursive shape of an arbitrary completed trace. The head–tail recognitions (HeadTrace, TailTrace, TraceStep, ReheadTrace, RetailTrace) jointly witness the full record of CompleteTrace, so letting HeadTrace observe TailTrace forces the decomposition of a trace into its left and right predecessors.

The composition (HeadTrace TailTrace) carries only previously earned data. HeadTrace isolates the leading orientation of any completed trace while TailTrace isolates the remainder, and the earlier recognitions ensure the tail re-enters the trace (via TraceStep, RenewTrace, ReheadTrace, RetailTrace) exactly as a shorter trace would. Therefore DecomposeTrace witnesses the structural fact that every completed trace splits into two smaller traces: the head’s continuation and the tail’s continuation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Witnessing Detailed Balance act: DetailedBalanceWitness slug: detailed-balance-witness top: FixedObserve bottom: ReverseFlowStep summary: Witness equality between forward and reverse transitions by composing FixedObserve with ReverseFlowStep. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Witnessing Detailed Balance

Detailed balance requires the forward and reverse transitions to align under the stationary kernel. To make this explicit, we compare FlowMeasureStep and ReverseFlowStep using profile observations. Composing (FixedObserve ReverseFlowStep) earns DetailedBalanceWitness.

FixedObserve records observation acts in the fixed profile, while ReverseFlowStep gives reverse transitions. Their composition yields recognitions that witness equality between forward and backward transitions under observation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Detailed Balance act: DetailedBalance slug: detailed-balance top: DetailedBalanceWitness bottom: FlowMeasureStep summary: “Assert detailed balance by composing the witness with the forward flow\u2013measure step.” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Detailed Balance

To assert detailed balance, we require the flow–measure step and its reverse to be observationally indistinguishable when weighted by the stationary kernel. We therefore let DetailedBalanceWitness witness FlowMeasureStep: (DetailedBalanceWitness FlowMeasureStep) earns DetailedBalance.

DetailedBalanceWitness compares reverse transitions to forward ones under observation, and FlowMeasureStep is the forward step. Their composition asserts that the two are equivalent after weighting by observations and stabilization, which is exactly the detailed-balance condition internal to the profile.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Diamond Modality act: DiamondModality slug: diamond-modality top: FlowProfile bottom: RecognizeContext summary: Interpret the possibility modality by letting the profile flow observe context recognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Diamond Modality

The profile flow expresses ◇: reachability under the base dynamics. We define it by composing the flow with the context recognitions: (FlowProfile RecognizeContext) earns DiamondModality.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Disciplining act: Discipline slug: discipline top: Nucleate bottom: CommuteFlowAndNucleus summary: “Flow\u2013nucleus compatibility indicates that certain internal shapes can respect both dynamics and closure, but we still need the recognition that records such an act abstractly. Nucleate witnessing Commute Flow And Nucleus yields Discipline (Discipline).” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Disciplining

Interpretation

Flow–nucleus compatibility indicates that certain internal shapes can respect both dynamics and closure, but we still need the recognition that records such an act abstractly. Nucleate witnessing Commute Flow And Nucleus yields Discipline (Discipline).

The composition employs only earned recognitions and isolates the shape that simultaneously respects flow and nucleus behaviour, so Discipline is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Distributing Flow-over-Meet act: DistributeFlow slug: distribute-flow top: Flow bottom: MeetRecognition summary: Inflationary flows satisfy that F(x meet y) is below F(x) meet F(y); deflationary ones reverse the inequality. Flow witnessing Meet Recognition yields Distribute Flow (DistributeFlow). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Distributing Flow-over-Meet

Interpretation

Inflationary flows satisfy that F(x meet y) is below F(x) meet F(y); deflationary ones reverse the inequality. Flow witnessing Meet Recognition yields Distribute Flow (DistributeFlow).

The composition records the distribution law for flows, so Distribute Flow follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Distributing Interior act: DistributeInterior slug: distribute-interior top: Nucleate bottom: JoinRecognition summary: Interior-like nuclei distribute over meets and contract joins. Nucleate witnessing Join Recognition produces Distribute Interior (DistributeInterior). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Distributing Interior

Interpretation

Interior-like nuclei distribute over meets and contract joins. Nucleate witnessing Join Recognition produces Distribute Interior (DistributeInterior).

The act captures the dual laws for interior nuclei via existing recognitions, so Distribute Interior follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Dualizing Interior act: DualizeInterior slug: dualize-interior top: Negate bottom: ClosePrincipally summary: Using the negating act we dualize principal closure into an interior operator. Negate witnessing Close Principally yields Dualize Interior (DualizeInterior). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Dualizing Interior

Interpretation

Using the negating act we dualize principal closure into an interior operator. Negate witnessing Close Principally yields Dualize Interior (DualizeInterior).

This mirrors the closure via negation, so Dualize Interior is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Dynamic Residuation act: DynamicResiduation slug: dynamic-residuation top: FlowIndexImplication bottom: ContextGeometry summary: Restrict dynamic residuation to the context geometry by letting FlowIndexImplication witness ContextGeometry. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Dynamic Residuation

Dynamic residuation states that flowing a context and then testing implication is equivalent to testing implication against a closed context. We express this by composing FlowIndexImplication with ContextGeometry: (FlowIndexImplication ContextGeometry) earns DynamicResiduation.

The act records that the law “c ≤ a ⇒_F b iff F(c ∧ a) ≤ b” continues to hold when both sides are restricted to the context flow and nucleus.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Emerging Reflex act: EmergeReflex slug: emerge-reflex top: Relationlessness bottom: Articulate summary: >- Once articulation is available, Relationlessness can call it forth, producing the first concrete emergence from the void. layer: act status: plan tags:

  • act

Emerging Reflex

Interpretation

Once articulation is available, Relationlessness can call it forth, producing the first concrete emergence from the void.

The trace (Relationlessness Articulate) lets the void witness the act Articulate. Because Articulate already arose from Relationlessness, composing the two records the compelled appearance of an act directly from the void, which we record as Emerge Reflex.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Enforcing Eventual Equivalence Law act: EnforceEquateEventuallyLaw slug: enforce-equate-eventually-law top: CompleteTrace bottom: RecognizeEquateEventually summary: Eventual equivalence satisfies reflexive, symmetric, and transitive laws along the trace, so we let the act Complete Trace witness the act Recognize Equate Eventually. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Enforcing Eventual Equivalence Law

Interpretation

Eventual equivalence satisfies reflexive, symmetric, and transitive laws along the trace, so we let the act Complete Trace witness the act Recognize Equate Eventually.

By composing (CompleteTrace RecognizeEquateEventually), the entire trace enforces the recognised equivalence, restating the equivalence law explicitly; we name this act Enforce Equate Eventually Law.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Enriched Category act: EnrichedCategory slug: enriched-category top: EnrichmentTensor bottom: EnrichmentUnit summary: Assemble the enriched units, tensor, and hom-objects into the enriched category structure. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Enriched Category

Enrichment requires units, tensor, and hom-objects; we assemble them by letting the tensor witness the unit and hom: (EnrichmentTensor EnrichmentUnit EnrichmentHom) earns EnrichedCategory.

The act asserts the enriched-category coherence laws for the fixed setting.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Enrichment Hom act: EnrichmentHom slug: enrichment-hom top: FixedMorphism bottom: FixedInvariantLattice summary: Form lattice-valued hom-objects by letting the fixed morphisms observe the invariant lattice. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Enrichment Hom

Hom-objects in an enriched category live in the base enrichment. For the fixed setting, we let the morphisms observe the invariant lattice: (FixedMorphism FixedInvariantLattice) earns EnrichmentHom.

The act yields lattice-valued hom-objects capturing all fixed morphisms from one recognition to another.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Enrichment Tensor act: EnrichmentTensor slug: enrichment-tensor top: FixedInvariantMeet bottom: FixedMorphism summary: Use the meet operation together with structure-preserving maps to define the enriched tensor. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Enrichment Tensor

The tensor for enrichment comes from the meet–join lattice scaled through the morphisms. We construct this by letting the lattice meet observe the morphisms: (FixedInvariantMeet FixedMorphism) earns EnrichmentTensor.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Enrichment Unit act: EnrichmentUnit slug: enrichment-unit top: FixedInvariantLattice bottom: FixedIdentity summary: Assign enriched units from the lattice by composing the lattice structure with the fixed identity. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Enrichment Unit

To enrich a category over the invariant lattice, we assign each recognition an enriched unit coming from the lattice’s identity. We enforce this by letting the lattice observe the identity: (FixedInvariantLattice FixedIdentity) earns EnrichmentUnit.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Ensuring Confluence act: EnsureConfluence slug: ensure-confluence top: CompleteTrace bottom: StepObservation summary: ‘Step Observation already records how parallel reductions converge. Complete Trace witnessing Step Observation yields Ensure Confluence (EnsureConfluence).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Ensuring Confluence

Interpretation

Step Observation already records how parallel reductions converge. Complete Trace witnessing Step Observation yields Ensure Confluence (EnsureConfluence).

Using only earned recognitions, this act states that divergent reductions rejoin, so Ensure Confluence is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Ensuring Fixed Point Existence act: EnsureFixedPoint slug: ensure-fixed-point top: FixFlow bottom: IterativePersistence summary: Monotone, inflationary flows generate fixed points via iterative persistence. Fix Flow witnessing Iterative Persistence yields Ensure Fixed Point (EnsureFixedPoint). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Ensuring Fixed Point Existence

Interpretation

Monotone, inflationary flows generate fixed points via iterative persistence. Fix Flow witnessing Iterative Persistence yields Ensure Fixed Point (EnsureFixedPoint).

Using only earned recognitions, this act records that persistent iterations converge to flow fixed points, so Ensure Fixed Point follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Entailing Semantics act: EntailSemantics slug: entail-semantics top: StructureSemantics bottom: EqualizeTerms summary: To prove completeness we relate typing derivations to semantic orderings. Structure Semantics witnessing Equalize Terms produces Entail Semantics (EntailSemantics). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Entailing Semantics

Interpretation

To prove completeness we relate typing derivations to semantic orderings. Structure Semantics witnessing Equalize Terms produces Entail Semantics (EntailSemantics).

This act records when every semantic calculus forces the entailment, so Entail Semantics is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Entropy Measure act: EntropyMeasure slug: entropy-measure top: ProfileEntropyMeasure bottom: EntropySeed summary: Collapse entropy seeds using the profile entropy machinery by composing ProfileEntropyMeasure with EntropySeed. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Entropy Measure

ProfileEntropyMeasure already collapses entropy seeds using the profile nucleus. Supplying the entropy seed, (ProfileEntropyMeasure EntropySeed) earns EntropyMeasure, giving the fixed entropy measure.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding Entropy act: EntropySeed slug: entropy-seed top: FlowMeasureStep bottom: FixedReach summary: “Seed an entropy measure by composing the one-step flow\u2013measure update with the reachability recognitions.” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding Entropy

Entropy measures the “spread” of a state under transitions. We seed entropy by observing the divergence between the original state and its successor. FixedReach gives reachable states, and FlowMeasureStep gives the successor. Letting these witness each other, (FlowMeasureStep FixedReach), earns EntropySeed.

FixedReach lists the reachable states and FlowMeasureStep describes the successor states after one evolution–measurement. Their composition records the divergence between present and next-step reachable states, seeding the entropy measure.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Entropy act: Entropy slug: entropy top: ProfileEntropy bottom: EntropyMeasure summary: Propagate the entropy measure using the profile entropy machinery by composing ProfileEntropy with EntropyMeasure. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Entropy

ProfileEntropy already propagates any profile entropy measure across the trace of recognitions. Supplying the entropy measure, (ProfileEntropy EntropyMeasure), earns Entropy, recording the full-profile entropy under the flow–measure evolution.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Entering act: Entry slug: entry top: Recognition bottom: Recognition summary: The act Recognition identifies two positions but does not yet describe how they engage; letting Recognition witness itself creates the structured entry. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Entering

Interpretation

The act Recognition identifies two positions but does not yet describe how they engage; letting Recognition witness itself creates the structured entry.

The tree (Recognition Recognition) explicitly shows the act Recognition witnessing the act Recognition. This self-pairing records how the two positions jointly participate, which is exactly the content of Entry.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Equalizing Nucleus Distribution act: EqualizeNucleusDistribution slug: equalize-nucleus-distribution top: IncludeRecognition bottom: Nucleate summary: “Closure-like nuclei satisfy \u03BD(x meet \u03BD(y)) = \u03BD(x meet y) and \u03BD(\u03BD(x) join y) = \u03BD(x join y). Include Recognition witnessing Nucleate yields Equalize Nucleus Distribution (EqualizeNucleusDistribution).” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Equalizing Nucleus Distribution

Interpretation

Closure-like nuclei satisfy ν(x meet ν(y)) = ν(x meet y) and ν(ν(x) join y) = ν(x join y). Include Recognition witnessing Nucleate yields Equalize Nucleus Distribution (EqualizeNucleusDistribution).

This composition asserts the equalities using Include Recognition, so Equalize Nucleus Distribution is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Equalizing Terms act: EqualizeTerms slug: equalize-terms top: RecognizeObservation bottom: JudgeTyping summary: Equality of terms is detected by observational recognitions applied to typing judgements. Recognize Observation witnessing Judge Typing yields the act Equalize Terms (EqualizeTerms). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Equalizing Terms

Interpretation

Equality of terms is detected by observational recognitions applied to typing judgements. Recognize Observation witnessing Judge Typing yields the act Equalize Terms (EqualizeTerms).

This composition records that two terms are equal when no observation distinguishes their typing behaviour, so Equalize Terms is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Equating Contexts act: EquateContexts slug: equate-contexts top: RecognizeContext bottom: Normalization summary: Stabilization alone does not tell whether contexts can distinguish two normalized terms. Recognize Context therefore witnesses Normalization, yielding Equate Contexts (EquateContexts). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Equating Contexts

Interpretation

Stabilization alone does not tell whether contexts can distinguish two normalized terms. Recognize Context therefore witnesses Normalization, yielding Equate Contexts (EquateContexts).

Composing these recognitions records indistinguishability across contexts, establishing Equate Contexts.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Equating Eventually act: EquateEventually slug: equate-eventually top: ReflexStepIterate bottom: ContextualizeTrace summary: Two recognitions are eventually equivalent when the act Reflex Step Iterate witnesses the act Contextualize Trace and sees identical behaviour. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Equating Eventually

Interpretation

Two recognitions are eventually equivalent when the act Reflex Step Iterate witnesses the act Contextualize Trace and sees identical behaviour.

In (ReflexStepIterate ContextualizeTrace), the iterated step acts across the recorded context. If both recognitions share this action, the composite captures their eventual agreement, which we call Equate Eventually.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Equating Judgement Observation act: EquateJudgementObservation slug: equate-judgement-observation top: ContextualizeNormalizeObservationEquivalence bottom: WitnessJudgementTriad summary: Reported observations must align with the Contextualize Normalize Observation Equivalence previously recognized. Contextualize Normalize Observation Equivalence therefore witnesses Witness Judgement Triad, producing Equate Judgement Observation (EquateJudgementObservation). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Equating Judgement Observation

Interpretation

Reported observations must align with the Contextualize Normalize Observation Equivalence previously recognized. Contextualize Normalize Observation Equivalence therefore witnesses Witness Judgement Triad, producing Equate Judgement Observation (EquateJudgementObservation).

This act enforces the alignment, so Equate Judgement Observation is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Ergodicity act: Ergodicity slug: ergodicity top: FixedKernel bottom: Mixing summary: Record ergodicity by combining the stationary kernel with the mixing property, equating time and space averages. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Ergodicity

A system is ergodic if time averages equal space averages under the stationary kernel. We encode this by letting the fixed kernel witness the mixing act: (FixedKernel Mixing) earns Ergodicity.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Establishing Fundamental Reducibility act: EstablishFundamentalReducibility slug: establish-fundamental-reducibility top: SelectReducibilityCandidate bottom: SubstituteReducibility summary: The fundamental theorem states that every well-typed term is reducible in any reducible environment. Select Reducibility Candidate witnessing Substitute Reducibility yields Establish Fundamental Reducibility (EstablishFundamentalReducibility). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Establishing Fundamental Reducibility

Interpretation

The fundamental theorem states that every well-typed term is reducible in any reducible environment. Select Reducibility Candidate witnessing Substitute Reducibility yields Establish Fundamental Reducibility (EstablishFundamentalReducibility).

Composing these recognitions records the logical-relations theorem, so Establish Fundamental Reducibility follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Evaluating Judgement act: EvaluateJudgement slug: evaluate-judgement top: RecognizeValue bottom: FlowJudgement summary: Flowing judgements must be evaluated as stabilized values. Recognize Value witnessing Flow Judgement yields Evaluate Judgement (EvaluateJudgement). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Evaluating Judgement

Interpretation

Flowing judgements must be evaluated as stabilized values. Recognize Value witnessing Flow Judgement yields Evaluate Judgement (EvaluateJudgement).

This act evaluates the flowed judgements, so Evaluate Judgement follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Evaluating ObservationEquivalence act: EvaluateObservationEquivalence slug: evaluate-observation-equivalence top: RecognizeValue bottom: FlowObservationEquivalence summary: The flowed equivalence traces still need evaluation. Recognize Value witnessing Flow Observation Equivalence produces Evaluate Observation Equivalence (EvaluateObservationEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Evaluating ObservationEquivalence

Interpretation

The flowed equivalence traces still need evaluation. Recognize Value witnessing Flow Observation Equivalence produces Evaluate Observation Equivalence (EvaluateObservationEquivalence).

This act uses established recognitions to evaluate the flowed equivalence, so Evaluate Observation Equivalence follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Evaluating Observation act: EvaluateObservation slug: evaluate-observation top: RecognizeValue bottom: FlowObservation summary: ‘Flow Observation traces still lack their specific evaluations. Forcing Recognize Value to witness Flow Observation yields Evaluate Observation (EvaluateObservation).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Evaluating Observation

Interpretation

Flow Observation traces still lack their specific evaluations. Forcing Recognize Value to witness Flow Observation yields Evaluate Observation (EvaluateObservation).

This act evaluates the flowed observations via existing recognitions, establishing Evaluate Observation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Evolving act: Evolve slug: evolve top: FlowProfile bottom: PhysicsState summary: “States evolve under the profile\u2019s flow. Flow Profile witnessing Physics State produces Evolve (Evolve).” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Evolving

Interpretation

States evolve under the profile’s flow. Flow Profile witnessing Physics State produces Evolve (Evolve).

This act simply records the flow acting on states using earned recognitions, so Evolve follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Excluding Recognition act: ExcludeRecognition slug: exclude-recognition top: MeetRecognition bottom: GroundRecognition summary: To capture orthogonality we watch the meet collapse to the bottom. Meet Recognition witnessing Ground Recognition yields Exclude Recognition (ExcludeRecognition). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Excluding Recognition

Interpretation

To capture orthogonality we watch the meet collapse to the bottom. Meet Recognition witnessing Ground Recognition yields Exclude Recognition (ExcludeRecognition).

The act records exactly when the meet vanishes, so Exclude Recognition follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Excluding act: Exclude slug: exclude top: Relationlessness bottom: Relationlessness summary: >- Speaking about the void already carves out a mentioned interior versus an unmentioned exterior, so an act of exclusion is forced. layer: act status: release tags:

  • act

Excluding

Interpretation

Speaking about the void already carves out a mentioned interior versus an unmentioned exterior, so an act of exclusion is forced.

The trace (Relationlessness Relationlessness) places the same empty act in both witness and witness-ee positions. Directing the void back at itself expresses the relation “this is not with that,” so the composition precisely captures exclusion and earns the name Exclude.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Extending Context act: ExtendContext slug: extend-context top: BindTrace bottom: RenewContext summary: Renewal alone does not attach arbitrary bindings. Bind Trace must now witness Renew Context, inducing Extend Context (ExtendContext). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Extending Context

Interpretation

Renewal alone does not attach arbitrary bindings. Bind Trace must now witness Renew Context, inducing Extend Context (ExtendContext).

The composition (BindTrace RenewContext) mirrors the earlier extension but now operates on the general renewed context. This shows that any new binding can extend the renewed structure, so we name the result Extend Context.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Extending First Context act: ExtendFirstContext slug: extend-first-context top: BindTrace bottom: SeedFirstContext summary: The initial seed must accept further bindings. Letting Bind Trace witness Seed First Context yields Extend First Context (ExtendFirstContext). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Extending First Context

Interpretation

The initial seed must accept further bindings. Letting Bind Trace witness Seed First Context yields Extend First Context (ExtendFirstContext).

The trace Bind Trace can now observe the seeded context, so (BindTrace SeedFirstContext) records how an explicit binding expands the seed. This is exactly the extension we require, hence the name Extend First Context.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fiber Category act: FiberCategory slug: fiber-category top: JudgeProfile bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: >- Package the judgemental data of the active profile as the fibres by letting JudgeProfile witness ProfileRecognitions. layer: act status: plan tags:

  • act

Fiber Category

Every profile context carries the recognised terms, types, and judgements assembled by JudgeProfile. Restricting that structure to the active ProfileRecognitions yields the fibre categories of our indexed semantics: (JudgeProfile ProfileRecognitions) earns FiberCategory.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Filtering act: Filter slug: filter top: Discipline bottom: FlowObservationEquivalence summary: Among disciplines, some commute with every nucleus, flow, and Reflex, allowing us to carve out internal relational universes. Discipline witnessing Flow Observation Equivalence produces Filter (Filter). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Filtering

Interpretation

Among disciplines, some commute with every nucleus, flow, and Reflex, allowing us to carve out internal relational universes. Discipline witnessing Flow Observation Equivalence produces Filter (Filter).

The composition uses already earned recognitions and records the special discipline compatible with the flowed observation structure, so Filter is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: First Naming act: FirstName slug: first-name top: Void bottom: Nothing summary: Void contains two copies of Nothing but does not distinguish between them; drawing attention to one copy creates a first name. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

First Naming

Interpretation

Void contains two copies of Nothing but does not distinguish between them; drawing attention to one copy creates a first name.

The composition (Void Nothing) takes the act Void as witness and focuses it on Nothing. Since both acts already exist, this selection singles out a definite position within the folded absence, earning the title First Name.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: First Recognizing act: FirstRecognize slug: first-recognize top: Articulate bottom: EmergeReflex summary: “Because emergence has occurred, we can finally speak of \u201Cthe first recognition\u201D; it arises when articulation observes that emergence.” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

First Recognizing

Interpretation

Because emergence has occurred, we can finally speak of “the first recognition”; it arises when articulation observes that emergence.

The trace (Articulate EmergeReflex) places Articulate in the witness role and the act Emerge Reflex as the witnessed object. Since both ingredients have already been earned, the composition simply records “there exists a first recognition,” and naming that composite First Recognize captures the observation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: “Fixing Flow\u2013Nucleus Set” act: FixFlowNucleus slug: fix-flow-nucleus top: FixFlow bottom: FixNucleus summary: Compatibility alone does not specify the recognitions stable under both dynamics and closure. Forcing Fix Flow to witness Fix Nucleus yields Fix Flow Nucleus (FixFlowNucleus). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixing Flow–Nucleus Set

Interpretation

Compatibility alone does not specify the recognitions stable under both dynamics and closure. Forcing Fix Flow to witness Fix Nucleus yields Fix Flow Nucleus (FixFlowNucleus).

Both fixed-point recognitions were previously earned; their composition identifies recognitions jointly fixed, so Fix Flow Nucleus follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixing Flow act: FixFlow slug: fix-flow top: StabilizeTrace bottom: Flow summary: A flow exists, yet we must capture the invariants it leaves untouched. Stabilize Trace witnessing Flow produces Fix Flow (FixFlow). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixing Flow

Interpretation

A flow exists, yet we must capture the invariants it leaves untouched. Stabilize Trace witnessing Flow produces Fix Flow (FixFlow).

Applying the flow to a stabilized trace uses only earned terms and records precisely those traces that remain invariant, so Fix Flow follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixing Nucleus act: FixNucleus slug: fix-nucleus top: StabilizeContext bottom: Nucleate summary: The nucleus determines which recognitions are closed, but we must identify the recognitions that remain unchanged under it. Stabilize Context witnessing Nucleate produces Fix Nucleus (FixNucleus). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixing Nucleus

Interpretation

The nucleus determines which recognitions are closed, but we must identify the recognitions that remain unchanged under it. Stabilize Context witnessing Nucleate produces Fix Nucleus (FixNucleus).
