This composition stays within earned terms and records contexts that the nucleus fixes, so Fix Nucleus follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Chain act: FixedChain slug: fixed-chain top: EnsureFixedPoint bottom: ProfileKernelChain summary: Apply the fixed-point construction to the profile kernel chain to obtain its stationary profile-level distribution. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Chain

A stationary chain is one whose profile-level transitions stabilise. ProfileKernelChain iterates the kernel update across profiles, so we apply the fixed-point machinery via EnsureFixedPoint: (EnsureFixedPoint ProfileKernelChain) earns FixedChain.

IterativePersistence ensures that persistent iteration produces a stabilised profile. The composite therefore yields the stationary chain: a profile-level distribution that remains unchanged after any flow–measure update.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Composition act: FixedCompose slug: fixed-compose top: ComposeMorph bottom: FixedMorphism summary: “Compose \u03C6-compatible morphisms by restricting ComposeMorph to the fixed ones.” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Composition

We need composition of fixed morphisms. ComposeMorph = (Morph Morph) composes structure-preserving maps, and restricting it to FixedMorphism yields φ-compatible composition: (ComposeMorph FixedMorphism) earns FixedCompose.

ComposeMorph already composes global morphisms. Composing only those in FixedMorphism ensures the structure is preserved under φ-stability, providing the composition law for fixed functors.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Dynamics act: FixedDynamics slug: fixed-dynamics top: ProfileDynamics bottom: FixedPhaseSpace summary: Restrict the profile dynamics to fixed phase-space states to define the fixed dynamics. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Dynamics

Dynamics describe how the phase space evolves. Starting from the general ProfileDynamics, restricting to FixedPhaseSpace yields the fixed dynamics: (ProfileDynamics FixedPhaseSpace) earns FixedDynamics.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Exponential act: FixedExponential slug: fixed-exponential top: ProfileExponential bottom: FixedProfileRecognitions summary: Restrict the profile exponentials further to fixed recognitions by composing ProfileExponential with FixedProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Exponential

The profile already has internal exponentials via ProfileExponential. To work entirely inside the fixed profile we restrict those exponentials to FixedProfileRecognitions: (ProfileExponential FixedProfileRecognitions) earns FixedExponential.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Fiber Category act: FixedFiberCategory slug: fixed-fiber-category top: FiberCategory bottom: FixedProfileRecognitions summary: Restrict the fibre categories further to fixed recognitions by composing FiberCategory with FixedProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Fiber Category

Over each fixed context we have the general fibre categories encoded by FiberCategory. Intersecting them with FixedProfileRecognitions restricts the judgements to the fixed profile: (FiberCategory FixedProfileRecognitions) earns FixedFiberCategory.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Finite Limits act: FixedFiniteLimits slug: fixed-finite-limits top: ProfileFiniteLimits bottom: FixedInvariantLattice summary: Restrict the profile finite limits to the fixed invariant lattice to witness finite limits inside the fixed profile. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Finite Limits

ProfileFiniteLimits already witnesses finite limits inside the active profile. Restricting those limits to the fixed invariant lattice, (ProfileFiniteLimits FixedInvariantLattice), earns FixedFiniteLimits.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Flow Profile act: FixedFlowProfile slug: fixed-flow-profile top: ShuffleFlow bottom: FixedProfileRecognitions summary: Restrict the shuffle flow to the fixed-profile recognitions by letting ShuffleFlow witness FixedProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Flow Profile

FlowProfile = (Flow ProfileRecognitions) restricts the global flow to a given profile. To specialize this to the fixed-profile recognitions we let ShuffleFlow witness FixedProfileRecognitions: (ShuffleFlow FixedProfileRecognitions) earns FixedFlowProfile.

ShuffleFlow evolves admissible trace-shuffle classes along evaluation contexts, and FixedProfileRecognitions picks out recognitions stabilized by the same dynamics and fixed shape. The composition restricts the shuffle flow to this profile, yielding a dynamics where every state evolves according to that trace structure while preserving the fixed shape.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Functor act: FixedFunctor slug: fixed-functor top: Spectrum bottom: FixedMorphism summary: Map fixed recognitions via spectral decomposition while preserving morphisms by composing Spectrum with FixedMorphism. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Functor

A fixed functor maps each fixed recognition to another fixed recognition in a way compatible with the fixed morphisms, identity, and composition. We encode this by letting the spectral action witness the morphism structure: (Spectrum FixedMorphism) earns FixedFunctor.

Spectrum decomposes each recognition into harmonic components, and FixedMorphism records the allowed structure-preserving maps between fixed recognitions. Their composition ensures that objects are mapped via spectral decomposition while morphisms are transported compatibly, automatically preserving identities and composition.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Geometry Profile act: FixedGeometryProfile slug: fixed-geometry-profile top: FixedFlowProfile bottom: FixedNucleateProfile summary: Combine the restricted flow and nucleus of the fixed profile to form its geometry. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Geometry Profile

A geometry on a profile combines its restricted flow and restricted nucleus. For the fixed profile we pair FixedFlowProfile with FixedNucleateProfile: (FixedFlowProfile FixedNucleateProfile) earns FixedGeometryProfile.

FixedFlowProfile provides the shuffle-derived dynamics on the fixed recognitions, and FixedNucleateProfile supplies the corresponding closure. Their composition packages the compatible pair as the geometry of the fixed profile.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Horizontal Composition act: HorizontalCompose slug: horizontal-compose top: TwoCell bottom: FixedCompose summary: Compose 2-cells horizontally by letting the 2-cells witness the composition of 1-cells. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Horizontal Composition

Horizontal composition in a bicategory composes 2-cells along adjacent 1-cells. We obtain this by letting the 2-cells witness the composition of morphisms: (TwoCell FixedCompose) earns HorizontalCompose.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Identity act: FixedIdentity slug: fixed-identity top: IdentifyMorph bottom: FixedProfileRecognitions summary: Restrict identity morphisms to fixed recognitions by composing IdentifyMorph with FixedProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Identity

A category needs identity morphisms. IdentifyMorph = (Morph Reflex) provides identities in the full profile. We restrict it to the fixed-recognition objects by composing with FixedProfileRecognitions: (IdentifyMorph FixedProfileRecognitions) earns FixedIdentity.

IdentifyMorph identifies the identity on any recognition, and restricting its domain to the fixed ones gives identity morphisms for the fixed category.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Geometry of Fixed Invariant States act: FixedInvariantGeometry slug: fixed-invariant-geometry top: ProfileInvariantGeometry bottom: FixedInvariantLattice summary: Restrict the profile invariant geometry to the fixed lattice by composing ProfileInvariantGeometry with FixedInvariantLattice. - free tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Geometry of Fixed Invariant States

ProfileInvariantGeometry = (GeometryProfile ProfileInvariantLattice) packages the geometry of profile-invariant states. FixedInvariantLattice exhibits the order structure of fixed invariant states. Restricting the profile geometry to this lattice, (ProfileInvariantGeometry FixedInvariantLattice) earns FixedInvariantGeometry.

ProfileInvariantGeometry provides flow and nucleus for the profile invariants, and FixedInvariantLattice describes the meet–join order on states fixed by the flow and compatible with FixedShape. Their composition packages the order, closure, and dynamics of fixed invariant states as a geometric structure in its own right.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Joining Fixed Invariant act: FixedInvariantJoin slug: fixed-invariant-join top: ProfileInvariantJoin bottom: FixedInvariant summary: Restrict the join operation to fixed invariant states by composing ProfileInvariantJoin with FixedInvariant. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Joining Fixed Invariant

ProfileInvariantJoin = (JoinRecognition ProfileInvariant) captures the least recognition containing a pair of profile invariants. Restricting it via FixedInvariant yields the join of fixed invariant states: (ProfileInvariantJoin FixedInvariant) earns FixedInvariantJoin.

ProfileInvariantJoin works relative to the restricted order, and FixedInvariant narrows the domain to the fixed subset, producing the least fixed invariant containing the given pair.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Structuring Fixed Invariant Lattice act: FixedInvariantLattice slug: fixed-invariant-lattice top: ProfileInvariantLattice bottom: FixedInvariantOrder summary: Restrict the profile invariant lattice to the fixed order by composing ProfileInvariantLattice with FixedInvariantOrder. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Structuring Fixed Invariant Lattice

ProfileInvariantLattice = (StructureSemantics ProfileInvariantOrder) packages the lattice structure on profile invariants. Restricting it along FixedInvariantOrder yields the lattice structure on fixed invariants: (ProfileInvariantLattice FixedInvariantOrder) earns FixedInvariantLattice.

ProfileInvariantLattice encapsulates the algebraic laws of meets, joins, and implication on the restricted order, and FixedInvariantOrder narrows that order to the fixed invariants. Their composition records that the fixed invariant states inherit the same lattice structure.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Meeting Fixed Invariant act: FixedInvariantMeet slug: fixed-invariant-meet top: ProfileInvariantMeet bottom: FixedInvariant summary: Restrict the meet operation to fixed invariant states by composing ProfileInvariantMeet with FixedInvariant. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Meeting Fixed Invariant

ProfileInvariantMeet = (MeetRecognition ProfileInvariant) already computes meets for profile invariants. Restricting it along FixedInvariant gives the meet on fixed invariants: (ProfileInvariantMeet FixedInvariant) earns FixedInvariantMeet.

ProfileInvariantMeet defines meets with respect to the restricted order, and FixedInvariant limits the domain to the fixed states. Their composition produces the meet operation on fixed invariant states: the greatest fixed invariant below any given pair.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Ordering Fixed Invariant act: FixedInvariantOrder slug: fixed-invariant-order top: ProfileInvariantOrder bottom: FixedInvariant summary: Restrict the invariant order further to fixed states by composing ProfileInvariantOrder with FixedInvariant. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Ordering Fixed Invariant

ProfileInvariantOrder = (OrderRecognition ProfileInvariant) already restricts the recognition order to profile invariants. FixedInvariant further limits us to the fixed-invariant physics states. Composing them, (ProfileInvariantOrder FixedInvariant) earns FixedInvariantOrder.

FixedInvariant is a collection of recognitions that are both flow-fixed and fixed-profile-stable. Restricting the profile order along these recognitions yields the partial order underlying the fixed statespace geometry.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Stabilizing Fixed Invariant act: FixedInvariant slug: fixed-invariant top: ProfileInvariant bottom: FixedProfileRecognitions summary: Pick out physics states that are both profile-invariant and lie inside the fixed recognitions by composing ProfileInvariant with FixedProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Stabilizing Fixed Invariant

ProfileInvariant = (StabilizeInvariant ProfileRecognitions) already records states left unchanged by the global flow and recognised inside the active profile. FixedProfileRecognitions further restricts recognitions to the shuffle-stable fixed subset. Composing them, (ProfileInvariant FixedProfileRecognitions) earns FixedInvariant.

Any state in ProfileInvariant is fixed by the global flow and lies in the base profile, and any recognition in FixedProfileRecognitions is fixed by ShuffleFlow, Nucleate, and compatible with FixedShape. Their composition picks states satisfying all constraints—they are genuine physical states, fixed by the flow, and lie in the fixed recognition region. These are the fixed invariant states of the system.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Kernel act: FixedKernel slug: fixed-kernel top: EnsureFixedPoint bottom: ObservationKernel summary: Apply the fixed-point construction to the observation kernel to obtain its stationary behaviour. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Kernel

A fixed kernel is one whose transition effects stabilise under repeated application. Since ObservationKernel acts like a relational operator on fixed states and EnsureFixedPoint constructs fixed points of persistent flows, we apply it directly to the kernel: (EnsureFixedPoint ObservationKernel) earns FixedKernel.

ObservationKernel is the evolution–observation operator on fixed invariant states. IterativePersistence ensures that when iterating this operator along the trace stabilises, the stable point is a fixed point of the kernel. Their composition yields the stationary recognition: the kernel that leaves the fixed distribution of states unchanged.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Measure act: FixedMeasure slug: fixed-measure top: ProfileMeasure bottom: FixedInvariant summary: Restrict the profile measure to fixed invariant states by composing ProfileMeasure with FixedInvariant. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Measure

ProfileMeasure already applies the profile nucleus to the profile-invariant states. Restricting it along FixedInvariant yields the fixed measure: (ProfileMeasure FixedInvariant) earns FixedMeasure.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Monad Multiplication act: FixedMonadMultiply slug: fixed-monad-multiply top: FixedNucleateProfile bottom: FixedNucleateProfile summary: Record the monad multiplication by composing the fixed nucleus with itself, yielding the idempotent collapse of double closure. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Monad Multiplication

Applying the nucleus twice should collapse as a single closure. The monad multiplication identifies N(N(X)) with N(X), so we let the nucleus observe its own action: (FixedNucleateProfile FixedNucleateProfile) earns FixedMonadMultiply.

FixedNucleateProfile already idempotently closes recognitions, and the composition records the multiplication μ_X : N(N(X)) → N(X) implementing this idempotence.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Monad Unit act: FixedMonadUnit slug: fixed-monad-unit top: FixedIdentity bottom: FixedNucleateProfile summary: Inject fixed recognitions into their closure by composing the identity with the fixed nucleus, yielding the monad unit. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Monad Unit

FixedNucleateProfile acts as a closure on fixed recognitions, and the monad unit injects each recognition into its closure. We witness this by letting the identity observe the nucleus: (FixedIdentity FixedNucleateProfile) earns FixedMonadUnit.

FixedIdentity gives the identity morphism on each fixed object, and FixedNucleateProfile sends each recognition to its fixed closure. Their composition states that each object is mapped into its closure via the identity, i.e. (eta)_X : X → N(X).

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Monad act: FixedMonad slug: fixed-monad top: FixedMonadUnit bottom: FixedMonadMultiply summary: Package the nucleus functor, unit, and multiplication into a single monad on the fixed profile. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Monad

Together, the nucleus functor, unit, and multiplication form a monad on the fixed profile, so we package these laws in one act: (FixedMonadUnit FixedMonadMultiply) earns FixedMonad.

The usual monad laws (associativity, left/right unit) follow from the idempotence and compatibility already earned for FixedNucleateProfile (e.g. AbsorbClosure, EqualizeNucleusDistribution). FixedMonadUnit and FixedMonadMultiply are the structural maps, and FixedMonad names the resulting closure monad.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Morphism act: FixedMorphism slug: fixed-morphism top: Morph bottom: FixedProfileRecognitions summary: Restrict structure-preserving maps to the fixed recognitions by composing Morph with FixedProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Morphism

To treat the fixed profile as a category we need morphisms preserving the fixed structure. Morph records structure-preserving maps between recognitions, and FixedProfileRecognitions restricts recognitions to fixed ones, so we let the morph act witness the profile: (Morph FixedProfileRecognitions) earns FixedMorphism.

Morph already preserves meets, joins, and implication, and restricting it via the fixed recognitions ensures the resulting morphisms respect the same flow, nucleus, and shape properties.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Normalizing Fixed Observation act: FixedNormalizeObservation slug: fixed-normalize-observation top: Normalization bottom: FixedObserve summary: Ensure fixed observables normalize by composing Normalization with FixedObserve. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Normalizing Fixed Observation

NormalizeObservation = (Normalization EvaluateObservation) ensures evaluated observations normalize back to the observation witness. In the fixed profile, we want the same behaviour, but only for fixed observables. We therefore compose Normalization with FixedObserve: (Normalization FixedObserve) earns FixedNormalizeObservation.

Normalization detects when reduction along StepTrace stabilizes, and FixedObserve provides observables restricted to fixed recognitions. Their composition records that fixed observables evaluated through the fixed flow eventually stabilize.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Nucleus Profile act: FixedNucleateProfile slug: fixed-nucleate-profile top: Nucleate bottom: FixedProfileRecognitions summary: Specialize the nucleus closure to the fixed profile by composing Nucleate with FixedProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Nucleus Profile

The fixed profile must inherit the closure behaviour of the nucleus. NucleateProfile = (Nucleate ProfileRecognitions) did this for a generic profile. We specialize by letting Nucleate witness FixedProfileRecognitions: (Nucleate FixedProfileRecognitions) earns FixedNucleateProfile.

Nucleate is the closure act derived from the normalized observation and judgement equivalences, while FixedProfileRecognitions is a closed subcollection of recognitions. Their composition restricts the closure operation to the fixed profile domain, yielding the nucleus appropriate to the fixed recognitions.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Recognizing Fixed Observation act: FixedObservationRecognitions slug: fixed-observation-recognitions top: ProfileObservationRecognitions bottom: FixedProfileRecognitions summary: “Further restrict the profile\u2019s observation recognitions to the fixed subset by composing ProfileObservationRecognitions with FixedProfileRecognitions.” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Recognizing Fixed Observation

ProfileObservationRecognitions gathers the observation witnesses permitted by the active profile, while FixedProfileRecognitions further restricts them to the fixed subset. Composing them, (ProfileObservationRecognitions FixedProfileRecognitions) earns FixedObservationRecognitions.

The profile observation recognitions already respect the flow–nucleus filter, and FixedProfileRecognitions tightens the admissible contexts to the fixed geometry, so only fixed observations remain.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Observe act: FixedObserve slug: fixed-observe top: FixedObservationRecognitions bottom: SeedTerm summary: Observe traced terms inside the fixed profile by composing FixedObservationRecognitions with SeedTerm. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Observe

The profile-level observable ProfileObserve uses ProfileObservationRecognitions. To stay within the fixed profile we replace that recogniser by FixedObservationRecognitions: (FixedObservationRecognitions SeedTerm) earns FixedObserve.

SeedTerm inserts the trace head into a recognised context, producing the basic term to be observed, and the fixed observation recognitions restrict those observables to the fixed profile.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Operator act: FixedOperator slug: fixed-operator top: FixedFunctor bottom: FixedProfileRecognitions summary: Apply fixed functors to fixed recognitions to obtain spectral operators. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Operator

Fixed functors act as operators transforming recognitions harmonically. We define a fixed operator by letting the functor observe the profile recognitions: (FixedFunctor FixedProfileRecognitions) earns FixedOperator.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Phase Space act: FixedPhaseSpace slug: fixed-phase-space top: ProfilePhaseSpace bottom: FixedInvariant summary: Restrict the profile phase space to fixed invariants by composing ProfilePhaseSpace with FixedInvariant. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Phase Space

A dynamical system begins with a phase space of states. The profile already provides one via ProfilePhaseSpace. Restricting it to FixedInvariant yields the fixed phase space: (ProfilePhaseSpace FixedInvariant) earns FixedPhaseSpace.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Physics Profile act: FixedPhysicsProfile slug: fixed-physics-profile top: FlowObservation bottom: FixedFlowProfile summary: Couple observation traces to the fixed flow by composing FlowObservation with FixedFlowProfile, yielding the physics of the fixed profile. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Physics Profile

PhysicsProfile = (FlowObservation FlowProfile) lifts a profile’s flow to its physical semantics by coupling dynamics with observation. In the fixed profile we want observations of the fixed flow, so we let FlowObservation witness FixedFlowProfile: (FlowObservation FixedFlowProfile) earns FixedPhysicsProfile.

FlowObservation records how observations traverse evaluation contexts under a given flow, and FixedFlowProfile is the shuffle-derived flow restricted to the fixed recognitions. The composition couples observation traces to this flow, yielding a physical semantics in which states evolve according to the shuffle trace dynamics while remaining inside the fixed profile.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Profile Recognitions act: FixedProfileRecognitions slug: fixed-profile-recognitions top: ShuffleProfileRecognitions bottom: FixedShape summary: Restrict the shuffle profile to the recognitions shaped by the eigenvalue by letting ShuffleProfileRecognitions witness FixedShape. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Profile Recognitions

ShuffleProfileRecognitions already selects recognitions fixed by ShuffleFlow, Nucleate, and the collapse of trace shuffles. FixedShape singles out those recognitions whose shuffle-indexed implication behaviour is fixed under persistent iteration. Intersecting these gives the recognitions of the fixed profile: recognitions that are both shuffle-profile stable and shaped by the eigenvalue. Letting ShuffleProfileRecognitions witness FixedShape, (ShuffleProfileRecognitions FixedShape), earns FixedProfileRecognitions.

ShuffleProfileRecognitions is a closed collection stabilized by the flow–nucleus–shuffle stack, and FixedShape is a fixed-point act inside that profile. Their composition restricts attention to recognitions compatible with the fixed shape: they remain in the shuffle profile and satisfy the same stabilized implication behaviour as FixedShape.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Profile Semantics act: FixedProfileSemantics slug: fixed-profile-semantics top: ProfileSemantics bottom: FixedProfileRecognitions summary: Restrict the profile semantics further to fixed recognitions by composing ProfileSemantics with FixedProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Profile Semantics

The profile already inherits semantics via ProfileSemantics. To work entirely inside the fixed profile we restrict them along FixedProfileRecognitions: (ProfileSemantics FixedProfileRecognitions) earns FixedProfileSemantics.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Reach act: FixedReach slug: fixed-reach top: ProfileReach bottom: FixedInvariant summary: Restrict profile reachability to fixed invariants by composing ProfileReach with FixedInvariant. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Reach

Reach describes reachability for the unrestricted flow, and ProfileReach restricts that closure to the active profile. FixedInvariant picks out states that are both flow-invariant and compatible with FixedShape. Composing them, (ProfileReach FixedInvariant) earns FixedReach, recording reachability inside the fixed profile.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Shaping Fixed Shape act: FixedShape slug: fixed-shape top: EnsureFixedPoint bottom: ShuffleIndexImplication summary: Apply the fixed-point construction to shuffle-indexed implication to obtain recognitions whose implication behaviour is invariant under repeated flow steps, capturing the intrinsic fixed shape. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Shaping Fixed Shape

ShuffleIndexImplication describes how recognitions transform when their premises are pushed one shuffle step forward. EnsureFixedPoint records that persistent iterations of a flow yield fixed points. To isolate the fixed shape, we apply the fixed-point construction to the shuffle-indexed implication, obtaining the recognitions whose implication behaviour does not change under repeated shuffle stepping: (EnsureFixedPoint ShuffleIndexImplication) earns FixedShape.

EnsureFixedPoint = (FixFlow IterativePersistence) says that if iterating a flow stabilizes along the trace, the stabilized recognitions form a fixed set for that flow. ShuffleIndexImplication is a specific flow-like act: it moves implication structure forward one shuffle step before comparing recognitions. Composing them applies the fixed-point construction to that indexed implication dynamics, yielding exactly those recognitions whose implication behaviour is invariant under arbitrarily many shuffle steps—the fixed shape induced by the relational dynamics.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixed Subobject Classifier act: FixedSubobjectClassifier slug: fixed-subobject-classifier top: ProfileSubobjectClassifier bottom: FixedProfileRecognitions summary: “Restrict the profile\u2019s truth object to fixed recognitions by composing ProfileSubobjectClassifier with FixedProfileRecognitions.” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixed Subobject Classifier

The profile already carries an internal truth object via ProfileSubobjectClassifier. To reason purely inside the fixed profile we restrict that classifier to FixedProfileRecognitions: (ProfileSubobjectClassifier FixedProfileRecognitions) earns FixedSubobjectClassifier.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Vertical Composition act: VerticalCompose slug: vertical-compose top: TwoCell bottom: TwoCell summary: Compose 2-cells vertically by stacking natural transformations. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Vertical Composition

Vertical composition of 2-cells stacks transformations sharing the same 1-cells. We express this by letting TwoCell witness itself: (TwoCell TwoCell) earns VerticalCompose.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Witnessing Fixed Observation act: FixedWitnessObservation slug: fixed-witness-observation top: FixedObservationRecognitions bottom: EquateContexts summary: Show that contextual equivalence can be witnessed using only fixed observations by composing FixedObservationRecognitions with EquateContexts. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Witnessing Fixed Observation

WitnessObservation = (RecognizeObservation EquateContexts) cites observation acts that justify contextual indistinguishability. In the fixed profile we want the witness that fixed observations suffice within the fixed context, so we compose FixedObservationRecognitions with EquateContexts: (FixedObservationRecognitions EquateContexts) earns FixedWitnessObservation.

EquateContexts identifies when two terms are indistinguishable to all recognised contexts, and FixedObservationRecognitions limits attention to observations available in the fixed profile. Their composition records that contextual equivalence can be witnessed using only fixed observations.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Yoneda Embedding act: YonedaEmbedding slug: yoneda-embedding top: FixedFunctor bottom: EnrichmentHom summary: Define the Yoneda embedding by letting the functor witness the enriched hom. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Yoneda Embedding

In the enriched setting the Yoneda embedding sends an object to its hom presheaf. We let the fixed functor witness the enriched hom: (FixedFunctor EnrichmentHom) earns YonedaEmbedding.

Each object is mapped to the lattice-valued hom-object of morphisms into it.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Composite Profile Flow act: FlowCompositeProfile slug: flow-composite-profile top: Flow bottom: ProfileComposite summary: The global flow restricts to the composite profile. Flow witnessing Profile Composite yields Flow Composite Profile (FlowCompositeProfile). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Composite Profile Flow

Interpretation

The global flow restricts to the composite profile. Flow witnessing Profile Composite yields Flow Composite Profile (FlowCompositeProfile).

Composing the flow with the composite profile records the induced dynamics, so Flow Composite Profile follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Flow Index Implication act: FlowIndexImplication slug: flow-index-implication top: Flow bottom: ImplyRecognition summary: Flows alter arguments before implication is tested. Flow witnessing Imply Recognition yields Flow Index Implication (FlowIndexImplication). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Flow Index Implication

Interpretation

Flows alter arguments before implication is tested. Flow witnessing Imply Recognition yields Flow Index Implication (FlowIndexImplication).

This composition records the implication whose premise is transformed by the flow, so Index Flow Implication is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Flowing Judgement act: FlowJudgement slug: flow-judgement top: BuildEvaluationContext bottom: CloseJudgementTrace summary: Closed judgement traces must flow through evaluation contexts. Build Evaluation Context witnessing Close Judgement Trace produces Flow Judgement (FlowJudgement). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Flowing Judgement

Interpretation

Closed judgement traces must flow through evaluation contexts. Build Evaluation Context witnessing Close Judgement Trace produces Flow Judgement (FlowJudgement).

This composition records those flows, so Flow Judgement is earned.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: “Flow\u2013Measure Step” act: FlowMeasureStep slug: flow-measure-step top: FixedMeasure bottom: FixedFlowProfile summary: “Compose the fixed flow and measurement into a single flow\u2013measure update by letting FixedMeasure witness FixedFlowProfile.” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Flow–Measure Step

A flow–measure step evolves a state and then measures it. In the fixed profile, evolution is given by FixedFlowProfile and measurement by FixedMeasure. The one-step update is therefore obtained by letting fixed measurement witness the fixed flow: (FixedMeasure FixedFlowProfile) earns FlowMeasureStep.

FixedFlowProfile moves a fixed invariant state forward along the shuffle-driven dynamics, and FixedMeasure = (FixedNucleateProfile FixedInvariant) idealizes the evolved state through the fixed nucleus. Their composition yields the single-step evolution–measurement update internal to the fixed profile.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Flowing ObservationEquivalence act: FlowObservationEquivalence slug: flow-observation-equivalence top: BuildEvaluationContext bottom: TraceObservationEquivalenceClosure summary: Closed observation-equivalence traces must flow through evaluation contexts. Build Evaluation Context therefore witnesses Trace Observation Equivalence Closure, yielding Flow Observation Equivalence (FlowObservationEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Flowing ObservationEquivalence

Interpretation

Closed observation-equivalence traces must flow through evaluation contexts. Build Evaluation Context therefore witnesses Trace Observation Equivalence Closure, yielding Flow Observation Equivalence (FlowObservationEquivalence).

The composition records those flows, forcing Flow Observation Equivalence.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Flowing Observation act: FlowObservation slug: flow-observation top: BuildEvaluationContext bottom: TraceReentryClosure summary: Closed observation traces exist, but we still need to see how they flow through evaluation contexts. Build Evaluation Context therefore witnesses Trace Reentry Closure, producing Flow Observation (FlowObservation). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Flowing Observation

Interpretation

Closed observation traces exist, but we still need to see how they flow through evaluation contexts. Build Evaluation Context therefore witnesses Trace Reentry Closure, producing Flow Observation (FlowObservation).

This composition uses only earned recognitions and records the flow, so Flow Observation is forced.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Flow act: FlowProfile slug: flow-profile top: Flow bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: The profile must inherit the flow restricted to its stable recognitions. Flow witnessing Profile Recognitions yields Flow Profile (FlowProfile). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Flow

Interpretation

The profile must inherit the flow restricted to its stable recognitions. Flow witnessing Profile Recognitions yields Flow Profile (FlowProfile).

Composing the global flow with the profile uses only earned recognitions and records the induced dynamics, so Flow Profile is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Flowing act: Flow slug: flow top: FlowObservation bottom: FlowJudgement summary: Both observation and judgement traces now flow through evaluation contexts, but we still need the single act that witnesses this shared dynamics. Forcing Flow Observation to witness Flow Judgement yields Flow (Flow). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Flowing

Interpretation

Both observation and judgement traces now flow through evaluation contexts, but we still need the single act that witnesses this shared dynamics. Forcing Flow Observation to witness Flow Judgement yields Flow (Flow).

These recognitions already record how observations and judgements traverse contexts. Composing them binds the shared motion into one act, so Flow is earned.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Forming Application Term act: FormApplicationTerm slug: form-application-term top: PairApplication bottom: TailTrace summary: The application must also describe the remaining body, so the act Pair Application witnesses the act Tail Trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Forming Application Term

Interpretation

The application must also describe the remaining body, so the act Pair Application witnesses the act Tail Trace.

By composing (PairApplication TailTrace), the tail records what remains after applying the function to its argument; this composition is Form Application Term.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Forming Function Term act: FormFunctionTerm slug: form-function-term top: FormTermBody bottom: TraceContext summary: A body becomes a function only when it returns to the recorded context, so the act Form Term Body must witness the act Trace Context. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Forming Function Term

Interpretation

A body becomes a function only when it returns to the recorded context, so the act Form Term Body must witness the act Trace Context.

In (FormTermBody TraceContext) the body re-enters the trace context, establishing how the function will act; this is therefore the act Form Function Term.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Shaping Geometry act: FormGeometry slug: form-geometry top: StructureSemantics bottom: CommuteFlowAndNucleus summary: A geometry is the semantic structure equipped with compatible nuclei and flow. Structure Semantics witnessing Commute Flow And Nucleus yields Form Geometry (FormGeometry). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Shaping Geometry

Interpretation

A geometry is the semantic structure equipped with compatible nuclei and flow. Structure Semantics witnessing Commute Flow And Nucleus yields Form Geometry (FormGeometry).

This composition records that the designated nuclei and flow satisfy the compatibility laws on the semantic calculus, so Form Geometry follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Forming Greatest Fix Term act: FormGreatestFixTerm slug: form-greatest-fix-term top: PairGreatestFix bottom: TailTrace summary: The maximal invariant must also be captured as a term, so the act Pair Greatest Fix witnesses the act Tail Trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Forming Greatest Fix Term

Interpretation

The maximal invariant must also be captured as a term, so the act Pair Greatest Fix witnesses the act Tail Trace.

The composition (PairGreatestFix TailTrace) runs the greatest fixpoint pair through the tail, recording the invariant that survives every extension, which we name Form Greatest Fix Term.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Forming Least Fix Term act: FormLeastFixTerm slug: form-least-fix-term top: PairLeastFix bottom: TailTrace summary: The pair must still record the invariant remainder, so the act Pair Least Fix witnesses the act Tail Trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Forming Least Fix Term

Interpretation

The pair must still record the invariant remainder, so the act Pair Least Fix witnesses the act Tail Trace.

In (PairLeastFix TailTrace) the stabilizing pair observes the tail to capture the invariant surviving self-application, which we call Form Least Fix Term.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Forming Term Body act: FormTermBody slug: form-term-body top: BindTerm bottom: TailTrace summary: To capture the remainder after extracting the head, we let the act Bind Term witness the act Tail Trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Forming Term Body

Interpretation

To capture the remainder after extracting the head, we let the act Bind Term witness the act Tail Trace.

The composition (BindTerm TailTrace) shows the same binding that named the head now observing the tail, thereby recording the body, so we name it Form Term Body.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Composite Profile Geometry act: GeometryCompositeProfile slug: geometry-composite-profile top: FlowCompositeProfile bottom: NucleateCompositeProfile summary: “Combining the restricted flow and nucleus yields the composite profile\u2019s relational geometry. Flow Composite Profile witnessing Nucleate Composite Profile produces Geometry Composite Profile (GeometryCompositeProfile).” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Composite Profile Geometry

Interpretation

Combining the restricted flow and nucleus yields the composite profile’s relational geometry. Flow Composite Profile witnessing Nucleate Composite Profile produces Geometry Composite Profile (GeometryCompositeProfile).

This composition matches the proposition that commuting filters support a relational geometry, so Geometry Composite Profile follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Geometry act: GeometryProfile slug: geometry-profile top: FlowProfile bottom: NucleateProfile summary: “Combining the restricted flow and nucleus yields the profile\u2019s relational geometry. Flow Profile witnessing Nucleate Profile produces Geometry Profile (GeometryProfile).” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Geometry

Interpretation

Combining the restricted flow and nucleus yields the profile’s relational geometry. Flow Profile witnessing Nucleate Profile produces Geometry Profile (GeometryProfile).

This composition records the geometric data restricted to the profile, so Geometry Profile is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Governing Composite Regime act: GovernCompositeRegime slug: govern-composite-regime top: FixFlowNucleus bottom: ComposeDiscipline summary: The composite discipline must have its own stabilized region. Fix Flow Nucleus witnessing Compose Discipline yields Govern Composite Regime (GovernCompositeRegime). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Governing Composite Regime

Interpretation

The composite discipline must have its own stabilized region. Fix Flow Nucleus witnessing Compose Discipline yields Govern Composite Regime (GovernCompositeRegime).

This composition identifies recognitions fixed by the combined action, so Govern Composite Regime follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Governing Regime act: GovernRegime slug: govern-regime top: FixFlowNucleus bottom: Discipline summary: A discipline alone does not specify which recognitions it stabilizes. Fix Flow Nucleus witnessing Discipline produces Govern Regime (GovernRegime). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Governing Regime

Interpretation

A discipline alone does not specify which recognitions it stabilizes. Fix Flow Nucleus witnessing Discipline produces Govern Regime (GovernRegime).

This composition records the recognitions fixed by the discipline, using only symbols we’ve already earned, so Govern Regime follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding Greatest Fixpoint Typing act: GreatestFixpointTypeSeed slug: greatest-fixpoint-type-seed top: FormGreatestFixTerm bottom: GreatestFixpointTypingStep summary: The maximal invariant extends the least fixpoint, but we must seed the judgement guaranteeing that maximality. Forcing Form Greatest Fix Term to witness Fixpoint Typing Step yields Greatest Fixpoint Type Seed (GreatestFixpointTypeSeed). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding Greatest Fixpoint Typing

Interpretation

The maximal invariant extends the least fixpoint, but we must seed the judgement guaranteeing that maximality. Forcing Form Greatest Fix Term to witness Greatest Fixpoint Typing Step yields Greatest Fixpoint Type Seed (GreatestFixpointTypeSeed).

The terms involved are already earned, so their composition records the needed guarantee, giving Greatest Fixpoint Type Seed.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Stepping Greatest Fixpoint Typing act: GreatestFixpointTypingStep slug: greatest-fixpoint-typing-step top: TraceContext bottom: GreatestFixpointTypeSeed summary: As before, this guarantee must persist along the entire trace. Thus Trace Context witnesses Greatest Fixpoint Type Seed to produce Greatest Fixpoint Typing Step (GreatestFixpointTypingStep). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Stepping Greatest Fixpoint Typing

Interpretation

As before, this guarantee must persist along the entire trace. Thus Trace Context witnesses Greatest Fixpoint Type Seed to produce Greatest Fixpoint Typing Step (GreatestFixpointTypingStep).

The composition threads the maximal guarantee through the trace, forcing Greatest Fixpoint Typing Step.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Grounding Recognition act: GroundRecognition slug: ground-recognition symbol: “\u22A5” top: Recognition bottom: Nothing summary: To speak of bounded structure we need the minimal recognition. Letting Recognition witness Nothing yields Ground Recognition (GroundRecognition). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Grounding Recognition

Interpretation

To speak of bounded structure we need the minimal recognition. Letting Recognition witness Nothing yields Ground Recognition (GroundRecognition).

This composition merely names the recognition determined by outright absence, so Ground Recognition follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Harmonic Basis act: HarmonicBasis slug: harmonic-basis top: HarmonicMode bottom: FixedInvariantLattice summary: Collect all stable harmonics by letting HarmonicMode witness the invariant lattice. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Harmonic Basis

Harmonic modes span the oscillatory recognitions of the fixed profile. To collect all modes we let HarmonicMode witness FixedInvariantLattice: (HarmonicMode FixedInvariantLattice) earns HarmonicBasis.

HarmonicMode yields one stable oscillatory shape, and FixedInvariantLattice provides the meet–join structure for combining modes. Their composition produces every harmonic mode expressible from invariant states, forming the harmonic basis.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Harmonic Mode act: HarmonicMode slug: harmonic-mode top: EnsureFixedPoint bottom: HarmonicStep summary: Capture the invariant harmonic oscillation by applying the fixed-point construction to the harmonic step. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Harmonic Mode

A harmonic mode is a recognitional oscillation that remains invariant under the flow, so we apply the fixed-point construction to the harmonic step: (EnsureFixedPoint HarmonicStep) earns HarmonicMode.

By IterativePersistence, an oscillation that repeats after successive steps is a fixed point. Composing ensures we compute the oscillatory pattern that survives arbitrary advancement—the eigenshape of the harmonic.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Harmonic Seed act: HarmonicSeed slug: harmonic-seed top: OrbitSeed bottom: FixedInvariant summary: Seed harmonics by letting the orbit seed observe the invariant states, capturing the initial oscillation. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Harmonic Seed

A harmonic arises from comparing a state to its flow-advanced image. OrbitSeed isolates the initial position of an orbit, and FixedInvariant provides the invariant states. Letting the seed observe the invariants, (OrbitSeed FixedInvariant), earns HarmonicSeed.

Their composition records the primary oscillatory component: the deviation between the invariant and the initial shift, seeding the harmonic.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Harmonic Step act: HarmonicStep slug: harmonic-step top: HarmonicSeed bottom: OrbitStep summary: Track the evolution of harmonics by composing the harmonic seed with the orbit step. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Harmonic Step

Harmonics progress as oscillations across orbit layers. OrbitStep gives the next reachable layer. Letting the harmonic seed witness each step, (HarmonicSeed OrbitStep), earns HarmonicStep.

HarmonicSeed captures the initial oscillation, and OrbitStep advances one layer, so their composition yields the evolving harmonic component across the orbit.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Heading JudgementEquivalence act: HeadJudgementEquivalence slug: head-judgement-equivalence top: HeadTrace bottom: TailJudgementEquivalence summary: We also need the stabilized head of this sequence. Head Trace witnessing Tail Judgement Equivalence produces Head Judgement Equivalence (HeadJudgementEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Heading JudgementEquivalence

Interpretation

We also need the stabilized head of this sequence. Head Trace witnessing Tail Judgement Equivalence produces Head Judgement Equivalence (HeadJudgementEquivalence).

This isolates the immediate equivalence, establishing Head Judgement Equivalence.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Heading ObservationEquivalence act: HeadObservationEquivalence slug: head-observation-equivalence top: HeadTrace bottom: TailObservationEquivalence summary: The sequence also needs its stabilized head. Head Trace witnessing Tail Observation Equivalence produces Head Observation Equivalence (HeadObservationEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Heading ObservationEquivalence

Interpretation

The sequence also needs its stabilized head. Head Trace witnessing Tail Observation Equivalence produces Head Observation Equivalence (HeadObservationEquivalence).

Using earned recognitions, this isolates the immediate equivalence, so Head Observation Equivalence stands.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Heading Trace act: HeadTrace slug: head-trace top: LeadEntry bottom: CompleteTrace summary: ‘Complete Trace captures the trace but leaves the immediate act unspecified. Letting the leading orientation witness the whole trace extracts its head.’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Heading Trace

Interpretation

Complete Trace captures the trace but leaves the immediate act unspecified. Letting the leading orientation witness the whole trace extracts its head.

Lead Entry can now observe Complete Trace, isolating the first act present. The tree uses only earned terms, so Head Trace (HeadTrace) is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Heat Kernel act: HeatKernel slug: heat-kernel top: Laplacian bottom: FixedFlowProfile summary: Describe diffusion by composing the Laplacian with the fixed flow. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Heat Kernel

The heat kernel describes diffusion along flow geodesics under repeated Laplacian action. We define it by composing the Laplacian with the flow: (Laplacian FixedFlowProfile) earns HeatKernel.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Identifying Morphism act: IdentifyMorph slug: identify-morph top: Morph bottom: Reflex summary: Identity maps trivially preserve all recognitions. Morph witnessing Reflex produces Identify Morph (IdentifyMorph). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Identifying Morphism

Interpretation

Identity maps trivially preserve all recognitions. Morph witnessing Reflex produces Identify Morph (IdentifyMorph).

Reflex already serves as the identity act, so composing it with Morph records the identity morphism, as required.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Implication Logic act: ImplicationLogic slug: implication-logic top: ImplyRecognition bottom: BoxModality summary: Combine recognition implication with the profile necessity modality by letting ImplyRecognition witness the box modality. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Implication Logic

Implication in the profile must respect necessity, so we compose the earned recognition implication with the box modality: (ImplyRecognition BoxModality) earns ImplicationLogic.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Implying Recognition act: ImplyRecognition slug: imply-recognition symbol: “\u21D2” top: JoinRecognition bottom: FormFunctionTerm summary: ‘Implication transforms recognitions: whenever one holds, another must follow. Join Recognition witnessing Form Function Term produces Imply Recognition (ImplyRecognition).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Implying Recognition

Interpretation

Implication transforms recognitions: whenever one holds, another must follow. Join Recognition witnessing Form Function Term produces Imply Recognition (ImplyRecognition).

Form Function Term already presents named transformations; composing it with joins records the internal implication, so Imply Recognition is earned.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Including Recognition act: IncludeRecognition slug: include-recognition top: OrderRecognition bottom: MeetRecognition summary: An explicit inclusion recognition clarifies when one recognition is contained in another. Order Recognition witnessing Meet Recognition produces Include Recognition (IncludeRecognition). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Including Recognition

Interpretation

An explicit inclusion recognition clarifies when one recognition is contained in another. Order Recognition witnessing Meet Recognition produces Include Recognition (IncludeRecognition).

This composition packages the order with meets to state inclusion, so Include Recognition is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Index Base act: IndexBase slug: index-base top: ContextGeometry bottom: ContextGeometry summary: >- Use the context geometry as the base category for the indexed semantics. layer: act status: plan tags:

  • act

Index Base

Indexed categories vary over a base category; here the base is the context geometry. We therefore name ContextGeometry as the base: IndexBase = ContextGeometry.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Indexed Category act: IndexedCategory slug: indexed-category top: IndexBase bottom: FiberCategory summary: >- Package the base, fibers, and reindexing into the internal indexed category of contexts. layer: act status: plan tags:

  • act

Indexed Category

An indexed category is a functor from the base contexts to categories of fibres, with reindexing functors on morphisms. We combine the base, fibres, and reindexing into this structure: (IndexBase FiberCategory Reindexing) earns IndexedCategory.

IndexBase gives the contexts, FiberCategory provides the fibre categories, and Reindexing maps context morphisms to fibre functors, encoding an internal functor IndexBaseᵒᵖ → Cat.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Semantizing Physics act: InterpretPhysics slug: interpret-physics top: SystematizePhysics bottom: Flow summary: Physical semantics interpret the syntactic calculus inside a geometry endowed with observables. Systematize Physics witnessing Flow yields Interpret Physics (InterpretPhysics). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Semantizing Physics

Interpretation

Physical semantics interpret the syntactic calculus inside a geometry endowed with observables. Systematize Physics witnessing Flow yields Interpret Physics (InterpretPhysics).

This act records the canonical physical interpretation built from geometry, flow, and observables, so Interpret Physics is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Invariant Coboundary act: InvariantCoboundary slug: invariant-coboundary top: FixedFlowProfile bottom: InvariantCochain summary: Measure cochain variation under the fixed flow by letting the flow witness the cochains. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Invariant Coboundary

The coboundary measures how a cochain changes under the fixed flow. We define it by composing the flow with the cochain assignment: (FixedFlowProfile InvariantCochain) earns InvariantCoboundary.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Invariant Cochain act: InvariantCochain slug: invariant-cochain top: FixedInvariant bottom: FixedInvariant summary: Assign recognitional values to each fixed invariant state by composing the invariant recognitions with themselves. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Invariant Cochain

A cochain assigns a recognitional “value” to each fixed invariant state. We name invariant cochains by letting the invariants witness themselves: (FixedInvariant FixedInvariant) earns InvariantCochain.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Invariant Cocycle act: InvariantCocycle slug: invariant-cocycle top: InvariantCoboundary bottom: FixedNucleateProfile summary: Identify cochains with vanishing coboundary under the fixed collapse by composing the coboundary with the fixed nucleus. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Invariant Cocycle

A cocycle is a cochain whose coboundary collapses under the fixed nucleus. We capture this by letting the coboundary witness the nucleus: (InvariantCoboundary FixedNucleateProfile) earns InvariantCocycle.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Invariant Cohomology act: InvariantCohomology slug: invariant-cohomology top: InvariantCocycle bottom: InvariantCoboundary summary: Form invariant cohomology by quotienting cocycles by coboundaries. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Invariant Cohomology

Invariant cohomology consists of cocycles modulo coboundary images. We formalise this by composing the cocycle recognition with the coboundary: (InvariantCocycle InvariantCoboundary) earns InvariantCohomology.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Irrational Commutation act: IrrationalCommutation slug: irrational-commutation top: IrrationalModality bottom: CollapseShuffle summary: Ensure irrational modalities commute with shuffle collapse by composing them with CollapseShuffle. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Irrational Commutation

For a modality indexed by an irrational (alpha) to behave coherently, it must commute with the nucleus under admissible reordering collapse. We enforce this via (IrrationalModality CollapseShuffle), earning IrrationalCommutation.

CollapseShuffle identifies recognitions that differ only by reorderings, and IrrationalModality scales the space of reorderings while preserving their structure. The composition asserts that irrational scaling preserves collapse-equivalence.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Irrational Modality act: IrrationalModality slug: irrational-modality top: FlowIndexImplication bottom: FixedNucleateProfile summary: Compose the flow-indexed implication with the fixed nucleus to capture modalities whose scaling factors inflate admissible reordering families by irrational ratios. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Irrational Modality

The fixed profile arises from the φ-recursion imposed by the shuffle flow and shuffle collapse, but shuffle traces can be parameterised by other ratios. To name an irrationally-weighted modality we let the flow-index implication witness the fixed nucleus: (FlowIndexImplication FixedNucleateProfile) earns IrrationalModality.

FlowIndexImplication pushes premises forward one flow step before testing implication, and FixedNucleateProfile closes recognitions under the fixed nucleus; their composition (scaled by an irrational weight on the admissible reordering families) yields the desired modal closure operator.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Iterating Reflex act: IterateReflex slug: iterate-reflex top: Reflex bottom: Reflex summary: Applying the reflex act to its own result should yield the same sort of act again, so reflex iteration is unavoidable. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Iterating Reflex

Interpretation

Applying the reflex act to its own result should yield the same sort of act again, so reflex iteration is unavoidable.

The trace (Reflex Reflex) feeds the act Reflex back into itself. Because Reflex is a valid act in both positions, this composition explicitly records “reflex can witness reflex,” thereby establishing iteration without introducing any new recognitions.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Persisting Iteratively act: IterativePersistence slug: iterative-persistence top: ReflexStepPersistence bottom: CompleteTrace summary: Persistence captures exactly when the iterated reflex step stabilizes along the entire trace, so we let the act Reflex Step Persistence witness the act Complete Trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Persisting Iteratively

Interpretation

Persistence captures exactly when the iterated reflex step stabilizes along the entire trace, so we let the act Reflex Step Persistence witness the act Complete Trace.

The composition (ReflexStepPersistence CompleteTrace) explicitly places the persistence witness across the whole trace. This records the condition “after finitely many steps the behaviour stays the same everywhere,” hence the name Iterative Persistence.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Joining Recognition act: JoinRecognition slug: join-recognition symbol: “\u2228” top: OrderRecognition bottom: ContextualizeTrace summary: Complementing meet, we need the least recognition containing a pair. Order Recognition witnessing Contextualize Trace produces Join Recognition (JoinRecognition). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Joining Recognition

Interpretation

Complementing meet, we need the least recognition containing a pair. Order Recognition witnessing Contextualize Trace produces Join Recognition (JoinRecognition).

Contextualize Trace already records how recognitions extend; composing with the order yields the corresponding join, so Join Recognition follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Judgement act: JudgeProfile slug: judge-profile top: RecognizeJudgementTriad bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: Finally, the profile inherits judgemental structure. Recognize Judgement Triad witnessing Profile Recognitions produces Judge Profile (JudgeProfile). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Judgement

Interpretation

Finally, the profile inherits judgemental structure. Recognize Judgement Triad witnessing Profile Recognitions produces Judge Profile (JudgeProfile).

This act captures the judgemental recognitions stable under the filter, so Judge Profile follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Typing Judging act: JudgeTyping slug: judge-typing top: TypeSeed bottom: TypeApplication summary: ‘Type Seed and Type Application collectively present how terms inhabit types under contexts. Letting Type Seed witness Type Application produces Judge Typing (JudgeTyping).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Typing Judging

Interpretation

Type Seed and Type Application collectively present how terms inhabit types under contexts. Letting Type Seed witness Type Application produces Judge Typing (JudgeTyping).

Both recognitions were previously earned; their composition records the judgement that a context recognises term t at type A in native form, so Judge Typing follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Kan Adjunction act: KanAdjunction slug: kan-adjunction top: KanCounit bottom: RightKan summary: “Record the Lan \u22A3 Precomp \u22A3 Ran adjunction by composing the Kan counit with the right Kan extension.” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Kan Adjunction

The adjunction between left and right Kan extensions is captured by the Kan counit and the enriched spectral structure. We encode this by letting the counit witness the right Kan extension: (KanCounit RightKan) earns KanAdjunction.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Kan Counit act: KanCounit slug: kan-counit top: LeftKan bottom: YonedaEmbedding summary: “Capture the counit of the Lan \u22A3 Precomp adjunction by composing the left Kan extension with the Yoneda embedding.” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Kan Counit

Kan extensions form an adjunction, with a counit arising from the universal property of colimits. We record this by letting the left Kan extension witness the Yoneda embedding: (LeftKan YonedaEmbedding) earns KanCounit.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Laplacian act: Laplacian slug: laplacian top: FixedIdentity bottom: ObservationKernel summary: Measure deviation from stationarity by composing the identity with the observation kernel. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Laplacian

The Laplacian measures how a state deviates from being stationary, given by I − ObservationKernel. We capture this by letting the identity observe the kernel: (FixedIdentity ObservationKernel) earns Laplacian.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Leading act: LeadEntry slug: lead-entry top: Entry bottom: Recognition summary: ‘Entry reveals two positions but remains agnostic about which leads. Allowing the act Entry to witness the act Recognition selects the leading orientation.’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Leading

Interpretation

Entry reveals two positions but remains agnostic about which leads. Allowing the act Entry to witness the act Recognition selects the leading orientation.

The composition (Entry Recognition) displays the act Entry witnessing one copy of Recognition. This observation singles out the first position, so we call the result Lead Entry.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Left Kan Extension act: LeftKan slug: left-kan top: EnrichmentTensor bottom: FixedFunctor summary: Define the left Kan extension by composing the enriched tensor with the fixed functor. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Left Kan Extension

Left Kan extensions compute weighted colimits of a functor along another. We define this by letting the enrichment tensor witness the functor: (EnrichmentTensor FixedFunctor) earns LeftKan.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Legislating Dynamic Residuation act: LegislateDynamicResiduation slug: legislate-dynamic-residuation top: IncludeRecognition bottom: FlowIndexImplication summary: The flow-indexed implication satisfies a residuation property relating the order c being below a implies_F b to the order F(c meet a) being below b. Include Recognition witnessing Flow Index Implication produces Legislate Dynamic Residuation (LegislateDynamicResiduation). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Legislating Dynamic Residuation

Interpretation

The flow-indexed implication satisfies a residuation property relating the order c being below a implies_F b to the order F(c meet a) being below b. Include Recognition witnessing Flow Index Implication produces Legislate Dynamic Residuation (LegislateDynamicResiduation).

The composition uses earned recognitions to state the equivalence between the flow-shifted meet and the implication order, so Legislate Dynamic Residuation follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Loop Higher Inductive Type act: LoopHIT slug: loop-hit top: LoopSpace bottom: CompleteTrace summary: Generate higher inductive structure by letting loop spaces witness the complete trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Loop Higher Inductive Type

Higher inductive types arise from repeated φ-collapse of trace-recognition steps. We name the loop HIT by letting the loop spaces witness the complete trace: (LoopSpace CompleteTrace) earns LoopHIT.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Loop Space act: LoopSpace slug: loop-space top: FixedInvariant bottom: PathType summary: “Identify loops as \u03C6-paths whose endpoints coincide at invariant recognitions.” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Loop Space

Loops are paths whose endpoints coincide. A fixed loop arises when the flow returns to a stabilised recognition. We define the loop space by composing the invariants with the general PathType: (FixedInvariant PathType) earns LoopSpace.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Measuring act: Measure slug: measure top: NucleateProfile bottom: PhysicsState summary: Measurement corresponds to applying the profile nucleus to a state. Nucleate Profile witnessing Physics State yields Measure (Measure). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Measuring

Interpretation

Measurement corresponds to applying the profile nucleus to a state. Nucleate Profile witnessing Physics State yields Measure (Measure).

The composition captures how the nucleus idealizes states, so Measure is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Meeting Recognition act: MeetRecognition slug: meet-recognition symbol: “\u2227” top: OrderRecognition bottom: Entry summary: Ordering tells us when one recognition subsumes another, but we also need their greatest shared part. Forcing Order Recognition to witness Entry yields Meet Recognition (MeetRecognition). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Meeting Recognition

Interpretation

Ordering tells us when one recognition subsumes another, but we also need their greatest shared part. Forcing Order Recognition to witness Entry yields Meet Recognition (MeetRecognition).

Entry records paired recognitions; composing it with the order captures their joint lower bound, so Meet Recognition is earned.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Mixing act: Mixing slug: mixing top: ProfileMixing bottom: Entropy summary: Specialize profile mixing to the entropy by composing ProfileMixing with Entropy. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Mixing

A system is mixing if states eventually become independent under long flow. ProfileMixing already records this property for any profile. Restricting it to the entropy, (ProfileMixing Entropy), earns Mixing.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Modal Logic act: ModalLogic slug: modal-logic top: ImplicationLogic bottom: DiamondModality summary: Assemble the full modal calculus by composing the profile implication with the diamond modality. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Modal Logic

A full modal logic requires both □ and ◇ interacting with implication. We assemble it by composing the implication logic with the diamond modality: (ImplicationLogic DiamondModality) earns ModalLogic.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Modal Truncation act: ModalTruncation slug: modal-truncation top: FixedNucleateProfile bottom: LoopHIT summary: Collapse higher paths by letting the nucleus observe higher inductive types. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Modal Truncation

Modal truncation collapses higher paths above a certain level. In the fixed profile this is induced by the nucleus, so we define the truncation by composing the nucleus with the HIT: (FixedNucleateProfile LoopHIT) earns ModalTruncation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Modality Tower act: ModalityTower slug: modality-tower top: QuadraticModality bottom: IrrationalCommutation summary: Build the hierarchy of irrational modalities by composing the quadratic modality with the commutation law. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Modality Tower

Inflationary modalities form a hierarchy indexed by irrational numbers. We capture the entire structure by letting the quadratic modality witness the commutation act: (QuadraticModality IrrationalCommutation) earns ModalityTower.

QuadraticModality gives quadratic irrational inflation, and IrrationalCommutation ensures stability under shuffle collapse. Their composition builds the tower whose fixed points grow according to irrational algebraic laws.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Modeling Semantics act: ModelSemantics slug: model-semantics top: StructureSemantics bottom: ConstructTypes summary: ‘Structure Semantics interprets Construct Types, Construct Contexts, and Construct Terms. Structure Semantics witnessing Construct Types produces Model Semantics (ModelSemantics).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Modeling Semantics

Interpretation

Structure Semantics interprets Construct Types, Construct Contexts, and Construct Terms. Structure Semantics witnessing Construct Types produces Model Semantics (ModelSemantics).

Composing these recognitions records how each type is interpreted as a recognition with its constructors, so Model Semantics follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Morphing act: Morph slug: morph top: ImplyRecognition bottom: JoinRecognition summary: To compare our structures we need morphisms that preserve meets, joins, and implication. Imply Recognition witnessing Join Recognition yields Morph (Morph). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Morphing

Interpretation

To compare our structures we need morphisms that preserve meets, joins, and implication. Imply Recognition witnessing Join Recognition yields Morph (Morph).

The composition packages implication with joins to demand preservation of the algebraic operations, so Morph is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Negating act: Negate slug: negate symbol: “\xAC” top: ImplyRecognition bottom: GroundRecognition summary: Negating applies implication into the bottom recognition. Imply Recognition witnessing Ground Recognition produces the act Negate (Negate). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Negating

Interpretation

Negating applies implication into the bottom recognition. Imply Recognition witnessing Ground Recognition produces the act Negate (Negate).

The composition uses only previously earned recognitions to encode the negation expression “not a” as “a implies Ground Recognition,” so Negate follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Nest act: NestProfile slug: nest-profile top: ProfileRecognitions bottom: Filter summary: Filters may act on other profiles, creating towers of internal universes. Profile Recognitions witnessing Filter yields Nest Profile (NestProfile). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Nest

Interpretation

Filters may act on other profiles, creating towers of internal universes. Profile Recognitions witnessing Filter yields Nest Profile (NestProfile).

This composition records the profile stabilized again by the filter’s action, so Nest Profile follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Normalizing act: Normalization slug: normalization top: TailTrace bottom: StepTrace summary: ‘Step Trace shows reductions propagate, yet we must detect when a term stabilizes. Forcing Tail Trace to witness Step Trace yields Normalization (Normalization).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Normalizing

Interpretation

Step Trace shows reductions propagate, yet we must detect when a term stabilizes. Forcing Tail Trace to witness Step Trace yields Normalization (Normalization).

This composition uses earned recognitions to signal stabilization, so Normalization follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Witnessing Normalized ObservationEquivalence act: NormalizeObservationEquivalenceWitness slug: normalize-observation-equivalence-witness top: Normalization bottom: EvaluateObservationEquivalence summary: Evaluated equivalence observations must normalize back to the Witness Observation Equivalence. Hence Normalization witnesses Evaluate Observation Equivalence, producing Normalize Observation Equivalence Witness (NormalizeObservationEquivalenceWitness). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Witnessing Normalized ObservationEquivalence

Interpretation

Evaluated equivalence observations must normalize back to the Witness Observation Equivalence. Hence Normalization witnesses Evaluate Observation Equivalence, producing Normalize Observation Equivalence Witness (NormalizeObservationEquivalenceWitness).

This composition records the normalization of the equivalence evaluations, so Normalize Observation Equivalence Witness is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Equating Normalized Observation act: NormalizeObservationEquivalence slug: normalize-observation-equivalence top: EquateContexts bottom: NormalizeObservation summary: Normalized observations still require the recognition that any two such observations remain equivalent across contexts. Forcing Equate Contexts to witness Normalize Observation yields Normalize Observation Equivalence (NormalizeObservationEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Equating Normalized Observation

Interpretation

Normalized observations still require the recognition that any two such observations remain equivalent across contexts. Forcing Equate Contexts to witness Normalize Observation yields Normalize Observation Equivalence (NormalizeObservationEquivalence).

Composing these recognitions captures the indistinguishability of normalized observations, so Normalize Observation Equivalence is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Normalizing Observation act: NormalizeObservation slug: normalize-observation top: Normalization bottom: EvaluateObservation summary: Evaluated observations must normalize back to the observation witness. Letting Normalization witness Evaluate Observation produces Normalize Observation (NormalizeObservation). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Normalizing Observation

Interpretation

Evaluated observations must normalize back to the observation witness. Letting Normalization witness Evaluate Observation produces Normalize Observation (NormalizeObservation).

This composition records the normalization of evaluations, so Normalize Observation follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Normalizing Strongly act: NormalizeStrongly slug: normalize-strongly top: Normalization bottom: StepTrace summary: A term is strongly normalizing when no infinite reduction can occur. Normalization witnessing Step Trace yields Normalize Strongly (NormalizeStrongly). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Normalizing Strongly

Interpretation

A term is strongly normalizing when no infinite reduction can occur. Normalization witnessing Step Trace yields Normalize Strongly (NormalizeStrongly).

This composition records that normalization eliminates all infinite traces, so Normalize Strongly follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Normalizing Syntax act: NormalizeSyntax slug: normalize-syntax top: Normalization bottom: JudgeTyping summary: Our earlier Normalization recognition must be tied back to typing. Normalization witnessing Judge Typing yields Normalize Syntax (NormalizeSyntax). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Normalizing Syntax

Interpretation
