Our earlier Normalization recognition must be tied back to typing. Normalization witnessing Judge Typing yields Normalize Syntax (NormalizeSyntax).

This composition records that well-typed syntactic acts normalize, so Normalize Syntax follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Equating Normalized Judgement act: NormalizedJudgementEquivalence slug: normalized-judgement-equivalence top: EquateContexts bottom: NormalizedJudgement summary: Finally, Normalized Judgement must remain indistinguishable to all contexts. Equate Contexts witnessing Normalized Judgement yields Normalized Judgement Equivalence (NormalizedJudgementEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Equating Normalized Judgement

Interpretation

Finally, Normalized Judgement must remain indistinguishable to all contexts. Equate Contexts witnessing Normalized Judgement yields Normalized Judgement Equivalence (NormalizedJudgementEquivalence).

This act enforces the indistinguishability, so Normalized Judgement Equivalence is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Normalizing Judgement act: NormalizedJudgement slug: normalized-judgement top: Normalization bottom: EvaluateJudgement summary: Evaluated judgements must normalize back to the triadic witness. Normalization witnessing Evaluate Judgement produces Normalized Judgement (NormalizedJudgement). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Normalizing Judgement

Interpretation

Evaluated judgements must normalize back to the triadic witness. Normalization witnessing Evaluate Judgement produces Normalized Judgement (NormalizedJudgement).

This composition records the normalization, establishing Normalized Judgement.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Negating Relation act: NotRelate slug: not-relate top: Reflex bottom: Relate summary: “Any affirmation of relation also reveals the possibility of denial, so we let reflex point specifically to that denial to name \u201Cnot relate.\u201D” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Negating Relation

Interpretation

Any affirmation of relation also reveals the possibility of denial, so we let reflex point specifically to that denial to name “not relate.”

In (Reflex Relate) the act Reflex serves as witness for the already earned act Relate. Observing Relate through Reflex isolates the complementary behaviour “refusing relation,” so we designate this composition Not Relate.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Witnessing Nothing act: Nothing slug: nothing top: Witness bottom: Something summary: “Having \u201Csomething\u201D forces us to examine what Witness sees when it looks directly at that something; the result is \u201Cnothing.\u201D” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Witnessing Nothing

Interpretation

Having “something” forces us to examine what Witness sees when it looks directly at that something; the result is “nothing.”

In (Witness Something) the act Witness observes Something. Both were already earned, so their composition captures the unresolved situation where testimony points to its own bearer, producing the recognition we call Nothing.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Composite Profile Nucleus act: NucleateCompositeProfile slug: nucleate-composite-profile top: Nucleate bottom: ProfileComposite summary: Nuclei likewise restrict to the composite profile. Nucleate witnessing Profile Composite produces Nucleate Composite Profile (NucleateCompositeProfile). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Composite Profile Nucleus

Interpretation

Nuclei likewise restrict to the composite profile. Nucleate witnessing Profile Composite produces Nucleate Composite Profile (NucleateCompositeProfile).

The act records the inherited closure on the composite domain, so Nucleate Composite Profile is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Nucleus act: NucleateProfile slug: nucleate-profile top: Nucleate bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: Similarly, the closure behaviour must restrict to the profile. Nucleate witnessing Profile Recognitions produces Nucleate Profile (NucleateProfile). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Nucleus

Interpretation

Similarly, the closure behaviour must restrict to the profile. Nucleate witnessing Profile Recognitions produces Nucleate Profile (NucleateProfile).

This composition captures the inherited closure, so Nucleate Profile follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Nucleating act: Nucleate slug: nucleate top: NormalizeObservationEquivalence bottom: NormalizedJudgementEquivalence summary: Normalized observation and judgement equivalences each close their respective acts, but we need the unified closure operator that acts on both. Forcing Normalize Observation Equivalence to witness Normalized Judgement Equivalence yields Nucleate (Nucleate). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Nucleating

Interpretation

Normalized observation and judgement equivalences each close their respective acts, but we need the unified closure operator that acts on both. Forcing Normalize Observation Equivalence to witness Normalized Judgement Equivalence yields Nucleate (Nucleate).

These equivalences were previously earned; composing them records the shared closure law, so Nucleate is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Structuring Nucleus Semantics act: NucleusSemantics slug: nucleus-semantics top: StructureSemantics bottom: FixNucleus summary: Nucleus-closed recognitions inherit the semantic structure. Structure Semantics witnessing Fix Nucleus yields Nucleus Semantics (NucleusSemantics). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Structuring Nucleus Semantics

Interpretation

Nucleus-closed recognitions inherit the semantic structure. Structure Semantics witnessing Fix Nucleus yields Nucleus Semantics (NucleusSemantics).

This composition records that the closed recognitions form a complete substructure, so Nucleus Semantics is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Observation Kernel act: ObservationKernel slug: observation-kernel top: FixedObservationRecognitions bottom: TransitionLaw summary: Combine the transition law with fixed observations by composing FixedObservationRecognitions with TransitionLaw. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Observation Kernel

A kernel couples transition structure with observable outcomes. TransitionLaw provides the transition step, and FixedObservationRecognitions restricts observations to fixed recognitions. Letting the observation act witness the transition law, (FixedObservationRecognitions TransitionLaw), earns ObservationKernel.

TransitionLaw determines how fixed states map to successor recognitions, and FixedObservationRecognitions selects observables available in the fixed profile. Their composition assigns to each fixed invariant state the observable distribution induced by one flow–measure step, yielding the profile’s kernel.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Observing Judgement act: ObserveJudgement slug: observe-judgement top: StabilizeObservation bottom: WitnessJudgement summary: Classified judgemental acts still need their stabilized observational content. Stabilize Observation witnessing Witness Judgement produces Observe Judgement (ObserveJudgement). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Observing Judgement

Interpretation

Classified judgemental acts still need their stabilized observational content. Stabilize Observation witnessing Witness Judgement produces Observe Judgement (ObserveJudgement).

This composition records the observational report of stabilized judgements, so Observe Judgement is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Observing act: Observe slug: observe top: RecognizeObservation bottom: SeedTerm summary: ‘Recognize Observation records contextual indistinguishability, but an observable must address a concrete traced term. Forcing Recognize Observation to witness Seed Term yields Observe (Observe).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Observing

Interpretation

Recognize Observation records contextual indistinguishability, but an observable must address a concrete traced term. Forcing Recognize Observation to witness Seed Term yields Observe (Observe).

Both recognitions were previously earned, so their composition records a designated observable act on a traced term, establishing Observe.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Operator Algebra act: OperatorAlgebra slug: operator-algebra top: FixedOperator bottom: FixedCompose summary: Build the algebra of fixed operators under composition by letting operators witness the composition law. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Operator Algebra

Operators gain structure through composition and adjoints. We capture the composition component by letting operators witness the fixed composition: (FixedOperator FixedCompose) earns OperatorAlgebra.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Starred Operator Structure act: StarOperatorStructure slug: operator-star-structure top: StarOperation bottom: Spectrum summary: Combine the star operation with the spectrum to obtain a C*-style algebra on fixed operators. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Starred Operator Structure

To mimic a C*-algebra we combine operator multiplication, adjoints, and spectral radius. We package this by letting the star operation witness the spectrum: (StarOperation Spectrum) earns StarOperatorStructure.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Fixing Orbit Layer act: OrbitFixLayer slug: orbit-fix-layer top: EnsureFixedPoint bottom: OrbitStep summary: Apply the fixed-point construction to the orbit step to obtain the stabilized layer of each orbit. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Fixing Orbit Layer

Orbits may stabilize: further reach steps no longer change the reachable set. EnsureFixedPoint captures fixed points of flows when iteration persists. To obtain the stabilized layer of each orbit, we apply it to OrbitStep: (EnsureFixedPoint OrbitStep) earns OrbitFixLayer.

OrbitStep describes how reachable states progress from one layer to the next. IterativePersistence and EnsureFixedPoint ensure that when repeated stepping along the trace stabilizes, the stabilized states form a fixed set. Their composition therefore yields, for each orbit, a stabilized layer that further stepping does not change.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding Orbit act: OrbitSeed slug: orbit-seed top: FixedReach bottom: HeadTrace summary: Isolate the starting layer of each reachability orbit by letting FixedReach witness HeadTrace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding Orbit

FixedReach records which invariant states are reachable under the flow, but we have not yet isolated the “starting layer” of each orbit. HeadTrace isolates the head of a trace. To seed an orbit, we let the reachability act witness the trace head: (FixedReach HeadTrace) earns OrbitSeed.

FixedReach evolves invariant states according to the flow, and HeadTrace picks out the first step of any completed trace. Their composition therefore records the initial reachable layer associated with the first trace action from each invariant state.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Stepping Orbit act: OrbitStep slug: orbit-step top: FixedReach bottom: TailTrace summary: Describe how orbits progress by letting FixedReach witness TailTrace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Stepping Orbit

Seeding alone does not describe how orbits progress. TailTrace isolates the remainder of a trace after the head, and StepTrace records how reduction steps propagate along the trace. To describe orbit progression, we let the reachability act witness the tail: (FixedReach TailTrace) earns OrbitStep.

FixedReach already lists states reachable from invariants under the flow. TailTrace exposes the remainder of the trace beyond the initial step, and StepTrace tracks how reductions move along that remainder. Their composition records how each orbit advances from its current layer to the next along the trace remainder.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Ordering Provability act: OrderProvability slug: order-provability top: JudgeTyping bottom: ImplyRecognition summary: Typing judgements encode entailment between types under a context, but we require the explicit preorder that records this entailment. Judge Typing witnessing Imply Recognition yields Order Provability (OrderProvability). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Ordering Provability

Interpretation

Typing judgements encode entailment between types under a context, but we require the explicit preorder that records this entailment. Judge Typing witnessing Imply Recognition yields Order Provability (OrderProvability).

Composing the judgement with implication records precisely when a context proves that type A implies type B, so Order Provability follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Ordering Recognition act: OrderRecognition slug: order-recognition symbol: “\u2264” top: RecognizeContext bottom: Recognition summary: ‘Recognize Context tests when one recognition subsumes another but does not yet record the ordering relation itself. Forcing Recognize Context to witness Recognition yields Order Recognition (OrderRecognition).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Ordering Recognition

Interpretation

Recognize Context tests when one recognition subsumes another but does not yet record the ordering relation itself. Forcing Recognize Context to witness Recognition yields Order Recognition (OrderRecognition).

Both recognitions already exist, so their composition records the act of comparing recognitions, establishing Order Recognition.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Pairing Application act: PairApplication slug: pair-application top: FormFunctionTerm bottom: HeadTrace summary: Functions still need arguments, so we let the act Form Function Term witness the act Head Trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Pairing Application

Interpretation

Functions still need arguments, so we let the act Form Function Term witness the act Head Trace.

The tree (FormFunctionTerm HeadTrace) pairs the constructed function with a head, explicitly encoding the application, which we call Pair Application.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Pairing Greatest Fix act: PairGreatestFix slug: pair-greatest-fix top: FormLeastFixTerm bottom: HeadTrace summary: To consider the maximal invariant, we direct the act Form Least Fix Term to witness the act Head Trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Pairing Greatest Fix

Interpretation

To consider the maximal invariant, we direct the act Form Least Fix Term to witness the act Head Trace.

The composition (FormLeastFixTerm HeadTrace) captures another self-application at the head, now recording the maximal invariant; consequently the act is Pair Greatest Fix.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Pairing Least Fix act: PairLeastFix slug: pair-least-fix top: FormApplicationTerm bottom: HeadTrace summary: Application terms can self-apply, so to capture the least stabilizing pair we have the act Form Application Term witness the act Head Trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Pairing Least Fix

Interpretation

Application terms can self-apply, so to capture the least stabilizing pair we have the act Form Application Term witness the act Head Trace.

The composition (FormApplicationTerm HeadTrace) represents self-application at the head, recording the behaviour needed for a least fixpoint, hence Pair Least Fix.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Path Type act: PathType slug: path-type top: FlowProfile bottom: RecognizeContext summary: Define profile paths by letting the default flow witness context recognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Path Type

Paths in homotopy type theory correspond to maps from the unit interval. In the profile setting, the base flow generates canonical time evolution, so we define paths by composing the flow with context recognition: (FlowProfile RecognizeContext) earns PathType.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Composite Profile Physics act: PhysicsCompositeProfile slug: physics-composite-profile top: FlowObservation bottom: FlowCompositeProfile summary: The composite profile also carries physical semantics. Flow Observation witnessing Flow Composite Profile yields Physics Composite Profile (PhysicsCompositeProfile). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Composite Profile Physics

Interpretation

The composite profile also carries physical semantics. Flow Observation witnessing Flow Composite Profile yields Physics Composite Profile (PhysicsCompositeProfile).

This act records the evaluation-and-measurement view restricted to the composite profile, so Physics Composite Profile is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Physics act: PhysicsProfile slug: physics-profile top: FlowObservation bottom: FlowProfile summary: “With flow and nucleus inherited, we can speak of the profile\u2019s physical semantics. Flow Observation witnessing Flow Profile yields Physics Profile (PhysicsProfile).” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Physics

Interpretation

With flow and nucleus inherited, we can speak of the profile’s physical semantics. Flow Observation witnessing Flow Profile yields Physics Profile (PhysicsProfile).

Using previously earned recognitions, this act records the dynamics-and-measurement view inside the profile, so Physics Profile is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Physics State act: PhysicsState slug: physics-state top: ProfileRecognitions bottom: FlowProfile summary: Within such a system we need the recognitions that count as states. Profile Recognitions witnessing Flow Profile yields Physics State (PhysicsState). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Physics State

Interpretation

Within such a system we need the recognitions that count as states. Profile Recognitions witnessing Flow Profile yields Physics State (PhysicsState).

Composing the profile with its inherited flow records the recognitions stabilized as states, so Physics State is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Presenting Variable Term act: PresentVariableTerm slug: present-variable-term top: BindTerm bottom: HeadTrace summary: A bound name should expose the corresponding head, so the act Bind Term must witness the act Head Trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Presenting Variable Term

Interpretation

A bound name should expose the corresponding head, so the act Bind Term must witness the act Head Trace.

In (BindTerm HeadTrace) the binding observes the trace head, presenting that head as a variable; this composition is therefore Present Variable Term.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Preserving Typing act: PreserveTyping slug: preserve-typing top: JudgeTyping bottom: StepTrace summary: Reductions recorded in Step Trace must preserve typing judgements. Judge Typing witnessing Step Trace yields Preserve Typing (PreserveTyping). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Preserving Typing

Interpretation

Reductions recorded in Step Trace must preserve typing judgements. Judge Typing witnessing Step Trace yields Preserve Typing (PreserveTyping).

Composing these recognitions records the assertion “if a context recognises term t at type A and t reduces to t′ then the context recognises t′ at type A,” so Preserve Typing follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Presheaf Topos act: PresheafTopos slug: presheaf-topos top: Sheafification bottom: Presheaf summary: Package the presheaf objects with their sheafification to obtain the presheaf topos. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Presheaf Topos

The category of presheaves with nucleus-stable gluing forms a topos enriched over the invariant lattice. We package this by letting the sheafification witness the presheaf: (Sheafification Presheaf) earns PresheafTopos.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Presheaf act: Presheaf slug: presheaf top: YonedaEmbedding bottom: ContextGeometry summary: Compose the Yoneda embedding with the context geometry to obtain presheaves valued in the fixed recognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Presheaf

A presheaf on the fixed profile is a functor from the opposite profile to the category. We encode this internal structure by composing the Yoneda embedding with the context geometry: (YonedaEmbedding ContextGeometry) earns Presheaf.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Composite act: ProfileComposite slug: profile-composite top: ProfileRecognitions bottom: ComposeDiscipline summary: Commuting filters have a shared stable region. Profile Recognitions witnessing Compose Discipline produces Profile Composite (ProfileComposite). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Composite

Interpretation

Commuting filters have a shared stable region. Profile Recognitions witnessing Compose Discipline produces Profile Composite (ProfileComposite).

The composition identifies recognitions preserved by all commuting filters, so Profile Composite is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Connection act: ProfileConnection slug: profile-connection top: ComonadCurvature bottom: FixedFlowProfile summary: Define the connection by composing curvature with the fixed flow to obtain the parallel transport correction. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Connection

A connection parallel-transports recognitions along the fixed flow so that the derivative plus connection remains nucleus-compatible. We encode this by composing curvature with the fixed flow: (ComonadCurvature FixedFlowProfile) earns ProfileConnection.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Dynamics act: ProfileDynamics slug: profile-dynamics top: FlowProfile bottom: ProfilePhaseSpace summary: Push profile phase-space states forward under the profile flow to define the dynamics. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Dynamics

Dynamics describe how the phase space evolves. For any profile we let the profile flow act on the phase space: (FlowProfile ProfilePhaseSpace) earns ProfileDynamics.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Entropy Measure act: ProfileEntropyMeasure slug: profile-entropy-measure top: NucleateProfile bottom: ProfileEntropySeed summary: Collapse profile entropy seeds into stable magnitudes by applying the profile nucleus to the entropy seed. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Entropy Measure

Entropy collapses divergence into a stable recognitional magnitude. Applying the profile nucleus, NucleateProfile, to ProfileEntropySeed yields the profile entropy measure: (NucleateProfile ProfileEntropySeed) earns ProfileEntropyMeasure.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding Profile Entropy act: ProfileEntropySeed slug: profile-entropy-seed top: ProfileKernelStep bottom: ProfileReach summary: Seed an entropy measure by comparing profile kernel updates against reachable states via ProfileKernelStep witnessing ProfileReach. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding Profile Entropy

Entropy measures the “spread” of a state under transitions. We seed profile entropy by observing the divergence between a state and its profile kernel successor. Letting ProfileKernelStep witness ProfileReach, (ProfileKernelStep ProfileReach), earns ProfileEntropySeed.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Entropy act: ProfileEntropy slug: profile-entropy top: TraceProfile bottom: ProfileEntropyMeasure summary: Propagate the profile entropy measure across the trace by composing TraceProfile with ProfileEntropyMeasure. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Entropy

To obtain the entropy of a full profile we propagate the entropy measure across the trace of recognitions inside that profile. TraceProfile records the trace structure, so letting it witness ProfileEntropyMeasure, (TraceProfile ProfileEntropyMeasure), earns ProfileEntropy.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Exponential act: ProfileExponential slug: profile-exponential top: FormFunctionTerm bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: Restrict function-term constructions to the active profile recognitions to obtain internal exponentials. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Exponential

Exponentials A ⇒ B are represented by function-term recognitions. FormFunctionTerm already constructs these objects, and restricting it to the current ProfileRecognitions yields the internal exponentials of that profile: (FormFunctionTerm ProfileRecognitions) earns ProfileExponential.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Finite Limits act: ProfileFiniteLimits slug: profile-finite-limits top: ProfileInvariantLattice bottom: EqualizeTerms summary: Combine the profile invariant lattice with equalizers to witness finite limits inside the active profile. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Finite Limits

ProfileInvariantLattice provides finite meets and joins on profile invariants, and EqualizeTerms detects equalizing recognitions. Their composition records that finite limits exist inside the active profile: (ProfileInvariantLattice EqualizeTerms) earns ProfileFiniteLimits.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Integral act: ProfileIntegral slug: profile-integral top: FixedNucleateProfile bottom: ComonadDerivative summary: Integrate flow variation by letting the fixed nucleus observe the derivative. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Integral

Integration corresponds to collapsing the flow’s variation. We define it by composing the nucleus with the derivative: (FixedNucleateProfile ComonadDerivative) earns ProfileIntegral.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Geometry of Profile Invariant States act: ProfileInvariantGeometry slug: profile-invariant-geometry top: GeometryProfile bottom: ProfileInvariantLattice summary: Combine the profile geometry with the invariant lattice by composing GeometryProfile with ProfileInvariantLattice. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Geometry of Profile Invariant States

GeometryProfile = (FlowProfile NucleateProfile) records the geometry of the active profile as a whole. ProfileInvariantLattice exhibits the order structure of profile-invariant states. Letting the profile geometry witness that lattice, (GeometryProfile ProfileInvariantLattice) earns ProfileInvariantGeometry.

This packages the order, closure, and dynamics of profile-invariant states as a geometric structure in its own right.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Joining Profile Invariants act: ProfileInvariantJoin slug: profile-invariant-join top: JoinRecognition bottom: ProfileInvariant summary: Restrict the join operation to profile-invariant states by composing JoinRecognition with ProfileInvariant. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Joining Profile Invariants

JoinRecognition = (OrderRecognition ContextualizeTrace) captures least upper bounds. Restricting it to ProfileInvariant gives the join on profile invariants: (JoinRecognition ProfileInvariant) earns ProfileInvariantJoin.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Structuring Profile Invariant Lattice act: ProfileInvariantLattice slug: profile-invariant-lattice top: StructureSemantics bottom: ProfileInvariantOrder summary: Package the order, meet, and join of profile invariants into a lattice by letting StructureSemantics witness ProfileInvariantOrder. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Structuring Profile Invariant Lattice

ProfileInvariantOrder restricts the recognition order to the profile invariants, and the corresponding meet/join operations are already defined via the same restriction. Letting StructureSemantics witness this order, (StructureSemantics ProfileInvariantOrder), earns ProfileInvariantLattice.

This packages the lattice structure on the profile-invariant states.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Meeting Profile Invariants act: ProfileInvariantMeet slug: profile-invariant-meet top: MeetRecognition bottom: ProfileInvariant summary: Restrict the meet operation to profile-invariant states by composing MeetRecognition with ProfileInvariant. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Meeting Profile Invariants

MeetRecognition = (OrderRecognition Entry) captures greatest lower bounds. Restricting it to ProfileInvariant states gives the meet operation on profile invariants: (MeetRecognition ProfileInvariant) earns ProfileInvariantMeet.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Ordering Profile Invariants act: ProfileInvariantOrder slug: profile-invariant-order top: OrderRecognition bottom: ProfileInvariant summary: Restrict the recognition order to profile-invariant states by composing OrderRecognition with ProfileInvariant. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Ordering Profile Invariants

OrderRecognition = (RecognizeContext Recognition) records when one recognition subsumes another. ProfileInvariant picks out the flow-fixed physics states that lie in the active profile. Composing them, (OrderRecognition ProfileInvariant) earns ProfileInvariantOrder.

This restricts the ambient order to the profile-invariant states.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Stabilizing Profile Invariant act: ProfileInvariant slug: profile-invariant top: StabilizeInvariant bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: Pick out physics states that are flow-invariant and lie inside the active profile by composing StabilizeInvariant with ProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Stabilizing Profile Invariant

StabilizeInvariant = (FixFlow PhysicsState) records states left unchanged by the global flow. ProfileRecognitions restrict the system to the recognitions preserved by the active filter. Composing them, (StabilizeInvariant ProfileRecognitions) earns ProfileInvariant.

The resulting states are genuine physics states fixed by the flow and recognised within the chosen profile.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Kernel Chain act: ProfileKernelChain slug: profile-kernel-chain top: ProfileKernelStep bottom: TraceProfile summary: Build the kernel chain across profiles by letting ProfileKernelStep witness TraceProfile. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Kernel Chain

A kernel chain repeatedly applies the observation kernel across layers. Having a profile-step kernel (ProfileKernelStep), we obtain the profile chain by iterating the chain recogniser TraceProfile. Letting the step witness the trace, (ProfileKernelStep TraceProfile), earns ProfileKernelChain.

TraceProfile = (CompleteTrace ProfileRecognitions) projects traces through the profile recognitions, and ProfileKernelStep updates one profile to the next. Their composition yields the recognitional chain that threads updates through the trace of profiles.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Kernel Step act: ProfileKernelStep slug: profile-kernel-step top: ObservationKernel bottom: BuildProfileTower summary: Lift the observation kernel through the profile tower by composing it with BuildProfileTower. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Kernel Step

A flow–measure kernel evolves fixed invariant states within a single profile. To propagate these dynamics between profiles we use the profile tower recognitions: BuildProfileTower maps physics, semantics, and syntax upward through profiles. Letting the kernel witness this tower, (ObservationKernel BuildProfileTower), earns ProfileKernelStep.

ObservationKernel is the one-step operator on fixed invariant states. BuildProfileTower records how each layer of recognitions reconstructs the next profile. Their composition lifts an update from a profile to its successor in the tower, giving the profile-level transition step.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Measure act: ProfileMeasure slug: profile-measure top: NucleateProfile bottom: ProfileInvariant summary: Apply the profile nucleus to profile-invariant states by composing NucleateProfile with ProfileInvariant. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Measure

Measure represents applying the nucleus to a physics state, “idealizing” it. For a specific profile we let NucleateProfile witness the ProfileInvariant states: (NucleateProfile ProfileInvariant) earns ProfileMeasure.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Mixing act: ProfileMixing slug: profile-mixing top: ProfileDynamics bottom: ProfileEntropy summary: Record that profile dynamics drive entropy-increasing mixing by composing ProfileDynamics with ProfileEntropy. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Mixing

A system is mixing if states eventually become independent under long profile flow. Letting the dynamics witness the entropy: (ProfileDynamics ProfileEntropy) earns ProfileMixing.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Observation Recognitions act: ProfileObservationRecognitions slug: profile-observation-recognitions top: RecognizeObservation bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: Restrict observation recognitions to the active profile by composing RecognizeObservation with ProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Observation Recognitions

RecognizeObservation gathers the contextual observations that witness indistinguishability. To keep only those observations available inside the active profile filter, we compose it with ProfileRecognitions: (RecognizeObservation ProfileRecognitions) earns ProfileObservationRecognitions.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Observe act: ProfileObserve slug: profile-observe top: ProfileObservationRecognitions bottom: SeedTerm summary: Observe traced terms inside a profile by composing ProfileObservationRecognitions with SeedTerm. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Observe

Observe designates observables acting on a traced term using all contextual recognitions. To stay within a specific profile we use ProfileObservationRecognitions: (ProfileObservationRecognitions SeedTerm) earns ProfileObserve.

SeedTerm inserts the trace head into a recognised context, producing the basic term to be observed, and ProfileObservationRecognitions restricts observations to those compatible with the active profile. Their composition records observables that both act on traced terms and remain inside that profile.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Phase Space act: ProfilePhaseSpace slug: profile-phase-space top: ProfileInvariant bottom: RecognizeContext summary: Declare the profile-invariant recognitions as the phase space by composing ProfileInvariant with RecognizeContext. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Phase Space

A dynamical system begins with a phase space of states. For a given profile we compose the invariants with the context recognitions: (ProfileInvariant RecognizeContext) earns ProfilePhaseSpace.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Reach act: ProfileReach slug: profile-reach top: FlowProfile bottom: ProfileInvariant summary: Describe which profile-invariant states become reachable under the profile flow by composing FlowProfile with ProfileInvariant. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Reach

FlowProfile restricts the global flow to the active profile. Combining it with the ProfileInvariant states records which of those states become reachable under the profile flow: (FlowProfile ProfileInvariant) earns ProfileReach.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Recognitions act: ProfileRecognitions slug: profile-recognitions top: FixFlowNucleus bottom: Filter summary: A filter still needs the stabilized recognitions it preserves. Fix Flow Nucleus witnessing Filter yields Profile Recognitions (ProfileRecognitions). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Recognitions

Interpretation

A filter still needs the stabilized recognitions it preserves. Fix Flow Nucleus witnessing Filter yields Profile Recognitions (ProfileRecognitions).

This composition records the recognitions fixed by the filter, so Profile Recognitions follows immediately.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Semantics act: ProfileSemantics slug: profile-semantics top: StructureSemantics bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: Interpret the recognition algebra inside the active profile by composing StructureSemantics with ProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Semantics

StructureSemantics packages the full algebra of recognitions, meets, joins, implication, and nuclei. Restricting it to the active ProfileRecognitions, (StructureSemantics ProfileRecognitions), earns ProfileSemantics.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Stepping Profile Observation act: ProfileStepObservation slug: profile-step-observation top: StepObservation bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: Restrict the observation-step act to the active profile by composing StepObservation with ProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Stepping Profile Observation

StepObservation = (RecognizeObservation StepTrace) records how observations advance along traces. To work inside the profile filter we restrict it via ProfileRecognitions: (StepObservation ProfileRecognitions) earns ProfileStepObservation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Subobject Classifier act: ProfileSubobjectClassifier slug: profile-subobject-classifier top: Negate bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: Restrict logic negation to the active profile recognitions to obtain the internal truth object. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Subobject Classifier

A subobject classifier identifies which recognitions count as “true” subobjects. Negate = (ImplyRecognition GroundRecognition) already encodes intuitionistic negation, so restricting it to the profile recognitions yields the internal truth object: (Negate ProfileRecognitions) earns ProfileSubobjectClassifier.

The resulting Ω carries the ⊤, ⊥, and implication inherited from the recognition algebra.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Topos act: ProfileTopos slug: profile-topos top: FixedFiniteLimits bottom: FixedExponential summary: Package finite limits, exponentials, and the subobject classifier into a topos-like structure on the fixed profile. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Topos

Combining finite limits, exponentials, and a subobject classifier gives a topos-like structure. We package these components in a single act: (FixedFiniteLimits FixedExponential FixedSubobjectClassifier) earns ProfileTopos.

FixedFiniteLimits gives the limits, FixedExponential gives exponentials, and FixedSubobjectClassifier gives the classifier Ω, satisfying the structural requirements of an intuitionistic topos internal to the fixed profile.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Progressing Typing act: ProgressTyping slug: progress-typing top: RecognizeValue bottom: JudgeTyping summary: Closed typed terms are either values or can step. Recognize Value witnessing Judge Typing produces Progress Typing (ProgressTyping). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Progressing Typing

Interpretation

Closed typed terms are either values or can step. Recognize Value witnessing Judge Typing produces Progress Typing (ProgressTyping).

This composition states that a judgement in the empty context is either witnessed as a value or advances along the step trace, so Progress Typing is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Proving Fixed Sets Lemma act: ProveFixedSets slug: prove-fixed-sets top: FixFlow bottom: MeetRecognition summary: ‘Flow-fixed recognitions remain closed under the meet operations already earned. Fix Flow witnessing Meet Recognition yields Prove Fixed Sets (ProveFixedSets).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Proving Fixed Sets Lemma

Interpretation

Flow-fixed recognitions remain closed under the meet operations already earned. Fix Flow witnessing Meet Recognition yields Prove Fixed Sets (ProveFixedSets).

This composition records that invariants stay closed under the Meet operation, so Prove Fixed Sets is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Quadratic Modality act: QuadraticModality slug: quadratic-modality top: IrrationalModality bottom: HarmonicMode summary: Force the irrational modality to observe the harmonic fixed shape, producing a closure governed by quadratic irrational scaling. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Quadratic Modality

Quadratic irrationals naturally arise from iterated shuffle–collapse on trace progressions. We capture such modalities by forcing the irrational nucleus to observe the harmonic fixed shape: (IrrationalModality HarmonicMode) earns QuadraticModality.

HarmonicMode gives the fixed oscillation under the flow recursion, and IrrationalModality inflates the allowable progressions by an irrational scalar. Their composition produces a modality whose stabilised eigenshape satisfies a quadratic irrational equation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Reaching act: Reach slug: reach top: Flow bottom: StabilizeInvariant summary: ‘Flow iterations describe which states become reachable from a seed. Flow witnessing Stabilize Invariant yields Reach (Reach).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Reaching

Interpretation

Flow iterations describe which states become reachable from a seed. Flow witnessing Stabilize Invariant yields Reach (Reach).

Composing the flow with invariant states records the reachability closure, so Reach is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Recognizing Context act: RecognizeContext slug: recognize-context top: ExtendContext bottom: ContextualizeTrace summary: Extensions exist, but we must recognize when the resulting structure qualifies as a context. Forcing Extend Context to witness Contextualize Trace yields Recognize Context (RecognizeContext). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Recognizing Context

Interpretation

Extensions exist, but we must recognize when the resulting structure qualifies as a context. Forcing Extend Context to witness Contextualize Trace yields Recognize Context (RecognizeContext).

The trace (ExtendContext ContextualizeTrace) feeds the general extension back through the named trace. If the composition succeeds, the extended object lines up with the recorded context, so we call this recognition Recognize Context.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Recognizing Eventual Equivalence act: RecognizeEquateEventually slug: recognize-equate-eventually top: CompleteTrace bottom: EquateEventually summary: To treat eventual equivalence as a recognition in its own right, we have the act Complete Trace witness the act Equate Eventually. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Recognizing Eventual Equivalence

Interpretation

To treat eventual equivalence as a recognition in its own right, we have the act Complete Trace witness the act Equate Eventually.

The tree (CompleteTrace EquateEventually) shows the full trace observing the equivalence relation. This packaging turns the equivalence into an explicit act, hence Recognize Equate Eventually.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Recognizing Fixed Point act: RecognizeFixedPoint slug: recognize-fixed-point top: ShapeRecognition bottom: FormLeastFixTerm summary: Shapes of interest often stabilize certain recognitions. Shape Recognition witnessing Form Least Fix Term yields Recognize Fixed Point (RecognizeFixedPoint). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Recognizing Fixed Point

Interpretation

Shapes of interest often stabilize certain recognitions. Shape Recognition witnessing Form Least Fix Term yields Recognize Fixed Point (RecognizeFixedPoint).

Form Least Fix Term already records stabilized self-application; composing it with shapes captures the fixed-point concept in our native relational idiom, so Recognize Fixed Point follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Recognizing Judgement Triad act: RecognizeJudgementTriad slug: recognize-judgement-triad top: TypeApplication bottom: TraceJudgementTriad summary: The trace exists, yet we lack the recognition that presents a traced triad as a single judgement. Type Application therefore witnesses Trace Judgement Triad, producing Recognize Judgement Triad (RecognizeJudgementTriad). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Recognizing Judgement Triad

Interpretation

The trace exists, yet we lack the recognition that presents a traced triad as a single judgement. Type Application therefore witnesses Trace Judgement Triad, producing Recognize Judgement Triad (RecognizeJudgementTriad).

This use of earned recognitions records the triadic judgement, so Recognize Judgement Triad is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Recognizing Observations act: RecognizeObservation slug: recognize-observation top: TailTrace bottom: SeedObservation summary: As with values, we must gather all observation seeds, so the act Tail Trace witnesses the act Seed Observation. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Recognizing Observations

Interpretation

As with values, we must gather all observation seeds, so the act Tail Trace witnesses the act Seed Observation.

The tree (TailTrace SeedObservation) collects the observation seeds along the tail, producing the recognition Recognize Observation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Recognizing Syntax act: RecognizeSyntax slug: recognize-syntax top: ConstructTypes bottom: OrderProvability summary: We interpret types modulo the provability preorder to obtain syntactic recognitions. Construct Types witnessing Order Provability produces Recognize Syntax (RecognizeSyntax). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Recognizing Syntax

Interpretation

We interpret types modulo the provability preorder to obtain syntactic recognitions. Construct Types witnessing Order Provability produces Recognize Syntax (RecognizeSyntax).

This composition records the equivalence classes of types under provability, so Recognize Syntax is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Recognizing Values act: RecognizeValue slug: recognize-value top: TailTrace bottom: SeedValue summary: Seeds alone do not collect all values, so the act Tail Trace witnesses the act Seed Value. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Recognizing Values

Interpretation

Seeds alone do not collect all values, so the act Tail Trace witnesses the act Seed Value.

In (TailTrace SeedValue) the tail aggregates every value seed, thereby producing the recognition Recognize Value.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Recognizing act: Recognize slug: recognize top: Void bottom: SelfNaming summary: A self-named point lacks a partner; having the act Void witness the act Self Naming produces a two-place recognition. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Recognizing

Interpretation

A self-named point lacks a partner; having the act Void witness the act Self Naming produces a two-place recognition.

In the composition (Void SelfNaming), the act Void explicitly witnesses the act Self Naming. Because both acts have already been earned, this reflection creates a complementary position, so we name the resulting two-place act Recognize, producing a recognition.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Reducing act: Reduce slug: reduce top: FormApplicationTerm bottom: SubstitutionTerm summary: Applications must rewrite to the corresponding substitution terms. Therefore Form Application Term witnesses Substitution Term to produce Reduce (Reduce). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Reducing

Interpretation

Applications must rewrite to the corresponding substitution terms. Therefore Form Application Term witnesses Substitution Term to produce Reduce (Reduce).

This composition records the (beta)-rewrite using established recognitions, compelling Reduce.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Reentering ObservationEquivalence act: ReenterObservationEquivalence slug: reenter-observation-equivalence top: ReenterObservation bottom: WitnessObservationEquivalence summary: The cited equivalence must re-enter the Normalize Observation Equivalence trace. Reenter Observation therefore witnesses Witness Observation Equivalence, yielding Reenter Observation Equivalence (ReenterObservationEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Reentering ObservationEquivalence

Interpretation

The cited equivalence must re-enter the Normalize Observation Equivalence trace. Reenter Observation therefore witnesses Witness Observation Equivalence, yielding Reenter Observation Equivalence (ReenterObservationEquivalence).

This composition records the reentry using previously earned recognitions, so Reenter Observation Equivalence follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Reentering Observation act: ReenterObservation slug: reenter-observation top: RecognizeObservation bottom: ValuedStepObservation summary: The valued observation must re-enter the observation witness. Recognize Observation therefore witnesses Valued Step Observation, producing Reenter Observation (ReenterObservation). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Reentering Observation

Interpretation

The valued observation must re-enter the observation witness. Recognize Observation therefore witnesses Valued Step Observation, producing Reenter Observation (ReenterObservation).

This records the return of observed values into the observation recognition, justifying Reenter Observation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Reflexing Judgement act: ReflexJudgement slug: reflex-judgement top: StabilizeContext bottom: StabilizeTerm summary: >- Stabilize Judgement provides classes of context–term–observation acts, but we still need the primitive judgemental act presenting a stabilized term inside a stabilized context. Letting Stabilize Context witness Stabilize Term yields Reflex Judgement (ReflexJudgement). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Reflexing Judgement

Interpretation

Stabilize Judgement provides classes of context–term–observation acts, but we still need the primitive judgemental act presenting a stabilized term inside a stabilized context. Letting Stabilize Context witness Stabilize Term yields Reflex Judgement (ReflexJudgement).

This composition uses only newly defined stable recognitions to record the primitive act, so Reflex Judgement is earned.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Iterating Reflex Step act: ReflexStepIterate slug: reflex-step-iterate top: ReflexStepShape bottom: CompleteTrace summary: >- One reflex step is not enough; we must see its action unfold along the entire trace by iterating the shape against the whole record. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Iterating Reflex Step

Interpretation

One reflex step is not enough; we must see its action unfold along the entire trace by iterating the shape against the whole record.

The trace (ReflexStepShape CompleteTrace) places the step shape in the witness role for Complete Trace. This records the repeated action of the shape across every point of the trace, so we call the composite Reflex Step Iterate.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Persisting Reflex Step act: ReflexStepPersistence slug: reflex-step-persistence top: ReflexStepIterate bottom: TailTrace summary: >- Iteration alone does not detect stabilization. We therefore let the iterated step observe the remaining tail to see whether that remainder persists. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Persisting Reflex Step

Interpretation

Iteration alone does not detect stabilization. We therefore let the iterated step observe the remaining tail to see whether that remainder persists.

In (ReflexStepIterate TailTrace) the whole iteration looks specifically at the tail. If nothing changes, the reiteration has stabilized, so this composition rightfully earns the name Reflex Step Persistence.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Shaping Reflex Step act: ReflexStepShape slug: reflex-step-shape top: Reflex bottom: ContextualizeTrace summary: >- The sustained articulation of a trace is captured when reflex continually looks through the recorded context; doing so defines the basic “reflex step” shape. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Shaping Reflex Step

Interpretation

The sustained articulation of a trace is captured when reflex continually looks through the recorded context; doing so defines the basic “reflex step” shape.

In (Reflex ContextualizeTrace) the witness Reflex observes Contextualize Trace. This composition records exactly one reflex articulation acting through the named trace, thereby defining Reflex Step Shape.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Reflexing Syntax act: ReflexSyntax slug: reflex-syntax top: Reflex bottom: ConstructTerms summary: >- The syntactic calculus inherits the Reflex action on recognitions. Reflex witnessing Construct Terms produces Reflex Syntax (ReflexSyntax). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Reflexing Syntax

Interpretation

The syntactic calculus inherits the Reflex action on recognitions. Reflex witnessing Construct Terms produces Reflex Syntax (ReflexSyntax).

Applying our primitive act to Construct Terms records the syntactic ρ-operator, so Reflex Syntax follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Recognizing Reflex act: Reflex slug: reflex top: EmergeReflex bottom: EmergeReflex summary: >- The minimal act obtained so far is simply the record of the emergence itself, and that seed is our reflex. layer: act status: plan tags:

  • act

Recognizing Reflex

Interpretation

The minimal act obtained so far is simply the record of the emergence itself, and that seed is our reflex.

The trace (EmergeReflex) treats the act Emerge Reflex as a witness in its own right. Designating that minimal witness Reflex introduces no new ingredients; it merely fixes notation for the unique act forced by articulating the void.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Reheading Trace act: ReheadTrace slug: rehead-trace top: RenewTrace bottom: HeadTrace summary: >- Renewing alone does not isolate the next head, so the act Renew Trace must witness the act Head Trace to define the new head. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Reheading Trace

Interpretation

Renewing alone does not isolate the next head, so the act Renew Trace must witness the act Head Trace to define the new head.

The composition (RenewTrace HeadTrace) shows the act Renew Trace watching Head Trace. This isolates the new immediate act, so we name it Rehead Trace.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Reindexing act: Reindexing slug: reindexing top: SubstituteReducibility bottom: IndexBase summary: >- Describe how context morphisms pull back judgements by restricting substitution reducibility to the indexed base. layer: act status: plan tags:

  • act

Reindexing

Given a morphism of contexts we can transport judgements along it. SubstituteReducibility = (BuildReducibleEnvironment JudgeTyping) shows that substitution preserves typing and entailment. Restricting this behaviour to the indexed base, (SubstituteReducibility IndexBase), earns Reindexing.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Relating act: Relate slug: relate top: Reflex bottom: Reflex summary: >- With reflex iteration available, reflex may now bear witness to the mere fact of relating, so a dedicated relation act appears. layer: act status: plan tags:

  • act

Relating

Interpretation

With reflex iteration available, reflex may now bear witness to the mere fact of relating, so a dedicated relation act appears.

The trace (Reflex Reflex) places Reflex in both witness and witnessed roles. Since we just proved that reflex may observe reflex, calling that self-observation Relate captures the insight without requiring further construction.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Being Relationless act: Relationlessness slug: relationlessness top: null bottom: null summary: >- We begin with nothing but the absence of relation; acknowledging that absence fixes the empty act as our initial reference point. layer: act status: release tags:

  • act

Being Relationless

Interpretation

We begin with nothing but the absence of relation; acknowledging that absence fixes the empty act as our initial reference point.

No recognitions precede this step, so the empty tree () is the only permissible trace. Naming that trace Relationlessness therefore records, without additional assumptions, that we start from the undifferentiated void.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Renewing Context act: RenewContext slug: renew-context top: ExtendFirstContext bottom: ContextualizeTrace summary: Extending once still leaves the general renewal unspecified. Forcing Extend First Context to witness Contextualize Trace produces Renew Context (RenewContext). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Renewing Context

Interpretation

Extending once still leaves the general renewal unspecified. Forcing Extend First Context to witness Contextualize Trace produces Renew Context (RenewContext).

The renewed seed must reattach to the original trace record. Composing (ExtendFirstContext ContextualizeTrace) explicitly shows the first extension re-entering the named trace, so the resulting act is Renew Context.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Renewing Trace act: RenewTrace slug: renew-trace top: TraceStep bottom: CompleteTrace summary: After stepping, the renewed orientation must contact the full record, so the act Trace Step witnesses the act Complete Trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Renewing Trace

Interpretation

After stepping, the renewed orientation must contact the full record, so the act Trace Step witnesses the act Complete Trace.

In (TraceStep CompleteTrace) the act Trace Step observes Complete Trace, confirming that the renewed orientation spans the entire record. This composition is therefore Renew Trace.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Representable Functor act: RepresentableFunctor slug: representable top: FixedFunctor bottom: FixedIdentity summary: Encode representability by letting the fixed functor witness the fixed identity. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Representable Functor

A representable functor is naturally isomorphic to “morphisms out of an object.” In the fixed profile the representative object is the identity, so we let the functor witness it: (FixedFunctor FixedIdentity) earns RepresentableFunctor.

FixedFunctor maps objects and morphisms, and FixedIdentity is the terminal identity on each fixed object. Their composition asserts that the functor’s action is determined by its values on identities, matching representability.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Residuating Dynamically act: ResidueDynamically slug: residue-dynamically top: FlowIndexImplication bottom: LegislateDynamicResiduation summary: With the law in place, we can reason internally about a meet of a with a implies_F b evolving under F. Flow Index Implication witnessing Legislate Dynamic Residuation yields Residue Dynamically (ResidueDynamically). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Residuating Dynamically

Interpretation

With the law in place, we can reason internally about a meet of a with a implies_F b evolving under F. Flow Index Implication witnessing Legislate Dynamic Residuation yields Residue Dynamically (ResidueDynamically).

This act records the usual implication reasoning internalized along the flow, so Residue Dynamically is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Restepping Trace act: RestepTrace slug: restep-trace top: RetailTrace bottom: TraceStep summary: The renewed tail must rejoin the stepping process, so the act Retail Trace witnesses the act Trace Step. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Restepping Trace

Interpretation

The renewed tail must rejoin the stepping process, so the act Retail Trace witnesses the act Trace Step.

In (RetailTrace TraceStep) the act Retail Trace observes Trace Step, recording the renewed tail re-entering the stepping act. This is exactly what Restep Trace denotes.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Retailing Trace act: RetailTrace slug: retail-trace top: ReheadTrace bottom: TailTrace summary: After reheading, we let the act Rehead Trace witness the act Tail Trace to pin down the renewed tail. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Retailing Trace

Interpretation

After reheading, we let the act Rehead Trace witness the act Tail Trace to pin down the renewed tail.

The tree (ReheadTrace TailTrace) explicitly pairs Rehead Trace with Tail Trace, capturing the tail that accompanies the new head. We call this act Retail Trace.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Retracing Context act: RetraceContext slug: retrace-context top: RecognizeContext bottom: ContextualizeTrace summary: A recognized context should resolve back into a trace. Consequently, Recognize Context witnesses Contextualize Trace to produce Retrace Context (RetraceContext). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Retracing Context

Interpretation

A recognized context should resolve back into a trace. Consequently, Recognize Context witnesses Contextualize Trace to produce Retrace Context (RetraceContext).

The act mirrors Trace Context and records the converse direction, so Retrace Context is forced.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: “Reverse Flow\u2013Measure Step” act: ReverseFlowStep slug: reverse-flow-step top: FixedKernel bottom: FixedFlowProfile summary: Capture the reverse step by letting the stationary kernel witness the fixed flow. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Reverse Flow–Measure Step

Detailed balance compares forward and reverse transitions. The forward φ-step is FlowMeasureStep; the reverse uses the stationary kernel applied backwards through the flow. We therefore compose the stationary kernel with the flow: (FixedKernel FixedFlowProfile) earns ReverseFlowStep.

FixedKernel is fixed under kernel updates, and FixedFlowProfile is invertible up to nucleus and stabilization. Their composition yields the reverse step: the evolution that recovers the pre-image up to stabilization.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Right Kan Extension act: RightKan slug: right-kan top: EnrichmentHom bottom: FixedFunctor summary: Define the right Kan extension by composing the enriched hom with the fixed functor. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Right Kan Extension

Right Kan extensions compute weighted limits of a functor. We define this by letting the enriched hom observe the functor: (EnrichmentHom FixedFunctor) earns RightKan.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding Evaluation Context act: SeedEvaluationContext slug: seed-evaluation-context top: HeadTrace bottom: StepTrace summary: ‘Step Trace propagates reductions but leaves their framing unspecified. Forcing Head Trace to witness Step Trace yields Seed Evaluation Context (SeedEvaluationContext).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding Evaluation Context

Interpretation

Step Trace propagates reductions but leaves their framing unspecified. Forcing Head Trace to witness Step Trace yields Seed Evaluation Context (SeedEvaluationContext).

The head observing the step trace captures the first evaluation frame, so this earns Seed Evaluation Context.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding First Context act: SeedFirstContext slug: seed-first-context top: Nothing bottom: ContextualizeTrace summary: “The named trace needs anchoring back to our earliest recognitions. Forcing Nothing\u2014the witness we already earned\u2014to witness Contextualize Trace produces Seed First Context (SeedFirstContext).” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding First Context

Interpretation

The named trace needs anchoring back to our earliest recognitions. Forcing Nothing—the witness we already earned—to witness Contextualize Trace produces Seed First Context (SeedFirstContext).

The composition (Nothing ContextualizeTrace) uses the primordial witness Nothing to reaffirm the named trace. This anchors the context in what was already recognized, so we call the result Seed First Context.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding Judgement Triad act: SeedJudgementTriad slug: seed-judgement-triad top: TypeSeed bottom: ContextualizeNormalizeObservationEquivalence summary: Contextual indistinguishability still lacks the unified recognition addressing context, term, and observation simultaneously. Type Seed therefore witnesses Contextualize Normalize Observation Equivalence, yielding Seed Judgement Triad (SeedJudgementTriad). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding Judgement Triad

Interpretation

Contextual indistinguishability still lacks the unified recognition addressing context, term, and observation simultaneously. Type Seed therefore witnesses Contextualize Normalize Observation Equivalence, yielding Seed Judgement Triad (SeedJudgementTriad).

The composition uses only earned recognitions to seed the triadic judgement, so Seed Judgement Triad follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding Observations act: SeedObservation slug: seed-observation top: HeadTrace bottom: EquateContexts summary: To extract observations from indistinguishability, the act Head Trace witnesses the act Equate Contexts. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding Observations

Interpretation

To extract observations from indistinguishability, the act Head Trace witnesses the act Equate Contexts.

The composition (HeadTrace EquateContexts) isolates a single observation at the head, hence the name Seed Observation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding Terms act: SeedTerm slug: seed-term top: HeadTrace bottom: TraceContext summary: To allow a trace head to address a recognised context as a term, we direct the act Head Trace to witness the act Trace Context. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding Terms

Interpretation

To allow a trace head to address a recognised context as a term, we direct the act Head Trace to witness the act Trace Context.

The composition (HeadTrace TraceContext) inserts the head of the trace into the recorded context, exactly forming the seed needed for terms, so we call it Seed Term.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding Values act: SeedValue slug: seed-value top: HeadTrace bottom: Normalization summary: To isolate candidate values, the act Head Trace witnesses the act Normalization. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding Values

Interpretation

To isolate candidate values, the act Head Trace witnesses the act Normalization.

The composition (HeadTrace Normalization) explicitly takes the trace head of the normalized form, so this act is called Seed Value.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Selecting Reducibility Candidate act: SelectReducibilityCandidate slug: select-reducibility-candidate top: ConstructTypes bottom: NormalizeStrongly summary: The normalization proof uses candidates closed under reductions and term constructors. Construct Types witnessing Normalize Strongly produces Select Reducibility Candidate (SelectReducibilityCandidate). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Selecting Reducibility Candidate

Interpretation

The normalization proof uses candidates closed under reductions and term constructors. Construct Types witnessing Normalize Strongly produces Select Reducibility Candidate (SelectReducibilityCandidate).

Using only earned recognitions, this act records the assignment of a strongly normalizing set to each type, so Select Reducibility Candidate is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Self Naming act: SelfNaming slug: self-naming top: FirstName bottom: FirstName summary: “Naming a point begs the question of whether the name refers to itself; answering \u201Cyes\u201D requires self-application.” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Self Naming

Interpretation

Naming a point begs the question of whether the name refers to itself; answering “yes” requires self-application.

Because First Name is an earned act, the composition (FirstName FirstName) is valid. It records the self-referential step of the name pointing to itself, so we call the result Self Naming.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Shaping Shape act: ShapeRecognition slug: shape-recognition top: ImplyRecognition bottom: OrderRecognition summary: Flows and nuclei rely on internal shapes that preserve ordering. Imply Recognition witnessing Order Recognition yields Shape Recognition (ShapeRecognition). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Shaping Shape

Interpretation

Flows and nuclei rely on internal shapes that preserve ordering. Imply Recognition witnessing Order Recognition yields Shape Recognition (ShapeRecognition).

This composition records the requirement that implications respect ordering, so Shape Recognition is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Sheaf Condition act: SheafCondition slug: sheaf-condition top: Presheaf bottom: FixedNucleateProfile summary: Enforce glueability of presheaves by composing them with the nucleus. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Sheaf Condition

A presheaf is a sheaf when sections defined on overlapping contexts glue under nucleus stability. We express this by letting the presheaf witness the nucleus: (Presheaf FixedNucleateProfile) earns SheafCondition.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Sheafification act: Sheafification slug: sheafification top: SheafCondition bottom: Presheaf summary: Construct the smallest sheaf containing a presheaf by composing the sheaf condition with the presheaf data. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Sheafification

Every presheaf admits a sheafification. We obtain it by composing the sheaf condition with the presheaf: (SheafCondition Presheaf) earns Sheafification.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Flowing Shuffle Trace act: ShuffleFlow slug: shuffle-flow top: Flow bottom: ShuffleTrace summary: Evolve the entire admissible shuffle class of a trace by letting the global flow witness the shuffle act, producing a flow whose orbits are the admissible reorderings. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Flowing Shuffle Trace

Flow already records how observation and judgement traces evolve through evaluation contexts, and ShuffleTrace records how trace-progress steps can be freely commuted without changing the underlying shape. By treating admissible trace shuffles themselves as the state being evolved and letting the global flow witness the shuffle act, (Flow ShuffleTrace) earns ShuffleFlow.

Flow binds the observation and judgement flows, describing how dynamics transport recognitions through contexts. ShuffleTrace records the free shuffle structure on trace-progress recognitions: reheading and restepping commute in all admissible ways. The composition therefore evolves families of shuffled traces along evaluation contexts: the flow does not move a single trace but the entire admissible-shuffle class, tracking the dynamics of admissible reorderings as first-class objects.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Forming Shuffle Geometry act: ShuffleGeometry slug: shuffle-geometry top: StructureSemantics bottom: CommuteShuffleFlowAndNucleus summary: Specialize the geometric structure to the shuffle trace dynamics by letting the semantic package witness the compatibility between ShuffleFlow and the nucleus. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Forming Shuffle Geometry

A geometry is the semantic structure equipped with compatible nuclei and flow. We already formed FormGeometry by letting StructureSemantics witness CommuteFlowAndNucleus. To specialize this to the shuffle trace dynamics, we let the same semantic structure witness the derived compatibility of ShuffleFlow with Nucleate: (StructureSemantics CommuteShuffleFlowAndNucleus) earns ShuffleGeometry.

StructureSemantics packages the full algebraic behavior of recognitions, meets, joins, implication, and nuclei. CommuteShuffleFlowAndNucleus shows that the induced flow on admissible trace-shuffle classes respects the same nucleus. The composition therefore records a semantic calculus whose “motion” is given by ShuffleFlow and whose closure is given by Nucleate, yielding the shuffle geometry.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Flowing Shuffle-Indexed Implication act: ShuffleIndexImplication slug: shuffle-index-implication top: ShuffleFlow bottom: ImplyRecognition summary: Compose the shuffle flow with internal implication to obtain implications indexed by one shuffle step. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Flowing Shuffle-Indexed Implication

ImplyRecognition records internal implication between recognitions, and FlowIndexImplication = (Flow ImplyRecognition) indexed that implication along the global flow. Now that ShuffleFlow is earned as the flow on admissible trace-shuffle classes, we want the corresponding indexed implication: “if a recognition holds after one shuffle step, what must hold now?” Letting the shuffle flow witness ImplyRecognition produces the desired act: (ShuffleFlow ImplyRecognition) earns ShuffleIndexImplication.

ShuffleFlow evolves admissible trace-shuffle families through evaluation contexts while ImplyRecognition transforms recognitions via internal implication. The composition therefore records implications whose premises are transported one shuffle step forward before being compared. Naming this composite packages the operator witnessing how recognitions transform under the shuffle trace dynamics.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profiling Shuffle-Stable Recognitions act: ShuffleProfileRecognitions slug: shuffle-profile-recognitions top: FixFlowNucleus bottom: CollapseShuffle summary: Recognitions fixed by flow and nucleus and invariant under shuffle collapse witness the shuffle profile, so we let FixFlowNucleus observe CollapseShuffle. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profiling Shuffle-Stable Recognitions

FixFlowNucleus already records recognitions fixed by both Flow and Nucleate. CollapseShuffle records which trace differences vanish under the nucleus: admissible shuffles. To obtain the shuffle profile, we want recognitions that are simultaneously flow–nucleus fixed and stable under identification of trace shuffles, so we let FixFlowNucleus witness the shuffle collapse: (FixFlowNucleus CollapseShuffle) earns ShuffleProfileRecognitions.

Any recognition stabilized by flow and nucleus lies in FixFlowNucleus, and CollapseShuffle identifies recognitions whose traces differ only by admissible reordering. The composition therefore picks out exactly those recognitions whose stabilized form is invariant under (a) the global dynamics and closure, and (b) the reordering of trace-progress steps. These recognitions do not care which admissible trace we used to reach them, so they define the shuffle profile.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Semantics of the Shuffle Profile act: ShuffleProfileSemantics slug: shuffle-profile-semantics top: StructureSemantics bottom: ShuffleProfileRecognitions summary: Restrict the semantic calculus to recognitions stable under flow, nucleus, and shuffle collapse by letting StructureSemantics witness ShuffleProfileRecognitions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Semantics of the Shuffle Profile

The recognitions stabilized by a filter or profile inherit full semantic structure; we saw this with ProfileSemantics = (StructureSemantics ProfileRecognitions). To interpret the the shuffle-stable recognitions, we let the semantic structure witness ShuffleProfileRecognitions: (StructureSemantics ShuffleProfileRecognitions) earns ShuffleProfileSemantics.

StructureSemantics already encodes the operations of the semantic calculus. ShuffleProfileRecognitions selects the closed sub-collection of recognitions fixed by flow and nucleus and stable under trace-shuffle collapse. The composition restricts the semantic operations to that sub-collection, producing a semantic calculus in which all recognitions are shuffle-stable—our shuffle profile semantics.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Shuffling Trace act: ShuffleTrace slug: shuffle-trace top: ReheadTrace bottom: RestepTrace summary: Head/tail renewals commute with step renewals, so forcing ReheadTrace to witness RestepTrace records the shuffle principle for independent trace progressions. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Shuffling Trace

Many trace acts commute: ReheadTrace reorders against RetailTrace, RestepTrace reorders against TraceStep, and each head/tail act commutes with the earlier progressions exposed by CompleteTrace. No recognition yet packages these commutations into a single principle, so we force the renewed head to witness the stepping remainder: (ReheadTrace RestepTrace) earns ShuffleTrace.

ReheadTrace and RestepTrace already demonstrate the two independent ways a trace can progress: renewing the head or renewing the remainder. Their composition shows these progressions can be swapped without violating any earned admissibility. Thus ShuffleTrace records that the family of trace-progressing acts forms a free shuffle algebra—any ordering built from these steps is admissible, and admissibility is closed under permutation of independent steps.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Earning Something act: Something slug: something top: Reflex bottom: Witness summary: “Testifying to relation still leaves unanswered the question \u201Cwhat carries the testimony?\u201D\u2014we point reflex at that testimony to produce \u201Csomething.\u201D” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Earning Something

Interpretation

Testifying to relation still leaves unanswered the question “what carries the testimony?”—we point reflex at that testimony to produce “something.”

Since Witness is established, the composition (Reflex Witness) applies reflex to the testimony itself. This self-application records that an act exists which bears the testimony, so we designate the composite as Something.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}
