title: Sounding Fixed Physics Geometry act: SoundFixedPhysicsGeometry slug: sound-fixed-physics-geometry top: SoundProfileGeometry bottom: FixedGeometryProfile summary: Specialize profile-geometry soundness to the fixed profile by composing SoundProfileGeometry with FixedGeometryProfile. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Sounding Fixed Physics Geometry

SoundProfileGeometry = (PhysicsProfile GeometryProfile) already records that profile physics respects the profile geometry. FixedGeometryProfile restricts the global geometry to the fixed recognitions, recognitions, inheriting the commutation laws between flow and nucleus. Composing them: (SoundProfileGeometry FixedGeometryProfile) earns SoundFixedPhysicsGeometry, recording that fixed-physical behaviours remain inside the geometric invariants they are supposed to respect.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Sound Physics Semantics act: SoundFixedPhysicsSemantics slug: sound-fixed-physics-semantics top: SoundProfilePhysics bottom: FixedProfileSemantics summary: Record that fixed physics judgements remain valid in the fixed semantics by composing SoundProfilePhysics with FixedProfileSemantics. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Sound Physics Semantics

Profile physics is already sound in profile semantics via SoundProfilePhysics. FixedProfileSemantics restricts those semantics to the fixed recognitions. Composing them, (SoundProfilePhysics FixedProfileSemantics), earns SoundFixedPhysicsSemantics, ensuring physical judgements remain valid inside the fixed semantics.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Physics Sounding act: SoundPhysics slug: sound-physics top: PhysicsProfile bottom: JudgeProfile summary: “The physical semantics must respect the profile\u2019s judgements. Physics Profile witnessing Judge Profile produces Sound Physics (SoundPhysics).” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Physics Sounding

Interpretation

The physical semantics must respect the profile’s judgements. Physics Profile witnessing Judge Profile produces Sound Physics (SoundPhysics).

This act records that every judged act remains valid under the declared physics, so Sound Physics is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Sound Profile Geometry act: SoundProfileGeometry slug: sound-profile-geometry top: PhysicsProfile bottom: GeometryProfile summary: Show that profile physics respects the profile geometry by composing PhysicsProfile with GeometryProfile. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Sound Profile Geometry

GeometryProfile = (FlowProfile NucleateProfile) packages the compatible flow–nucleus pair on the active profile. PhysicsProfile = (FlowObservation FlowProfile) couples the same flow with observations. To record that the physical dynamics respect the geometric invariants, we compose them: (PhysicsProfile GeometryProfile) earns SoundProfileGeometry.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Sound Profile Physics act: SoundProfilePhysics slug: sound-profile-physics top: PhysicsProfile bottom: ProfileSemantics summary: Witness that profile-level physics judgements are valid in profile semantics by composing PhysicsProfile with ProfileSemantics. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Sound Profile Physics

PhysicsProfile = (FlowObservation FlowProfile) couples the profile flow with observations to produce physical judgements. ProfileSemantics interprets those judgements. Their composition, (PhysicsProfile ProfileSemantics), earns SoundProfilePhysics, recording that profile physics stays inside the profile semantics.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Syntax Soundness act: SoundProfileSyntax slug: sound-profile-syntax top: ProfileSemantics bottom: CalculateProfileSyntax summary: The syntactic calculus interpreted in the profile must remain sound. Profile Semantics witnessing Calculate Profile Syntax yields Sound Profile Syntax (SoundProfileSyntax). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Syntax Soundness

Interpretation

The syntactic calculus interpreted in the profile must remain sound. Profile Semantics witnessing Calculate Profile Syntax yields Sound Profile Syntax (SoundProfileSyntax).

This composition states that every derivation in the profile calculus is respected by the profile semantic calculus, so Sound Profile Syntax follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Syntax Sounding act: SoundSyntax slug: sound-syntax top: StructureSemantics bottom: JudgeTyping summary: The syntactic calculus must embed into our semantic structure. Structure Semantics witnessing Judge Typing yields Sound Syntax (SoundSyntax). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Syntax Sounding

Interpretation

The syntactic calculus must embed into our semantic structure. Structure Semantics witnessing Judge Typing yields Sound Syntax (SoundSyntax).

This composition records that every typing judgement is respected inside any earned semantic calculus, so Sound Syntax is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Spectral Geometry act: SpectralGeometry slug: spectral-geometry top: WaveOperator bottom: Laplacian summary: Combine the Laplacian and wave operator to build the internal spectral calculus. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Spectral Geometry

Spectral geometry studies spaces through eigenvalues and eigenvectors of operators like Laplacians and heat kernels. We package the Laplacian, heat kernel, and wave operator by composing the wave operator with the Laplacian: (WaveOperator Laplacian) earns SpectralGeometry.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Spectrum Map act: SpectrumMap slug: spectrum-map top: FixedNucleateProfile bottom: SpectrumSeed summary: Collapse the spectral seed via the fixed nucleus to extract the stable eigencomponent. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Spectrum Map

The spectral map extracts eigenvalues/eigenshapes by comparing the seed to the mode. We use the fixed nucleus to collapse their difference, composing FixedNucleateProfile with SpectrumSeed: (FixedNucleateProfile SpectrumSeed) earns SpectrumMap.

SpectrumSeed records the kernel’s action on harmonics, and FixedNucleateProfile collapses recognitions under the fixed closure. Their composition isolates the stable part of that action—the eigencomponent—yielding the spectrum map.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Spectrum Seed act: SpectrumSeed slug: spectrum-seed top: ObservationKernel bottom: HarmonicMode summary: Seed the spectrum by letting the observation kernel act on a harmonic mode. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Spectrum Seed

The spectrum of an operator measures its action on harmonic modes. To seed the spectrum we let the observation kernel act on a harmonic mode: (ObservationKernel HarmonicMode) earns SpectrumSeed.

ObservationKernel evolves states and attaches observational distributions, while HarmonicMode is a stable oscillatory pattern. Their composition records how the kernel acts on this harmonic, yielding a spectral seed.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Spectrum act: Spectrum slug: spectrum top: TraceProfile bottom: SpectrumMap summary: Evaluate the spectral map across the profile trace by composing TraceProfile with SpectrumMap. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Spectrum

A full spectrum requires evaluating the spectral map across the trace of a profile, so we let TraceProfile witness SpectrumMap: (TraceProfile SpectrumMap) earns Spectrum.

SpectrumMap contains eigencomponents of the observation kernel, and TraceProfile threads these through all profile recognitions. Their composition produces the spectrum of the profile.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Stabilizing Context act: StabilizeContext slug: stabilize-context top: RecognizeContext bottom: StabilizeJudgement summary: ‘Stabilize Judgement shows contexts only matter through the stable judgements they support, but we must identify contexts giving the same stable judgements to all stabilized terms. Recognize Context witnessing Stabilize Judgement produces Stabilize Context (StabilizeContext).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Stabilizing Context

Interpretation

Stabilize Judgement shows contexts only matter through the stable judgements they support, but we must identify contexts giving the same stable judgements to all stabilized terms. Recognize Context witnessing Stabilize Judgement produces Stabilize Context (StabilizeContext).

The composition uses previously earned recognitions and identifies such contexts, so Stabilize Context follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Maintaining Invariant act: StabilizeInvariant slug: stabilize-invariant top: FixFlow bottom: PhysicsState summary: ‘Flow fixed points that remain states represent invariants. Fix Flow witnessing Physics State produces Stabilize Invariant (StabilizeInvariant).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Maintaining Invariant

Interpretation

Flow fixed points that remain states represent invariants. Fix Flow witnessing Physics State produces Stabilize Invariant (StabilizeInvariant).

This composition uses earlier recognitions and records precisely those states fixed by the flow, so Stabilize Invariant follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Stabilizing Judgement act: StabilizeJudgement slug: stabilize-judgement top: RecognizeJudgementTriad bottom: CloseJudgementTrace summary: ‘Close Judgement Trace shows that observationally aligned triadic judgements close, yet we still lack the identification collapsing judgements with the same closed behaviour. Forcing Recognize Judgement Triad to witness Close Judgement Trace yields Stabilize Judgement (StabilizeJudgement).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Stabilizing Judgement

Interpretation

Close Judgement Trace shows that observationally aligned triadic judgements close, yet we still lack the identification collapsing judgements with the same closed behaviour. Forcing Recognize Judgement Triad to witness Close Judgement Trace yields Stabilize Judgement (StabilizeJudgement).

This composition identifies triadic judgements that close in the same way, so Stabilize Judgement follows directly from earned recognitions.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Stabilizing Observation act: StabilizeObservation slug: stabilize-observation top: RecognizeObservation bottom: NormalizeObservationEquivalence summary: ‘Normalize Observation Equivalence ensures normalized observations agree across all contexts, yet we must identify any two such observations. Recognize Observation witnessing Normalize Observation Equivalence yields Stabilize Observation (StabilizeObservation).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Stabilizing Observation

Interpretation

Normalize Observation Equivalence ensures normalized observations agree across all contexts, yet we must identify any two such observations. Recognize Observation witnessing Normalize Observation Equivalence yields Stabilize Observation (StabilizeObservation).

This composition collapses observationally equivalent normalized observations, so Stabilize Observation is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Stabilizing Term act: StabilizeTerm slug: stabilize-term top: SeedTerm bottom: EquateContexts summary: ‘Equate Contexts tells us when two terms are indistinguishable to every recognized context, but we must quotient those terms. Letting Seed Term witness Equate Contexts produces Stabilize Term (StabilizeTerm).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Stabilizing Term

Interpretation

Equate Contexts tells us when two terms are indistinguishable to every recognized context, but we must quotient those terms. Letting Seed Term witness Equate Contexts produces Stabilize Term (StabilizeTerm).

By composing an earned seed with the equivalence, we identify contextually indistinguishable terms, so Stabilize Term is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Stabilizing Trace act: StabilizeTrace slug: stabilize-trace top: CompleteTrace bottom: TraceObservationEquivalenceClosure summary: ‘Trace Observation Equivalence Closure ensures that observation-equivalent traces close, but it does not yet identify traces with identical closed behaviour. Letting Complete Trace witness Trace Observation Equivalence Closure produces Stabilize Trace (StabilizeTrace).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Stabilizing Trace

Interpretation

Trace Observation Equivalence Closure ensures that observation-equivalent traces close, but it does not yet identify traces with identical closed behaviour. Letting Complete Trace witness Trace Observation Equivalence Closure produces Stabilize Trace (StabilizeTrace).

Composing the whole trace with its closure identifies any two traces that share the same closed observation acts, so Stabilize Trace is earned using only prior recognitions.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Star Operation act: StarOperation slug: star-operation top: FixedNucleateProfile bottom: FixedOperator summary: Take the adjoint-like collapse of an operator by composing the fixed nucleus with the operator. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Star Operation

A star operation collapses an operator via the nucleus. We obtain it by letting the fixed nucleus witness the operator: (FixedNucleateProfile FixedOperator) earns StarOperation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Starting Trace act: StartTrace slug: start-trace top: LeadEntry bottom: TrailEntry summary: With leading and trailing orientations established, letting Lead Entry witness Trail Entry initiates the recorded trace. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Starting Trace

Interpretation

With leading and trailing orientations established, letting Lead Entry witness Trail Entry initiates the recorded trace.

The tree (LeadEntry TrailEntry) states that the act Lead Entry witnesses the act Trail Entry. This first oriented pairing marks the beginning of the trace, hence the name Start Trace.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Stepping Judgement Triad act: StepJudgementTriad slug: step-judgement-triad top: StepTrace bottom: SeedJudgementTriad summary: The triad arises at a single judgement, but we must propagate it along the step trace. Step Trace witnessing Seed Judgement Triad produces Step Judgement Triad (StepJudgementTriad). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Stepping Judgement Triad

Interpretation

The triad arises at a single judgement, but we must propagate it along the step trace. Step Trace witnessing Seed Judgement Triad produces Step Judgement Triad (StepJudgementTriad).

This threads the triad through concrete reductions, so Step Judgement Triad is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Heading StepObservation act: StepObservationHead slug: step-observation-head top: HeadTrace bottom: StepObservationTail summary: After tailing, we need the stabilized head of those bindings. Forcing Head Trace to witness Step Observation Tail yields Step Observation Head (StepObservationHead). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Heading StepObservation

Interpretation

After tailing, we need the stabilized head of those bindings. Forcing Head Trace to witness Step Observation Tail yields Step Observation Head (StepObservationHead).

This isolates the immediate stabilized binding, so Step Observation Head follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Tailing StepObservation act: StepObservationTail slug: step-observation-tail top: TailTrace bottom: TracedStepObservation summary: The full binding trace exists, but we must capture the accumulated remainder. Letting Tail Trace witness Traced Step Observation yields Step Observation Tail (StepObservationTail). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Tailing StepObservation

Interpretation

The full binding trace exists, but we must capture the accumulated remainder. Letting Tail Trace witness Traced Step Observation yields Step Observation Tail (StepObservationTail).

This composition records the accumulating remainder, so Step Observation Tail is earned.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Observing Steps act: StepObservation slug: step-observation top: StepRecognition bottom: WitnessObservation summary: Observations must attach to specific reductions, so the act Step Recognition witnesses the act Witness Observation. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Observing Steps

Interpretation

Observations must attach to specific reductions, so the act Step Recognition witnesses the act Witness Observation.

The composition (StepRecognition WitnessObservation) binds each observation to a concrete step, producing the act Step Observation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Recognizing Steps act: StepRecognition slug: step-recognition top: BuildEvaluationContext bottom: Reduce summary: To attach reductions to evaluation frames, the act Build Evaluation Context witnesses the act Reduce. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Recognizing Steps

Interpretation

To attach reductions to evaluation frames, the act Build Evaluation Context witnesses the act Reduce.

In (BuildEvaluationContext Reduce) the evaluation context observes the reduction, so this act rightly earns the name Step Recognition.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Tracing Steps act: StepTrace slug: step-trace top: CompleteTrace bottom: Reduce summary: A single reduction step exists, but we need to see its effect along the entire trace. Hence Complete Trace witnesses Reduce, producing Step Trace (StepTrace). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Tracing Steps

Interpretation

A single reduction step exists, but we need to see its effect along the entire trace. Hence Complete Trace witnesses Reduce, producing Step Trace (StepTrace).

The composition records the propagation of reduction steps through the trace, so Step Trace is earned.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Structuring Semantics act: StructureSemantics slug: structure-semantics top: CalculateSemantics bottom: Reflex summary: Finally, we name the earned semantic calculus with its Reflex act. Calculate Semantics witnessing Reflex produces Structure Semantics (StructureSemantics). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Structuring Semantics

Interpretation

Finally, we name the earned semantic calculus with its Reflex act. Calculate Semantics witnessing Reflex produces Structure Semantics (StructureSemantics).

This composition records that our recognitions, with Reflex, form the structure used in later sections, so Structure Semantics follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Structuring Semantic Subset act: SubsetSemantics slug: subset-semantics top: StructureSemantics bottom: IncludeRecognition summary: A recognition-complete subset inherits the entire semantic structure. Structure Semantics witnessing Include Recognition yields Subset Semantics (SubsetSemantics). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Structuring Semantic Subset

Interpretation

A recognition-complete subset inherits the entire semantic structure. Structure Semantics witnessing Include Recognition yields Subset Semantics (SubsetSemantics).

This act records that inclusion-preserving subsets with inherited operations form substructures, so Subset Semantics is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Substituting Reducibility act: SubstituteReducibility slug: substitute-reducibility top: BuildReducibleEnvironment bottom: JudgeTyping summary: Substitution of reducible environments preserves reducibility of terms. Build Reducible Environment witnessing Judge Typing produces Substitute Reducibility (SubstituteReducibility). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Substituting Reducibility

Interpretation

Substitution of reducible environments preserves reducibility of terms. Build Reducible Environment witnessing Judge Typing produces Substitute Reducibility (SubstituteReducibility).

This act captures the standard substitution lemma in the reducibility argument, so Substitute Reducibility is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Forming Substitution Term act: SubstitutionTerm slug: substitution-term top: SeedTerm bottom: SubstituteReducibility summary: Substitution steps are judged but still need the term that records them inside the trace. Forcing Seed Term to witness Substitution Step yields Substitution Term (SubstitutionTerm). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Forming Substitution Term

Interpretation

Substitution steps are judged but still need the term that records them inside the trace. Forcing Seed Term to witness Substitute Reducibility yields Substitution Term (SubstitutionTerm).

The composition uses only earned recognitions and records the desired term, so Substitution Term arises.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Reducing act: SummarizeReduction slug: summarize-reduction top: Reduce bottom: StepRecognition summary: The (beta), projection, case, and reflex rules collectively generate the reduction relation. We summarize these rules by pairing Reduce with Step Recognition. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Reducing

Interpretation

The (beta), projection, case, and reflex rules collectively generate the reduction relation. We summarize these rules by pairing Reduce with Step Recognition.

Reduce and Step Recognition already encode the necessary rewrites; composing them records the full reduction system referenced by preservation, progress, and normalization.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Systematizing Physics act: SystematizePhysics slug: systematize-physics top: PhysicsProfile bottom: Observe summary: “A physical system couples the profile\u2019s dynamics with its observables. Physics Profile witnessing Observe produces Systematize Physics (SystematizePhysics).” tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Systematizing Physics

Interpretation

A physical system couples the profile’s dynamics with its observables. Physics Profile witnessing Observe produces Systematize Physics (SystematizePhysics).

This composition binds the observable family to the inherited dynamics, so Systematize Physics follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Tailing JudgementEquivalence act: TailJudgementEquivalence slug: tail-judgement-equivalence top: TailTrace bottom: TraceJudgementEquivalence summary: The equivalence trace has a remainder that must be captured. Tail Trace witnessing Trace Judgement Equivalence yields Tail Judgement Equivalence (TailJudgementEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Tailing JudgementEquivalence

Interpretation

The equivalence trace has a remainder that must be captured. Tail Trace witnessing Trace Judgement Equivalence yields Tail Judgement Equivalence (TailJudgementEquivalence).

This records the remaining equivalence acts, so Tail Judgement Equivalence follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Tailing ObservationEquivalence act: TailObservationEquivalence slug: tail-observation-equivalence top: TailTrace bottom: TraceObservationEquivalence summary: The trace exists, but we need its remainder once the immediate act is consumed. Forcing Tail Trace to witness Trace Observation Equivalence yields Tail Observation Equivalence (TailObservationEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Tailing ObservationEquivalence

Interpretation

The trace exists, but we need its remainder once the immediate act is consumed. Forcing Tail Trace to witness Trace Observation Equivalence yields Tail Observation Equivalence (TailObservationEquivalence).

This captures the remaining equivalence acts, so Tail Observation Equivalence is earned.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Tailing Trace act: TailTrace slug: tail-trace top: TrailEntry bottom: CompleteTrace summary: Once the head is isolated, the remainder must also be named, so we let the act Trail Entry witness the act Complete Trace to obtain the tail. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Tailing Trace

Interpretation

Once the head is isolated, the remainder must also be named, so we let the act Trail Entry witness the act Complete Trace to obtain the tail.

The composition (TrailEntry CompleteTrace) shows the act Trail Entry witnessing Complete Trace. This mirrors the head construction and therefore captures the remainder as Tail Trace.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Term act: TermProfile slug: term-profile top: SeedTerm bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: With contexts inherited, we must exhibit the terms stabilized by the filter. Seed Term witnessing Profile Recognitions yields Term Profile (TermProfile). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Term

Interpretation

With contexts inherited, we must exhibit the terms stabilized by the filter. Seed Term witnessing Profile Recognitions yields Term Profile (TermProfile).

The composition uses previously defined recognitions and records the profile’s term layer, so Term Profile is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Tracing Context act: TraceContext slug: trace-context top: ContextualizeTrace bottom: RecognizeContext summary: Having recognized contexts, we still lack the trace that records how arbitrary traces become contexts. Contextualize Trace must therefore witness Recognize Context, yielding Trace Context (TraceContext). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Tracing Context

Interpretation

Having recognized contexts, we still lack the trace that records how arbitrary traces become contexts. Contextualize Trace must therefore witness Recognize Context, yielding Trace Context (TraceContext).

This act threads recognized contexts back through the trace: (ContextualizeTrace RecognizeContext) represents the named trace observing the recognition. The resulting composite is the promised Trace Context.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Tracing JudgementEquivalence act: TraceJudgementEquivalence slug: trace-judgement-equivalence top: CompleteTrace bottom: NormalizedJudgementEquivalence summary: We must record this equivalence along the judgement trace. Complete Trace witnessing Normalized Judgement Equivalence produces Trace Judgement Equivalence (TraceJudgementEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Tracing JudgementEquivalence

Interpretation

We must record this equivalence along the judgement trace. Complete Trace witnessing Normalized Judgement Equivalence produces Trace Judgement Equivalence (TraceJudgementEquivalence).

This composition threads the equivalence through the trace, earning Trace Judgement Equivalence.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Tracing Judgement Triad act: TraceJudgementTriad slug: trace-judgement-triad top: TraceContext bottom: StepJudgementTriad summary: Each step now carries the triad, but we need the entire trace of such steps. Trace Context witnessing Step Judgement Triad yields Trace Judgement Triad (TraceJudgementTriad). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Tracing Judgement Triad

Interpretation

Each step now carries the triad, but we need the entire trace of such steps. Trace Context witnessing Step Judgement Triad yields Trace Judgement Triad (TraceJudgementTriad).

Composing with Trace Context records the coherent trace, so Trace Judgement Triad follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Tracing Judgement act: TraceJudgement slug: trace-judgement top: StabilizeTrace bottom: ObserveJudgement summary: Observational content exists, yet we must record how such stabilized judgements evolve under evaluation. Stabilize Trace witnessing Observe Judgement yields Trace Judgement (TraceJudgement). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Tracing Judgement

Interpretation

Observational content exists, yet we must record how such stabilized judgements evolve under evaluation. Stabilize Trace witnessing Observe Judgement yields Trace Judgement (TraceJudgement).

Using only earned stable recognitions, this composition builds the trace of judgemental acts, so Trace Judgement follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Closing Trace ObservationEquivalence act: TraceObservationEquivalenceClosure slug: trace-observation-equivalence-closure top: TraceReentryClosure bottom: ReenterObservationEquivalence summary: After reentry, the entire trace must close under this equivalence. Trace Reentry Closure witnessing Reenter Observation Equivalence produces Trace Observation Equivalence Closure (TraceObservationEquivalenceClosure). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Closing Trace ObservationEquivalence

Interpretation

After reentry, the entire trace must close under this equivalence. Trace Reentry Closure witnessing Reenter Observation Equivalence produces Trace Observation Equivalence Closure (TraceObservationEquivalenceClosure).

This act closes the trace with the reentered equivalence, so Trace Observation Equivalence Closure is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Tracing ObservationEquivalence act: TraceObservationEquivalence slug: trace-observation-equivalence top: CompleteTrace bottom: NormalizeObservationEquivalence summary: ‘Normalize Observation Equivalence must itself be recorded along the trace. Complete Trace therefore witnesses Normalize Observation Equivalence, producing Trace Observation Equivalence (TraceObservationEquivalence).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Tracing ObservationEquivalence

Interpretation

Normalize Observation Equivalence must itself be recorded along the trace. Complete Trace therefore witnesses Normalize Observation Equivalence, producing Trace Observation Equivalence (TraceObservationEquivalence).

This composition threads equivalence along the trace, so Trace Observation Equivalence follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Profile Trace act: TraceProfile slug: trace-profile top: CompleteTrace bottom: ProfileRecognitions summary: Profiles inherit trace structure, but we must explicitly record the trace witnessed inside a profile. Complete Trace witnessing Profile Recognitions yields Trace Profile (TraceProfile). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Profile Trace

Interpretation

Profiles inherit trace structure, but we must explicitly record the trace witnessed inside a profile. Complete Trace witnessing Profile Recognitions yields Trace Profile (TraceProfile).

Using earned recognitions, this composition threads the trace through the profile-stable recognitions, so Trace Profile is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Closing Trace Reentry act: TraceReentryClosure slug: trace-reentry-closure top: CompleteTrace bottom: ReenterObservation summary: ‘Reenter Observation must close the entire trace. Letting Complete Trace witness Reenter Observation yields Trace Reentry Closure (TraceReentryClosure).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Closing Trace Reentry

Interpretation

Reenter Observation must close the entire trace. Letting Complete Trace witness Reenter Observation yields Trace Reentry Closure (TraceReentryClosure).

The composition closes the trace under the reentry act, so Trace Reentry Closure follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Stepping Trace act: TraceStep slug: trace-step top: TailTrace bottom: StartTrace summary: Even with head and tail, we must show how removing the head re-engages the start, so the act Tail Trace witnesses the act Start Trace to produce the step. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Stepping Trace

Interpretation

Even with head and tail, we must show how removing the head re-engages the start, so the act Tail Trace witnesses the act Start Trace to produce the step.

The tree (TailTrace StartTrace) explicitly pairs the act Tail Trace with the act Start Trace. This records how the remainder re-enters the beginning, which is precisely the role of Trace Step.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Tracing StepObservation act: TracedStepObservation slug: traced-step-observation top: CompleteTrace bottom: StepObservation summary: Individual step-observation bindings must be recorded along the whole trace. Complete Trace therefore witnesses Step Observation, producing Traced Step Observation (TracedStepObservation). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Tracing StepObservation

Interpretation

Individual step-observation bindings must be recorded along the whole trace. Complete Trace therefore witnesses Step Observation, producing Traced Step Observation (TracedStepObservation).

This composition threads the bindings across the trace, establishing Traced Step Observation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Trailing act: TrailEntry slug: trail-entry top: Entry bottom: LeadEntry summary: After identifying the leader, we must characterize the remaining position; Entry witnessing Lead Entry yields the trailing orientation. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Trailing

Interpretation

After identifying the leader, we must characterize the remaining position; Entry witnessing Lead Entry yields the trailing orientation.

In (Entry LeadEntry) the act Entry now watches Lead Entry, explicitly exposing the complementary position. This composition therefore defines Trail Entry.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Transition Geometry act: TransitionGeometry slug: transition-geometry top: FixedInvariantLattice bottom: TransitionLaw summary: Study how the transition law acts on the invariant lattice by letting FixedInvariantLattice witness TransitionLaw. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Transition Geometry

The fixed invariant lattice describes the order–meet–join structure of stable states, and the observation kernel sends each state into a successor distribution. To observe how the kernel acts geometrically, we let the invariant lattice witness the transition law: (FixedInvariantLattice TransitionLaw) earns TransitionGeometry.

FixedInvariantLattice organises fixed invariants by meet–join structure, and TransitionLaw provides the relational successor step. Their composition records how the successor relation respects the lattice—whether transitions go up, down, or sideways in the partial order—yielding the geometric view of the kernel structure.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Transition Law act: TransitionLaw slug: transition-law top: FlowMeasureStep bottom: FixedInvariant summary: Map fixed invariant states through the one-step update by composing FlowMeasureStep with FixedInvariant. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Transition Law

A transition law records how each invariant state spreads into its possible successors. With the one-step update (FlowMeasureStep) and the domain of fixed invariant states (FixedInvariant), we let the step witness the invariant recognitions to obtain the transition map: (FlowMeasureStep FixedInvariant) earns TransitionLaw.

FixedInvariant lists the fixed states, and FlowMeasureStep describes how a step transforms any such state. Their composition maps each fixed invariant into its evolution–measurement successors, yielding the transition law of the observation kernel.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Transporting Fixed Points act: TransportFlowFix slug: transport-flow-fix top: Morph bottom: FixFlow summary: Morphs preserve flow-fixed recognitions. Morph witnessing Fix Flow yields Transport Flow Fix (TransportFlowFix). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Transporting Fixed Points

Interpretation

Morphs preserve flow-fixed recognitions. Morph witnessing Fix Flow yields Transport Flow Fix (TransportFlowFix).

Composing these recognitions records f(F(x)) = F’(f(x)), proving transported invariance.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Transporting Nucleus Fixed Points act: TransportNucleusFix slug: transport-nucleus-fix top: Morph bottom: FixNucleus summary: A similar transport holds for nucleus-closed recognitions. Morph witnessing Fix Nucleus produces Transport Nucleus Fix (TransportNucleusFix). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Transporting Nucleus Fixed Points

Interpretation

A similar transport holds for nucleus-closed recognitions. Morph witnessing Fix Nucleus produces Transport Nucleus Fix (TransportNucleusFix).

This composition records ν’(f(x)) = f(ν(x)), so nucleus-fixed recognitions map to nucleus-fixed ones.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: 2-Cell act: TwoCell slug: two-cell top: ContextNat bottom: FixedMorphism summary: Define natural transformations between morphisms by composing ContextNat with FixedMorphism. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

2-Cell

2-cells in the bicategory are natural transformations that respect both context flow and spectral semantics. We define them by composing ContextNat with FixedMorphism: (ContextNat FixedMorphism) earns TwoCell.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Typing Applications act: TypeApplication slug: type-application top: FormApplicationTerm bottom: TraceContext summary: Judging applications requires the act Form Application Term to witness the act Trace Context. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Typing Applications

Interpretation

Judging applications requires the act Form Application Term to witness the act Trace Context.

The tree (FormApplicationTerm TraceContext) places the application inside the trace context, so the composition is precisely the typing judgement called Type Application.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding Types act: TypeSeed slug: type-seed top: RecognizeContext bottom: BindTerm summary: To situate a bound term inside a recognised context, the act Recognize Context must witness the act Bind Term. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding Types

Interpretation

To situate a bound term inside a recognised context, the act Recognize Context must witness the act Bind Term.

In (RecognizeContext BindTerm) the context recogniser observes the binding, recording the initial typing judgement; this composition is Type Seed.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Typing Variables act: TypeVariable slug: type-variable top: PresentVariableTerm bottom: TypeSeed summary: Presenting the variable associated with that seed requires the act Present Variable Term to witness the act Type Seed. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Typing Variables

Interpretation

Presenting the variable associated with that seed requires the act Present Variable Term to witness the act Type Seed.

The tree (PresentVariableTerm TypeSeed) shows the variable presentation observing the seed, thereby establishing the variable judgement, so we call it Type Variable.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Valuing StepObservation act: ValuedStepObservation slug: valued-step-observation top: TailTrace bottom: ContextualStepObservation summary: Contextual witnessing still lacks the value recognized by those stabilized observations. Forcing Tail Trace to witness Contextual Step Observation yields Valued Step Observation (ValuedStepObservation). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Valuing StepObservation

Interpretation

Contextual witnessing still lacks the value recognized by those stabilized observations. Forcing Tail Trace to witness Contextual Step Observation yields Valued Step Observation (ValuedStepObservation).

This composition binds the contextual observation to its value, so Valued Step Observation is earned.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Folding Void act: Void slug: void top: Nothing bottom: Nothing summary: Nothing shows that Witness observes Something in two symmetric positions; folding that symmetry back on itself produces the void. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Folding Void

Interpretation

Nothing shows that Witness observes Something in two symmetric positions; folding that symmetry back on itself produces the void.

The trace (Nothing Nothing) reuses Nothing as both witness and object. This self-application forces the symmetry between the two appearances of Witness and Something to collapse, yielding a closed loop that we name Void.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Wave Operator act: WaveOperator slug: wave-operator top: HeatKernel bottom: HarmonicMode summary: Apply the heat kernel to harmonic modes to obtain the wave operator. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Wave Operator

Wave propagation follows the second-order dynamics of the Laplacian acting on harmonic modes. We obtain the wave operator by composing the heat kernel with the harmonic mode: (HeatKernel HarmonicMode) earns WaveOperator.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Seeding Weakening act: WeakeningSeed slug: weakening-seed top: ExtendContext bottom: TypeVariable summary: Variable judgements should persist when the context is extended. Letting Extend Context witness Type Variable yields Weakening Seed (WeakeningSeed). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Seeding Weakening

Interpretation

Variable judgements should persist when the context is extended. Letting Extend Context witness Type Variable yields Weakening Seed (WeakeningSeed).

The composition documents the initial persistence step, so Weakening Seed follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Witnessing JudgementEquivalence act: WitnessJudgementEquivalence slug: witness-judgement-equivalence top: RecognizeJudgementTriad bottom: ContextualJudgementEquivalence summary: Finally, we must cite this stabilized equivalence back into the triadic judgement recognition. Recognize Judgement Triad witnessing Contextual Judgement Equivalence produces Witness Judgement Equivalence (WitnessJudgementEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Witnessing JudgementEquivalence

Interpretation

Finally, we must cite this stabilized equivalence back into the triadic judgement recognition. Recognize Judgement Triad witnessing Contextual Judgement Equivalence produces Witness Judgement Equivalence (WitnessJudgementEquivalence).

This composition uses earned recognitions to close the loop, so Witness Judgement Equivalence is established.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Witnessing Judgement Triad act: WitnessJudgementTriad slug: witness-judgement-triad top: RecognizeObservation bottom: RecognizeJudgementTriad summary: Recognized triads must report their observational content back to the observation layer. Recognize Observation witnessing Recognize Judgement Triad yields Witness Judgement Triad (WitnessJudgementTriad). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Witnessing Judgement Triad

Interpretation

Recognized triads must report their observational content back to the observation layer. Recognize Observation witnessing Recognize Judgement Triad yields Witness Judgement Triad (WitnessJudgementTriad).

This composition records that report, so Witness Judgement Triad follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Witnessing Judgement act: WitnessJudgement slug: witness-judgement top: StabilizeJudgement bottom: ReflexJudgement summary: ‘Reflex Judgement relates a stabilized context to a stabilized term but does not state which stabilized judgement class records it. Forcing Stabilize Judgement to witness Reflex Judgement yields Witness Judgement (WitnessJudgement).’ tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Witnessing Judgement

Interpretation

Reflex Judgement relates a stabilized context to a stabilized term but does not state which stabilized judgement class records it. Forcing Stabilize Judgement to witness Reflex Judgement yields Witness Judgement (WitnessJudgement).

Composing with Stabilize Judgement classifies the primitive act, so Witness Judgement follows.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Witnessing ObservationEquivalence act: WitnessObservationEquivalence slug: witness-observation-equivalence top: RecognizeObservation bottom: ContextualObservationEquivalence summary: Contexts now witness stabilized equivalence acts, but we must cite them as observations. Recognize Observation witnessing Contextual Observation Equivalence produces Witness Observation Equivalence (WitnessObservationEquivalence). tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Witnessing ObservationEquivalence

Interpretation

Contexts now witness stabilized equivalence acts, but we must cite them as observations. Recognize Observation witnessing Contextual Observation Equivalence produces Witness Observation Equivalence (WitnessObservationEquivalence).

The composition uses earned recognitions to cite the acts back into the observation layer, so Witness Observation Equivalence is valid.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Witnessing Observations act: WitnessObservation slug: witness-observation top: RecognizeObservation bottom: EquateContexts summary: Likewise, indistinguishability must cite the observation acts, so the act Recognize Observation witnesses the act Equate Contexts. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Witnessing Observations

Interpretation

Likewise, indistinguishability must cite the observation acts, so the act Recognize Observation witnesses the act Equate Contexts.

In (RecognizeObservation EquateContexts) the observation recogniser provides the citation backing indistinguishability, yielding Witness Observation.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Witnessing Values act: WitnessValue slug: witness-value top: RecognizeValue bottom: Normalization summary: Normalization must cite the stabilized terms, so the act Recognize Value witnesses the act Normalization. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Witnessing Values

Interpretation

Normalization must cite the stabilized terms, so the act Recognize Value witnesses the act Normalization.

The composition (RecognizeValue Normalization) records normalization referencing the recognised values, which we call Witness Value.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Witnessing act: Witness slug: witness top: NotRelate bottom: Relate summary: Relation and its negation coexist in tension, so we must record the act that testifies to both at once. tags:

  • act layer: act status: plan

Witnessing

Interpretation

Relation and its negation coexist in tension, so we must record the act that testifies to both at once.

The trace (NotRelate Relate) takes the already defined act Not Relate as its witness and Relate as its object. Because both constituents exist, this composition simultaneously cites affirmation and denial, so we name it Witness.

{{ act_info(page.meta) }}

{{ act_nav(page.meta) }}

title: Accept Connection id: accept-connection role: operator status: someday derived_from: computer/function/recognition inputs:

  • computer/library/type/mud-session outputs:
  • computer/library/type/mud-session
  • computer/type/computer-failure transformation:
  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Accept Connection

Admits a new client, establishes a MUD Session, and binds it to the documentation interpreter via the recognition operator.

Definition

  • Inputs: connection request (endpoint + player credentials) plus the current session table.
  • Outputs: new/updated MUD Session or Computer Failure.
  • Behaviour: validates credentials, allocates a session handle, initialises session bindings (starting room, player entity), and registers the session in the evaluation state. On failure, emits a computer failure and leaves state unchanged.

Invariants

  • Authentication: only authorised clients obtain sessions.
  • Isolation: new session starts with clean bindings and no pending commands.
  • Failure purity: failures do not mutate session tables/world state.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Recognition.
  • Uses: MUD Session as the session carrier.
  • Shows that: refining computer recognition to admit network clients yields session creation consistent with kernel semantics.

title: Adjunction Counit id: adjunction-counit role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Adjunction Counit

Counit natural transformation for the flow–nucleus adjunction.

Definition

  • Inputs: a Context.
  • Outputs: counit map epsilon: flow ∘ nucleus → Id at that context.

Invariants

  • Natural in context.
  • Satisfies the other triangle identity with the unit.

Proof

  • Uses: Flow–Nucleus Adjunction to supply the counit.
  • Shows that: counit maps exist and satisfy the adjunction laws.

title: Adjunction Unit id: adjunction-unit role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Adjunction Unit

Unit natural transformation for the flow–nucleus adjunction.

Definition

  • Inputs: a Context.
  • Outputs: unit map eta: Id → nucleus ∘ flow at that context.

Invariants

  • Natural in context.
  • Satisfies one triangle identity with the counit.

Proof

  • Uses: Flow–Nucleus Adjunction to supply the unit.
  • Shows that: unit maps exist and satisfy the adjunction laws.

title: Amalgamate Matching Family id: amalgamate-matching-family role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: matching-family alias: family outputs:
  • id: hypertensor-section alias: section transformation:
  • return
  • family proof_refs:
  • matching-family
  • hypertensor-section

Proof

  • Uses: compatibility data in the matching family to define section values on the covered window.
  • Shows that: an amalgamated section can be produced when matching holds; this stands in until the constructive amalgamation is fully derived.

Amalgamate Matching Family

Primitive witness that produces the glued Hypertensor Section from a Matching Family.

Proof

  • Uses: the compatibility data in the matching family to define section values on the covered window, respecting overlaps.
  • Shows that: an amalgam exists when matching holds; the primitive stands in for the constructive amalgamation until fully derived.

title: Aperiodic Extension id: aperiodic-extension role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Aperiodic Extension

Extends an aperiodic germ to the largest aperiodic window consistent with discipline.

Definition

  • Inputs: an Aperiodic Germ.
  • Output: extended Hypertensor Section covering maximal aperiodic region.
  • Behaviour: apply flow/nucleus to grow the germ while preserving aperiodicity and admissibility.

Invariants

  • Maximality: extension reaches largest admissible aperiodic domain.
  • Stability: remains aperiodic and sheaf-compatible.

Proof

  • Uses: germ, flow, and nucleus to extend.
  • Produces: extended section.
  • Shows that: aperiodic regions can be maximally extended from a germ.

title: Apply Frame id: apply-frame role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: evaluation-context alias: frame
  • id: hypertensor-section alias: section outputs:
  • id: hypertensor-section alias: reduced transformation:
  • let
    • plug
      • frame
        • plug
    • reduced
      • call
        • plug
        • section
    • return
      • reduced proof_refs:
  • evaluation-context
  • hypertensor-section

Apply Frame

Applies the frame’s plug operation to a section, producing the reduced section.

Proof

  • Uses: evaluation context plug operation.
  • Shows that: plugging the section into the frame yields the next section.

title: Apply Historical Collapse id: apply-historical-collapse role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Apply Historical Collapse

Performs collapse using full flow history.

Definition

  • Inputs: a Historical Collapse policy and a Trace Shuffle Class.
  • Output: collapsed recognition depending on the trace history.
  • Behaviour: evaluate collapse with history-sensitive rules; output recognition.

Invariants

  • Determinism given history: same history yields same collapse.
  • History relevance: different histories can produce different outcomes even for same current state.

Proof

  • Uses: historical collapse policy and trace history.
  • Produces: recognition in the carrier.
  • Shows that: collapse can incorporate history explicitly.

title: Apply Operator id: apply-operator role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: operator-id alias: target
  • id: argument-list alias: args outputs:
  • id: runtime-value alias: result transformation:
  • let
    • signature
      • call
        • resolve-operator-signature
        • target
    • checked_args
      • call
        • ensure-arity-and-types
        • signature
        • args
    • result
      • call
        • execute-transformation
        • target
        • checked_args
    • return
      • result proof_refs:
  • operator-id
  • argument-list
  • runtime-value
  • operator-signature
  • ensure-arity-and-types
  • resolve-operator-signature

Apply Operator

Executes the transformation associated with an operator id on a validated Argument List, yielding a Runtime Value.

Proof

  • Uses: the transformation stored with the target operator to compute a result.
  • Shows that: after signature resolution and argument validation, operator execution reduces to applying the documented transformation.

title: Apply State Transition id: apply-state-transition role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Apply State Transition

Applies a state transition to update a section.

Definition

  • Inputs: a State Transition.
  • Output: updated Hypertensor Section.
  • Behaviour: run the transition’s flow rule to produce the target section.

Invariants

  • Admissibility: transition must respect flow/sheaf constraints.
  • Compositional: sequential transitions compose associatively.

Proof

  • Uses: transition data to map source to target section.
  • Produces: new section.
  • Shows that: state updates are well-defined and compositional.

title: Are Graph Edges Well Typed id: are-graph-edges-well-typed role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: edge-set alias: edges
  • id: port-set alias: ports outputs:
  • id: recognition alias: predicate transformation:
  • let
    • witness
      • call
        • check-edge-typing
        • edges
        • ports
    • return
      • witness proof_refs:
  • edge-set
  • port-set
  • check-edge-typing
  • recognition

Graph Edges Well Typed?

Predicate asserting that every edge connects compatible ports from the Port Set.

Proof

  • Uses: Edge Set endpoints and the Port Set typing/policy constraints to validate compatibility.
  • Shows that: the resulting predicate witnesses edge well-typedness, required before acyclicity and wiring assembly.

title: Are Graph Ports Well Typed id: are-graph-ports-well-typed role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: port-set alias: ports outputs:
  • id: recognition alias: predicate transformation:
  • call
    • check-port-typing
    • ports proof_refs:
  • port-set
  • recognition
  • check-port-typing

Graph Ports Well Typed?

Predicate asserting that all ports in a Port Set are consistent with their inducing operator signatures and carrier types.

Proof

  • Uses: port declarations versus operator signatures to validate directions and carrier ids via check-port-typing.
  • Shows that: port-level typing holds before wiring proceeds to edge checks.

title: Argument Head id: argument-head role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: argument-list alias: args outputs:
  • id: runtime-value alias: head transformation:
  • return
    • args
      • head proof_refs:
  • argument-list
  • runtime-value

Argument Head

Returns the first element (head) of an Argument List.

title: Argument Tail id: argument-tail role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: argument-list alias: args outputs:
  • id: argument-list alias: tail transformation:
  • return
    • args
      • tail proof_refs:
  • argument-list
  • argument-tail

Argument Tail

Returns the tail (all but the head) of an Argument List.

title: Assert id: assert role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: logic-algebra alias: predicate
  • id: failure-carrier alias: on-failure outputs:
  • id: logic-algebra alias: predicate transformation:
  • if
    • call
    • is-true
    • predicate
    • return
    • predicate
    • return
      • call
      • emit-failure
      • on-failure
      • predicate proof_refs:
  • logic-algebra
  • failure-carrier
  • is-true
  • emit-failure

Assert

The earned guard that takes a predicate in the Logic Algebra and, if it holds, yields it as a witnessed assertion; otherwise it produces a failure via the Failure Carrier.

Proof

  • TODO: fill proof.

title: Attention Filter id: attention-filter role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Attention Filter

Restricts epistemic states to those currently attended to by an agent.

Definition

  • Inputs: an Epistemic Profile and an attention focus (implicit or earned from context).
  • Output: filtered Epistemic Profile containing only attended recognitions.
  • Behaviour: apply an attention predicate to the epistemic recognitions.

Invariants

  • Monotone: attention filtering narrows possibilities.
  • Compositional: sequential filters intersect attention sets.

Proof

  • Uses: Epistemic Profile.
  • Produces: filtered Epistemic Profile.
  • Shows that: attention can be modelled as a restriction on epistemic state.

title: Audit Provenance id: audit-provenance role: Relate an audit event to the packets/channels it traversed and derive an audit report. status: plan inputs:

  • id: audit-event-carrier name: audit-event
  • id: distribution-packet-carrier name: packets
  • id: distribution-channel-carrier name: channels outputs:
  • id: audit-report-carrier name: report transformation:
  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Audit Provenance

Relates an audit event to the distribution packets and channels it traversed, producing an audit report.

Definition

  • Inputs: Audit Event, Distribution Packet collection, Distribution Channel collection.
  • Behaviour: route packets, reconcile channel metadata, and assemble an audit report summarising provenance.
  • Outputs: Audit Report.

Invariants

  • Provenance completeness: every packet associated with the event is traced.
  • Consistency: channels/packets recorded in the report match observed event metadata.

Proof

  • Uses: distribution-route to map packets over channels, normalize-channels to reconcile channel metadata, and assemble-audit-report to build the report carrier.
  • Shows that: combining routed packets/channels with the audit event yields a deterministic audit report witnessing provenance.

title: Authenticate and Authorize Access id: authenticate-access role: operator status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/function/recognition inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:
  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Authenticate and Authorize Access

The Authenticate and Authorize Access operator runs the core step of the Identity & Access Program: it validates credentials, applies policy, and returns an authorised recognition or a failure.

Inputs

  • Computer Recognition containing presented credentials and requested scopes.
  • Identity & Access Program wiring that specifies which checks to run.

Outputs

  • Computer Recognition enriched with authorised scopes, audit tags, and topology hints on success.
  • Computer Failure when credentials are invalid or policy is violated.

Behaviour

  1. Validate the recognition carrier against the wiring plan (structure, fields).
  2. Verify credentials (signature/expiry/issuer) and requested scopes.
  3. Apply policy checks (role, separation of duty, topology scope).
  4. Emit an enriched recognition carrier on success; otherwise emit a failure carrier with origin and violated recognitions.

Proof

Refines computer/function/recognition.md by constraining the recognition to identity/policy carriers and requiring an explicit failure path for invalid credentials or policy violations. The refinement preserves the kernel’s recognition semantics and failure handling while specialising to the identity/access domain.

title: Backflow Operator id: backflow-operator role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Backflow Operator

Partial reverse flow that reconstructs plausible ancestors of a recognition.

Definition

  • Inputs: a recognition and admissible reverse/adjoint flow rules.
  • Output: set of plausible ancestor recognitions.
  • Behaviour: apply adjoint/approximate inverse of flow consistent with nucleus and discipline.

Invariants

  • Soundness: ancestors produce the target under forward flow (up to nucleus).
  • Non-deterministic: may yield multiple ancestors.

Proof

  • Uses: flow adjunction to reverse steps.
  • Produces: ancestor recognitions.
  • Shows that: backward traces can be generated consistently.

title: Base-Change Square id: base-change-square role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Base-Change Square

Verifies the Base-Change condition on a given pullback square: pullback and pushforward commute on subobjects/recognitions.

Definition

  • Inputs: a pullback square in the Relational Sheaf Topos, and subobjects expressed via Subobject Classifier.
  • Outputs: a Base-Change Condition witness for that square.
  • Behaviour: constructs characteristic maps, applies the pushforward/pullback adjunctions available in the sheaf topos, and checks that the two routes coincide.

Invariants

  • Commutation: pullback ∘ pushforward = pushforward ∘ pullback for the square.
  • Logical stability: characteristic maps are natural under base change.
  • Coverage respect: refinements of the square induce compatible base-change witnesses.

Proof

  • Uses: pullbacks/pushforwards and classifiers in the relational sheaf topos to express subobjects.
  • Produces: Base-Change Condition when the commutation holds.
  • Shows that: the given square satisfies Base-Change, yielding the condition witness used for substitution and logical relations.

title: Baseline Convergence Test id: baseline-convergence role: operator status: plan derived_from: acts/baseline-convergence inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Baseline Convergence Test

Statement

The act baseline-convergence witnesses a test that detects exactly when a trace tail collapses to the void.

Definition

  • Inputs: Baseline Remainder, Step Trace context.
  • Outputs: updated Baseline Remainder indicating collapse to baseline.
  • Behaviour: applies the act Baseline Convergence to check iterative reflex steps on a trace tail; if the iteration stabilizes against Nothing, the tail collapses to the baseline.

Invariants

  • Detects collapse without altering the trace tail unless convergence is witnessed.
  • Resulting baseline remainder matches the earned convergence behaviour (indistinguishable from baseline).

Proof

  • Derived from: Baseline Convergence.
  • Shows that: comparing iterative reflex steps against Nothing on a trace tail yields a baseline remainder exactly when the tail collapses; uniqueness up to earned equivalence follows from the act.

Given the earned trace relation and the convergence act, this test is fixed up to nucleus equivalence.

title: Baseline Limit Extraction id: baseline-limit role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Baseline Limit Extraction

Statement

The act baseline-limit applies the convergence test to tail-trace to isolate the stabilized tail BaselineRemainder.

Proof sketch

BaselineLimit = (BaselineConvergence TailTrace) records that once convergence is known, watching the tail through it isolates the exact remainder. Any other observation factors through this composite, so the limit is canonical.

Uniqueness

Because BaselineRemainder is earned by these acts, limit extraction is fixed up to nucleus equivalence.

title: Baseline Renewal id: baseline-renewal role: Refresh the baseline remainder within a storage snapshot. status: plan inputs:

  • id: storage-snapshot name: snapshot outputs:
  • id: storage-snapshot name: renewed transformation:
  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Baseline Renewal

Refreshes the baseline remainder inside a Storage Snapshot.

Behaviour

  • Extract the current baseline remainder from the snapshot.
  • Compute its limit via baseline-limit.
  • Write the renewed baseline back into the storage snapshot.

Proof

  • Uses: baseline-limit to compute the stabilized remainder, extraction/update helpers on storage snapshots.
  • Shows that: renewing the baseline remainder yields a new storage snapshot with an up-to-date baseline under earned limits.

title: Bayes Kernel Update id: bayes-kernel-update role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Bayes Kernel Update

Applies a Bayesian kernel to reweight epistemic profiles given an observation.

Definition

  • Inputs: a Bayesian Kernel, an Epistemic Profile, and an Observation.
  • Output: updated Epistemic Profile reweighted and renormalised.
  • Behaviour: evaluate observation likelihoods, apply kernel transition, and normalise via nucleus/entropy.

Invariants

  • Normalisation: resulting profile weights sum to one.
  • Entropy: expected entropy decreases under informative observations.

Proof

  • Uses: Bayesian kernel mechanics plus observation evidence.
  • Shows that: kernel-based Bayesian updates are realisable in the recognition framework.

title: Bias Operator id: bias-operator role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Bias Operator

Systematically distorts epistemic recognitions according to historical or baseline patterns.

Definition

  • Inputs: an Epistemic Profile and an implicit/earned bias pattern (e.g., baseline remainder).
  • Output: biased Epistemic Profile.
  • Behaviour: apply transformation to recognitions to reflect bias (e.g., skewing probabilities, suppressing certain observations).

Invariants

  • Determinism: given the bias pattern, output is fixed.
  • Monotonicity: repeated application compounds bias.

Proof

  • Uses: Epistemic Profile and bias pattern.
  • Produces: biased Epistemic Profile.
  • Shows that: bias can be encoded as a transformation on epistemic states.

title: Build Recognition Telescope id: build-recognition-telescope role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Build Recognition Telescope

Constructs a telescope of recognitions via successive nucleus collapses.

Definition

  • Inputs: a recognition and a sequence of collapse operations (e.g., nucleus, tower projections).
  • Output: Recognition Telescope.
  • Behaviour: iteratively collapse and record each stage.

Invariants

  • Chain monotonicity: each stage is coarser/equal to previous.
  • Stabilisation: telescope may stabilise at a fixed recognition.

Proof

  • Uses: nucleus/collapse steps on recognitions.
  • Produces: telescope structure.
  • Shows that: collapse progression can be systematically recorded.

title: Build Struct id: build-struct role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: runtime-value alias: fields outputs:
  • id: runtime-value alias: struct transformation:
  • return
  • struct proof_refs:
  • runtime-value

Build Struct

Constructs a record/runtime value from a list of field pairs.

Proof

  • Uses: interpreter’s record construction routine.
  • Shows that: struct aggregation is available as a documented operator.

title: Build Wiring Plan id: build-wiring-plan role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: connection-graph alias: graph outputs:
  • id: wiring-plan alias: plan
  • id: failure-carrier alias: failure transformation:
