  • id: argument-list alias: args outputs:
  • id: runtime-value alias: head transformation:
  • return
  • head proof_refs:
  • argument-list
  • runtime-value

Primitive Argument Head

Extracts the first element of an Argument List.

title: Primitive Argument Tail id: argument-tail role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: argument-list alias: args outputs:
  • id: argument-list alias: tail transformation:
  • return
  • tail proof_refs:
  • argument-list

Primitive Argument Tail

Extracts the tail (all but the head) of an Argument List.

title: Primitive Evaluate Section Step id: evaluate-section-step role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: hypertensor-section alias: section
  • id: evaluation-context-stack alias: stack outputs:
  • id: hypertensor-section alias: next-section
  • id: evaluation-context-stack alias: next-stack transformation:
  • return
  • next-section
  • next-stack proof_refs:
  • hypertensor-section
  • evaluation-context-stack

Primitive Evaluate Section Step

Kernel small-step over a hypertensor section using the top frame of the evaluation context stack to compute the next section and updated stack.

Proof

  • Uses: admissible flow/nucleus and frame plugging.
  • Shows that: small-step reduction is available as a primitive transition.

title: Primitive Evaluate Section id: evaluate-section role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: hypertensor-section alias: section
  • id: evaluation-context-stack alias: stack outputs:
  • id: hypertensor-section alias: result-section
  • id: evaluation-context-stack alias: result-stack transformation:
  • return
  • result-section
  • result-stack proof_refs:
  • hypertensor-section
  • evaluation-context-stack

Primitive Evaluate Section

Performs big-step evaluation of a hypertensor section by iterating small-step reductions until normal form or failure.

Proof

  • Uses: repeated application of small-step primitive.
  • Shows that: big-step evaluation can be realized as a primitive loop.

title: Primitive Expression Children id: primitive-expression-children role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: expression alias: expr outputs:
  • id: argument-list alias: parts transformation:
  • return
  • parts proof_refs:
  • expression
  • argument-list

Primitive Expression Children

Returns the tail operands of a tagged expression as an Argument List; returns empty for literals.

Proof

  • Uses: structural slicing of the expression array.
  • Shows that: operands are available as ordered lists for dispatch.

title: Primitive Expression Tag id: primitive-expression-tag role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: expression alias: expr outputs:
  • id: runtime-value alias: tag transformation:
  • return
  • tag proof_refs:
  • expression
  • runtime-value

Primitive Expression Tag

Returns the leading tag of a nested-array expression (or literal for atomic values).

Proof

  • Uses: structural inspection of the expression.
  • Shows that: every expression yields a dispatchable tag.

title: Primitive Set Binding id: primitive-set-binding role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: evaluation-state alias: state
  • id: runtime-value alias: name
  • id: runtime-value alias: value outputs:
  • id: evaluation-state alias: updated transformation:
  • return
  • updated proof_refs:
  • evaluation-state
  • runtime-value

Primitive Set Binding

Primitive environment update that returns a new evaluation state with name bound to value.

Proof

  • Uses: environment update.
  • Shows that: binding extension is available as a primitive state change.

title: Profile Bisimulation id: profile-bisimulation role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Profile Bisimulation

Profile/flow acts provide a bisimulation principle: recognitions are bisimilar if they inhabit the same profile and evolve identically under inherited flows and nuclei.

Definition

  • Inputs/Outputs: recognitions within the same profile carrier.
  • Behaviour: two recognitions are bisimilar if their flows/nuclei evolve identically along the trace/profile.

Invariants

  • Profile alignment: bisimulation only within the same profile.
  • Flow/nucleus coherence: bisimulation respects earned dynamics.

Proof

  • Uses: Profile Recognitions with their earned Flow and Nucleus actions.
  • Shows that: requiring recognitions to evolve identically under the profile’s flow/nucleus dynamics yields a bisimulation relation stable within the profile.

title: Prohibition Operator id: prohibition-operator role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Prohibition Operator

Recognises recognitions that violate norms.

Definition

  • Inputs: Norm Regime and a recognition.
  • Output: violation recognition (e.g., bottom/contradiction) when input is outside the admissible region; neutral/top when compliant.
  • Behaviour: apply the regime’s predicate to classify prohibition.

Invariants

  • Soundness: prohibited recognitions always yield violation.
  • Completeness: any violation is detected; compliant recognitions are neutral.
  • Monotonicity: strengthening the regime can only increase prohibitions.

Proof

  • Uses: Norm Regime and Recognition Carrier.
  • Produces: Recognition Carrier marking prohibition.
  • Shows that: prohibitions are recognisable as operators within the earned logic algebra.

title: Project Graph Edges id: project-graph-edges role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: connection-graph alias: graph outputs:
  • id: edge-set alias: edges transformation:
  • return
  • edges proof_refs:
  • connection-graph
  • edge-set

Proof

  • Uses: edge declarations embedded in the connection graph.
  • Shows that: edges can be extracted for downstream typing and acyclicity checks without extra primitives.

Project Graph Edges

Projects the Edge Set from a Connection Graph.

Proof

  • Uses: the edge declarations embedded in the connection graph.
  • Shows that: edges can be extracted for downstream checks without primitive opcodes.

title: Project Graph Entry Steps id: project-graph-entry-steps role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: connection-graph alias: graph outputs:
  • id: step-set alias: entries transformation:
  • return
  • entries proof_refs:
  • connection-graph
  • step-set

Proof

  • Uses: edge relation in the connection graph to find nodes with in-degree zero.
  • Shows that: entry steps can be exposed for scheduling/topology.

Project Graph Entry Steps

Extracts the source steps (no incoming edges) from a Connection Graph.

Proof

  • Uses: the edge relation in the connection graph to find nodes with in-degree zero.
  • Shows that: entry steps can be exposed without primitive opcodes.

title: Project Graph Exit Steps id: project-graph-exit-steps role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: connection-graph alias: graph outputs:
  • id: step-set alias: exits transformation:
  • return
  • exits proof_refs:
  • connection-graph
  • step-set

Proof

  • Uses: edge relation in the connection graph to find nodes with out-degree zero.
  • Shows that: exit steps can be exposed for wiring outputs/termination.

Project Graph Exit Steps

Extracts the sink steps (no outgoing edges) from a Connection Graph.

Proof

  • Uses: the edge relation in the connection graph to find nodes with out-degree zero.
  • Shows that: exit steps can be exposed without primitive opcodes.

title: Project Graph Ports id: project-graph-ports role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: connection-graph alias: graph outputs:
  • id: port-set alias: ports transformation:
  • return
  • ports proof_refs:
  • connection-graph
  • port-set

Proof

  • Uses: the port declarations embedded in the connection graph.
  • Shows that: ports can be extracted as a set for typing/wiring checks without additional primitives.

Project Graph Ports

Projects the Port Set from a Connection Graph.

Proof

  • Uses: the port declarations embedded in the connection graph.
  • Shows that: ports can be extracted for typing/policy checks without primitive opcodes.

title: Promote Temporal Section id: promote-temporal-section role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Promote Temporal Section

Promotes a section into a temporal section by binding recognitions to time.

Definition

  • Inputs: a Hypertensor Section and Temporal Recognizers describing time anchoring.
  • Output: time-annotated Hypertensor Section.
  • Behaviour: annotate section recognitions with timestamps/time slices; enforce consistency with temporal flow.

Invariants

  • Temporal coherence: section evolution respects time ordering.
  • Compatibility: aligns with memory/time-slice structures for persistence.

Proof

  • Uses: recognizer/time metadata to augment sections.
  • Produces: temporal section ready for time-aware operations.
  • Shows that: sections can be given temporal structure without violating sheaf invariants.

title: Propagate Failure id: propagate-failure role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: runtime-value alias: payload outputs:
  • id: runtime-value alias: result transformation:
  • call
    • failure-absorb
    • payload proof_refs:
  • runtime-value
  • failure-algebra

Propagate Failure

Propagates failure recognitions through operations, aligning with failure algebra.

Definition

  • Inputs: recognition(s) possibly containing failure elements from Failure Algebra.
  • Output: combined recognition where failures are absorbed/propagated as per the algebra.
  • Behaviour: evaluate operation; if any input is failure, propagate failure unless an explicit handler is defined.

Invariants

  • Absorption: failure presence forces failure result (unless handled).
  • Determinism: propagation follows algebraic rules consistently.

Proof

  • Uses: failure algebra absorption rules.
  • Produces: resulting recognition.
  • Shows that: failure handling is consistent with algebraic semantics.

title: Propagate Flow and Reach on Tower id: propagate-flow-reach-tower role: operator status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/reach
  • acts/flow-profile inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:
  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Propagate Flow and Reach on Tower

The Propagate Flow and Reach on Tower operator applies the earned flow and reach along the levels of a Modality Tower.

Definition

  • Input: a modality tower.
  • Output: an updated modality tower with flow/reach propagated.
  • Behaviour: applies the flow profile and reach acts levelwise, ensuring compatibility with tower composition.

Invariants

  • Flow and reach respect tower faces/degeneracies.
  • Propagation stays within the earned tower structure.

Proof

Built from acts/flow-profile and acts/reach, applied levelwise to the modality tower. The acts ensure compatibility with tower laws.

title: Pullback Sheaf id: pullback-sheaf role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Pullback Sheaf

Pulls a hypertensor sheaf back along a site morphism, preserving sheaf structure.

Definition

  • Inputs: a Sheaf Morphism and a map in the Hypertensor Site.
  • Output: pulled Hypertensor Sheaf on the source site.
  • Behaviour: compose sections with the site map; gluing/locality inherited.

Invariants

  • Functoriality: pullback respects composition of site maps.
  • Sheaf preservation: pullback of a sheaf is a sheaf; matching/gluing preserved.

Proof

  • Uses: sheaf morphism naturality and site pullbacks.
  • Produces: pulled-back sheaf.
  • Shows that: sheaves restrict along site maps without breaking earned axioms.

title: Primitive Push Evaluation Context id: primitive-push-evaluation-context role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: evaluation-state alias: state
  • id: evaluation-context alias: frame outputs:
  • id: evaluation-state alias: updated transformation:
  • return
  • updated proof_refs:
  • evaluation-state
  • evaluation-context

Primitive Push Evaluation Context

Pushes a frame onto the evaluation context stack within an evaluation state.

Proof

  • Uses: stack operations on the state’s context_stack.
  • Shows that: pushing frames is available as a primitive state update.

title: Push Evaluation Context id: push-evaluation-context role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: evaluation-state alias: state
  • id: evaluation-context alias: frame outputs:
  • id: evaluation-state alias: updated transformation:
  • let
    • stack
      • state
        • context_stack
    • updated_stack
      • push
        • stack
        • frame
    • return
      • state
        • context_stack
          • updated_stack proof_refs:
  • evaluation-state
  • evaluation-context
  • evaluation-context-stack

Push Evaluation Context

Pushes a new evaluation frame onto the Evaluation Context Stack inside the Evaluation State.

Invariants

  • Stack grows by one frame; previous frames remain ordered below.
  • New frame becomes the head of the context stack for subsequent stepping.

title: Pushforward Sheaf id: pushforward-sheaf role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Pushforward Sheaf

Pushes a hypertensor sheaf along a site morphism, preserving exactness where available.

Definition

  • Inputs: a Sheaf Morphism along a map of Hypertensor Site objects.
  • Output: pushed Hypertensor Sheaf on the target site.
  • Behaviour: right Kan-like image of sections along the site map; preserves finite limits when the morphism is exact.

Invariants

  • Functoriality: composition of site maps corresponds to composition of pushforwards.
  • Exactness: preserves limits/gluing under exact site maps.

Proof

  • Uses: sheaf morphism and site structure to define direct image.
  • Produces: pushed sheaf.
  • Shows that: sheaves transport along site maps preserving earned structure.

title: Quadratic Modality id: quadratic-modality role: operator status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/quadratic-modality inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:
  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Quadratic Modality

Operator capturing the lax idempotence (stabilisation after one iteration) property.

Proof

Sketch: earned by QuadraticModality; show that iterating the modality stabilises after one step, mirroring the KZ property.

title: Quasi-Identity Operator id: quasi-identity-operator role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Quasi-Identity Operator

Applies an almost-identity flow to a recognition, collapsing deviations via nucleus.

Definition

  • Inputs: a recognition and a Quasi-Identity Flow.
  • Output: recognition nucleus-equivalent to the input.
  • Behaviour: execute the flow, then collapse deviations with nucleus to recover an equivalent recognition.

Invariants

  • Idempotent up to nucleus: repeated application yields same result.
  • Stability: deviations never push outside admissible nucleus class.

Proof

  • Uses: quasi-identity flows and nucleus closure.
  • Produces: recognition equivalent to input.
  • Shows that: near-identity flows can be treated as identity modulo nucleus.

title: Query Linked Data id: query-linked-data role: operator status: someday derived_from: computer/function/evaluate-step inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Query Linked Data

Executes queries over the internal Linked Data Graph.

Definition

  • Inputs: graph reference and query (e.g., SPARQL pattern).
  • Outputs: results encoded as a Linked Data Graph fragment or Computer Failure.
  • Behaviour: evaluates the query against stored triples/quads, producing a recognised subgraph or failure on malformed queries/policy violations.

Invariants

  • No mutation; read-only access to the graph.
  • Policy: queries respect access constraints; failures emitted on violation.
  • Deterministic: same graph/query yields same result.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Evaluate Step, restricting to pure query evaluation over linked data.

title: Primitive Lookup Binding id: primitive-lookup-binding role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: runtime-value alias: name outputs:

  • return
  • value proof_refs:
  • evaluation-state
  • runtime-value

Primitive Lookup Binding

Primitive environment lookup by name in an evaluation state’s binding environment.

Proof

  • Uses: environment lookup.
  • Shows that: binding resolution is available as a primitive.

title: Primitive Extract Graph Edges id: primitive-extract-graph-edges role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: connection-graph alias: graph outputs:
  • id: edge-set alias: edges transformation:
  • return
  • edges proof_refs:
  • connection-graph
  • edge-set

Primitive Extract Graph Edges

Extracts the Edge Set from a Connection Graph, retaining endpoint port ids and carrier types.

Proof

  • Uses: edge data stored in the graph.
  • Shows that: edge extraction is available as a primitive decomposition.

title: Primitive Get Graph Entry Steps id: primitive-get-graph-entry-steps role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: connection-graph alias: graph outputs:
  • id: runtime-value alias: entries transformation:
  • return
  • entries proof_refs:
  • connection-graph
  • runtime-value

Primitive Get Graph Entry Steps

Computes the list of entry steps (no incoming edges) in a Connection Graph.

Proof

  • Uses: graph edge relation.
  • Shows that: entry computation is available as a primitive query.

title: Primitive Get Graph Exit Steps id: primitive-get-graph-exit-steps role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: connection-graph alias: graph outputs:

  • connection-graph
  • runtime-value

Primitive Get Graph Exit Steps

Computes the list of exit steps (no outgoing edges) in a Connection Graph.

Proof

  • Uses: graph edge relation.
  • Shows that: exit computation is available as a primitive query.

title: Primitive Extract Graph Ports id: primitive-extract-graph-ports role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: connection-graph alias: graph outputs:
  • id: port-set alias: ports transformation:

  • ports proof_refs:

Primitive Extract Graph Ports

Expands a Connection Graph into its Port Set, inducing typed ports from operator signatures.

Proof

  • Uses: step signatures stored in the graph.
  • Shows that: port extraction is available as a primitive decomposition.

title: Read Operator Signature id: read-operator-signature role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: operator-id alias: target outputs:
  • id: operator-signature alias: signature transformation:
  • return
  • signature proof_refs:
  • operator-id
  • operator-signature

Read Operator Signature

Retrieves the Operator Signature for an operator id from the interpreter registry.

Proof

  • Uses: interpreter registry mapping ids to signatures.
  • Shows that: signature resolution is available as a documented side table.

title: Read Snapshot State id: read-snapshot-state role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: recognition alias: recognitions
  • id: evaluation-state alias: eval-state outputs:
  • id: snapshot alias: snapshot
  • id: failure-carrier alias: failure transformation:
  • return
    • snapshot
    • failure proof_refs:
  • recognition
  • evaluation-state
  • snapshot
  • failure-carrier

Read Snapshot State

Reads recognitions and evaluation state to produce snapshot content, or a failure if state cannot be read consistently.

Invariants

  • Point-in-time consistency: captures recognitions and evaluation state atomically.
  • Non-mutating: does not modify inputs; failures leave state unchanged.

title: Computer Recognition id: recognition-api role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Computer Recognition

Definition

  • Inputs: recognition request (criteria/policy) as a Recognition Carrier plus ambient Policy Regime and Trace Carrier.
  • Outputs: accepted recognition carrier or Failure Algebra element.
  • Behaviour:
    1. Validate criteria and policy scopes against admissible recognitions.
    2. Invoke earned recognition operators (Recognize Observation, Recognize Context, etc.) on the supplied carrier.
    3. If criteria and policy hold, return the recognised carrier tagged with provenance; otherwise emit a failure witness with diagnostics and trace span.

Invariants

  • Preservation: successful recognitions coincide with earned recognition semantics; policy/trace witnesses are recorded.
  • Safety: failures leave carriers unchanged; no state mutation on failure.

Proof

  • Uses: recognition operators plus policy/trace carriers to check admissibility.
  • Shows that: applying earned recognition with explicit policy/trace context yields either a recognised carrier or a failure witness without mutating state.

title: Recognition Convolution id: recognition-convolution role: operator status: plan inputs: [] outputs: [] transformation:

  • return
  • value proof_refs: []

Recognition Convolution

Combines two recognitions via convolution across flow paths.

Definition

  • Inputs: recognitions a,b and flow rules.
  • Output: recognition a ⋆ b aggregating outcomes of flowing along a then b over admissible paths.

Invariants

  • Associative up to nucleus where flow composes.
  • Identity given by neutral recognition if available.

Proof

  • Uses: flow context to define composite effects.
  • Produces: convoluted recognition.
  • Shows that: recognitions can compose via flow convolution.

    • support
      • call

      • call

          • predicate

          • scope

        • support
        • window
        • predicate
