  • Compatibility: flow/nucleus and logical structure preserved componentwise.
  • Universality: satisfies sum/product universal properties in the world category.

Proof

  • Uses: Relational World structure and World Morphism to define categorical constructions.
  • Produces: Relational World realising sum or product with inherited invariants.
  • Shows that: multi-world combinations respect earned geometry and logic.

title: Write Snapshot id: write-snapshot role: operator status: plan inputs:

  • id: snapshot alias: snapshot outputs:
  • id: snapshot alias: persisted
  • id: failure-carrier alias: failure transformation:
  • return
    • persisted
    • failure proof_refs:
  • snapshot
  • failure-carrier

Write Snapshot

Atomically writes an encoded snapshot to storage, returning the stored handle or failure.

Invariants

  • Atomic write: either persists the snapshot or yields an explicit failure.
  • Does not mutate the input snapshot; returns stored handle/provenance.

title: Abstraction Theorem id: abstraction-theorem role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Abstraction Theorem

States that parametric relations imply contextual indistinguishability (a relational Reynolds-style theorem).

Definition

  • Premise: two interpretations are related by all Parametric Relations compatible with Parametric Uniformity.
  • Conclusion: no context in the index base can distinguish them; they are observationally equivalent.

Invariants

  • Context insensitivity: parametric relatedness implies contextual equality.
  • Stability: holds across all base morphisms and reindexings.

Proof

  • Uses: Parametric Uniformity and Parametric Relation to lift relational reasoning to contextual equivalence.
  • Shows that: uniformity under all reindexings yields contextual indistinguishability.

title: ActivityPub Program id: activitypub-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

ActivityPub Program

Program-level sketch to expose the relational computer through ActivityPub: map internal knowledge/runbooks to ActivityStreams activities and actors while keeping all storage as earned recognitions.

Behaviour

  • Translate ActivityStreams JSON to internal recognitions and runbooks via Recognition and Recognize Spec; apply policy/topology constraints.
  • Build/execute wiring plans for inbox/outbox handling via Plan Runbook, Evaluate Step, Detect Plan Conflicts, and Stabilize Plan.
  • Publish posts/resources as ActivityStreams objects; map to internal Program Command and Program Wiring Plan.
  • Snapshot ActivityPub state via Snapshot Plan using Program Snapshot and Program Transcript.
  • Do not introduce new storage; reuse the knowledge graph and traceability.

Goals

  • Federate site/knowledge content via ActivityPub.
  • Maintain semantic alignment with the internal knowledge graph and RDF/OWL correspondences.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Adjoint Profile Pair id: adjoint-profile-pair role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Adjoint Profile Pair

Two profiles whose recognitions stand in an adjunction via flow/nucleus.

Definition

  • Components: a pair of Interprofile Functors L ⊣ R with unit/counit compatible with Flow–Nucleus Adjunction.
  • Behaviour: transport recognitions between profiles with adjoint structure.

Invariants

  • Triangle identities for the adjunction.
  • Preservation of flow/nucleus when moving along L/R.
  • Logical/classifier coherence between the profiles.

Proof

  • Uses: interprofile functor data and flow/nucleus adjunction.
  • Shows that: profile pairs can be related via adjoint functors in the relational setting.

title: Admissible Morphism id: admissible-morphism role: structure status: plan derived_from: theorem/internal/stratified-site-structure proof_refs: []

Admissible Morphism

Definition

  • Carrier: admissible morphisms Adm ⊂ Mor(C) in a stratified ∞-site.
  • Source: induced by the stratified site structure stratified-site-structure.

Invariants

  • Stratified polarity: admissible morphisms respect the stratification step.
  • Locality: admissibility is local on the coverage; pullbacks along covers exist and glue.
  • Factorisation: every admissible morphism factors as Gen ∘ Obs, stable under pullback/composition.

Proof

  • Derived from: stratified-site-structure which earns the site, coverage, and stratification data.
  • Shows that: the earned site structure determines the admissible class with polarity, locality, and factorisation properties; uniqueness up to earned equivalence follows from the site derivation.

title: Admissible Normal Form id: admissible-normal-form role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/normalize-strongly proof_refs: []

Admissible Normal Form

Canonical normal forms on admissible morphisms (Adm), earned by strong normalization and reducibility acts.

Components

  • Rewrite relation ⇒ on Adm generated by eta/beta/tau-style steps from the normalization acts.
  • Well-founded measure on Adm ensuring termination of ⇒.
  • Confluence up to coherent cells, yielding unique normal forms in the 1-truncation.
  • Normal-form map N∞ : Adm → Adm sending each admissible morphism to its canonical normal representative.

Proof

Sketch: use NormalizeStrongly, NormalizeSyntax, and SummarizeReduction to define the rewrite relation on Adm (eta/beta/tau steps). Show termination via the earned measure and confluence via critical-pair joins, yielding unique normal forms. Define N∞ accordingly.

title: Agent Newsletters Program id: agent-newsletters-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Agent Newsletters Program

Program idea to generate and distribute newsletters for agents using the relational computer.

Behaviour

  • Aggregate recognitions (updates, runbook summaries, telemetry) relevant to agents.
  • Compile into newsletter content; dispatch via interfaces respecting policy and traceability.
  • Store provenance and links back to underlying recognitions.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Agent id: agent role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Agent

Relational agent with epistemic and normative profiles and local flow/nucleus.

Definition

  • Components: an Epistemic Profile, a Norm Regime, and local flow/nucleus data from Flow–Nucleus Adjunction.
  • Behaviour: epistemic updates and norm enforcement act on the agent’s local recognitions and contexts.

Invariants

  • Commutation: epistemic updates and norm enforcement commute with the local flow/nucleus.
  • Stability: agent state remains inside admissible recognitions given its norm regime.
  • Monotonicity: new observations refine, not expand, epistemic possibilities.

Proof

  • Uses: Epistemic Profile, Norm Regime, and Flow–Nucleus Adjunction to define the agent’s state/evolution.
  • Shows that: agents can evolve under observations and norms without breaking earned flow/nucleus structure.

title: Ancestry Tree id: ancestry-tree role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Ancestry Tree

Tree of all backflow expansions of a recognition.

Definition

  • Nodes: recognitions reachable by applying backflow steps from a target.
  • Edges: backflow transitions consistent with discipline/nucleus.

Invariants

  • Finiteness/branching as governed by admissible backflows.
  • Collapse: branches may merge under nucleus equivalence.

Proof

  • Uses: backflow mechanics to expand ancestors.
  • Shows that: ancestral provenance forms a tree-like carrier.

title: Aperiodic Germ id: aperiodic-germ role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Aperiodic Germ

Smallest aperiodic-local window from which a global section can be reconstructed.

Definition

  • Carrier: minimal Hypertensor Window exhibiting aperiodicity that can generate the global pattern via sheaf gluing.
  • Role: seed for aperiodic extensions.

Invariants

  • Minimality: removing any part destroys aperiodicity/generativity.
  • Compatibility: fits coverage/sheaf constraints for reconstruction.

Proof

  • Uses: hypertensor window and aperiodicity acts to identify minimal seeds.
  • Shows that: aperiodic structure can be seeded from a germ.

title: Archives and Media Program id: archives-media-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Archives and Media Program

Program idea to manage media and archives within the relational computer.

Behaviour

  • Ingest media/archives as recognitions with provenance and metadata.
  • Provide retrieval, cataloging, and lifecycle runbooks (backup, migration).
  • Expose interfaces for browsing/searching under policy/trace constraints.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Argument List id: argument-list role: structure status: plan proof_refs:

  • expression
  • runtime-value fields:
  • name: head type: runtime-value description: first argument in order of appearance.
  • name: tail type: argument-list description: remaining arguments after the head (empty list when length=1).
  • name: items type: runtime-value description: ordered sequence of evaluated runtime values.
  • name: length type: runtime-value description: derived size of items.
  • name: provenance type: runtime-value description: optional trace witnesses per argument.

Argument List

Ordered list of runtime values supplied to an operator invocation.

Definition

  • head: first Runtime Value in the list.
  • tail: remaining arguments packaged as an Argument List (empty when length is 1).
  • items: ordered sequence of Runtime Value entries.
  • length: derived size of items.
  • provenance: optional trace witness showing where each argument was produced.

Invariants

  • Head/tail decomposition matches items and length.
  • Order is preserved exactly as written in the expression syntax.
  • Elements are already evaluated (arguments are values, not raw expressions).
  • Length/arity will be checked against the target Operator Signature.

Proof

  • Uses: call expressions in the nested-array syntax produce ordered arguments.
  • Shows that: packaging evaluated arguments with length/provenance yields a carrier the dispatcher can type-check against an operator signature.

title: Astrology Aquarian Math Program id: astrology-aquarian-math-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Astrology Aquarian Math Program

Speculative program to explore astrology/“aquarian math” concepts on the relational computer, modelling them as recognitions and analyses.

Behaviour

  • Represent astrological data/structures as recognitions.
  • Run analyses/interpretations via runbooks; record provenance/traceability.
  • Treat this as exploratory; aligns with kernel without new semantics.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Astronomy Program id: astronomy-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Astronomy Program

Program idea to represent and analyse astronomical data within the relational computer.

Behaviour

  • Ingest observational data as recognitions.
  • Run analysis runbooks (simulation, cataloging) with traceability.
  • Expose results via interfaces under policy/measurement constraints.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Recognition Attracting Basin id: attracting-basin role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Recognition Attracting Basin

Set of recognitions that flow into a stable fixed shape.

Definition

  • Carrier: recognitions whose flow/nucleus iterations converge to a given fixed recognition or shape.
  • Basin: equivalence class under convergence.

Invariants

  • Closure: closed under flow toward the attractor.
  • Partition: basins partition the state space around attractors.

Proof

  • Uses: flow and nucleus to define convergence.
  • Shows that: attractor basins exist and can be collected as carriers.

title: Audit Event Carrier id: audit-event-carrier role: Events emitted by introspection probes packaged with trace/channel metadata. status: plan inputs:

  • id: trace-carrier name: trace outputs:
  • id: audit-event-carrier name: audit-event proof_refs: []

Audit Event Carrier

Definition

  • Carrier: events emitted by Introspection Probe over Introspection Channel, packaged into Distribution Packets with trace context.
  • Fields: probe id, channel endpoints, payload (observables), trace span, storage metadata, invariants snapshot.

Invariants

  • Trace alignment: events carry the trace span of the probe/channel.
  • Integrity: storage metadata covers payload/invariants.
  • Provenance: probe/channel identifiers recorded for auditability.

Proof

  • Derived from: Trace Carrier to sequence events.
  • Uses: introspection probe/channel and distribution packets to define payload and transport context.
  • Shows that: packaging observables with trace/channel/storage metadata yields an audit event carrier suitable for reporting.

title: Audit Program id: audit-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Audit Program

Program to manage auditing within the relational computer.

Behaviour

  • Collect audit recognitions for operations/runbooks.
  • Provide browsing/querying under policy controls.
  • Integrate with measurement/traceability for tamper-evidence.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Audit Report Carrier id: audit-report-carrier role: Aggregated summaries over audit events. status: plan inputs: [] outputs:

  • id: audit-report-carrier name: audit-report proof_refs: []

Audit Report Carrier

Definition

  • Carrier: summaries aggregating Audit Event entries with provenance, state deltas, and integrity checks.
  • Fields: set of event ids, aggregation metrics (counts, severities), trace spans covered, policy context, integrity hash.

Invariants

  • Completeness: reports reference only existing audit events.
  • Integrity: hash covers included events and summary fields.
  • Trace scope: report trace span is the union of event spans.

Proof

  • Derived from: Audit Event Carrier.
  • Shows that: aggregating audit events yields a report carrier suitable for governance and monitoring without altering event semantics.

title: Autonomous Relational Agents Program id: autonomous-relational-agents-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Autonomous Relational Agents Program

Program idea for agents that operate entirely within the relational computer, using earned recognitions and runbooks.

Behaviour

  • Represent agent state and goals as recognitions.
  • Execute runbooks under policy/topology constraints.
  • Log traceability/measurement for explainability.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Backup, Archive, Migration Program id: backup-archive-migration-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Backup, Archive, Migration Program

Program idea to manage backup, archive, and migration workflows for the relational computer.

Behaviour

  • Plan and execute backups/archives; manage retention policies.
  • Migrate data across storage tiers/regions using storage/replication runbooks.
  • Maintain traceability and audit for all operations.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Base-Change Condition id: base-change-condition role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/acts/sheaf-condition proof_refs: []

Base-Change Condition

Structure stating that pullback and pushforward along a pullback square commute, giving substitution stability for recognitions/subobjects in the sheaf setting.

Definition

  • Context: pullback squares in a sheaf or presheaf topos built on a Coverage Site.
  • Condition: for any subobject classified by Recognition Classifier, the pullback of a pushforward equals the pushforward of a pullback.
  • Expression: characteristic maps into Subobject Classifier are natural with respect to base change, making logical predicates stable.

Invariants

  • Stability: characteristic maps commute with pullback/pushforward for every admissible square.
  • Naturality: substitution along base change preserves logical structure and recognitions.
  • Compatibility: holds for recognitions/subobjects across the sheaf topos and is preserved by refinements of the coverage.

Proof

  • Derived from: SheafCondition which demands stability of restriction/gluing under reindexing; base change is the precise formulation of that stability.
  • Shows that: the topos satisfies Base-Change, ensuring substitution stability for recognitions/logical predicates and making adjoints/pushforwards behave predictably.

title: Baseline Remainder id: baseline-remainder role: structure status: plan derived_from: acts/baseline-limit proof_refs: []

Baseline Remainder

Definition

  • Carrier: the stabilized tail of a trace.
  • Source: earned via Baseline Limit, using convergence of Baseline Convergence on Tail Trace.

Invariants

  • Stability: tail remains fixed under further renewal/iteration.
  • Compatibility: tail aligns with the earned baseline convergence behaviour.

Proof

  • Derived from: Baseline Limit.
  • Shows that: applying baseline convergence to tail traces yields a stabilized remainder, unique up to earned equivalence.

title: Bayesian Kernel id: bayesian-kernel role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Bayesian Kernel

Stochastic kernel that updates epistemic profiles in proportion to how recognitions survive observation collapses.

Definition

  • Carrier: transition map from an Epistemic Profile to a probability/weighting over profiles, guided by the Entropy Functional.
  • Acts like a Markov kernel on recognitions: reweights and normalises according to observation likelihood.

Invariants

  • Normalisation: outgoing weights sum to one.
  • Entropy monotonicity: expected entropy decreases under informative updates.
  • Stability: kernel respects earned recognitions and collapses via nucleus.

Proof

  • Uses: epistemic profiles and entropy to define stochastic transitions.
  • Shows that: Bayesian-style updates can be represented as kernels on earned recognition carriers.

title: Bayesian Profile id: bayesian-profile role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Bayesian Profile

Epistemic profile enriched with probabilistic recognitions via entropy measures.

Definition

  • Carrier: Epistemic Profile annotated with probability/ entropy data from the Entropy Functional.
  • Structure: recognitions carry weights; entropy measures uncertainty.

Invariants

  • Normalisation: probabilities sum to 1 in each context; entropy is well-defined.
  • Refinement: updates lower entropy when evidence arrives.

Proof

  • Uses: Epistemic Profile and Entropy Functional.
  • Shows that: probabilistic reasoning can be overlaid on epistemic profiles while retaining earned recognitions.

title: Belief Base id: belief-base role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Belief Base

Stable but revisable set of recognitions representing an agent’s beliefs.

Definition

  • Carrier: subset of an Epistemic Profile designated as current beliefs.
  • Structure: closed under earned inference but subject to revision.

Invariants

  • Consistency: may be paraconsistent; contradictions tracked explicitly.
  • Revisability: can be updated by revision operators while retaining coherence.

Proof

  • Uses: Epistemic Profile as the ambient belief space.
  • Shows that: beliefs can be packaged as a revisable base for revision/AGM style operations.

title: Binding Environment id: binding-environment role: structure status: plan fields:

  • name: env type: runtime-value description: list or map of {id, value} entries.
  • name: parent type: binding-environment description: optional outer environment for lexical scoping. proof_refs:
  • relationlessness

Binding Environment

Finite mapping from identifiers (port ids or local names) to runtime values, used during evaluation to resolve variables and ports.

Definition

  • env: list or map of {id, value} pairs where value is a Runtime Value.
  • parent: optional link to an outer environment for lexical scoping.

Invariants

  • Uniqueness: ids are unique within a single environment layer.
  • Shadowing: inner environments may shadow outer ids; lookup searches innermost first.
  • Type discipline: values respect the carrier types declared for ids (ports or variables).
  • Persistence: lookups are pure; updates produce a new environment (or explicitly mutate via documented operators).

Proof

  • Uses: the need to resolve identifiers during evaluation.
  • Shows that: an explicit environment structure suffices to bind names to values with lexical scoping and type discipline.

title: Bioinformatics & Genomics Program id: bioinformatics-genomics-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Bioinformatics & Genomics Program

Program idea to ingest, analyse, and manage bioinformatics/genomics data within the relational computer, treating sequences/variants/annotations/experiments as recognitions linked to health and lab domains.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • SequenceRecognition: DNA/RNA/protein sequences.
    • VariantRecognition: SNPs, indels, structural variants.
    • AnnotationRecognition: functional annotations, domains, motifs.
    • OmicsExperimentRecognition: transcriptomics/proteomics assays.
  • Topology/measurement:
    • gene networks, pathways, regulatory relationships.
    • measurements: expression levels, effect sizes, confidence scores.
  • Runbooks:
    • ingest sequencing/omics datasets with provenance;
    • alignment/variant calling/annotation with traceability;
    • manage storage/policy/sharing of results.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Blockchain On-Chain Reading Program id: blockchain-onchain-reading-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Blockchain On-Chain Reading Program

Program idea to read and interpret on-chain data using the relational computer, mirroring on-chain history as read-only recognitions.

Behaviour

  • Core recognitions:
    • ChainRecognition: chain id/endpoints/consensus/avg block time.
    • BlockRecognition: height/hash/parent/time/validator/gas, linked to chain.
    • TransactionRecognition: from/to/value/gas/status/logs; linked to blocks and higher-level recognitions (contracts, governance events, markets).
    • ContractRecognition: address/code hash/ABI/deployment tx.
    • StateSnapshotRecognition: captured contract state (balances/config).
  • Runbooks:
    • import_block_range: fetch blocks/txs, normalise via intake/policy, persist.
    • import_contract_events: query logs/events, normalise to domain recognitions.
    • import_account_activity: fetch txs for address, link to portfolios/DAOs.
  • Topology:
    • Link on-chain recognitions to markets/governance/legal/ecology graphs.
  • Measurement:
    • Track per-address/contract/chain metrics (activity, gas, latency, reorgs).

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Bookmarks & Saved Links Program id: bookmarks-saved-links-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Bookmarks & Saved Links Program

Program idea to manage bookmarks and saved links as recognitions in the relational computer with useful context.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • BookmarkRecognition: URL, title, tags, saved date.
    • ContextNoteRecognition: why it was saved, links to projects/ideas.
  • Measurement/topology:
    • revisit rates, age, link rot detection.
    • links between bookmarks ↔ projects/topics/people.
  • Runbooks:
    • save_bookmark, review_bookmarks, prune_stale_links.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Boundary Recognition id: boundary-recognition role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Boundary Recognition

Identifies the “edge” of a relational region: those recognitions where context flow cannot extend without violating admissibility.

Definition

  • Carrier: subset of a Hypertensor Section over a Hypertensor Window consisting of points with no admissible outward extension.
  • Witness: recognition marking boundary points and their local obstructions.

Invariants

  • Minimality: every boundary point is adjacent to inadmissible extension.
  • Stability: applying nucleus/flow within the window preserves boundary marks.
  • Coverage compatibility: boundaries respect window covers and restriction maps.

Proof

  • Uses: section/window to define admissibility and flow; boundary is the locus where extension fails.
  • Shows that: boundaries are recognisable carriers suitable for holonomy and sheaf-gluing diagnostics.

title: Branching Time World id: branching-time-world role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Branching Time World

Tree of time slices representing possible futures in a relational world.

Definition

  • Components: a Relational World and a tree/poset of Time Slices connected by forward edges.
  • Structure: flow applies along forward edges; rollbacks create branches.

Invariants

  • Forward flow: context/section evolution only along forward edges.
  • Branching: rollback spawns new branches; branches inherit world geometry/logics.
  • Consistency: slices respect causal/flow constraints of the underlying world.

Proof

  • Uses: Relational World and Time Slice to organise temporal branching.
  • Shows that: possible futures can be modelled as a branching structure with earned flow and rollback semantics.

title: Calendar Event id: calendar-event role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-recognition proof_refs: []

Calendar Event

Recognition of a calendar commitment/task with time, recurrence, and policy metadata.

Definition

  • Fields: title/description, start/end (instant/duration), recurrence reference, timezone reference, constraints (dependencies, location), provenance/policy.
  • Carrier: refinement of Computer Recognition scoped to calendar semantics.

Invariants

  • Well-formed instants/durations; references to timezone/recurrence must exist.
  • Policy bindings match the event’s context and participants.
  • Traceability: retains provenance and trace span.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Recognition.
  • Shows that: specialising recognitions to calendar events yields an event carrier suitable for scheduling operators.

title: Calendar Schedule id: calendar-schedule role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-wiring-plan proof_refs: []

Calendar Schedule

Executable wiring plan of events/recurrences/constraints over the computer kernel carriers.

Definition

  • Carrier: Computer Wiring Plan specialised to calendar operators and event instances.
  • Fields: expanded event instances, dependency edges, recurrence expansions, timezone-normalised instants, policy bindings.

Invariants

  • Acyclic, well-typed plan; respects event dependencies/recurrence expansions.
  • Timezone-normalised instants; no ambiguous offsets.
  • Failure propagation: conflicts/failures flow via computer failure carriers.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Wiring Plan.
  • Shows that: specialising a wiring plan to calendar semantics yields an executable schedule over computer operators/carriers.

title: Calendar Scheduling Program id: calendar-scheduling-program role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Calendar Scheduling Program

Program that models commitments, tasks, and schedules as recognitions and evaluates valid execution traces consistent with policy and context.

Definition

  • Inputs: events/tasks/deadlines recognised via computer Recognition into Computer Recognition carriers; initial Computer Evaluation State.
  • Plan: construct a Computer Wiring Plan encoding constraints (dependencies, durations, policies) and search over admissible trace orderings using Evaluate Step.
  • Persistence: checkpoint plans/state via Computer Storage and Computer Snapshot.
  • Outputs: stable wiring plan representing a schedule; failures surfaced as Computer Failure.

Behaviour

  • Represent events, tasks, constraints as recognitions with provenance/policy.
  • Model scheduling as search over valid trace orderings under commutation and reachability.
  • Measure plan stability, slack, and conflict zones via flow/nucleus dynamics.

Invariants

  • Policy/trace compliance: all plans respect recognised policies and trace ordering; no bypass of computer operators.
  • Determinism: given the same inputs and snapshots, the same plan/result is produced.
  • Failure purity: conflicts/violations emit computer failures; state unchanged on failure.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Kernel.
  • Shows that: scheduling can be expressed as trace evolution and stabilization via computer carriers/operators without extending machine semantics.

title: Calendar Service Program id: calendar-service-program role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Calendar Service Program

Program-layer API for relational calendar handling (events, recurrence, time zones) built on the documentation interpreter.

Definition

  • Inputs: event specifications recognised via computer Recognition and Recognize Spec into Program Command and calendar event carriers; initial Program Session.
  • Plan: build/refresh a Program Wiring Plan for recurrence rules, time zones, dependencies, conflict resolution via Plan Runbook; execute with computer Evaluate Step; check with Detect Plan Conflicts and Stabilize Plan.
  • Persistence: save/restore calendars via Snapshot Plan using Program Snapshot and Program Transcript.
  • Outputs: updated wiring plan/session and conflict/stability reports; failures as Computer Failure.

Structures

  • Calendar Event: recognition with start/end, recurrence, timezone, constraints.
  • Timezone Profile: offsets/DST rules as parameters.
  • Recurrence Rule: earned recurrence specification (freq, interval, exceptions).
  • Conflict Report: conflicts detected among events (overlaps, policy violations).
  • Calendar Schedule: binding of events into an executable wiring plan.

Operators (API)

  • recognize_event: ingest event specs into calendar event recognitions.
  • normalize_time: apply timezone/offset rules to event instants.
  • plan_schedule: build/refresh the wiring plan respecting dependencies, commutation, recurrence expansions.
  • detect_conflicts: compute [Conflict Report] when overlaps or constraints collide.
  • stabilize_schedule: adjust plan under changes, maintaining trace/policy invariants.
  • snapshot_schedule: persist/restore calendar state.

Invariants

  • Time/zone fidelity: normalization uses explicit timezone profiles; no hidden offsets.
  • Recurrence determinism: recurrence expansions are deterministic for given rules.
  • Conflict purity: conflicts reported without mutating state; mutations flow through evaluate-step/plan.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: calendar handling (including Gregorian/timezone rules) can be expressed via computer recognition/evaluation/storage with domain parameters supplied as inputs, without extending machine semantics.

title: Calendar & Tasks Program id: calendar-tasks-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Calendar & Tasks Program

Program idea to manage calendars, tasks, and habits within the relational computer.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • EventRecognition: start/end, location, participants, related projects.
    • TaskRecognition: description, due date, status, estimated effort.
    • HabitRecognition: recurring actions with desired frequency and measurement channels.
  • Topology/measurement:
    • events/tasks linked to projects, people, locations, resources.
    • measurements: time spent, completion rates, context switching, daily/weekly load.
  • Runbooks/views:
    • plan week, rebalance tasks, negotiate conflicts, schedule focus time.
    • calendar as time projection; kanban as status projection; “life load” charts from measurement channels.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Capability Gate id: capability-gate role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Capability Gate

Capability-based gate controlling privileged operations on ports/channels.

Definition

  • Components: Gate Recognition tokens bound to Ports Carrier operations.
  • Purpose: only holders of capability tokens may perform certain actions.

Invariants

  • Non-forgeability: capabilities must be earned/issued, not guessed.
  • Enforcement: privileged ops require valid capability gates.

Proof

  • Uses: gate recognitions and ports to define capabilities.
  • Shows that: capability gating can enforce privilege separation.

title: Care Networks Program id: care-networks-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Care Networks Program

Program idea to model and support care networks (people, relationships, needs) within the relational computer to enable transparent, humane mutual aid.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • PersonRecognition: consent, needs, capacities.
    • NeedRecognition: concrete requests (rides, money, listening, research).
    • OfferRecognition: what someone can provide.
    • ExchangeRecognition: when a need and an offer meet.
  • Topology/measurement:
    • networks/affinity groups/households/orgs.
    • measurements: load (giving/receiving), unmet needs, burnout risk.
  • Runbooks:
    • match_needs_offers, check_in, rebalance_load to support communities.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Channel State id: channel-state role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Channel State

Snapshot of in-flight messages on a channel as trace prefixes/suffixes.

Definition

  • Components: partial traced steps from Traced Step Observation along a Message Channel; markers for snapshot boundaries.
  • Structure: records sent/received observations around a cut.

Invariants

  • Consistency: partial receives always correspond to sent observations in the snapshot window.
  • Ordering: respects channel order; markers bracket the captured segment.

Proof

  • Uses: Message Channel and Traced Step Observation to record in-flight messages.
  • Shows that: channel state can be captured consistently for distributed snapshots.

title: City Infrastructure Program id: city-infrastructure-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

City Infrastructure Program

Program idea to model and manage city infrastructure (services, assets, workflows) using the relational computer.

Behaviour

  • Represent infrastructure components and services as recognitions.
  • Coordinate maintenance/operations runbooks under policy/topology constraints.
  • Track measurements/telemetry for infrastructure health.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Client SDK Program id: client-sdk-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Client SDK Program

A program-layer wiring that exposes the kernel via client SDKs. It wraps intake, policy, and telemetry so external applications interact with the machine without bypassing earned constraints.

Behaviour

  • Validate and normalise client requests via computer Recognition and Recognize Spec; attach auth/trace context into Computer Evaluation State.
  • Build/execute runbooks for SDK calls via Plan Runbook, Evaluate Step, Detect Plan Conflicts, and Stabilize Plan.
  • Forward requests to recognition/storage/evaluation endpoints; validate responses and update the Program Wiring Plan.
  • Snapshot SDK state via Snapshot Plan using Program Snapshot and Program Transcript.
  • Emit telemetry via measurement fabric; surface failures as Computer Failure.

Invariants

  • Requests are policy-compliant and traceable.
  • Responses validate against computer recognition carriers.
  • Failures surface as Computer Failure; state unchanged on failure.
  • Deterministic processing for a given request/runbook; failures are explicit (auth/policy violations, type errors, plan conflicts).

Proof

Refines the computer Wiring Plan by fixing endpoints and policy checks for SDK calls while preserving kernel failure semantics and exposing only computer carriers/operators; uses shared program-layer primitives for command normalisation, planning, conflict handling, and snapshotting.

title: Coalition Structure id: coalition-structure role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Coalition Structure

Groups of agents sharing recognitions stable under mutual flow.

Definition

  • Carrier: partitions of agents into coalitions where applying Coordination Operator within the group yields stable shared recognitions.
  • Structure: coalition recognitions and joint flows.

Invariants

  • Stability: intra-coalition recognitions remain aligned under social flow.
  • Closure: coalitions are maximal sets with stable shared recognitions.

Proof

  • Uses: Agent set and Coordination Operator.
  • Shows that: coalitions are recognisable as stable alignment groups.

title: Code Refactoring Program id: code-refactoring-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Code Refactoring Program

Program idea to apply refactoring tasks to codebases using the relational computer for planning, execution, and traceability.

Behaviour

  • Represent code artifacts and refactoring tasks as recognitions.
  • Plan/apply refactorings via runbooks; ensure policy/testing gates.
  • Record provenance and measurements (coverage, complexity) for changes.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Coeffect Comonad id: coeffect-comonad role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Coeffect Comonad

Definition

  • Base: Comonad Fiber equips each fixed fibre with a comonad action.
  • Reindexing: Comonad Reindex transports comonadic objects along base morphisms while preserving the comonad laws.
  • Structure: assembles these into the indexed coeffect comonad over the fixed-profile fibres.

Invariants

  • Reindexing commutes with counit and comultiplication; pullbacks of comonadic objects preserve structure.
  • Functorial across the index base: identity and composition of base morphisms act as identity and composition on pulled-back comonadic objects.

Proof

  • Uses: Comonad Fiber for fibrewise comonads and Comonad Reindex for compatibility along the base.
  • Produced by: Comonad Reindex, which constructs the pulled-back comonadic objects used to assemble the indexed comonad.
  • Shows that: fibrewise comonads plus reindexing compatibility satisfy the definition of an indexed comonad, yielding the coeffect structure used by parametricity and uniformity results.

title: Collapse Manifold id: collapse-manifold role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Collapse Manifold

Set of recognitions sharing a nucleus limit under all flow extensions.

Definition

  • Carrier: recognitions whose flow extensions converge to the same nucleus limit, forming an equivalence class manifold.
  • Structure: captures basin of attraction toward a collapse point.

Invariants

  • Closure: closed under flow/nucleus toward the shared limit.
  • Partition: collapse manifolds form a partition of admissible recognitions.

Proof

  • Uses: flow and nucleus to define limits.
  • Shows that: recognitions can be grouped by shared collapse behaviour.

title: Combinatorial Scent Mereology Program id: combinatorial-scent-mereology-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Combinatorial Scent Mereology Program

Exploratory program to model combinatorial scent/mereology concepts as recognitions so olfactory experience and perfumery can live in the relational universe with explicit part–whole structure.

Behaviour

  • Represent odour/accord/whole scents as recognitions with explicit part–whole links:
    • OdorantRecognition: basic notes (descriptors, intensity ranges, volatility, associations).
    • ScentComponentRecognition: intermediate accords/blends linked to odorants with weights/interaction notes.
    • ScentWholeRecognition: perceived whole scent with context (place, time, weather), sources, subjective descriptors.
    • PartWholeRelationRecognition: mereological links (part contributions: foreground/background/base).
  • Measurement/topology:
    • intensity over time (evaporation curves), perceptual dimensions (fresh–stale, sweet–bitter, clean–dirty), association metrics (memories, moods).
    • scent-space neighbourhoods; graphs odorants→components→wholes; cross-links to places/events/foods/emotions.
  • Runbooks/use cases:
    • decompose_scent(whole_id): infer likely components/odorants from a whole.
    • compose_scent(target_profile): propose combinations to approximate a desired profile.
    • trace_scent_associations(whole_id): explore links between a scent, memories, moods, and paracosms/MUD locations.

Invariants

  • Uses only earned recognitions; no new semantics beyond kernel.
  • Part–whole relations and measurements are traceable and auditable.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Comonad Fiber id: comonad-fiber role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/structure/fixed-fiber-category proof_refs: []

Comonad Fiber

Definition

  • Carrier: for each fixed context, the fibre category from Fixed Fiber Category equipped with a pointwise comonad induced by the fixed flow (parameter).
  • Morphisms: fibre morphisms that commute with the comonad counit and comultiplication in each fixed context.
  • Parameters: a fixed-profile flow supplying the comonad structure across fibres.

Invariants

  • Functorial in contexts: reindexing between fixed fibres (defined separately) preserves the comonad action.
  • Comonad laws (counit and coassociativity) hold pointwise in every fibre, inherited from the fixed-profile flow.

Proof

  • Derived from: Fixed Fiber Category, adding the parameter of a fixed-profile flow to equip each fibre with a comonad.
  • Shows that: each fixed fibre supports a coherent comonad action, giving an indexed comonad over the fixed-profile fibres.

title: Complexity Profile id: complexity-profile role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Complexity Profile

Measures how “incompressible” a trace or recognition is, as a relational analog of Kolmogorov complexity.

Definition

  • Carrier: mapping from traces/recognitions to compressibility witnesses from Compressibility Recognition.
  • Interpretation: incompressible items carry high complexity; compressible ones lower complexity.

Invariants

  • Monotonicity: more compressible traces have lower complexity.
  • Stability: complexity respects shuffle equivalence classes.

Proof

  • Uses: Compressibility Recognition.
  • Shows that: complexity can be expressed via compressibility in the earned trace/shuffle framework.

title: Compressibility Cost id: compressibility-cost role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Compressibility Cost

Cost metric derived from compressibility recognition to guide scheduling and storage.

Definition

  • Measure: maps traces/recognitions to a cost based on Compressibility Recognition: incompressible items have higher cost.
  • Role: informs scheduling/storage decisions.

Invariants

  • Monotonic: higher compressibility → lower cost.
  • Stable under shuffle-equivalence.

Proof

  • Uses: compressibility recognition to assign costs.
  • Shows that: compressibility can be quantified as a cost metric.

title: Computer Evaluation State id: computer-evaluation-state role: structure status: later derived_from: machine/structure/evaluation-state proof_refs: []

Computer Evaluation State

Definition

  • Carrier: program-facing view of the machine Evaluation State with only SDK-safe fields exposed.
  • Fields (concrete, program-visible):
    • plan_ref: pointer to the active Computer Wiring Plan.
    • ready_queue: ordered list of step identifiers ready to run.
    • bindings: map from port identifiers to concrete computer carriers.
    • in_flight (optional): currently executing step metadata.
    • failure (optional): Computer Failure slot.
    • observability (optional): metrics/telemetry context for the run.
    • version (optional string): monotonically increasing counter for integrity.
  • Purpose: allow computer/SDK tooling to inspect or step evaluation without direct machine manipulation, with integrity preserved by computer operators.

Invariants

  • Mirrors the machine evaluation state; any mutation must go through computer-level operators that refine machine evaluation.
  • Well-formedness inherited from the machine evaluation-state derivation.

Proof

  • Derived from: Machine Evaluation State.
  • Shows that: exposing evaluation state at the computer layer preserves machine invariants while providing a stable API surface.

title: Computer Failure Carrier id: computer-failure role: structure status: later derived_from: machine/structure/failure-carrier proof_refs: []

Computer Failure Carrier

Definition

  • Carrier: typed failures exposed at the computer layer, derived from the machine Failure Carrier.
  • Fields (concrete at the computer layer):
    • failure_id (string/UUID): unique identifier for this failure event.
    • kind (enum string): one of input-missing, type-mismatch, reduction-undefined, policy-violation, integrity-failure, or implementation-defined subcodes.
    • origin (object {operator, step_id, plan_ref}): where the failure arose.
    • inputs_digest (string/SHA-256 hex): digest over the offending inputs.
    • diagnostics (array of strings/objects): human/machine-readable hints.
    • timestamp (string, ISO-8601 UTC): when the failure was emitted.
  • Purpose: surface machine failures to programs/SDKs in a stable, inspectable format with enough metadata for audit and replay.

Invariants

  • Failure kinds, origins, and diagnostics mirror machine failures; no additional failure semantics are introduced.
  • Integrity: inputs_digest must match the bound inputs at the time of failure.
  • Propagates through computer operators as alternate outputs; no state mutation beyond failure slot population.

Proof

  • Derived from: Machine Failure Carrier.
  • Shows that: carrying failures at the computer layer preserves machine diagnostics while enabling program-level handling.

title: Computer Recognition Carrier id: computer-recognition role: structure status: later derived_from: machine/structure/recognition proof_refs: []

Computer Recognition Carrier

Definition

  • Carrier: program-facing recognitions, derived from the machine Recognition Carrier.
  • Fields (concrete at the computer layer):
    • recognition_id (string/UUID): stable identifier for the recognition.
    • payload (object): typed content accepted by the recognised operator (schemas are declared in the wiring plan).
    • policy_scopes (array of strings): scopes that authorise subsequent use.
    • provenance (object {actor, origin, trace_ref}): where the recognition came from and which trace node it binds.
    • signature (optional string): signature or MAC over payload+provenance.
    • expiry (optional string, ISO-8601 UTC): validity window.
  • Purpose: expose recognition requests/results to computer/SDK code without leaking machine internals, while binding policy and provenance.

Invariants

  • Recognition data matches machine recognitions; no additional semantics are introduced.
  • Policy/provenance fields are required for any recognition that will be reused across steps.
  • Stable under machine validation; failures are represented separately via the computer failure carrier.

Proof

  • Derived from: Machine Recognition Carrier.
  • Shows that: lifting machine recognitions to the computer layer yields a carrier suitable for program consumption while preserving machine correctness.

title: Computer Snapshot id: computer-snapshot role: structure status: later derived_from: machine/structure/snapshot proof_refs: []

Computer Snapshot

Definition

  • Carrier: program-facing snapshot derived from the machine Snapshot.
  • Fields (concrete at the computer layer):
    • snapshot_id (string/UUID, required)
    • timestamp (string, ISO-8601 UTC, required)
    • state_digest (string/SHA-256 hex, required): digest of serialized state.
    • plan_ref (string, optional): wiring plan identifier in use.
    • bindings (object, optional): serialized bindings for replay.
    • provenance (object {actor, origin, trace_ref}, optional)
    • integrity_hash (string/SHA-256 hex, required): covers header + payload.
  • Purpose: enable programs to save/load state through the computer API without bypassing machine snapshot rules.

Invariants

  • Identifiers and contents match machine snapshots; integrity checks are enforced via computer operators.
  • Round-tripping through computer storage preserves machine snapshot semantics.
  • Integrity: integrity_hash must verify over the serialized header+payload.
  • Replayability: if bindings are present, restoring yields an evaluation state consistent with state_digest.

Proof

  • Derived from: Machine Snapshot.
  • Shows that: lifting snapshots to the computer layer yields a persistence carrier suitable for SDKs while preserving machine invariants.

title: Computer Wiring Plan id: computer-wiring-plan role: structure status: later derived_from: machine/structure/wiring-plan proof_refs: []

Computer Wiring Plan

Definition

  • Carrier: execution plan for programs, derived from the machine Wiring Plan.
  • Fields (concrete at the computer layer):
    • plan_id (string/UUID): unique identifier for this plan.
    • steps: map from step id to operator reference plus metadata (policy, retry, observability).
    • ports: map from port id to carrier type and direction.
    • edges: list of directed connections {from_port, to_port} forming a graph over steps/ports.
    • entry_steps / exit_steps: sources/sinks of the plan.
    • annotations (optional): policy, audit, failure-handling hints.
    • integrity_hash: digest over the plan fields to detect tampering.
  • Purpose: provide a program-facing runbook format that wires computer-level operators over computer carriers with explicit topology and integrity.
  • API formats (examples, implementation-dependent):
    • JSON Schema: ports typed by $ref to computer carriers; edges as array of {from, to}; steps declaring operator, inputs, outputs, policy.
    • YAML runbook: anchors for operators, steps keyed by id, ports: and edges: blocks, integrity_hash: at root.
    • Hashing rule: integrity_hash = SHA256(canonical_json(plan_id, steps, ports, edges, entry_steps, exit_steps, annotations)).

Invariants

  • Acyclic and well-typed with respect to computer operators/ports.
  • Integrity: integrity_hash verifies over the plan fields; any modification requires recomputation.
  • Refines the machine wiring invariants; every computer plan corresponds to a valid machine wiring plan.

Proof

  • Derived from: Machine Wiring Plan.
  • Shows that: lifting machine wiring to the computer layer yields a runbook format programs can author while respecting machine constraints.

title: Concurrency Program id: concurrency-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Concurrency Program

Program idea to explore and implement concurrency models using the relational computer.

Behaviour

  • Represent concurrent tasks/communications as recognitions.
  • Run scheduling/synchronization runbooks; enforce safety/liveness policies.
  • Measure performance/behaviour with traceability.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Conflict Report id: conflict-report role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-recognition proof_refs: []

Conflict Report

Recognition capturing detected overlaps/policy violations in a calendar schedule.

Definition

  • Fields: conflicting event ids, time spans, constraint/policy violated, suggested resolutions, provenance.
  • Carrier: refinement of Computer Recognition scoped to conflict diagnostics.

Invariants

  • References only existing events/schedules.
  • Diagnostics cite the violated constraint/policy.
  • Traceability: includes trace span where conflict arises.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Recognition.
  • Shows that: reporting conflicts as recognitions provides a carrier for resolution/stabilization operators.

title: Connection Graph id: connection-graph role: structure status: plan fields:

  • name: steps type: runtime-value description: set of step records {step_id, op_id, signature}.
  • name: ports type: port-set description: typed input/output ports derived from step signatures.
  • name: edges type: edge-set description: directed edges {edge_id, from_port, to_port, carrier_id}.
  • name: metadata type: runtime-value description: optional policy/discipline annotations for later checks. proof_refs:
  • operator-signature
  • port-set
  • edge-set

Connection Graph

Typed directed multigraph whose nodes are earned operator invocations with typed input/output ports, and whose edges connect compatible ports. It is the raw plan before acyclicity and scheduling checks.

Definition

  • steps: set of {step_id, op_id, signature} where signature is an Operator Signature.
  • ports: Port Set derived from steps, each port labelled with {port_id, step_id, direction ∈ {in,out}, carrier_id}.
  • edges: Edge Set of {edge_id, from_port, to_port, carrier_id}.
  • metadata: optional policy/discipline annotations for later checks.

Invariants

  • Port typing: each port carrier is drawn from its step’s operator signature.
  • Edge typing: each edge connects ports with identical carrier ids.
  • Step identity: step ids are unique; ports are unique per step.
  • Totality not yet required (multiple edges or cycles allowed at this stage).

Proof

  • Uses: earned operator signatures to generate ports and expected carrier types and to label edges with carriers.
  • Shows that: packaging steps, ports, and edges with carrier annotations yields a well-typed graph ready for further refinement (typing checks, acyclicity, scheduling) into a wiring plan; the graph is earned from operator interactions, not assumed from a base act.

title: Consensus Profile id: consensus-profile role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Consensus Profile

Fixed point of multi-agent dialogue updates where epistemic states agree.

Definition

  • Carrier: tuple of Agents whose epistemic profiles align on all stable recognitions after repeated Dialogue Update.
  • Condition: no agent can further refine beliefs without others agreeing.

Invariants

  • Agreement: epistemic recognitions coincide across agents.
  • Stability: further dialogue updates leave the consensus unchanged.

Proof

  • Uses: Agent and Dialogue Update.
  • Shows that: a dialogue-induced fixed point yields collective knowledge across the agent set.

title: Consent & Data Governance Program id: consent-data-governance-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Consent & Data Governance Program

Program idea to manage consent and data governance within the relational computer.

Behaviour

  • Represent consent policies and governance rules as recognitions.
  • Enforce policies via runbooks; track consents and data usage with traceability.
  • Integrate with policy/measurement stacks for compliance.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Construction Grammar Program id: construction-grammar-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Construction Grammar Program

Program idea to model construction grammar patterns as recognitions with explicit form–meaning pairings.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • ConstructionRecognition: schematic patterns (template + semantic/function).
    • ConstructionInstanceRecognition: specific use in an utterance linked to tokens/syntax/frames.
  • Topology/measurement:
    • networks of constructions per language/sociolect; links to stance/affect.
    • measurements: construction usage across domains; correlation with outcomes (persuasion/confusion/rapport).
  • Runbooks:
    • annotate_constructions(text_slice),
    • analyze_construction_usage(subject_slice),
    • generate_with_construction(agent_id, construction_id).

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Contacts & CRM Program id: contacts-crm-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Contacts & CRM Program

Program idea to treat contacts and simple CRM as recognitions in the relational graph.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • PersonRecognition: name/handles/contact info/roles.
    • OrganizationRecognition: groups/companies/collectives.
    • InteractionRecognition: meetings/messages/collaborations.
    • RelationshipRecognition: ongoing ties (friend/collaborator/client).
  • Topology/measurement:
    • social/affiliation/project graphs.
    • measurements: frequency/recency of contact, responsiveness, open threads.
  • Runbooks:
    • log_interaction, plan_checkins, review_relationships.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Context Adjunction Monad/Comonad id: context-adjunction-monad role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/context-adjunction-witness proof_refs: []

Context Adjunction Monad/Comonad

Monad and comonad induced by the context adjunction.

Components

  • Monad from flow ⊣ nucleus coincides with the profile nucleus monad.
  • Comonad from flow ⊣ nucleus coincides with the profile flow comonad.

Proof

Sketch: use the unit/counit from the adjunction (ContextAdjunction) to build the monad/comonad and identify them with the earned profile closure/flow structures.

title: Context Dynamics id: context-dynamics role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/context-dynamics proof_refs: []

Context Dynamics

The Context Dynamics is the stabilised fragment of contexts under the interaction of context flow and context nucleus.

Definition

  • Components: the Context Geometry (flow–nucleus compatibility) and the fixed points of that interaction.
  • Stabilised contexts: contexts unchanged by further flow–nucleus interplay, earned by Context Dynamics.

Invariants

  • Stabilised contexts are fixed under both flow and nucleus.
  • The flow–nucleus interaction preserves admissibility of contexts.

Proof

Refines acts/context-dynamics by identifying the fixed points of the context geometry. Because the act ensures compatibility of flow and nucleus, the resulting stabilised subset is well-defined and inherits the earned invariants.

title: Context Geometry id: context-geometry role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/context-geometry proof_refs: []

Context Geometry

The Context Geometry packages the compatibility between context flow and context nucleus into a single structure.

Definition

  • Components: the Flow Context and Get Context Nucleus operators, with the coherence laws earned by acts/context-geometry.
  • Relation: flow and nucleus commute on contexts; the geometry records this compatibility.

Invariants

  • Applying flow then nucleus equals applying nucleus then flow on contexts.
  • Contexts remain within the earned admissible fragment under both operators.

Proof

Refines acts/context-geometry by assembling the earned flow and nucleus operators into a coherent structure and recording their commutation law. The act ensures the required coherence, so the geometry is well-formed.

title: Context-Insensitive Program id: context-insensitive-program role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Context-Insensitive Program

Programs whose behaviour is fully determined by parametric semantics and thus invariant under all admissible reindexings.

Definition

  • Carrier: Judgment Terms or interpretations related by all Parametric Relations.
  • Property: behaviour unchanged under any reindexing in the index base.

Invariants

  • Optimization safety: such programs can be transformed/migrated without changing meaning across contexts.
  • Parametric stability: holds under earned modal/flow structure in fibres.

Proof

  • Uses: Parametric Relation to characterise insensitivity and Judgment Term as the program carrier.
  • Shows that: programs captured here are context-invariant by construction.

title: Context Renewal id: context-renewal role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/renew-context proof_refs: []

Context Renewal

Renewed context threading extensions along the trace, earned by RenewContext and ExtendContext.

Components

  • Renewal witness threading first extension back through the trace.
  • Extension propagation: bindings commute with renewal.

Proof

Sketch: RenewContext = (ExtendFirstContext ContextualizeTrace) records re-entry; (BindTrace RenewContext) states bindings commute with renewal. Iterate to propagate arbitrary extensions.

title: Context Seed id: context-seed role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/seed-first-context proof_refs: []

Context Seed

Initial context seeded from a trace, earned by SeedFirstContext and extended by ExtendFirstContext.

Components

  • Seed context anchored in Nothing.
  • Extension witness: bindings added by BindTrace stay attached to the seed.

Proof

Sketch: SeedFirstContext anchors the trace in Nothing; ExtendFirstContext shows bindings attach. Combine to define the seed context carrier.

title: Context id: context role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/construct-contexts proof_refs: []

Context

The Context carrier is earned from the act construct-contexts. It represents the contextual environment in which recognitions and evaluations take place.

Definition

  • Elements: contexts constructed by the act construct-contexts, equipped with the recognitions induced by context acts.
  • Structure: supports flow and nucleus operators defined over contexts.

Invariants

  • Contexts are well-formed per the construction act and admit earned flow and nucleus operations.

Proof

Directly derived from the act construct-contexts, which supplies the carrier and its admissibility conditions. This structure packages that carrier for use by operators such as Flow Context and Nucleate Context.

title: Cosmogenesis Program id: cosmogenesis-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Cosmogenesis Program

Program idea to treat “universes” (laws/constants/initial conditions) as recognitions and run cosmological simulations within the relational computer.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • UniverseBlueprintRecognition: physics stack, geometry/topology, constants.
    • CosmicInitialConditionRecognition: matter/energy distributions, fluctuations, boundary conditions.
    • CosmicEvolutionRuleRecognition: evolution scheme (acts/dynamics/integrators, coarse-graining).
    • UniverseRunRecognition: specific run with trajectories of observables.
    • CosmicObservableRecognition: expansion/clustering/entropy/stable structure metrics.
  • Topology/measurement:
    • Blueprints tie into relational physics correspondences; towers over scales.
    • Ensemble graph of runs for comparison across blueprints/initial conditions.
    • Metrics: law stability, structure emergence, anthropic measures; ensemble distributions.
  • Runbooks:
    • run_generate_universe_blueprint,
    • run_cosmogenesis_simulation,
    • run_compare_universes,
    • run_zoom_universe_slice.
  • Cross-domain links: paracosms/MUDs/space programs can embed slices; physics calculator/quantum/Stochastic stacks underpin the dynamics.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Coverage Runtime Type id: coverage-runtime role: structure status: plan derived_from: machine/structure/coverage-runtime proof_refs: []

Coverage Runtime Type

SDK-facing projection of the kernel Coverage Runtime Carrier exposing stable fields and failure signalling for programs.

Definition

  • Fields:
    • covers: persistent map of cover identifiers to cover arrows.
    • restrict: stable API for invoking restriction with deterministic results or typed failure responses.
    • overlaps: read-only view of cached overlaps and their freshness tokens.
    • matches: stream/log of Matching Family witnesses available for gluing operators.
  • Access patterns: all mutations go through transaction tokens; read operations are monotone and monotonic snapshots can be taken for program-layer reasoning.

Invariants

  • Backwards compatibility: field names and failure codes remain stable across kernel revisions.
  • Safety: restrict returns either updated sections or explicit FailureCarrier payloads, never silent drops.
  • Observability: snapshots expose provenance for auditing coverage propagation.

Proof

  • Derived from: Coverage Runtime Carrier by freezing field names, failure channels, and access patterns for SDK use.
  • Shows that: programs can orchestrate cover refinement, restriction, and matching/gluing without referencing machine internals directly.

title: Coverage Site id: coverage-site role: structure status: plan proof_refs:

  • sheaf-condition fields:
  • name: objects type: hypertensor-window description: windows/contexts forming the site objects.
  • name: covers type: hypertensor-coverage description: admissible covering families {Ui → U} with coverage stability data.
  • name: restrictions type: matching-family description: restriction data along cover arrows.
  • name: overlaps type: matching-family description: overlap objects and agreement data for matching families.

Coverage Site

Carrier turning the sparse traces of Relationlessness into a site equipped with covers, restrictions, and matching conditions that support sheaves and path gluing.

Definition

  • Underlying category: contexts/recognitions produced by the first deviations from relationlessness, with morphisms given by refinements or inclusions.
  • Covering families: collections of morphisms {Ui → U} that jointly detect the presence of relations on U; they express how absence is probed by local witnesses.
  • Restrictions: for each cover arrow, a restriction map sends a section over U to a section over Ui, preserving composition and identities.
  • Matching data: overlap objects Ui ×_U Uj ensure restrictions agree on intersections, providing the inputs to the Sheaf Condition.
  • Base change: pullback of covers along any morphism produces another cover, making the coverage stable under reindexing.

Invariants

  • Coverage stability: covers are closed under refinement and base change.
  • Restriction coherence: restriction along composites matches iterated restriction; identity restrictions are neutral.
  • Matching completeness: any matching family over a cover lives in Matching Family and can be glued uniquely when the sheaf condition holds.
  • Fundamental groupoid compatibility: paths in the Fundamental Groupoid respect covers and keep endpoints inside a chosen patch.

Proof

  • Derived from: SheafCondition plus the earned coverage/restriction data on contexts/recognitions.
  • Shows that: the coverage site is the structural layer supporting restrictions, matching families, sheaf checks, and path composition across local patches; coverage stability and overlap data are explicit, not assumed.

title: Craft & Making Program id: craft-making-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Craft & Making Program

Program idea to model making processes (cooking, woodworking, etc.) as recognitions for reproducibility and cross-domain links.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • RecipeRecognition / BuildPlanRecognition: steps, materials, parameters.
    • RunRecognition: specific execution of a recipe/build.
    • MaterialRecognition: ingredients, lumber, hardware, tools.
    • OutcomeRecognition: result with quality notes/photos.
  • Measurement/runbooks:
    • measurements: time, cost, success/failure, sensory ratings, durability.
    • runbooks: plan_build, execute_build, review_build (and cooking analogues).

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Creative Writing & Worldbuilding Program id: creative-worldbuilding-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Creative Writing & Worldbuilding Program

Program idea to represent stories and worlds as recognitions so narrative structure is inspectable and recomposable.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • CharacterRecognition: traits, arcs, relationships.
    • LocationRecognition: places, histories, connections to other settings.
    • TimelineRecognition: events, causal relations, alternate branches.
    • ThemeRecognition: motifs, questions, tensions.
  • Uses:
    • Keep story consistency (who knows what, when; what happened where).
    • Explore alternate storylines as counterfactual slices.
    • Connect fictional worlds to real data or MUD slices.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Critical Lenses, Affect, and Assemblages Program id: critical-lenses-assemblages-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Critical Lenses, Affect, and Assemblages Program

Program idea to represent genre, affect, power, and assemblages as recognitions so agents can respect cultural complexity.

Behaviour

  • Genre & discourse recognitions:
    • GenreRecognition: form/voice conventions; blend relations.
    • DiscourseMoveRecognition: local moves (hedging/asserting/joking, etc.) linked to text fragments.
  • Affect & stance recognitions:
    • AffectStateRecognition: emotional/energetic state (label/intensity/valence).
    • StanceRecognition: orientation (reverent, skeptical, ironic, etc.).
    • Measurements: affect time series by domain; stance–outcome correlations.
  • Critical lenses & power:
    • LensRecognition: critical lens (Marxist, feminist, queer, etc.).
    • PowerStructureRecognition: who benefits/harmed/invisible; actors/resources/norms.
    • LensApplicationRecognition: highlights/tensions from applying a lens.
  • Assemblages:
    • AssemblageRecognition: configuration of heterogeneous elements with binding relations and functions.
    • AssemblageElementRoleRecognition: roles elements play.
    • Topology/measurement: overlap/nesting/change; stability/fragility/power diffusion.
  • Cultural metrics:
    • RepresentationRecognition: who/what appears (axes tied to your vocab).
    • ImpactRecognition: assessed/experienced impact (stress/hope/etc.).
    • NormShiftRecognition: patterns suggesting changing norms.
  • Runbooks/agents:
    • analyze_with_lens, surface_hidden_costs, map_benefit_harm.
    • Agents choose genres/stances explicitly, run lenses before summarizing, watch representation/impact skew, and suggest rebalancing runbooks.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Critical Modality id: critical-modality role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Critical Modality

Modality whose collapse changes fixed-point structures at global scale.

Definition

  • Identifies modal operators in Modal Structure whose inclusion/exclusion alters fixed points or invariants.

Invariants

  • Sensitivity: small changes in the modality affect global fixed structures.
  • Witness: records impact on fixed invariants.

Proof

  • Uses: modal structure and fixed-point analysis.
  • Shows that: certain modalities are critical to global behaviour.

title: CrossFlow Exchange id: crossflow-exchange role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

CrossFlow Exchange

Law describing when two independent flow directions can be swapped (exchanged) without violating admissibility.

Definition

  • Components: two flow directions with commutation data encoded as shuffle equivalences and admissibility constraints.
  • Witness: recognition that exchanging the flows yields equivalent outcome.

Invariants

  • Commutation: exchange valid when flows commute up to shuffle/nucleus.
  • Safety: illegal exchanges detected by violation witnesses.

Proof

  • Uses: flow context and shuffle structure to encode exchange legality.
  • Shows that: multidimensional flows can swap when admissible.

title: Curvature Trace id: curvature-trace role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Curvature Trace

Witness of nontrivial path dependence: records holonomy around a loop.

Definition

  • Components: a loop in Directed Path space and its holonomy computed by Holonomy Operator.
  • Interpretation: if holonomy is nontrivial, the curvature trace records the deviation.

Invariants

  • Loop-based: tied to specific loops/shuffle classes.
  • Stability: invariant under homotopy of loops within the sheaf structure.

Proof

  • Uses: holonomy computation to capture curvature evidence.
  • Shows that: nontrivial holonomy can be packaged as a curvature witness.

title: Data Catalog Program id: data-catalog-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Data Catalog Program

Orchestrates cataloguing of snapshots, contexts, lineage, and usage. Wires storage, audit, and traceability recognitions into a catalog service.

Behaviour

  • Ingest snapshot metadata and validate against storage specs via Computer Recognition.
  • Build lineage graphs from audit/traceability recognitions.
  • Serve search/API with policy checks; emit telemetry.
  • Emit Computer Failure on schema mismatch or policy violation.

Invariants

  • Metadata matches actual snapshots.
  • Lineage is consistent with audit/traceability records.
  • Access is policy-controlled and traceable.

Proof

Refines the documentation interpreter by specialising wiring to catalog recognitions and lineage construction, keeping kernel failure semantics.

title: Dataview Query Language Program id: dataview-query-language-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Dataview-Style Query Language Program

Program idea for a user-facing query language/GUI to define views over recognitions (table/list/graph), grounded in our topology/traceability.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • QueryDefinitionRecognition: name/params/selection/projection/sort/group/view hints.
    • QueryResultRecognition: result snapshot with inputs/params/time.
    • QueryViewBindingRecognition: binds a query to a GUI panel or static doc.
  • Topology/measurement:
    • predicates over types/tags/relations/measurements/provenance.
    • metrics: query cost, frequency, staleness; suggest caching/indexing.
  • Runbooks:
    • run_register_query, run_execute_query, run_refresh_bound_views.
  • Cross-domain:
    • dashboards (tasks/health/finance), research/reading, MUD summaries, governance/policy audits; integrates with GUI workbench/static docs.

Proof
