  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Deception Profile id: deception-profile role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Deception Profile

Tracks divergence between an agent’s private and public epistemic profiles.

Definition

  • Components: private Epistemic Profile, public epistemic profile, and a divergence witness (recognition of mismatch).
  • Role: quantify or witness deception/strategy by comparing public claims to private beliefs.

Invariants

  • Divergence monotonicity: increasing mismatch increases divergence witness.
  • Consistency: when private = public, divergence collapses to neutral/top.

Proof

  • Uses: two Epistemic Profiles and recognition of their differences.
  • Shows that: epistemic mismatch can be encoded as a structure for reasoning about deception/strategy.

title: Deconstruction Program id: deconstruction-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Deconstruction Program

Program idea to make deconstructive reading explicit as operations on recognitions (binaries, traces, sliding meanings).

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • BinaryOppositionRecognition: paired terms/positions (nature/culture, center/margin, etc.).
    • TraceRecognition: presences/absences that haunt a text/discourse.
    • DifferanceRecognition: sites where meaning is deferred/unstable.
    • DeconstructiveReadingRecognition: pass over text collecting binaries/traces/différance.
  • Runbooks:
    • deconstruct(text_slice): produce a reading with binaries/traces/slippages.
    • compare_readings(text_slice, lens_ids): see how lenses highlight traces/binaries.
    • feed_back_to_agents: inform author/analyst agents.
  • Connections:
    • Links to semiosis, critical lenses/assemblages, newsfeeds/policy/contracts/MUD lore.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Defect Classification id: defect-classification role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Defect Classification

Classification of defects/boundaries in quasicrystalline hypertensor sections.

Definition

  • Defects: deviations in Boundary Recognition or nontrivial holonomy/curvature from Holonomy Operator.
  • Types: edge, corner, dislocation recognitions with associated invariants.

Invariants

  • Localisation: defects localised to boundary/defect region.
  • Stability: defect labels stable under allowed flow/gluing.

Proof

  • Uses: boundary and holonomy to detect/classify defects.
  • Shows that: quasicrystal defects can be recognised and typed.

title: Deleuzian Psychology Program id: deleuzian-psychology-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Deleuzian Psychology Program

Program idea to model desiring-machines, flows, and lines of flight as recognitions to explore desire/subjectivity in the graph.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • DesiringMachineRecognition: configurations (bodies/signs/machines/institutions) producing/channelling desire.
    • FlowRecognition: flows of desire/money/attention/affect/bodies/info across machines/assemblages.
    • LineOfFlightRecognition: movements that escape/reconfigure structures.
    • Territorialization / Deterritorialization recognitions for fixing/loosening patterns.
  • Topology/measurement:
    • networks of desiring-machines/flows; lines of flight connecting assemblages/domains.
    • measurements: intensity/direction of flows; stuckness vs creativity.
  • Runbooks:
    • map_flows(slice), identify_lines_of_flight(slice), assess_territorialization(slice).
    • Links to assemblage work and domains of creative/political/personal transformation.

Proof

  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Deployment & Upgrade Program id: deployment-upgrade-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Deployment & Upgrade Program

Wires kernel operators into an acyclic plan for deploying, upgrading, and rolling back modules while preserving earned invariants.

Behaviour

  • Validate artifacts and policies before rollout; capture pre-upgrade snapshots via Computer Storage.
  • Drain traffic, roll out new versions, resync state; monitor telemetry.
  • Validate topology constraints; run smoke tests; log traceability.
  • Emit Computer Failure on policy, telemetry, or integrity failures.

Invariants

  • No rollout without passing tests/policies and traceability reference.
  • Snapshots enable rollback; topology constraints are respected.
  • Telemetry confirms SLOs throughout the rollout.

Proof

Refines the documentation interpreter by specialising wiring to deployment tasks over snapshots and policy recognitions, preserving failure semantics and acyclicity.

title: Detailed Balance id: detailed-balance role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/detailed-balance-witness proof_refs: []

Detailed Balance

Structure expressing forward–reverse symmetry for a fixed flow/measure kernel.

Definition

  • Stationary kernel: Observation Kernel with Fixed Kernel Property and associated Fixed Invariant State.
  • Forward step F and reverse step R (earned by the detailed-balance act) act on observations/states.
  • Symmetry requirement: under the fixed invariant state, F and R have identical joint law; applying F then R (or R then F) yields stabilised equivalence.

Proof

Sketched from DetailedBalanceWitness, which enforces forward–reverse symmetry given a fixed kernel and forward/reverse steps. Under the fixed invariant state, this yields the usual detailed-balance condition and preserves the earned observation kernel and fixed-state carriers.

title: Developer Environment & Code Intelligence Program id: developer-environment-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Developer Environment & Code Intelligence Program

Program idea to represent codebases, editors, and development workflows as recognitions so the relational computer can act as an IDE/refactoring assistant.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • CodebaseRecognition: repo/project (languages, tooling, key dirs, links to specs/arch).
    • CodeElementRecognition: functions/classes/modules/schemas/tests with locations/types/deps.
    • DevWorkspaceRecognition: working context (open files, breakpoints, tasks, branches, test focus).
    • RefactoringPlanRecognition: planned changes (targets, effects, deps, rollback).
  • Topology/measurement:
    • dependency graphs (imports, calls, type refs, DB/API interactions).
    • links to architecture/spec/theorem recognitions.
    • metrics: coverage, build times, error rates, refactoring impact; developer flow (context switches, red/green cycles).
  • Runbooks:
    • run_index_codebase: parse/index code elements with topology.
    • run_suggest_refactorings: propose plans using code + spec/arch graphs.
    • run_track_dev_session: record dev sessions (edits/tests/decisions) for replay/analysis.
  • Cross-domain:
    • ties math → specs → arch → implementation; complements refactoring program.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Dialectics & Ideology Program id: dialectics-ideology-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Dialectics & Ideology Program

Program idea to model contradictions, mediations, and ideological formations as recognitions so the system can surface tensions and structural patterns.

Behaviour

  • Recognitions:
    • ContradictionRecognition: clashing recognitions/tendencies (values vs impacts, rights vs access, growth vs limits).
    • MediationRecognition: recognitions linking opposed poles (markets/law/norms/infrastructure).
    • TotalityRecognition: a slice treated as a whole (project/supply chain/media ecosystem/mode of production).
    • IdeologicalFormationRecognition: signs/practices that normalize/obscure/reproduce power.
  • Topology/measurement:
    • networks of contradictions/mediations across domains (news/policy/MUD/finance).
    • metrics: frequency/severity of contradictions; mediations resolving vs deepening tension; ideology vs harm/benefit correlations.
  • Runbooks:
    • surface_contradictions(slice), trace_mediations(contradiction_id), analyze_ideology(slice, lens_id).
  • Connections:
    • value/ethics, governance, markets, socio-ecology, narrative domains.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Directed Homotopy Simulation Program id: directed-homotopy-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Directed Homotopy Simulation Program

Program to realise relational directed homotopy: simulate oriented paths and higher cells using flow/nucleus operations and profile towers.

Behaviour

  • State representation:
    • directed space X as states with partial order/transition relation; each state stores stabilised recognitions.
    • flow operator advancing states along the directed relation.
    • nucleus operator collapsing states, commuting with flow.
    • profile tower tracking higher cells; each level inherits directed relation.
  • Inputs:
    • base directed graph/partial order; flow/nucleus definitions satisfying commutation laws; tower description (filters/disciplines).
  • Update procedure:
    • flow step: apply flow to generate successors.
    • nucleus collapse: stabilise and discard nucleus-equivalent states.
    • tower update: recompute induced flow relations; ensure level compatibility.
    • observables/reach: propagate observables; compute reachable components.
  • Invariants:
    • flow/nucleus commute; tower coherence across levels; reachability stable under further flow.
  • Outputs:
    • directed homotopy classes (reachable components), tower summaries, and diagnostics for commutation/coherence.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program simulates directed homotopy concepts atop the documentation interpreter without extending machine semantics.

title: Directed Path id: directed-path role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/acts/loop-hit proof_refs: []

Directed Path

Carrier of directed paths between recognitions/contexts, earned from the loop act; forms the morphisms for the fundamental groupoid.

Definition

  • Carrier: tuples (source, target, homotopy_class) where source/target are earned recognitions/contexts (from Recognition Carrier) and the homotopy class is traced by the LoopHIT act.
  • Structure: source/target projections; reversal is generally not available (directed).

Invariants

  • Source/target are stable recognitions.
  • Homotopies respect the directed flow; reversal may not exist.
  • Composition domain: paths compose only when target of the first equals source of the second.

Proof

  • Derived from: LoopHIT, which earns loops and directed path identifications.
  • Uses: Recognition Carrier as endpoints.
  • Shows that: directed paths form a carrier for composing flows/proofs and serve as morphisms in the fundamental groupoid.

title: Directed Space Compiler Program id: directed-space-compiler-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Directed Space Compiler Program

Compiles directed spaces, flows, and stratified sites for runtime and analytics consumers. Wires directed-homotopy and stratified-site recognitions into validated topology bundles.

Behaviour

  • Ingest strata definitions and coverage rules; validate directed morphisms via Computer Recognition.
  • Compute flow/nucleus coherence and tower consistency; emit diagnostics.
  • Export site bundles with traceability and topology metadata.
  • Emit Computer Failure on coherence or coverage violations.

Invariants

  • Flow and nucleus commute for each published stratum.
  • Coverage families remain closed under pullback/composition.
  • Exports are traceable to earned acts/specs.

Proof

Refines the documentation interpreter by specialising wiring to directed-space compilation while preserving kernel failure semantics.

title: Disaster Preparedness and Response Program id: disaster-response-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Disaster Preparedness and Response Program

This program treats hazards, assets, plans, and incidents as recognitions so preparedness and response can be audited and improved. It assumes the Relational Machine Kernel and the system carriers it lifts.

Behaviour

  • Define recognition types for hazards, assets, plans, and incidents, each with clear measurement channels (risk, readiness, coverage, unmet needs).
  • Map geographic, infrastructural, and social topologies so response routes and dependencies are explicit.
  • Provide runbooks (e.g. run_drill, activate_plan, review_incident) that connect preparedness and response into a traceable loop, recording outcomes and follow‑ups.

Intended operators

  • Ingest hazard and asset inventories into recognition carriers.
  • Score readiness and response time; flag coverage gaps against the mapped topology.
  • Activate plans, record incidents, and attach measurements and trace for after‑action review.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Discourse and Conversation Program id: discourse-conversation-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Discourse and Conversation Program

This program models speech acts, discourse relations, and conversational structure so the machine can track what people do with language.

Behaviour

  • Recognise turns, adjacency pairs, presuppositions, implicatures, and RST-like discourse relations as structured recognitions.
  • Trace conversations as sequences of turns with participants, topics, and phases (opening, body, closing), keeping provenance for later analysis.
  • Measure balance of speech acts, repair and acknowledgment patterns, and conversational equity (who is interrupted or ignored).

Intended operators

  • Annotate logs to produce speech-act and discourse-relation recognitions.
  • Analyse conversation dynamics to surface control of the floor and breakdowns.
  • Feed measurements to governance/support programs that depend on discourse health.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Distributed Relationality Program id: distributed-relationality-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Distributed Relationality Program

This program explains how multiple installations share one relational universe rather than diverging graphs. Recognitions are content-addressed; identical content under the earned calculus yields the same ID everywhere.

Behaviour

  • Treat each installation as a partial cache of the canonical relational space, with local annotations (measurement, policy, runbook history).
  • Synchronise by exchanging recognition IDs and neighbourhoods instead of reconciling separate ontologies.
  • Keep conflicts in annotations (measurements, policies), not in the recognitions themselves.

Intended operators

  • Pull/push slices of recognitions by ID/hash with provenance and trace.
  • Publish recognitions by hashing canonical content and optionally anchoring hashes to external logs.
  • Exchange installation stances (accept/reject/quarantine) as recognitions when installations meet.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Distribution Channel Carrier id: distribution-channel-carrier role: Channels carrying packets along compatible ports with trace/storage discipline. status: plan inputs: [] outputs:

  • id: distribution-channel-carrier name: channel proof_refs: []

Distribution Channel Carrier

Channels keyed by compatible interface ports that transport distribution packets along the trace.

Proof

  • TODO: fill proof.

title: Distribution Packet Carrier id: distribution-packet-carrier role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/structure/trace-carrier proof_refs: []

Distribution Packet Carrier

Definition

  • Carrier: packets tagged with source/destination Interface Ports, payload (intake observations/contexts), policy/trace metadata, and storage metadata for buffering.
  • Relation: compatible with Distribution Channel Carrier endpoints and scoped to a Trace Carrier.

Invariants

  • Port compatibility: packet endpoints match port types/policy regimes.
  • Trace alignment: packets carry trace spans consistent with channels/tasks.
  • Integrity: storage metadata matches payload for buffering/serialization.

Proof

  • Derived from: Trace Carrier to carry sequencing context.
  • Uses: Interface Port for endpoints and Distribution Channel Carrier for channel compatibility.
  • Shows that: packaging payloads with endpoints, policy, trace, and storage yields a distribution packet suitable for routing.

title: Distributional Coverage Site id: distributional-coverage-site role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/structure/parameterized-coverage-site proof_refs: []

Distributional Coverage Site

System projection of the parameterized coverage site where covers, restrictions, and matching checks are distributed across interfaces and policy-controlled nodes.

Definition

  • Ports: ingest cover descriptions, emit refined covers, and expose restriction services keyed by cover indices.
  • Tasks: coordinate overlap computation, schedule matching-family checks, and trigger sheaf gluing when compatibility succeeds.
  • Policies: control refinement depth, caching of restrictions, and admissible base-change operations when covers move between nodes.
  • Interfaces: message shapes for cover tables, restriction payloads, and matching-family witnesses.

Invariants

  • Distribution safety: every distributed restriction is traceable to the underlying parameterized coverage and respects refinement policies.
  • Liveness: matching-family checks eventually return either compatibility data or explicit incompatibility to avoid silent stalls.
  • Reversibility hooks: path-composition requests retain provenance so failures can be replayed or rolled back without violating coverage invariants.

Proof

  • Derived from: Parameterized Coverage Site by assigning interfaces, tasks, and policies for distributed execution.
  • Shows that: coverage, restriction, and matching logic survive distribution and are ready for kernel realisation and SDK projection.

title: Recognition Divergence Angle id: divergence-angle role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Recognition Divergence Angle

Angle-like measure between two directions of flow at a recognition.

Definition

  • Components: two flow vectors/directions at a recognition; metric from Relational Metric to measure divergence.
  • Output: angular measure of how flows differ.

Invariants

  • Zero when flows align; maximal when opposed.
  • Symmetry: angle(a,b) = angle(b,a) in symmetric metrics.

Proof

  • Uses: flow directions and relational metric to compute divergence.
  • Shows that: directional differences can be quantified angularly.

title: Divergent History id: divergent-history role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Divergent History

Detects two traces that share a past (nucleus-equivalent) but diverge under flow.

Definition

  • Components: pair of traces in a Trace Shuffle Class that agree up to a Time Slice and diverge thereafter.
  • Witness: recognition of divergence with provenance of the split point.

Invariants

  • Shared prefix: histories coincide up to the split.
  • Divergence: subsequent flow yields non-equivalent recognitions.

Proof

  • Uses: trace equivalence and time slices to identify common past and divergence.
  • Shows that: branching histories can be recognised and tracked.

title: Documentation Command id: documentation-command role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-recognition proof_refs: []

Documentation Command

Program-level command carrier representing a validated documentation rewrite or measurement task.

Definition

  • Carrier: derived from Computer Recognition, scoped to documentation actions (measure, split, link, derive, mark-provisional).
  • Fields: action verb, target docs, parameters (e.g., new paths, derived_from), provenance, and constraints.
  • Purpose: normalize planned rewrites/measurements into executable commands over the documentation workspace.

Invariants

  • Validated: commands must reference existing docs or declared new ones with proper layer/kind metadata.
  • Layer-safe: commands must not introduce upward edges or missing proofs.
  • Traceable: commands carry provenance for audit and replay.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Recognition Carrier.
  • Shows that: restricting recognitions to documentation actions yields an executable command carrier aligned with the computer recognition semantics.

title: Documentation Graph id: documentation-graph role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/structure proof_refs: []

Documentation Graph

The Documentation Graph is the system-level structure whose nodes are documentation artefacts and whose edges are their declared dependencies.

Definition

  • Nodes: documentation files participating in the relational computer:
    • acts in docs/math/acts/,
    • math structures/operators,
    • system/machine/computer/program structures/operators.
  • Edges: typed dependency relations (e.g., derived_from, uses, produces) declared in frontmatter.

Invariants

  • Each edge respects layer ordering (no upward deps).
  • Each node belongs to exactly one layer (act, math, system, machine, computer, program).

Proof

Refines the generic structure act to the documentation corpus, interpreting nodes as docs and edges as declared dependencies. Layer invariants follow from the linter-enforced rules.

title: Documentation Interpreter id: documentation-interpreter role: structure status: plan proof_refs:

  • recognition
  • evaluation-state
  • snapshot
  • wiring-plan
  • failure-carrier
  • call
  • evaluate-expression
  • map-evaluate
  • return
  • struct

Documentation Interpreter

Documentation interpreter over the documentation DAG: the minimal carriers/operators that execute nested-array transformations over earned math structures.

Definition

  • Carriers: minimal runtime state and data:
    • Recognition
    • Evaluation State
    • Snapshot
    • Wiring Plan
    • Failure Carrier
  • Operators (interpreter opcodes):
    • call dispatches nested-array invocations via dispatch-operator.
    • evaluate-expression, evaluate-with-bindings, and dispatch-expression interpret expressions relative to an evaluation state.
    • map-evaluate sequences evaluations over lists; do, if, and let provide control.
    • return, struct, identity, and last provide value construction and flow control.
  • API surface: the interpreter is defined entirely by these carriers/operators; no external primitives are assumed.

Invariants

  • Fidelity: interpreter executes only transformations encoded in doc frontmatter; no hidden semantics beyond the listed operators.
  • Closure: interpreter operators consume/produce only the declared carriers.
  • Stability: interpreter behaviour is determined by the documented DAG and interpreter operators.

Proof

  • Uses: the earned runtime carriers above.
  • Shows that: a minimal documentation interpreter suffices to execute the nested-array transformations defined in frontmatter without additional primitives; operators listed above are sufficient to interpret documented transformations.

title: Documentation Measurement Report id: documentation-measurement-report role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-evaluation-state proof_refs: []

Documentation Measurement Report

A Documentation Measurement Report is a program-layer refinement of the computer Evaluation State that summarises measurement results over a Documentation Snapshot. It is defined for any documentation corpus measured by the program.

Fields

  • Blocking counts: number of missing metadata/proofs/deps by layer.
  • Refactor counts: number of legacy/structural issues by layer.
  • Top destabilizers: ordered list of files with scores (blocking/refactor weights and proximity to acts).
  • Issue frequencies: histogram of recurring issue codes (e.g., missing layer, missing kind, missing derived_from).
  • Frontmatter conformance: per-file validation of YAML parseability and required keys (title, kind, layer, status, deps).

Invariants

  • All counts and scores are derived from the measured snapshot; no stale entries.
  • Ordering of destabilizers follows proximity to acts (higher priority printed later).
  • Report references only files present in the measured snapshot.

Proof

Refines computer/type/computer-evaluation-state.md by constraining evaluation data to documentation measurement outputs. The report inherits evaluation-state integrity (well-formed state that can be stored and reused) while specialising the content to measurement metrics.

title: Documentation Program id: documentation-program role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Documentation Program

The Documentation Program is a program-layer runbook over the computer kernel specialised to maintain and improve documentation corpora. It packages computer carriers/operators into an SDK-facing pipeline that accepts a snapshot, measures it, rewrites it, and persists transcripts. It is defined for any corpus that satisfies its carrier contracts; our repository is one such corpus.

Carrier

  • Plan: a finite acyclic wiring over documentation operators: Documentation Measurement, Documentation Nucleation, plus computer operators (Recognition, Evaluate Step, Storage, Wiring).
  • Inputs: documentation carriers (Documentation Workspace, Documentation Session, Documentation Command, Documentation Transcript, Documentation Style).
  • Outputs: updated documentation transcripts and Documentation Measurement Reports that cite every markdown file and its YAML frontmatter conformance; may emit Computer Failure via underlying operators.

Behaviour

  1. Load a documentation snapshot into the Documentation Workspace and Documentation Session; validate that every markdown file carries YAML frontmatter with title, kind, layer, status, and earned dependency fields (derived_from or uses/produces) that obey layer ordering.
  2. Measure the corpus (for example, via the linter) to produce a Documentation Measurement Report that scores missing frontmatter, broken links, and layer violations.
  3. Apply nucleation rewrites (split omnibus documents, tighten links) over the workspace according to the program wiring and documented commands.
  4. Store updated transcripts/snapshots and emit failures when the plan cannot progress.

Invariants

  • Plan must remain acyclic (inherits the computer Wiring Plan constraint).
  • Every operator invocation consumes/produces the correct computer carriers and may emit Computer Failure.
  • Documentation snapshots/workspaces remain well-typed carriers for subsequent runs.

Proof

Derived from: Computer Kernel. Shows that: constraining the computer operators to documentation measurement and rewrite tasks yields a runnable documentation pipeline over computer carriers, preserving kernel semantics while specialising to documentation corpora.

title: Documentation Session id: documentation-session role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-evaluation-state proof_refs: []

Documentation Session

Program-level session carrier binding a workspace to an evaluation context for measurement/rewrite runs.

Definition

  • Carrier: projection of Computer Evaluation State scoped to documentation tasks (pending measurements/rewrites, ready queue, failure slot).
  • Fields: wiring plan reference, ready tasks, bindings to workspace/snapshot, last failure (if any).
  • Purpose: drive measurement and nucleation steps deterministically over a documentation corpus.

Invariants

  • Task ordering: respects precedence derived from wiring plan and dependency graph.
  • Failure purity: failures do not mutate workspace; failure slot records cause.
  • Stability: session can be snapshotted and resumed via documentation transcripts.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Evaluation State.
  • Shows that: specialising evaluation state to documentation tasks yields a safe session carrier for repeated measurement and rewrite runs.

title: Documentation Snapshot id: documentation-snapshot role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-snapshot proof_refs: []

Documentation Snapshot

A Documentation Snapshot is a program-layer refinement of the computer Snapshot whose payload is an arbitrary documentation corpus and its dependency graph. Our repository is one such corpus; the carrier is defined independently of its contents.

Carrier

  • Corpus: the set of documentation files (acts, math, calculus, system, machine, computer, program).
  • Graph: dependency edges (derived_from, uses, produces) between documentation nodes.
  • Metadata: per-file frontmatter and link table ensuring every noun is bound to an earned doc.

Invariants

  • Snapshot is closed under links: every linked doc is present in the corpus.
  • Graph respects layer ordering (no upward edges).
  • Frontmatter is well-formed (title, kind, layer, status, deps).

Proof

Refines computer/type/computer-snapshot.md by restricting contents to documentation artefacts and enriching the payload with link and dependency metadata. The refinement preserves computer snapshot semantics (load/save, integrity) while narrowing the domain to documentation state.

title: Documentation Style id: documentation-style role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/sdk-surface proof_refs: []

Documentation Style

The Documentation Style is a program-layer structure composed with the Documentation Program. It constrains how any documentation snapshot is written so that every technical word can be unfolded into earned structures and operators. The style is generic; our repository is one instantiation.

Language and register

  • Use concise, direct prose; avoid rhetoric.
  • Prefer a single canonical term per concept and reuse it across acts, math, calculus, system, machine, and computer layers.
  • When introducing a new term, define it locally or link to the doc that earns it.

Vocabulary determinism

  • Terms such as “flow”, “nucleus”, “structure”, “function”, “context”, “recognition”, “trace”, “section”, “profile”, and “tower” are earned notions; link them to their defining docs.
  • If a term is provisional (no earned doc), mark it as such and plan to replace it with an earned structure/operator.

Syntactic vs semantic

  • Syntactic: about admissible derivations (lawful flows).
  • Semantic: about normalisation equivalence (nucleus classes).

Use these only with explicit links to the underlying math/theorem documents once those are atomised.

Frontmatter and linking discipline

  • Frontmatter must declare title, kind, layer, status, and either derived_from (single, prior-layer) or same-layer uses/produces.
  • kind matches the path (structure or operator).
  • Link every noun that names an earned object; avoid bare code spans.
  • Do not use depends_on; use typed deps enforced by the linter.
  • Markdown body must explain carriers/behaviour with explicit links; avoid placeholder stubs.
  • YAML must be valid and parseable; missing keys are blocking for measurement.

Git and history

  • Make small, coherent commits with informative messages.
  • Treat commit messages as recognitions about documentation rewrites.
  • Keep history aligned with the vocabulary discipline above.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer SDK Surface, restricting program-visible documentation artefacts to those that obey the documented style.
  • Shows that: enforcing vocabulary, linking, and YAML frontmatter rules over documentation carriers preserves kernel semantics while enabling deterministic measurement and rewrite.

title: Documentation Transcript id: documentation-transcript role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-snapshot proof_refs: []

Documentation Transcript

Program-level snapshot/transcript carrier capturing ordered documentation measurements and rewrites for replay/audit.

Definition

  • Carrier: derived from Computer Snapshot, extended with an ordered log of documentation commands and outcomes.
  • Fields: snapshot id/hash, timestamp, provenance, workspace digest, ordered events (command + outcome), integrity hash.
  • Purpose: support persistence, crash recovery, and replay for documentation evolution.

Invariants

  • Order-preserving: events are recorded in evaluation/trace order.
  • Integrity: hashes cover workspace digest + event log; verifies on load.
  • Replayable: applying the log to the referenced snapshot reproduces the workspace up to observational equivalence.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Snapshot.
  • Shows that: extending snapshots with ordered documentation events yields a replayable transcript consistent with computer storage semantics.

title: Documentation Workspace id: documentation-workspace role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/relational-state proof_refs: []

Documentation Workspace

Program-level projection of the computer relational state into a documentation corpus: files, links, dependency graph, and metadata ready for measurement and rewrite.

Definition

  • Carrier: projection of Computer Relational State containing documentation files, link graph, and frontmatter metadata.
  • Fields: file contents, dependency edges (derived_from, uses, produces), link table, vocabulary/act references, and per-file YAML frontmatter decoded into typed keys (title, kind, layer, status, deps).
  • Purpose: input/output state for measurement and nucleation steps.

Invariants

  • Link closure: every link points to a present doc; unresolved links are marked and surfaced as measurement issues.
  • Layer discipline: dependency edges respect layer ordering.
  • Frontmatter validity: files carry required metadata and parse as YAML; no missing or malformed keys.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Relational State.
  • Shows that: specialising the relational state to documentation artefacts yields a workspace carrier suitable for measurement and rewrite while preserving kernel semantics.

title: Domain Definition Program id: domain-definition-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Domain Definition Program

This program makes domain slices (finance, MUD, space program, quantum sim, etc.) first-class. Each domain declares its recognition schemas, runbook patterns, and handler bindings, all grounded in the earned acts and the Documentation Interpreter.

Behaviour

  • Define DomainDefinition, DomainSchema, DomainRunbookPattern, and DomainHandlerBinding recognitions, each with stable IDs, descriptions, allowed acts, and measurement channels.
  • Link domains to the acts and specifications they use, so each domain is a typed slice of the global instruction set.
  • Track coverage: which acts/specs are exercised, which schemas and runbooks exist, and where conflicts or overlaps appear between domains.

Intended operators

  • Register domains, validating that referenced acts/specs exist and schemas embed cleanly into the global recognition schema.
  • Introspect runtime bindings to discover or verify handler attachments.
  • Validate coverage so every public API/runbook lives inside an owned domain, surfacing gaps and collisions for refactor.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Dreams and Inner Work Program id: dreams-inner-work-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Dreams and Inner Work Program

This program represents dreams, inner-work sessions, and insights as recognitions so patterns and shifts are traceable without flattening experience.

Behaviour

  • Capture dreams, themes, sessions, and insights as structured recognitions with provenance, respecting privacy policies.
  • Relate dreams to life events and symptoms; surface recurring motifs and their intensity over time.
  • Measure mood, energy, and sleep quality as supporting annotations.

Intended operators

  • Ingest and tag dream journals and session notes.
  • Cluster themes and track their frequency and intensity.
  • Produce summaries for personal reflection or therapeutic support while keeping data local when desired.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Dynamics Orbit Fixpoint id: dynamics-orbit-fix role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/orbit-fix-layer proof_refs: []

Dynamics Orbit Fixpoint

Fixed layer of the orbit under OrbitStep, earned by OrbitFixLayer.

Components

  • Fixed layer OrbitFix such that OrbitStep(OrbitFix) = OrbitFix.
  • Minimality: least fixed point under the invariant order.

Proof

Sketch: apply EnsureFixedPoint to OrbitStep on the orbit layers; by the fixed-point construction, obtain the least layer stable under OrbitStep.

title: Dynamics Orbit id: dynamics-orbit role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/orbit-seed proof_refs: []

Dynamics Orbit

Structure capturing the stratified orbit of an invariant state along a trace, seeded by OrbitSeed and iterated by OrbitStep.

Components

  • Seed OrbitSeed from an invariant state (fixed reach + head trace).
  • Step OrbitStep advancing along the trace (fixed reach + tail trace).
  • Layers Orbit_n obtained by iterating OrbitStep from the seed.

Proof

Sketch: use FixedReach with HeadTrace/TailTrace to define the orbit seed and step. Show that iteration yields a stratified sequence of reachable states.

title: EarthSystem Flow Mapping Program (ERA5) id: earthsystem-flow-mapping-program role: structure status: flagship derived_from: computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

EarthSystem Flow Mapping Program (ERA5)

This program ingests ERA5 atmospheric/oceanic numerical fields and models them as earned recognitions, enabling relational flow analysis over real physical data.

Dataset (External Source Reference)

  • ERA5 Reanalysis (ECMWF Copernicus Climate Data Store)
    • hourly global fields
    • 0.25° resolution
    • multi-variable (pressure, wind, humidity, temperature, radiation, waves)
    • historically complete back to 1940
    • gridded, structured, and time-indexed

Note: this is metadata; the program treats numeric arrays as inputs to recognition operators. No external action or simulation is performed by the kernel.

Behaviour

  • Represent cells, regions, winds, fronts, fluxes, storms, and anomalies as recognitions with numeric measurement provenance.
  • Construct flow profiles for wind/radiation/moisture flux using measurement → flow induction.
  • Build nucleus profiles for stable structures (jet streams, Hadley cells, long-lived fronts).
  • Use trace carriers to track evolution of structures hour-by-hour or decade-by-decade.
  • Compute entropy maps, mixing measures, and profile invariants across spatial windows.
  • Perform window gluing to assemble global flows from regional analyses.

Intended operators (conceptual)

  1. recognize_field(cell_id, variable, value, provenance)
    Converts numeric atmospheric measurements into recognitions (e.g., T2m, u/v wind components, CAPE, surface pressure).
  2. derive_flow(window_id)
    Builds a flow profile from recognised gradients (wind flow, heat flux, moisture transport) using directional induction rather than numeric simulation.
  3. detect_nucleus(window_id)
    Computes stable structures (storm centers, jet stream attractors, pressure nuclei, cyclone/anticyclone cores) as nucleus profiles.
  4. glue_windows(w1, w2)
    Builds planetary-scale flow via sheaf gluing of overlapping windows; enforces consistent flow boundaries.
  5. trace_evolution(profile, time_range)
    Produces a trace mapping how profiles emerge, merge, split, or fade.
  6. stabilize_profile(profile)
    Computes flow/nucleus invariants (e.g., long-term climate structures vs weather noise).

Example outputs (relational, not numeric)

  • Earned recognition: “A North Atlantic storm system emerging from temperature gradients.”
  • Trace: “Storm nucleus persists 74 hours; moves 1.2 degrees/day NE; splits twice.”
  • Invariant: “Jet stream remains nucleus-stable across 40 days despite local shear variation.”
  • Profile: “Hadley cell intensity decreased relative to baseline 1981–2010 profile.”

These aren’t forecasts or actionable models; they’re semantic maps of real numeric data.

Invariants

  • Fidelity: numeric ERA5 data → recognitions without reinterpretation.
  • Stability: flow and nucleus profiles remain well-formed under time evolution.
  • Compatibility: operators refine the documentation interpreter; no new semantics introduced.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: numeric datasets can be represented as recognitions and processed through flow/nucleus/trace operators without extending machine semantics.

title: Edge Set id: edge-set role: structure status: plan proof_refs:

  • connection-graph fields:
  • name: edges type: runtime-value description: list of {edge_id, from_port, to_port, carrier_id} referencing ports.
  • name: metadata type: runtime-value description: optional policy/integrity annotations per edge.

Edge Set

Carrier for the collection of directed edges between ports, annotated with port endpoints and any integrity or policy metadata.

Definition

  • edges: list of {edge_id, from_port, to_port, carrier_id} referencing port ids in a Port Set.
  • metadata: optional policy/integrity annotations per edge.

Invariants

  • Typing: each edge’s carrier_id matches both endpoint port carrier ids.
  • Direction: from_port is an output port; to_port is an input port.
  • Uniqueness: edge ids are unique; no duplicate identical edges unless allowed by policy metadata.

Proof

  • Uses: Connection Graph edge data tied to typed ports.
  • Shows that: packaging edges as a set supports typing and acyclicity checks needed for wiring plans; edges are earned from the graph rather than assumed.

title: Effect Monad id: effect-monad role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Effect Monad

Definition

  • Base: Monad Fiber equips each fixed fibre with a monad action.
  • Reindexing: Monad Reindex transports monadic objects along base morphisms while preserving the monad laws.
  • Structure: assembles these into the indexed effect monad over the fixed-profile fibres.

Invariants

  • Reindexing commutes with the monad unit and multiplication; pullbacks of monadic objects preserve structure.
  • Functorial across the index base: identity and composition of base morphisms act as identity and composition on pulled-back monadic objects.

Proof

  • Uses: Monad Fiber for fibrewise monads and Monad Reindex for compatibility along the base.
  • Produced by: Monad Reindex, which constructs the pulled-back monadic objects used to assemble the indexed monad.
  • Shows that: fibrewise monads plus reindexing compatibility satisfy the definition of an indexed monad, yielding the effect structure used by parametricity and uniformity results.

title: Relational Email and Messaging Program id: email-messaging-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Relational Email and Messaging Program

This program treats messages, conversations, and notifications as recognitions so “inbox” views become relational slices instead of flat lists.

Behaviour

  • Define message, conversation, and notification recognitions with headers, bodies, attachments, participants, and threading topology.
  • Enforce intake policy: schema checks, identity/auth (DKIM/DMARC), spam/abuse checks, and provenance recorded in trace carriers.
  • Expose views such as Inbox, Priority, and Muted as queries over the message topology and measurements (latency, read/reply rates).

Intended operators

  • Ingest external protocols into recognition carriers with verified provenance.
  • Triage, label, snooze, or escalate threads into tasks/runbooks.
  • Generate digests and alerts using measurement slices and policy filters.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Embeddings and Learned Representations Program id: embeddings-representations-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Embeddings and Learned Representations Program

This program treats embedding spaces and their vectors as recognitions that can be inspected, compared, and related to semantics.

Behaviour

  • Define embedding spaces (dimensionality, training method, domain) and vectors tied to source recognitions (tokens, utterances, images, audio).
  • Track clusters and their alignment with semantic frames or symbols across modalities.
  • Measure distances, densities, cluster quality, and drift of embeddings over time with provenance.

Intended operators

  • Embed recognitions into chosen spaces with traceable configuration.
  • Analyse clusters and align them with semantic fields.
  • Compare or map spaces to keep embeddings interpretable rather than opaque.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Entropy Functional id: entropy-functional role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/entropy proof_refs: []

Entropy Functional

Entropy functional on a fixed profile, earned by the act Entropy (TraceProfile EntropyMeasure).

Components

  • Entropy measure H over the fixed profile (FixedChain).
  • Monotonicity under flow–measure evolution until the stationary chain.
  • Minimum attained exactly at the stationary profile.
  • In numeric semantics: proportional scaling by leading spectral ratio until convergence.

Proof

Sketch: Entropy uses TraceProfile with EntropyMeasure to define H on the fixed profile recognised by FixedChain. Show monotonicity under the earned flow/measure acts and that the minimum occurs at the stationary profile. In numeric semantics, derive the spectral proportionality from the leading eigenvalue.

title: Entropy Spectral Fixed Point id: entropy-spectral-fixed-point role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Entropy Spectral Fixed Point

Theorem-level structure stating that entropy along hypertensor stochastic evolution is monotone and converges to a spectral fixed point determined by the leading harmonic mode/eigen-spectrum of the observation/profile kernels.

Statement

  • Context: Hypertensor Stochastic Evolution over Observation Kernel / Profile Kernel.
  • Assumptions (stochastic setting):
    • Kernels are positive and conservative (stochastic transition kernels).
    • Detailed balance or entropy contractivity holds for the Entropy Functional (e.g., log-Sobolev or convexity along the flow).
    • Mixing Property and Ergodicity Property are earned.
  • Entropy monotonicity: along the evolution the Entropy Functional (e.g., relative entropy to the invariant measure) is non-increasing; strict decrease holds under the mixing hypotheses unless at the invariant state. Entropy production is bounded below by the Dirichlet form induced by the kernel.
  • Spectral fixed point: the limit state is the Fixed Kernel/Fixed Chain characterised by the leading Harmonic Mode in the Spectrum.
  • Uniqueness: mixing/ergodicity imply a unique Fixed Invariant State; convergence rate governed by the spectral gap (difference between the leading mode and the next).

Proof sketch

  • Monotonicity: Hypertensor Stochastic Evolution composed with Entropy Functional yields a contractive flow (entropy production non-negative); mixing/ergodicity make it strictly contractive unless already at a fixed point. The entropy production inequality yields d/dt H ≤ 0, with equality only at the invariant.
  • Spectral convergence: spectral decomposition of the Observation/Profile Kernel shows higher modes decay at rates set by their eigenvalues; the leading Harmonic Mode remains, yielding the Fixed Kernel/Chain/(fixed-chain.md).
  • Uniqueness: Mixing Property and Ergodicity Property force a single stationary state; entropy minimum attained only there.

Proof

  • Uses: Entropy Functional, Hypertensor Stochastic Evolution, Observation Kernel, Profile Kernel, Harmonic Mode, Spectrum, Fixed Kernel, Fixed Chain, Mixing Property, Ergodicity Property.
  • Produces: Fixed Invariant State as the entropy minimiser / spectral fixed point.
  • Shows that: along hypertensor stochastic evolution, entropy decreases and the state converges to the spectral fixed point; mixing/ergodicity guarantee uniqueness and alignment between entropy minimum and leading spectral data.

title: Episodic Trace id: episodic-trace role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/acts/complete-trace proof_refs: []

Episodic Trace

Recognised trace segment with contextualisation marks for persistence.

Definition

  • Components: slice of the earned Complete Trace annotated by Contextualize Trace to anchor entry/exit points for persistence.
  • Marks: save/load anchors indicating where snapshots can capture/replay.

Invariants

  • Contextual consistency: anchors respect trace ordering and context flow.
  • Persistence-ready: episodes can be handed to save/load operators without losing earned recognitions.

Proof

  • Derived from: Complete Trace with contextualisation from Contextualize Trace.
  • Shows that: trace segments can be isolated as episodes suitable for memory persistence.

title: Epistemic Profile id: epistemic-profile role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/structure/profile-topos proof_refs: []

Epistemic Profile

Profile whose recognitions are interpreted as epistemic states—what an agent or group takes to be possible, necessary, or ruled out—and equipped with the modal structure needed for epistemic operators.

Definition

  • Carrier: the fixed-profile recognitions inside the Profile Topos, tagged as epistemic states.
  • Observations: observation acts restricted to the profile (Observation); epistemic updates refine the carrier by intersecting with observed evidence.
  • Modal structure: inherits necessity/possibility operators from Modal Structure to interpret epistemic modalities.

Invariants

  • Closure: closed under profile flow/nucleus and modal operators; epistemic recognitions remain inside the profile.
  • Consistency: epistemic states respect observation compatibility in the profile topos; contradictions collapse to bottom.
  • Monotonicity: observations and modal operators are monotone on epistemic recognitions.

Proof

  • Derived from: Profile Topos, restricting recognitions to epistemic interpretations and importing modal structure.
  • Uses: Observation to scope evidence; Modal Structure to supply necessity/possibility.
  • Shows that: epistemic states form a stable subprofile suitable for knowledge and belief operators.

title: Equivalence Shock id: equivalence-shock role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Equivalence Shock

Sudden collapse of two previously distinct recognitions into one nucleus class after a perturbation.

Definition

  • Event: perturbation/flow causes recognitions a and b to become nucleus-equivalent abruptly.
  • Records pre/post states and the collapse point.

Invariants

  • Discontinuity: collapse is abrupt in flow parameter.
  • Stability: post-shock equivalence persists under further flow.

Proof

  • Uses: flow and nucleus behaviour to detect sudden equivalence.
  • Shows that: abrupt collapses can be identified and recorded.

title: Ergodicity Property id: ergodicity-property role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/ergodicity proof_refs: []

Ergodicity Property

Structure capturing ergodicity: time averages equal space averages with respect to the fixed invariant state.

Definition

  • Carrier: predicates tying a Fixed Invariant State to long-run averages along the kernel evolution, assuming Mixing Property.
  • Data: statements that for any observable, the time average along trajectories converges to the invariant-state expectation.

Invariants

  • Uniqueness: ergodicity implies a unique invariant state up to null sets.
  • Convergence: time averages converge to expectations for all integrable observables.

Proof

  • Derived from: Ergodicity, combining the fixed kernel with mixing.
  • Uses: Mixing Property and Fixed Invariant State.
  • Shows that: the earned ergodicity act yields a property carrier used in entropy/spectral convergence arguments.

title: Error and Repair in Language Production Program id: error-repair-production-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Error and Repair in Language Production Program

This program models slips and repairs in speech and writing so difficulty, sensitivities, and care patterns become observable.

Behaviour

  • Capture slips, self-repairs, other-repairs, and repair episodes as recognitions with context and provenance.
  • Link repairs to subjects, topics, interlocutors, and situational factors to see where language work is hard.
  • Measure frequency, location, and affective correlates (anxiety, excitement) of repairs over time.

Intended operators

  • Annotate conversations for slips and repairs.
  • Analyse repair patterns by subject/topic or participant.
  • Guide agent behaviour to repair gently and transparently.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Evaluation Context Stack id: evaluation-context-stack role: structure status: plan proof_refs:

  • evaluation-context
  • hypertensor-section
  • runtime-value fields:
  • name: head type: evaluation-context description: top frame to apply next (may be the neutral frame).
  • name: tail type: evaluation-context-stack description: remaining frames after popping the head; empty when stack depth is one.
  • name: length type: runtime-value description: derived depth of the stack.
  • name: frames type: evaluation-context description: ordered list of evaluation-context frames (stack).

Evaluation Context Stack

Stack/continuation structure for evaluating hypertensor sections; models evaluation contexts as transforms on sections.

Definition

  • Frames: Evaluation Context frames with holes.
  • Stack: ordered list of frames; head is the current continuation, tail the rest.
  • Transform: applying the head frame to a Hypertensor Section yields the next section plus possibly updated stack; length tracks depth.

Invariants

  • Well-formed: frames compose to a total evaluation on the section.
  • Determinism: given a section and stack, the next step is determined by the head frame.
  • Preservation: stack manipulation respects admissible flow/nucleus on sections.
  • Depth accounting: length matches the number of frames; popping/pushing updates head/tail coherently.

Proof

  • Uses: section structure to define evaluation frames and composition.
  • Shows that: evaluation contexts can be represented as a stack of section transforms for big/small-step semantics.

title: Evaluation Context id: evaluation-context role: structure status: plan emerges_from: seed-evaluation-context proof_refs:

  • seed-evaluation-context
  • build-evaluation-context fields:
  • name: frame type: expression description: partial expression/section with a distinguished hole.
  • name: plug type: expression description: plug operation encoded as an expression to fill the hole.
  • name: empty type: evaluation-context description: neutral frame acting as identity under plug/compose.

Evaluation Context

Frame with a single distinguished hole used to direct evaluation inside a hypertensor section. Generated from the acts Seed Evaluation Context and Build Evaluation Context.

Definition

  • frame: partial term/section with one hole.
  • plug: operation that fills the hole with a subexpression/section.
  • empty: neutral frame acting as identity.

Invariants

  • Neutrality: plugging into empty yields the original expression/section.
  • Closure: plugging any well-formed subexpression yields a well-formed section.
  • Composability: frames compose associatively where holes nest.

Proof

  • Uses: acts Seed Evaluation Context and Build Evaluation Context to generate frames, plug, and neutral elements.
  • Shows that: frames form the basis for evaluation contexts with well-defined plug/compose operations and a neutral element.

title: Evaluation State id: evaluation-state role: structure status: plan proof_refs:

  • task-precedence
  • wiring-plan
  • failure-carrier
  • snapshot
  • evaluation-context-stack
  • binding-environment fields:
  • name: plan_ref type: wiring-plan description: active wiring plan being executed.
  • name: ready_queue type: step-set description: steps ready to fire (dependencies satisfied).
  • name: bindings type: binding-environment description: name/port bindings to values/recognitions.
  • name: context_stack type: evaluation-context-stack description: nested evaluation contexts for expressions/sections.
  • name: in_flight type: recognition description: current step id and inputs or null when idle.
  • name: failure_slot type: failure-carrier description: failure record or null.
  • name: provenance type: trace-carrier description: {execution_id, actor, timestamp, trace} for this evaluation.

Evaluation State

Evaluation state carrying the active wiring plan, ready queue, bindings, frames, and provenance needed to drive single-step evaluation.

Definition

  • execution_id: UUID for this evaluation run.
  • plan_ref: identifier of the active Wiring Plan.
  • ready_queue: ordered list of plan step ids whose dependencies are satisfied.
  • bindings: Binding Environment mapping port ids to carrier instances.
  • context_stack: Evaluation Context Stack for expression-level evaluation.
  • in_flight: {step_id, inputs} or null when idle.
  • failure_slot: FailureCarrier or null.
  • timestamp: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of last update.
  • provenance: {actor, origin, trace} recording who/what advanced the state.
  • integrity_hash: SHA-256 over canonical JSON of the above fields.

Invariants

  • Acyclic order: every step in ready_queue has all predecessors bound; no step id appears twice.
  • Type coherence: every bindings entry matches the port’s declared carrier type; only failures occupy failure_slot.
  • Context coherence: context_stack frames are consistent with current expression evaluation; pushes/pops maintain stack invariants.
  • Determinism: selecting the head of ready_queue and applying the referenced operator yields a unique successor state or a failure.
  • Provenance: execution id/timestamps are updated on each transition; failures move into failure_slot, not hidden in state.

Proof

  • Uses: task-precedence to order steps from a Wiring Plan; Binding Environment and Evaluation Context Stack to evaluate expressions; Failure Carrier and Snapshot to surface errors and persist state.
  • Shows that: carrying the plan, queue, typed bindings, and context frames suffices to encode a single-step evaluation state; scheduling invariants follow from precedence and explicit bindings.

title: Evaluator id: evaluator role: structure status: plan emerges_from: evaluate-judgement proof_refs:

  • evaluate-judgement
  • evaluate-observation
  • evaluate-section
  • build-evaluation-context
  • seed-evaluation-context
  • ensure-confluence fields:
  • name: support type: hypertensor-section description: section/window being evaluated.
  • name: scope type: coverage-site description: locality/coverage in which evaluation proceeds.
  • name: context_stack type: evaluation-context-stack description: stacked frames directing sub-evaluations.
  • name: state type: evaluation-state description: bindings, queues, and wiring for step-wise evaluation.
  • name: recognitions type: recognition description: predicates/failures accumulated during evaluation.
  • name: flow_nucleus type: flow-nucleus-adjunction description: compatibility ensuring evaluation respects flow/nucleus on sections.

Evaluator

Evaluator is the earned transformation executor over sections in the quasicrystalline hypertensor topos. It combines evaluation contexts, wiring, bindings, recognitions, and flow/nucleus constraints to step earned transformations safely.

Invariants

  • Locality: evaluation stays within support/scope; restriction/glue on the coverage site preserves results.
  • Context discipline: context_stack obeys the evaluation-context acts (seed/build), ensuring plug/compose laws.
  • State coherence: state bindings and wiring match declared signatures; steps fire only when ready.
  • Recognition/failure: predicates/failures are explicit in recognitions; failures do not bypass invariants.
  • Flow/nucleus stability: evaluation steps commute with flow/nucleus actions on sections, maintaining earned dynamics.

Proof

  • Uses: evaluation acts (evaluate-judgement, evaluate-observation, evaluate-section) plus context acts (seed/build evaluation-context) to justify the context/stack/state discipline; coverage/sheaf acts to justify locality; flow/nucleus acts for stability.
  • Shows that: packaging support/scope, contexts, state, recognitions, and flow/nucleus compatibility yields an earned evaluator structure capable of executing transformations without violating sheaf or flow invariants.

title: Expression id: expression role: structure status: plan fields:

  • name: tag type: runtime-value description: head symbol of the nested array (literal|call|if|let|do|struct|return).
  • name: children type: argument-list description: ordered child expressions; for literals the head is the payload.
  • name: provenance type: trace-carrier description: optional {origin, timestamp, trace} for replay/inspection. proof_refs:
  • relationlessness
  • argument-list
  • runtime-value

Expression

Nested-array syntax tree evaluated by the documentation interpreter. Each expression is normalised into a tag plus children, retaining provenance so the evaluator can replay or inspect execution without hidden primitives.

Shapes (canonicalised)

  • Literal: tag = "literal", children.head is the literal payload, tail empty.
  • Call: tag = "call", children.head is the operator id, tail are operand expressions.
  • Control: tag = "if", children are predicate/then/else expressions (in order).
  • Binding: tag = "let", children are a binding map then the body expression.
  • Sequencing: tag = "do", children are the expression sequence.
  • Struct: tag = "struct", children hold the constructor id and field expressions.
  • Return: tag = "return", children.head is the value expression to surface.

Invariants

  • Well-formed: tag is a runtime symbol matching an earned operator or control form; children preserves the written order.
  • Canonical: every nested array is normalised into tag plus children (head/tail) with derived length; literal payloads live in children.head.
  • Typed intent: tags reference earned operators; children are expressions that will be evaluated to the carriers expected by the operator signatures.
  • Pure syntax: expression construction is side-effect free; evaluation effects live in interpreter state, not inside expressions.

Proof

  • Uses: nested-array documentation-program encoding to make every operator call explicit and inspectable.
  • Shows that: constraining expressions to tagged nested arrays, reified as tag plus ordered children, keeps evaluation well-formed and grounded in documented operators/carriers without hidden primitives.

title: External Integrations and Connectors Program id: external-integrations-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

External Integrations and Connectors Program

This program models external APIs and services as recognitions so integrations stay explicit, governed, and auditable.

Behaviour

  • Define integrations (service description), connectors (code/config), and sync jobs as recognitions with clear provenance.
  • Attach policy to each integration: who may use it, data-flow constraints, and obligations (auth, rate limits).
  • Measure request volumes, error rates, latency, and data freshness to detect drift or breakage early.

Intended operators

  • Configure integrations and bind connectors safely.
  • Run sync jobs on schedule or ad hoc with trace and policy enforcement.
  • Disable or quarantine integrations when measurements or policy checks fail.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Failure Algebra id: failure-algebra role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Failure Algebra

Math-level algebra of failures aligning with machine failure carriers.

Definition

  • Carrier: failure recognitions (errors, contradictions) in the Recognition Carrier.
  • Operations: join/meet with normal recognitions; failure is absorbing for operations that cannot proceed.

Invariants

  • Absorption: failure ∧ X = failure; failure composed with flow yields failure.
  • Propagation: failures propagate through evaluation unless explicitly handled.

Proof

  • Uses: recognition lattice to model failure absorption/propagation.
  • Shows that: failures form a subalgebra consistent with machine-level failure carriers.

title: Failure Carrier id: failure-carrier role: structure status: plan proof_refs:

  • relationlessness

Failure Carrier

Typed container for failures emitted by operators; carries origin, arguments, and diagnostics.

Fields

  • origin: operator/id where the failure occurred.
  • inputs: snapshot of inputs that led to the failure (if safe to record).
  • reason: machine-readable code plus human-readable message.
  • timestamp: when the failure was produced.
  • trace: optional trace/ref to the evaluation step.

Invariants

  • Failures are explicit; normal outputs are not mixed into the failure carrier.
  • Contents must be sufficient to diagnose and handle the failure.
  • Isolation: failure carriers do not mutate other carriers; they are passed explicitly along operator outputs.

Proof

  • Shows that: emitting failures through this carrier keeps failure paths explicit and type-safe across operators; this carrier is earned from the need to propagate failures alongside other carriers, not assumed from a base act.

title: Fiber Category id: fiber-category role: structure status: plan derived_from: acts/judge-profile proof_refs: []

Fiber Category

Definition

  • Carrier: for each context in the Index Base, the category whose objects are recognised terms, types, and judgements established by ProfileRecognitions.
  • Morphisms: judgemental morphisms between recognised objects that respect the profile’s typing and entailment rules.
  • Structure: inherits identities and composition from the judgemental data supplied by JudgeProfile.

Invariants

  • Every fibre is cartesian closed to the extent earned by the profile recognitions; morphisms preserve typing and recognised equalities.
  • Reindexing along context morphisms (defined separately) acts functorially between fibres, preserving identities and composition.

Proof

  • Derived from: JudgeProfile, which equips each context with recognised terms, types, and judgements.
  • Shows that: the judgemental data of the profile forms a category in each context; identities and composition follow from the profile’s typing and entailment closure, yielding the fibre categories used in indexed semantics.

title: Filesystem Adapter Program id: filesystem-adapter-program role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Filesystem Adapter Program

Program-level adapter that maps computer snapshots to host filesystem representations for persistence and restore.

Definition

  • Inputs: Computer Snapshot identifiers/contents.
  • Behaviour:
    1. Serialize snapshots to host filesystem files/directories with integrity metadata.
    2. On restore, read/verify filesystem content and invoke computer Storage to reconstruct computer carriers.
  • Outputs: persisted snapshots on the host FS or reconstructed snapshots; errors surfaced as Computer Failure.

Invariants

  • Integrity: filesystem representation must match snapshot hash/metadata.
  • Round-trip: save + load yields the same snapshot (up to earned equivalence).
  • Isolation: adapter never mutates computer state directly; uses computer storage operator.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Kernel.
  • Shows that: adapting computer storage to a filesystem target preserves kernel semantics while providing practical persistence.

title: Finance and Budget Program id: finance-budget-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Finance and Budget Program

This program models money and other resources as recognitions flowing through accounts, obligations, and plans.

Behaviour

  • Represent accounts, transactions, budgets, and obligations as recognitions with provenance and measurement channels.
  • Track cashflow, volatility, savings, and risk exposure as measurements.
  • Support scenario and counterfactual planning by running budget vs. actual comparisons as repeatable runbooks.

Intended operators

  • Ingest transactions into recognition carriers with policy checks.
  • Allocate and adjust budgets; schedule obligations and subscriptions.
  • Produce analyses (flow, risk, scenario) that can be audited later.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Fitness and Movement Program id: fitness-movement-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Fitness and Movement Program

This program represents movement as recognitions so patterns, training blocks, and recovery can be reasoned about.

Behaviour

  • Capture activities (walks, workouts, chores), plans/programs, and locations as recognitions with provenance.
  • Measure duration, intensity, heart rate, perceived exertion, and recovery to understand load and adaptation.
  • Link activities to locations and goals (e.g., training for a hike) for contextual planning.

Intended operators

  • Plan sessions, log activities, and review training blocks with trace.
  • Analyse trends in load and recovery to adjust plans.
  • Surface routes or venues that fit current goals and constraints.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Fixed Chain id: fixed-chain role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/fixed-chain proof_refs: []

Fixed Chain

The Fixed Chain is the stationary profile kernel chain earned by fixed-point iteration.

Definition

  • Built via acts/fixed-chain from the Profile Kernel Chain.

Invariants

  • Unique stationary distribution up to earned equivalence.
  • Inherits mixing from the underlying chain.

Proof

Derived from acts/fixed-chain; fixed-point iteration yields a stationary chain whose uniqueness and mixing follow from the chain dynamics.

title: Fixed Fiber Category id: fixed-fiber-category role: structure status: plan derived_from: acts/fixed-fiber-category proof_refs: []

Fixed Fiber Category

Definition

  • Carrier: the fibre categories of recognitions restricted to the fixed profile defined by FixedProfileRecognitions.
  • Morphisms: judgemental arrows between fixed recognitions that respect the fixed-profile typing and entailment rules.
  • Construction: inherits objects and morphisms from the general Fiber Category but intersects them with the fixed-profile recognitions earned by the act FixedFiberCategory.

Invariants

  • Closed under identities and composition inside each fibre; both are inherited from the underlying fibre categories.
  • Stable under the fixed-shape constraints imposed by FixedProfileRecognitions; morphisms preserve those constraints.

Proof

  • Derived from: FixedFiberCategory, which earns the restriction of fibre categories to fixed recognitions.
  • Shows that: the fixed-profile restriction forms a subcategory in each fibre, retaining categorical laws while enforcing the fixed-profile invariants.

title: Fixed Flow-Nucleus id: fixed-flow-nucleus role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/fix-flow-nucleus proof_refs: []

Fixed Flow–Nucleus

Structure capturing fixed points of the composite flow–nucleus modality, earned by the act FixFlowNucleus.

Components

  • Carrier FixFlowNucleus of points fixed by both flow and nucleus.
  • Inclusion ι_fn : FixFlowNucleus ↪ Carrier.
  • Stability: fixed under both flow and nucleus compositions.

Proof

Sketch: FixFlowNucleus selects points stabilised by the composite modality; show inclusion and simultaneous stability under both actions.

title: Fixed Flow id: fixed-flow role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/fix-flow proof_refs: []

Fixed Flow

Structure capturing fixed points of the flow modality, earned by the act FixFlow.

Components

  • Carrier FixFlow of fixed points.
  • Inclusion ι_flow : FixFlow ↪ Carrier.
  • Stability: flow ∘ ι_flow = ι_flow.

Proof

Sketch: FixFlow selects points stabilised by the flow act; show inclusion and stability using the earned flow modality.

title: Fixed Invariant Geometry id: fixed-invariant-geometry role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/fixed-invariant-geometry proof_refs: []

Fixed Invariant Geometry

The Fixed Invariant Geometry packages the flow–nucleus compatibility for the fixed invariant lattice, restricting the global geometry to invariant states.

Definition

  • Components: fixed flow and fixed nucleus acting on the Fixed Invariant Lattice, earned by acts/fixed-invariant-geometry.
  • Relation: flow and nucleus commute and preserve the lattice order/operations on fixed invariant states.

Invariants

  • Fixed flow and nucleus are monotone with respect to the invariant order.
  • Flow and nucleus preserve meet/join up to stabilisation.

Proof

Derived from acts/fixed-invariant-geometry, which restricts the global geometry to the invariant lattice and establishes the necessary compatibility laws. This structure records that restriction.

title: Fixed Invariant Lattice id: fixed-invariant-lattice role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/fixed-invariant-lattice proof_refs: []

Fixed Invariant Lattice

The Fixed Invariant Lattice is the lattice structure on fixed invariant states, with order, meet, and join earned by the fixed-invariant acts.

Definition

  • Carrier: Fixed Invariant State.
  • Order: induced by acts/fixed-invariant-order.
  • Meet/Join: induced by acts/fixed-invariant-meet and acts/fixed-invariant-join.

Invariants

  • Forms a lattice under the earned order, meet, and join.
  • Operations stay within the fixed invariant carrier.

Proof

Derived from the fixed-invariant lattice acts, which restrict the global lattice operations to the fixed invariant subset. The acts ensure closure and lattice laws hold on this carrier.

title: Fixed Invariant State id: fixed-invariant-state role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/fixed-invariant proof_refs: []

Fixed Invariant State

The Fixed Invariant State carrier is earned from acts/fixed-invariant. It represents states that are stabilised by a fixed profile of recognitions.

Definition

  • Elements: states whose recognitions satisfy the fixed-profile invariance conditions from acts/fixed-invariant.
  • Structure: admits earned flow and nucleus operators restricted to the fixed profile.

Invariants

  • Membership requires stability under the fixed profile recognitions.
  • Flow and nucleus operations on these states remain within the carrier.

Proof

Directly derived from acts/fixed-invariant, which provides the admissibility conditions and stability requirements. This structure packages those earned states for use by operators such as Compute Fixed Reach and Measure Fixed Invariant.

title: Fixed Kernel Property id: fixed-kernel-property role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/fixed-kernel proof_refs: []

Fixed Kernel Property

Property witnessing that an observation kernel is fixed under its own action.

Definition

  • Carrier: predicates asserting K * K = K for an Observation Kernel K.
  • Data: fixed-point equation and stability under iteration.

Invariants

  • Stability: applying the kernel again yields the same kernel.
  • Compatibility: fixed kernel respects the invariant lattice/entropy properties.

Proof

  • Derived from: Fixed Kernel act, providing the fixed-point equation.
  • Shows that: the act induces a property carrier used when checking mixing and ergodicity.

title: Fixed Kernel id: fixed-kernel role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/fixed-kernel proof_refs: []

Fixed Kernel

The Fixed Kernel is the stationary kernel earned by applying fixed-point iteration to the observation kernel/profile kernel chain.

Definition

  • Built via acts/fixed-kernel from the observation/profile kernel acts.
  • Represents the stationary transition rule for the profile dynamics.

Invariants

  • Unique up to earned equivalence.
  • Inherits mixing behaviour from the underlying profile dynamics.

Proof
