This program structures language learning as recognitions so progress and curricula are traceable.

Behaviour

  • Represent learning goals, lessons, exercises, and assessments as recognitions with provenance.
  • Link to skills, vocabularies, and curricula; track progress over time.
  • Measure practice frequency, mastery, and retention.

Intended operators

  • Plan and adapt lessons based on recorded performance.
  • Review progress and surface weak areas for targeted practice.
  • Integrate with other programs (conversation, translation) for applied usage.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Learning Curriculum Program id: learning-curriculum-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Learning Curriculum Program

This program defines curricula as recognitions so courses, modules, and dependencies are explicit and auditable.

Behaviour

  • Represent curricula, courses, modules, prerequisites, and outcomes as recognitions with provenance.
  • Map dependency graphs between modules and link to assessments and runbooks.
  • Measure coverage, mastery, and progression rates.

Intended operators

  • Design curricula and adjust based on learner progress.
  • Validate prerequisites before advancing learners.
  • Export curriculum slices for different audiences or domains.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Legal Contracts Program id: legal-contracts-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Legal Contracts Program

This program models legal contracts as recognitions so obligations, rights, and provenance are explicit and auditable.

Behaviour

  • Represent contracts, clauses, parties, obligations, and executions as recognitions with provenance and policy bindings.
  • Link contracts to related recognitions (budgets, decisions, assets, services) to trace impacts.
  • Measure compliance, execution status, and renewal timelines.

Intended operators

  • Draft, review, and execute contracts with traceable runbooks.
  • Monitor obligations and trigger renewals or enforcement actions.
  • Align contracts with policy and governance programs.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Lexical Fields and Thesaurus Program id: lexical-fields-thesaurus-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Lexical Fields and Thesaurus Program

This program organises lexical fields, synonymy, and contrast sets as recognitions to support semantic analysis and generation.

Behaviour

  • Represent lexical fields, synonyms, antonyms, and contrastive sets as recognitions with provenance and usage contexts.
  • Link lexical fields to domains, registers, and sociolects.
  • Measure usage frequency, register shifts, and semantic drift.

Intended operators

  • Build and align lexical fields; query contrasts for generation/analysis.
  • Track semantic drift over time or across domains.
  • Support other programs (frames, discourse, translation) with structured lexical knowledge.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Liminal Glitch Topology Program id: liminal-glitch-topology-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Liminal Glitch Topology Program

This program explores liminal and glitch topologies as recognitions, mapping transitions, discontinuities, and edge conditions in relational spaces.

Behaviour

  • Represent liminal states, glitches, and transitions as recognitions with contextual provenance.
  • Map where structures break, overlap, or mutate; record neighbourhoods of instability.
  • Measure frequency, duration, and impact of glitches/liminal passages.

Intended operators

  • Detect and annotate glitches in simulations or interactions.
  • Trace effects of liminal transitions on adjacent recognitions.
  • Use findings to harden or intentionally explore edge conditions.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Linearity Constraint id: linearity-constraint role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Linearity Constraint

Resource/linearity constraint for concurrent flows over hypertensor sections.

Definition

  • Scope: subset of a Hypertensor Section where flow operations must be single-use (no duplication/discard).
  • Relation: marks resources as linear/affine, restricting admissible flow steps.

Invariants

  • No duplication: linear resources cannot be copied.
  • No implicit discard: consumption must be explicit unless affine permission.
  • Compatibility: constraints respected under interleaving flow.

Proof

  • Uses: flow rules to annotate linear/affine usage.
  • Shows that: resource discipline can be imposed on section flows.

title: Linguistic Structure Program id: linguistic-structure-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Linguistic Structure Program

This program models linguistic structures (phonology, morphology, syntax, semantics) as recognitions so language analysis and generation stay grounded in the relational calculus.

Behaviour

  • Represent linguistic units and structures (segments, morphemes, phrases, dependency trees, semantic roles) as recognitions with provenance.
  • Link structures across levels (form↔meaning) and to domains (register, genre, sociolect).
  • Measure structure frequency, variation, and ambiguity.

Intended operators

  • Parse and annotate text into layered linguistic structures.
  • Compose/generate text from structured representations.
  • Compare structures across languages, registers, or sociolects.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Linked Data Graph id: linked-data-graph role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-recognition proof_refs: []

Linked Data Graph

Internal representation of external RDF/linked-data sources (e.g., Wikidata, Wiktionary) as computer-layer recognitions.

Definition

  • Carrier: refinement of Computer Recognition that stores RDF triples/quads plus provenance and mapping to internal ids.
  • Fields (computer layer):
    • graph_id (UUID)
    • triples (list of {subject, predicate, object, graph?} with IRIs/literals)
    • provenance (source URI, retrieval timestamp, hash)
    • mappings (optional): map external ids to internal entity ids
    • policy (access/usage constraints)

Invariants

  • Provenance recorded for each import; hashes/digests verify payload integrity.
  • Stable identifiers: external IRIs preserved; mappings maintained when used.
  • Policy compliance: usage aligns with declared policy/attribution.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Recognition.
  • Shows that: representing linked data as recognitions yields an internal graph carrier suitable for import/query/snapshot operations without extending kernel semantics.

title: LLM Interpretation Program id: llm-interpretation-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

LLM Interpretation Program

This program interprets large language model behaviour as recognitions so outputs can be analysed, aligned, and audited.

Behaviour

  • Represent prompts, completions, intermediate traces, and sampled paths as recognitions with provenance.
  • Attach measurements: likelihoods, entropy, diversity, error/repair events, alignment with value/policy recognitions.
  • Map failure modes and capabilities across tasks and contexts.

Intended operators

  • Record and trace LLM interactions; segment into recognitions for analysis.
  • Evaluate outputs against measurements and policies; surface anomalies.
  • Feed findings into policy, safety, and agent programs.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Local Rule Completeness id: local-rule-completeness role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Local Rule Completeness

Witness that finite local rules plus an aperiodic germ generate global sections (recognizable subshifts).

Definition

  • Components: Tiling Constraint and Aperiodic Germ guaranteeing unique extension to a global section.
  • Statement: any section satisfying the local rules is reconstructible from the germ; local rules are complete.

Invariants

  • Completeness: no admissible global section violates a local rule.
  • Recognizability: germ determines global configuration uniquely up to admissible symmetries.

Proof

  • Uses: Tiling Constraint plus germ extraction/extension (Extract Aperiodic Germ, Aperiodic Extension) to prove recognizability/subshift property.
  • Shows that: finite local rules suffice to determine the quasicrystalline section.

title: Logic Algebra Simulation Program id: logic-algebra-simulation-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Logic Algebra Simulation Program

This program realises the earned relational logic algebra as a finite working set for experimentation and verification; it does not introduce a new logic engine or additional truth values.

Behaviour

  • Maintain a finite registry of elements drawn from stabilized recognitions (Computer Recognition), plus a derived partial order for the working set.
  • Implement meet, join, implication, and negation via the earned logic algebra operations; verify the logic laws in the working set.
  • Keep invariants: antisymmetric/transitive order, absorption laws, top/bottom interactions, implication residuation, and double-negation monotonicity.

Intended operators

  • Initialise the working set from recognitions; compute closure under meet/join.
  • Compute implication and negation; check residuation a ∧ b ≤ c iff a ≤ (b ⇒ c).
  • Optionally evaluate against numeric semantics for debugging; emit proof/test logs for each check.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Kernel.
  • Shows that: this program simulates logic-algebra behaviour atop the computer kernel without extending machine semantics.

title: Logical Reasoning Program id: logical-reasoning-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Logical Reasoning Program

Runs logical reasoning tasks over earned recognitions. Wires evaluation and recognition operators to derive conclusions under policy and traceability.

Behaviour

  • Load premises as Computer Recognition.
  • Apply evaluation steps (proof search/normalisation) within the earned logic via Evaluate Step over a Computer Wiring Plan.
  • Emit derived recognitions or Computer Failure if obligations are unmet.

Invariants

  • Reasoning uses only earned operators; conclusions are traceable to premises.
  • Policy and topology constraints are respected.
  • Failures are emitted as Computer Failure.

Proof

Refines the Computer Kernel by specialising to reasoning wirings; proof shows preservation of kernel semantics and correct failure handling.

title: Logistics and Supply Chains Program id: logistics-supply-chains-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Logistics and Supply Chains Program

This program models supply chains and logistics flows as recognitions to make routing, inventory, and coordination auditable.

Behaviour

  • Represent shipments, routes, facilities, inventories, and constraints as recognitions with provenance.
  • Map topology of supply networks; track dependencies and bottlenecks.
  • Measure lead times, reliability, costs, emissions, and service levels.

Intended operators

  • Plan and execute shipments with traceable decisions.
  • Optimise routes and inventory against measurements and policies.
  • Audit disruptions and recoveries across the network.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Loop Space id: loop-space role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/acts/loop-hit proof_refs: []

Loop Space

Carrier of based loops (directed paths from a point to itself) with homotopy identifications from the loop HIT.

Definition

  • Basepoint: a chosen recognition/context x.
  • Loops: directed paths p: x → x modulo homotopy given by LoopHIT.
  • Operations: composition, unit loop, associator/unitor homotopies.

Invariants

  • Unit: identity loop acts as unit for composition up to earned homotopy.
  • Associativity: loop composition associative up to earned homotopy.
  • Directedness: reversal not assumed; if reversible, provide explicit inverse.

Proof

  • Derived from: LoopHIT which earns loops and their homotopies.
  • Uses: Directed Path to form loops.
  • Shows that: loops form a homotopy-algebraic structure supporting composition, unit, and associativity witnesses.

title: Markets Analysis Program id: markets-analysis-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Markets Analysis Program

This program models markets as recognitions so prices, flows, and sentiment can be analysed with provenance.

Behaviour

  • Represent instruments, orders, trades, participants, and venues as recognitions with provenance.
  • Link to external data (news, policy, macro indicators) without breaking relational invariants.
  • Measure liquidity, volatility, depth, spreads, and sentiment traces.

Intended operators

  • Ingest and normalise market data into recognitions.
  • Analyse market structure and dynamics; compare across venues or assets.
  • Trace how policy or events propagate through market measurements.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Stochastic Analytics Program id: markov-analytics-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Stochastic Analytics Program

Runs analytics based on relational Stochastic dynamics. It wires measurement, policy, and topology recognitions to compute transition metrics and forecasts.

Behaviour

  • Ingest Stochastic state/transition recognitions as Computer Recognition.
  • Compute metrics (steady-state, mixing, entropy) via measurement algebra.
  • Emit analytics results with traceability; emit Computer Failure on invalid inputs or violated constraints.

Invariants

  • Metrics are computed only over earned Stochastic recognitions.
  • Outputs are traceable to inputs; policy/topology respected.
  • Failures are FailureCarrier.

Proof

Refines the documentation interpreter by specialising to Stochastic analytics wiring while preserving kernel semantics and failure paths.

title: Stochastic Dynamics Program id: markov-dynamics-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Stochastic Dynamics Program

This program explores Stochastic dynamics over recognitions, connecting measured kernels, fixed chains, and evolution of states.

Behaviour

  • Represent transition kernels, chains, and states as recognitions with provenance and measurement channels.
  • Ensure row-stochasticity and consistency with fixed-chain theorems in the earned math.
  • Link dynamics to domains (physics, knowledge, policy) via shared recognitions.

Intended operators

  • Estimate and validate transition kernels from observations.
  • Evolve states under kernels; compute fixed points and chains.
  • Analyse mixing, ergodicity, and convergence with traceable results.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: “Markov\u2013Modal Fusion” id: markov-modal-fusion role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Markov–Modal Fusion

Fused stochastic–logical dynamics combining Markov evolution with modal tower traversal.

Definition

  • Components: stochastic evolution kernels acting over modal layers from Modal Structure.
  • Role: unified view where stochastic updates and modal shifts interact.

Invariants

  • Consistency: fused dynamics respect both stochastic and modal laws.
  • Normalisation: probabilities preserved; modal coherence maintained.

Proof

  • Uses: stochastic evolution and modal structure.
  • Shows that: combined stochastic–modal behaviour can be structured as a fused dynamic.

title: Matching Family id: matching-family role: structure status: plan proof_refs:

  • sheaf-condition
  • coverage-site
  • sheaf fields:
  • name: cover type: runtime-value description: family {Ui → U} in the coverage site.
  • name: sections type: runtime-value description: assignments si over each Ui.
  • name: overlaps type: runtime-value description: coherence witnesses for overlaps/triple intersections.

Matching Family

Carrier for compatible sections over a cover in a Coverage Site, built to feed the sheaf condition earned from SheafCondition.

Definition

  • Cover: a family {Ui → U} in the Coverage Site.
  • Sections: assignments si over each Ui whose restrictions agree on overlaps Ui ×_U Uj.
  • Overlap coherence: coherence witnesses for triple overlaps ensuring the pairwise agreements assemble into a simplicial compatibility.
  • Packaging: the structure stores the cover, sections, and compatibility data so gluing operators can produce a unique global section when permitted.

Invariants

  • Compatibility: si|Ui∩Uj = sj|Ui∩Uj for all indices with higher coherence on triple intersections.
  • Refinement stability: refining the cover produces another matching family via restriction; coherence is preserved.
  • Transportability: restriction along paths in the Fundamental Groupoid preserves the matching property so path composition does not break glueability.

Proof

  • Derived from: SheafCondition which states that such compatible families must glue uniquely.
  • Shows that: once covers and restrictions are fixed, the matching-family carrier captures exactly the compatible data required by sheaf gluing, making locality and coverage explicit.

title: MCP Endpoint id: mcp-endpoint role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-wiring-plan proof_refs: []

MCP Endpoint

Concrete wiring/metadata for an MCP (Model Context Protocol) endpoint exposed by the computer. Captures available commands, schemas, and auth requirements.

Definition

  • Carrier: refinement of Computer Wiring Plan restricted to MCP command dispatch.
  • Fields:
    • endpoint_id (string/UUID): identifier for this MCP endpoint.
    • commands (map command_name → schema ref + handler step id).
    • inputs/outputs: port typing for each command.
    • auth_policy (object): scopes/roles required per command.
    • integrity_hash (SHA-256 hex): covers endpoint spec.
  • Purpose: declarative description of the MCP command surface bound to the computer wiring plan.

Invariants

  • Every command maps to a step/port in the wiring plan and declares types.
  • Auth policy present for each command; defaults are explicit.
  • Integrity hash verifies against the canonical endpoint spec.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Wiring Plan.
  • Shows that: expressing MCP surface as a wiring-plan projection preserves plan invariants while exposing concrete command metadata.

title: MCP Request id: mcp-request role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-recognition proof_refs: []

MCP Request

Incoming MCP command invocation as a recognised computer-layer payload.

Definition

  • Carrier: refinement of Computer Recognition scoped to MCP requests.
  • Fields:
    • recognition_id (UUID/string)
    • command (string): MCP command name.
    • arguments (object): command arguments matching endpoint schema.
    • token (OAuth Token, optional): bearer/proof token.
    • provenance (object {actor, origin, trace_ref})
    • signature (optional string): signature/MAC over command+args.

Invariants

  • Command exists in the targeted MCP Endpoint.
  • Arguments conform to the declared schema.
  • Token (if present) is valid and matches required scopes.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Recognition.
  • Shows that: treating MCP invocations as recognitions keeps dispatch within the computer recognition model while adding concrete command fields.

title: MCP Response id: mcp-response role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-recognition proof_refs: []

MCP Response

Outgoing MCP command response as a recognised computer-layer payload.

Definition

  • Carrier: refinement of Computer Recognition scoped to MCP responses.
  • Fields:
    • recognition_id (UUID/string)
    • command (string): command name responded to.
    • result (object): command output payload.
    • observations (array, optional): streamed/logged observations.
    • trace_ref (string): traceability reference for the execution.
    • provenance (object {actor, origin, trace_ref})
    • signature (string, optional): signature/MAC over result.

Invariants

  • Result matches the command’s declared output schema.
  • Trace and provenance link back to the request and endpoint version.
  • If signature is present, it covers result and trace_ref.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Recognition.
  • Shows that: MCP outputs can be expressed as recognitions with concrete fields while remaining within computer-layer semantics.

title: MCP Server Program id: mcp-server-program role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

MCP Server Program

Program-layer assembly that exposes the computer through an MCP server, protecting commands with OAuth authentication.

Definition

  • Inputs: endpoint specification as MCP Endpoint; incoming MCP Request plus optional OAuth Credential.
  • Operators:
    • OAuth Validate to issue/validate tokens.
    • MCP Handle Request to dispatch commands.
  • Carriers: evaluation state/wiring from the documentation interpreter to execute handler steps.
  • Outputs: MCP Response or Computer Failure.

Behaviour

  1. Validate/issue tokens for incoming credentials using OAuth Validate.
  2. Validate MCP request against endpoint schema and policy.
  3. Bind arguments into the computer evaluation state and dispatch the handler via MCP Handle Request.
  4. Return responses or failures to the client; log traceability and provenance.

Invariants

  • Only registered commands execute; auth scopes enforced per command.
  • Failures surface as Computer Failure without mutating prior state.
  • Responses trace to endpoint version, request id, and evaluation trace.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: exposing the computer via MCP + OAuth can be expressed as a computer-layer program using earned carriers/operators without extending machine semantics.

title: Measure Carrier id: measure-carrier role: structure status: plan derived_from: acts/measure proof_refs: []

Measure Carrier

Definition

  • Carrier: measures/integrals over observables.
  • Source: earned from Measure; Profile Integral constrains integration to the profile measure space.

Invariants

  • Measure compatibility: measures align with the profile’s integral structure.
  • Stability: re-measuring within the same profile yields consistent measure values.

Proof

  • Derived from: Measure, which assigns measured values and integrals over observables.
  • Shows that: the measurement act induces a carrier of measures compatible with the profile-integral constraints.

title: Measurement Algebra Program id: measurement-algebra-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Measurement Algebra Program

This program organises measurement operations as recognitions so measurement remains algebraically sound and traceable.

Behaviour

  • Represent measurement spaces, observables, and combination rules as recognitions with provenance.
  • Ensure measurement operators respect earned algebraic laws (linearity, monotonicity, normalization) derived from the math layer.
  • Track provenance of measurement transformations and calibrations.

Intended operators

  • Combine and transform measurements while preserving trace and invariants.
  • Validate measurement pipelines against algebraic obligations.
  • Surface measurement coverage and gaps across domains.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Measurement Fabric Program id: measurement-fabric-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Measurement Fabric Program

This program treats the measurement fabric as recognitions that weave together intake, observables, and downstream analysis.

Behaviour

  • Represent measurement channels, schemas, and aggregation paths as recognitions with provenance.
  • Coordinate measurement flows across domains, ensuring units, scales, and normalization are explicit.
  • Record renewal/baseline checks and policy on measurement usage.

Intended operators

  • Register measurement channels and schemas.
  • Route observations through aggregation paths with trace.
  • Audit measurement completeness and renewal obligations.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Measurement Kernel Carrier id: measurement-kernel role: structure status: plan derived_from: acts/observation-kernel proof_refs: []

Measurement Kernel Carrier

Definition

  • Carrier: Stochastic-style kernels that transport observed measures along the earned observation flow.
  • Components: each kernel pairs an input measure with the corresponding output distribution induced by flow evolution.
  • Source: earned from Observation Kernel, which packages flow and observation into a transition operator.

Invariants

  • Kernel composition follows the flow composition law; identities act as measure-preserving kernels.
  • Measures pushed through the kernel respect observation coherence from Flow Observation.

Proof

  • Derived from: Observation Kernel, which establishes the existence of flow–measure kernels.
  • Shows that: the observation kernel act already furnishes the transition operators; enforcing flow-observation coherence yields a well-defined carrier of measurement kernels.

title: Measurements Ecology and Health Program id: measurements-ecology-health-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Measurements Ecology and Health Program

This program focuses measurement channels and practices for ecological and health domains.

Behaviour

  • Represent ecological and health metrics (species counts, emissions, canopy cover, morbidity, etc.) as recognitions with provenance and policy bindings.
  • Map metrics to places, populations, and time windows; keep unit/scale consistency explicit.
  • Track baselines, renewal checks, and intervention outcomes.

Intended operators

  • Register and ingest ecological/health measurements with traceable sources.
  • Aggregate and compare metrics across regions or cohorts.
  • Surface measurement gaps and policy-relevant trends.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Medical Data and Care Program id: medical-data-care-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Medical Data and Care Program

This program models medical data and care pathways as recognitions so clinical work is traceable and aligned with policy and measurement.

Behaviour

  • Represent patients, encounters, observations, orders, and care plans as recognitions with provenance and consent/policy bindings.
  • Link data to instruments, providers, and timelines; keep units and coding schemes explicit.
  • Measure outcomes, adherence, and safety signals.

Intended operators

  • Ingest clinical data with validation and policy checks.
  • Plan and update care pathways; track outcomes and adverse events.
  • Export auditable slices for research or coordination while preserving provenance and policy constraints.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Memory Carrier id: memory-carrier role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Memory Carrier

Carrier of persistable episodes: contexts, recognitions, and trace tails that can be saved and reloaded.

Definition

  • Components: Persistent Context Carrier, Persistent Recognition Carrier, and Episodic Trace.
  • Structure: packages the data needed to restore a saved episode faithfully.

Invariants

  • Reload fidelity: loading the memory yields nucleus-equivalent behaviour to the saved episode.
  • Baseline stability: preserved tails and baselines remain intact across save/load.

Proof

  • Uses: persistent carriers and episodic traces to assemble a reloadable snapshot.
  • Shows that: episodes can be persisted and restored without violating earned recognitions/contexts.

title: Message Channel id: message-channel role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Message Channel

Carrier for messages as traced observations between agents.

Definition

  • Components: per-edge Context, message traces as Observations, and ordering/causality constraints along the channel.
  • Structure: source/target contexts, buffer of in-flight observations, and admissible observation witnesses.

Invariants

  • Causality: message order respects channel context; no time-travel on a single channel.
  • Integrity: observations carry provenance; partial traces remain consistent.
  • Compatibility: channel restrictions/pullbacks preserve observation witnesses.

Proof

  • Uses: Context and Observation to define the channel.
  • Shows that: message exchange can be modelled as recognised observations along structured contexts, supporting social flow and distributed snapshots.

title: Metaphor and Figurative Language Program id: metaphor-figurative-language-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Metaphor and Figurative Language Program

This program models metaphors and figurative language as recognitions to keep their mappings and effects explicit.

Behaviour

  • Represent source/target mappings, frames, and figurative constructions as recognitions with context and provenance.
  • Link metaphors to domains and discourse settings; track how they shape interpretation.
  • Measure prevalence, spread, and shifts of figurative patterns.

Intended operators

  • Annotate text for metaphor/figurative structures.
  • Compare metaphor usage across domains or audiences.
  • Support generation/translation that respects figurative intent.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Micro Rituals and Everyday Magic Program id: micro-rituals-everyday-magic-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Micro Rituals and Everyday Magic Program

This program captures small rituals and everyday acts of meaning as recognitions so they can be nurtured and related to other practices.

Behaviour

  • Represent rituals, contexts, participants, and intentions as recognitions with provenance and policy (consent/boundary) bindings.
  • Link rituals to moods, events, or domains; observe how they propagate or anchor experience.
  • Measure frequency, felt impact, and change over time.

Intended operators

  • Record and schedule rituals; relate them to well-being measures.
  • Share or adapt rituals across contexts with explicit consent.
  • Reflect on which rituals sustain or destabilize desired states.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Mixing Property id: mixing-property role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/mixing proof_refs: []

Mixing Property

Structure capturing the mixing property of a flow/observation kernel: long-time evolution makes states asymptotically independent of initial conditions.

Definition

  • Carrier: predicates on Observation Kernel (and its iterates) stating that correlations decay to zero as time progresses.
  • Data: mixing rate or bounds (when earned) and the sigma-algebra/recognition tower over which independence is measured.

Invariants

  • Decay: correlations of observables composed with the kernel converge to the product of expectations.
  • Stability: preserved under iteration of the observation kernel; compatible with the profile tower when lifted.

Proof

  • Derived from: Mixing act, which witnesses decay of correlations under the kernel.
  • Uses: Observation Kernel as the underlying dynamic.
  • Shows that: the mixing act induces a well-defined property carrier for kernels used by entropy and spectral fixed-point arguments.

title: Mobile Devices and Sensors Program id: mobile-devices-sensors-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Mobile Devices and Sensors Program

This program treats data from mobile devices and sensors as recognitions so environmental context is traceable and policy-aware.

Behaviour

  • Represent devices, sensors, readings, and contexts (location, activity) as recognitions with provenance.
  • Handle calibration, sampling rates, and privacy/policy constraints explicitly in trace carriers.
  • Link sensor data to other programs (fitness, home automation, mobility).

Intended operators

  • Ingest and calibrate sensor streams with validation.
  • Aggregate and align sensor data with contextual recognitions.
  • Enforce privacy/policy before sharing or acting on sensor readings.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Modal Drift id: modal-drift role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Modal Drift

Gradual shift of a recognition’s modal status under long-term flow.

Definition

  • Components: track changes in modal layer membership over flow iterations.
  • Context: based on Modal Structure and Flow Context.

Invariants

  • Drift rate: measurable change per flow step.
  • Stabilisation: drift may converge to a modal fixed point.

Proof

  • Uses: modal operators and flow dynamics.
  • Shows that: modal status can drift and be quantified.

title: Modal Logic Program id: modal-logic-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Modal Logic Program

This program realises earned modal structures as a working modal logic for recognitions.

Behaviour

  • Represent modal frames, accessibility relations, and modal judgments as recognitions with provenance.
  • Ensure operations respect the earned modal structure from the math layer.
  • Measure proof properties (depth, branching) in modal derivations.

Intended operators

  • Form and check modal judgments; navigate accessibility relations.
  • Translate between modal and non-modal views where justified.
  • Export modal proof traces for other reasoning programs.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program evaluates modal logic over the documentation interpreter without extending machine semantics.

title: Modal Reasoning Program id: modal-reasoning-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Modal Reasoning Program

Runs modal reasoning (necessity/possibility) over earned recognitions. Extends the logical reasoning program with modality-specific operators and constraints.

Behaviour

  • Load modal premises as Computer Recognition with modal annotations.
  • Apply evaluation steps that respect modal rules and topology/policy scopes via Evaluate Step over a Computer Wiring Plan.
  • Emit derived modal recognitions or Computer Failure if obligations fail.

Invariants

  • Modal conclusions are traceable to premises and respect modal rules.
  • Policy and topology constraints are enforced.
  • Failures are emitted as FailureCarrier.

Proof

Refines the documentation interpreter by specialising to modal reasoning wirings, preserving kernel semantics and failure handling.

title: Modal Structure id: modal-structure role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/box-modality proof_refs: []

Modal Structure

The Modal Structure equips the recognition state space with modal operators and their iterated tower.

Definition

  • States: stabilised recognitions over the Recognition Carrier.
  • Modal operators: Box, Diamond from Box Modality and Diamond Modality; additional modalities (truncation/irrational/quadratic) from their acts.
  • Tower: iterated modalities via Modality Tower.

Invariants

  • Modal operators are monotone and preserve finite meets/joins of the Relational Logic Algebra.
  • Adjunctions (if earned) between modal pairs (e.g., box/diamond) hold fiberwise.
  • Towers iterate modalities coherently; tower levels commute with reindexing along contexts.

Proof

Derived from the modal acts; operators extend the logic algebra, and the tower act builds iterated applications. Compatibility with the algebra follows from the earned acts.

title: Modality Tower Program id: modality-tower-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Modality Tower Program

This program explores towers of modalities as recognitions, tracking their interactions and constraints.

Behaviour

  • Represent modality layers and their accessibility/interaction relations as recognitions with provenance.
  • Link towers to the earned modality-tower structure; ensure constraints are respected in use.
  • Map how modalities compose or restrict each other across domains.

Intended operators

  • Build and inspect modality towers for a domain.
  • Check interactions/commutations between modalities.
  • Use tower structure to guide allowable operations in downstream programs.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program explores modality towers atop the documentation interpreter without extending machine semantics.

title: Modality Tower id: modality-tower role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/modality-tower proof_refs: []

Modality Tower

The Modality Tower is the tower induced by repeated application of modal operators (e.g., □, ◇) over a Profile Tower.

Definition

  • Levels: modal layers applied to profile tower cells as earned by acts/modality-tower.
  • Structure: face/degeneracy maps respect modal structure.

Invariants

  • Modal application preserves tower composition laws.
  • Each modal level remains compatible with the underlying profile tower.

Proof

Derived from acts/modality-tower, which constructs modal layers over the profile tower with the required simplicial/coherence data.

title: Module id: module role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/operator/reindexing proof_refs: []

Module

Definition

  • Carrier: objects in each Fiber Category that remain invariant under the lifted reindexing action across the Index Base.
  • Morphisms: fibre morphisms between such objects that commute with reindexing along every base morphism.
  • Parameters: the reindexing operator and the underlying fibre categories.

Invariants

  • Reindexing-invariant: pulling a module along any base morphism yields an isomorphic module in the target fibre.
  • Closed under composition and identities within each fibre, respecting the lifted reindexing functoriality.

Proof

  • Derived from: Reindexing, using its functorial pullback behaviour as the constraint that defines invariance.
  • Shows that: demanding objects/morphisms to be stable under reindexing produces a well-defined subcategory in each fibre, giving the module structure used by parametricity.

title: Monad Fiber id: monad-fiber role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/structure/fixed-fiber-category proof_refs: []

Monad Fiber

Definition

  • Carrier: for each fixed context, the fibre category from Fixed Fiber Category equipped with a pointwise monad action induced by the fixed-profile nucleus (parameter).
  • Morphisms: fibre morphisms that commute with the monad unit and multiplication in each fixed context.
  • Parameters: a fixed-profile nucleus supplying the monad structure across fibres.

Invariants

  • Functorial in contexts: reindexing between fixed fibres (defined separately) preserves the monad action.
  • Monad laws (unit and associativity) hold pointwise in every fibre, inherited from the fixed-profile nucleus.

Proof

  • Derived from: Fixed Fiber Category, adding the parameter of a fixed-profile nucleus to equip each fibre with a monad.
  • Shows that: each fixed fibre supports a coherent monad action, giving an indexed monad over the fixed-profile fibres.

title: Monitoring and Audit Program id: monitoring-audit-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Monitoring and Audit Program

The Monitoring and Audit Program is a computer-layer refinement of the documentation interpreter. It assembles recognition, evaluation, and storage operators into an acyclic plan that ingests telemetry, evaluates invariants, records audit trails, and exposes traceable dashboards. Any deployment of monitoring/audit services should instantiate this program over the kernel.

Carrier

  • Wiring plan: finite acyclic wiring of monitoring/audit operators over computer carriers (recognition, evaluation, failure, snapshots) using Computer Wiring Plan.
  • Telemetry inputs: streams of observables conforming to measurement schemas; carried as Computer Recognition.
  • Audit outputs: persisted recognitions and snapshots that record tamper-evident audit events via Computer Snapshot.
  • Failure carriers: emitted when schemas are invalid or invariants fail as Computer Failure.

Behaviour

  1. Ingest telemetry (measurement fabric, introspection hooks) into recognition carriers and validate schemas.
  2. Evaluate invariants (expectations, thresholds) and raise alerts when violated.
  3. Persist audit events and traceability references for all alerts/actions.
  4. Emit dashboards/feeds that are policy-gated and traceable.
  5. Emit Computer Failure on schema mismatch, policy violation, or wiring error.

Invariants

  • Telemetry is immutable and schema-checked; invalid data yields failure.
  • Alerts cite the traceability nodes and acts/specs whose invariants failed.
  • Dashboards and APIs are policy-controlled; unauthorized access attempts are logged and surfaced via audit/telemetry.

Proof

Refines the documentation interpreter by specialising the operator set to monitoring/audit functions and constraining ports to telemetry/audit carriers. The refinement preserves acyclicity and failure semantics while ensuring every success path maintains the monitoring and audit invariants and every error path produces a Computer Failure.

title: MUD Command id: mud-command role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-recognition proof_refs: []

MUD Command

Program-level command carrier representing a parsed, validated action request from a session.

Definition

  • Carrier: derived from Computer Recognition, but scoped to the MUD command grammar (verbs, targets, adverbs).
  • Fields: verb (move/look/say/etc.), arguments (targets/directions), session context (player/entity reference), metadata (timestamp, provenance).
  • Purpose: normalize player/NPC input into recognised actions executable over the MUD world.

Invariants

  • Validated: commands must be syntactically well-formed and refer to reachable entities/rooms.
  • Authorised: commands respect session permissions (admin/player).
  • Traceable: includes provenance for auditing and transcript replay.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Recognition Carrier.
  • Shows that: restricting recognitions to the MUD command grammar yields an executable action carrier aligned with the computer recognition semantics.

title: Relational MUD Server Program id: mud-server-program role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Relational MUD Server Program

Program-level packaging of the kernel into a runnable multi-user dungeon server. It wires computer-layer carriers and operators into a persistent, multi-session world with telnet-like I/O.

Definition

  • Scope: single shard/world served over line-oriented clients (telnet/WebSocket frontends map onto the same session carrier).
  • Carriers: uses program-level structures (MUD World State, MUD Session, MUD Command, MUD Transcript) derived from computer carriers.
  • Operators: composes program operators (Accept Connection, Parse Command, Execute Command, Persist World) which refine the computer API.
  • Runbook: a Computer Wiring Plan wired to expose network ingress, command parsing/execution, and persistence over the documentation interpreter.

Invariants

  • All state transitions flow through computer operators; no bypass of machine semantics.
  • Sessions are isolated; shared world updates respect the evaluation/trace ordering earned in the computer layer.
  • Persistence uses computer snapshots; crash recovery replays from the last persisted snapshot plus transcripts.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: instantiating the documentation interpreter with MUD-specific carriers and operators yields an implementable server without extending the kernel.

title: MUD Session id: mud-session role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-evaluation-state proof_refs: []

MUD Session

Program-level session carrier mapping a connected client to its evaluation context within the MUD world.

Definition

  • Carrier: projection of Computer Evaluation State scoped to a player/NPC session (current room, bindings to player entity, ready queue for pending actions).
  • Fields: player identity, connection handle (abstracted endpoint), current room reference, pending command queue, last failure (if any).
  • Purpose: bind client I/O to evaluation steps and world updates.

Invariants

  • Isolation: session commands execute with player-scoped bindings; world mutations go through execute-command.
  • Ordering: commands queued per session preserve arrival order; global ordering is enforced by the evaluation state.
  • Failure visibility: last failure slot mirrors computer failure; no mutation on failure.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Evaluation State.
  • Shows that: specialising evaluation state per session yields a safe carrier for interactive play, preserving kernel scheduling/ordering.

title: Relational MUD Toy Program id: mud-toy-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Relational MUD Toy Program

Design sketch for a toy MUD built as a slice of the relational computer: all state is recognitions; all change is via earned rewrites; gameplay aligns with measurement, topology, and traceability. This doc now spells out concrete computer-layer carriers and operators the MUD uses, so it can be implemented as an SDK-driven runbook.

Core recognitions

  • World geometry: rooms/exits with topology metadata.
  • Entities: characters/NPCs, items with usage/effects.
  • Bodies/conditions: body graph, injuries, treatments, derived conditions.
  • Events/arcs: actions and quests linking recognitions over time.

Topology/measurement/traceability

  • Spatial, anatomical, and social graphs compiled by topology integration.
  • Measurement tracks vitals/environment/social stats; measurement fabric enforces schemas and invariants.
  • Traceability records causal chains (who/what/where) for injuries/events, used for explainability.

Runbooks/flows

  • Ingestion: load MUD schemas via the computer Recognition operator over Computer Recognition carriers.
  • Simulation: apply player/NPC actions as computer Evaluate Step over an earned Computer Evaluation State with a MUD-specific Computer Wiring Plan.
  • Persistence: use computer Storage with Computer Snapshot to save/restore worlds.
  • Lifecycle: manage resource/backups via storage/replication runbooks and snapshots.

Goals

  • Relational-first, explainable, composable, lightweight toy implementation.
  • Implementable via SDK: the above operators/carriers are sufficient to stand up a working MUD slice without extending the kernel.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics; all state/flow is expressed via computer carriers and operators.

title: MUD Transcript id: mud-transcript role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-snapshot proof_refs: []

MUD Transcript

Program-level snapshot/transcript carrier capturing ordered events and state changes for replay/audit.

Definition

  • Carrier: derived from Computer Snapshot, extended with an ordered log of commands, results, and world deltas.
  • Fields: snapshot id/hash, timestamp, provenance, world state digest, ordered events (command + outcome), and integrity hash.
  • Purpose: support persistence, crash recovery, and replay for debugging or storytelling.

Invariants

  • Order-preserving: events are recorded in evaluation/trace order.
  • Integrity: hashes cover world digest + event log; verifies on load.
  • Replayable: applying the log to the referenced snapshot reproduces the world state up to observational equivalence.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Snapshot.
  • Shows that: extending snapshots with ordered events yields a replayable transcript that remains consistent with computer storage semantics.

title: MUD World State id: mud-world-state role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/relational-state proof_refs: []

MUD World State

Program-level projection of the computer relational state into a MUD shard: rooms, exits, entities, items, and event history as recognitions and relations.

Definition

  • Carrier: projection of Computer Relational State containing MUD-specific recognitions (rooms, exits, entities, inventory, effects).
  • Fields: world graph (rooms/exits), entity registry (players/NPCs), inventory, active effects/conditions, event log pointers (for transcripts).
  • Access: read/write only through program operators that refine the computer kernel (e.g., execute-command, persist-world).

Invariants

  • Type coherence: recognitions stored respect schemas for world topology, entities, and items.
  • Trace order: updates follow the evaluation/trace order from the computer evaluation state; no out-of-order mutations.
  • Persistence-ready: state can be serialized via computer snapshots without loss of invariants.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Relational State.
  • Shows that: specialising the relational state to MUD recognitions yields a world carrier suitable for the MUD server while preserving kernel semantics.

title: Multi-Agent Program id: multi-agent-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Multi-Agent Program

This program coordinates multiple agents as recognitions so their roles, policies, and interactions are explicit.

Behaviour

  • Represent agents, roles, capabilities, policies, and coordination protocols as recognitions with provenance.
  • Track interactions, commitments, and outcomes between agents.
  • Measure performance, reliability, and alignment across teams of agents.

Intended operators

  • Register and provision agents with policies and capabilities.
  • Plan and execute coordinated tasks; record traces of interactions.
  • Analyse multi-agent behaviour and adjust policies or roles accordingly.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Multi-User Computer Program id: multi-user-computer-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Multi-User Computer Program

This program specifies multi-user operation of the relational computer so identities, sessions, and isolation are explicit.

Behaviour

  • Represent users, sessions, permissions, and resource allocations as recognitions with provenance.
  • Enforce isolation and policy boundaries between users while sharing the kernel and system carriers.
  • Track audit trails of user actions and shared artefacts.

Intended operators

  • Create/manage users and sessions with policy bindings.
  • Allocate resources and enforce quotas; mediate shared objects.
  • Audit multi-user activity and resolve conflicts.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: MultiAgent Fixed Shape id: multiagent-fixed-shape role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

MultiAgent Fixed Shape

Shape/recognition stable under joint flow from multiple agents’ flows.

Definition

  • Carrier: recognitions/sections that remain invariant when all agents’ flows act jointly via Social Flow.
  • Context: built from a set of Agents.

Invariants

  • Stability: joint flow leaves the shape unchanged up to nucleus.
  • Robustness: small perturbations by any agent flow return to the shape.

Proof

  • Uses: agents and social flow to define joint invariance.
  • Shows that: shapes can be fixed under multi-agent dynamics.

title: Multimodal Signals Program id: multimodal-signals-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Multimodal Signals Program

This program treats multimodal signals (text, audio, vision, sensors) as recognitions so they can be aligned and analysed jointly.

Behaviour

  • Represent modality-specific signals and their alignments as recognitions with provenance.
  • Track calibration, sampling, and synchronization across modalities.
  • Measure quality, latency, and cross-modal consistency.

Intended operators

  • Ingest and align multimodal streams.
  • Derive fused representations with traceable steps.
  • Surface inconsistencies or drift across modalities.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Museums and Collections Program id: museums-collections-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Museums and Collections Program

This program represents museum objects and collections as recognitions so curation and provenance are explicit.

Behaviour

  • Model objects, collections, exhibits, and provenance chains as recognitions.
  • Link objects to creators, periods, materials, and locations.
  • Measure conservation status, loan/visit metrics, and interpretation coverage.

Intended operators

  • Catalog and curate collections with traceable metadata.
  • Plan exhibits and track loans or movements.
  • Publish and align collection data with external knowledge graphs.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Music Practice Program id: music-practice-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Music Practice Program

This program models music practice as recognitions so sessions, repertoire, and progress are traceable.

Behaviour

  • Represent pieces, exercises, sessions, and techniques as recognitions with provenance.
  • Track goals, tempo, accuracy, and expressiveness measurements over time.
  • Link practice to performance contexts and feedback.

Intended operators

  • Plan and log practice sessions.
  • Analyse progress and adapt practice plans.
  • Integrate with generated music or theory programs for support.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Mythic Archetypes Program id: mythic-archetypes-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Mythic Archetypes Program

This program models mythic archetypes and motifs as recognitions to trace how they appear across narratives, dreams, and media.

Behaviour

  • Represent archetypes, motifs, and narrative patterns as recognitions with provenance and context.
  • Link archetypes to domains (dreams, literature, media, interpersonal).
  • Measure recurrence, variation, and transformation over time.

Intended operators

  • Annotate texts or experiences for archetypal motifs.
  • Compare archetype usage across domains and cultures.
  • Feed archetypal patterns into generation or analysis programs.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Naming Witness id: naming-witness role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/first-name
  • acts/self-naming proof_refs: []

Naming Witness

Structure showing how Witness binds to traces with external and self names.

Components

  • External label from FirstName.
  • Self label from SelfNaming.
  • Binding of Witness to traces carrying both names.

Proof

Sketch: FirstName seeds an external label; SelfNaming = (FirstName Reflex) reflects it back into the trace. Together they yield traces carrying external and internal names.

title: Network Channel id: network-channel role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/structure/trace-carrier proof_refs: []

Network Channel

Associates sequences of DistributionChannelCarrier packets to edges between compatible ports, preserving intake and persistence invariants.

Definition

  • Carrier: network edges annotated with Distribution Channel Carrier flows and trace spans (from Trace Carrier).
  • Fields: endpoints (ports), channel queue, trace span, integrity metadata.

Invariants

  • Port compatibility and trace ordering inherited from distribution channels.
  • Integrity of buffered data is maintained across network edges.
  • Acyclic/acyclicity constraints follow the underlying topology (if imposed).

Proof

  • Derived from: Trace Carrier for sequencing; uses Distribution Channel Carrier to define payload flows.
  • Shows that: annotating network edges with distribution channels yields a network channel carrier respecting intake/persistence invariants.

title: Network Controller Program id: network-controller-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Network Controller Program

The Network Controller Program is a computer-layer refinement of the documentation interpreter. It wires modules, routes data, and exposes policy-controlled APIs over authorised channels while respecting topology constraints and traceability.

Carrier

  • Module registry: recognitions describing modules, inputs/outputs, and dependencies as Computer Recognition.
  • Channel graph: wiring plan describing queues/streams and their connections as Computer Wiring Plan.
  • Policy and topology constraints: recognitions that annotate channels with allowed scopes and placement.
  • Failure carriers: emitted when routing or registration violates policy, topology, or wiring constraints as Computer Failure.

Behaviour

  1. Register modules and their ports in the module registry; validate against policy and topology constraints.
  2. Build channels/streams per the wiring plan; attach traceability references to each connection.
  3. Expose API endpoints (per interface specs) that enqueue requests after intake/policy checks.
  4. Route messages along authorised channels; apply backpressure when telemetry signals congestion.
  5. Emit Computer Failure when a routing or registration step violates constraints.

Invariants

  • Channels respect dependency order and topology constraints encoded in the wiring plan.
  • All requests and responses are validated, policy-compliant, and traceable to earned acts/specs.
  • Network state is observable via measurement/telemetry; failures are surfaced as failure carriers.

Proof

Refines the documentation interpreter by specialising the wiring to network channels and API intake. The refinement preserves acyclicity and failure semantics while constraining carriers to network registries, channels, and policy/topology recognitions.

title: Network Program id: network-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Network Program

This program covers networking for the relational computer: connections, protocols, and routing expressed as recognitions.

Behaviour

  • Represent endpoints, connections, protocols, and routes as recognitions with provenance and policy bindings.
  • Track topology, latency, throughput, and reliability measurements.
  • Enforce policy for access, encryption, and isolation.

Intended operators

  • Configure and monitor network links with trace.
  • Route and prioritize traffic according to measurements and policy.
  • Diagnose and remediate network issues via auditable runbooks.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Network Topology id: network-topology role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/structure/trace-carrier proof_refs: []

Network Topology

Defines ports and edges between compatible ports, extending the wiring structure to capture allowable data paths.

Definition

  • Carrier: graph of Interface Ports and edges annotated with trace spans and policy/compatibility constraints.
  • Fields: port set, edge set, compatibility relation.

Invariants

  • Port compatibility enforced on edges.
  • Trace coherence: edges respect trace ordering/causality when instantiated.
  • Acyclicity or allowed cycles specified per deployment.

Proof

  • Derived from: Trace Carrier to attach sequencing when instantiated.
  • Uses: Interface Port for endpoints.
  • Shows that: defining allowable port connections yields a topology carrier for system wiring/routing.

title: Newsfeeds Analysis Program id: newsfeeds-analysis-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Newsfeeds Analysis Program

This program analyses newsfeeds as recognitions to surface bias, coverage, and influence.

Behaviour

  • Represent articles, sources, topics, and timelines as recognitions with provenance.
  • Track information structure, framing, and repetition across feeds.
  • Measure coverage balance, sentiment, and propagation patterns.

Intended operators

  • Ingest and align newsfeeds with traceable metadata.
  • Compare topicalization and framing across sources.
  • Alert on anomalies or gaps in coverage.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Newtonian Physics Simulation Program id: newtonian-physics-sim-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Newtonian Physics Simulation Program

This program simulates Newtonian physics as recognitions so state and dynamics are traceable.

Behaviour

  • Represent bodies, forces, and states as recognitions with provenance.
  • Apply earned dynamics operators to evolve states; record traces and measurements.
  • Link simulations to measurement channels for validation.

Intended operators

  • Set up initial conditions; step simulations under Newtonian dynamics.
  • Compare simulations with observations; adjust parameters with trace.
  • Export simulation slices for other programs (visualization, education).

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Noninterference Theorem id: noninterference-theorem role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Noninterference Theorem

Theorem-level structure asserting that information-flow constraints enforce noninterference between high and low channels/ports.

Definition

  • Premise: labelled channels/ports and an Information Flow Constraint validated by Check Noninterference.
  • Claim: low-observable outputs are independent of high inputs.

Invariants

  • Security: no illicit flow from high to low.
  • Compositional: holds under composition of compliant channel networks.

Proof

  • Uses: constraint validation to show observational equivalence for low outputs regardless of high inputs.
  • Shows that: the policy enforces noninterference at the math level, aligning with security requirements for runtime.

title: Norm Regime id: norm-regime role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/acts/discipline proof_refs: []

Norm Regime

Discipline interpreted as a normative law over recognitions: which states are allowed or forbidden. Combines the earned discipline act with filtering of the recognition carrier.

Definition

  • Carrier: Recognition Carrier equipped with a discipline (from Discipline) that marks admissible vs forbidden recognitions; filters may further refine admissibility.
  • Law: a predicate/closure operator on recognitions indicating compliance or violation.

Invariants

  • Closure: admissible recognitions are closed under earned flow/nucleus where discipline allows.
  • Violation handling: forbidden recognitions map to bottom/contradiction.
  • Monotone: strengthening discipline never adds admissible recognitions.

Proof

  • Derived from: Discipline which provides the normative structure.
  • Uses: Recognition Carrier to host admissible/forbid markings.
  • Shows that: discipline acts can be viewed as normative regimes over recognitions, supporting operators that detect or enforce compliance.

title: Normalized Judgement id: normalized-judgement role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/normalized-judgement proof_refs: []

Normalized Judgement

Judgements that normalise back to the triadic witness, earned by NormalizedJudgement.

Components

  • Evaluation via EvaluateJudgement.
  • Normalisation back to the judgement triad.

Proof

Sketch: apply the normalisation act to evaluated judgements, showing they normalise to the earned judgement triad, respecting recognition/evaluation structure.

title: Notifications and Attention Routing Program id: notifications-attention-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Notifications and Attention Routing Program

This program routes notifications as recognitions so attention is managed explicitly.

Behaviour

  • Represent notifications, channels, preferences, and policies as recognitions with provenance.
  • Track context (urgency, user state) to route or defer notifications.
  • Measure delivery, engagement, and overload signals.

Intended operators

  • Ingest notification events; apply policy and context filters.
  • Route, batch, or suppress notifications with traceable decisions.
  • Adapt routing based on measured attention signals.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: OAuth Credential id: oauth-credential role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-recognition proof_refs: []

OAuth Credential

Concrete credential carrier for OAuth-style authentication at the computer layer. Encodes client identity and proof material as a recognised payload.

Definition

  • Carrier: refinement of Computer Recognition specialising the payload to OAuth fields.
  • Fields (concrete):
    • recognition_id (UUID/string): credential id.
    • client_id (string): registered client identifier.
    • client_secret (string, optional): secret or proof of possession.
    • redirect_uri (string): registered redirect URI.
    • scopes (array of strings): requested scopes.
    • provenance (object {actor, origin, trace_ref}).
    • signature (string, optional): signature/MAC over the request.
    • expiry (ISO-8601 UTC, optional): credential validity window.

Invariants

  • Client ids/redirect URIs match registry; signatures verify when present.
  • Scopes are declared and policy-checked before issuance of tokens.
  • All provenance fields are present for audit.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Recognition.
  • Shows that: treating OAuth credentials as recognitions fits the computer recognition semantics while adding concrete fields for authentication.

title: OAuth Token id: oauth-token role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-recognition proof_refs: []

OAuth Token

Token carrier issued after validating OAuth credentials, used to authorise MCP requests at the computer layer.

Definition

  • Carrier: refinement of Computer Recognition scoped to OAuth tokens.
  • Fields (concrete):
    • recognition_id (UUID/string): token id.
    • access_token (string): bearer or proof-of-possession token.
    • token_type (string enum): bearer or pop.
    • expires_at (ISO-8601 UTC): expiry time.
    • scopes (array of strings): granted scopes.
    • subject (string): principal id.
    • issuer (string): issuing authority.
    • signature (string): signature/MAC over token body.
    • provenance (object {actor, origin, trace_ref}).

Invariants

  • Signature/issuer verified; expiry respected.
  • Granted scopes are a subset of the validated credential scopes.
  • Tokens are bound to subject and issuer; provenance recorded for audit.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Recognition.
  • Shows that: issuing tokens as recognitions maintains the recognition model while adding concrete OAuth fields for computer-level auth.

title: Observable Algebra id: observable-algebra role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/profile-integral proof_refs: []

Observable Algebra

The Observable Algebra packages the earned observables, measures, and integration operations compatible with flow and nucleus.

Definition

  • Observables: earned by observation/normalisation acts.
  • Measure/integration: earned by acts/measure, acts/profile-measure, and acts/profile-integral.
  • Algebraic structure: lattice-ordered algebra with addition/scalar multiplication (profile entropy acts).

Invariants

  • Closed under flow observation; flow-identical observables are identified.
  • Compatible with fixed kernels/chains from Stochastic dynamics.

Proof

Derived from the measurement acts and flow-observation compatibility; algebraic laws and flow invariance are earned by the listed acts.

title: Observable Carrier id: observable-carrier role: structure status: plan derived_from: acts/measure proof_refs: []

Observable Carrier

Definition

  • Carrier: measured quantities obtained by applying the earned measurement act to recognised phenomena.
  • Components: each observable pairs a measurable phenomenon with its value in the profile measure space.
  • Source: earned from Measure; profile-specific refinements from Profile Measure constrain the value space.

Invariants

  • Observables are stable under profile measurement: re-measuring within the same profile yields the same value.
  • Composition of observables along traces respects the measurement functor induced by the profile measure.

Proof

  • Derived from: Measure, which provides the act of assigning measured values.
  • Shows that: the measurement act induces a carrier of measured quantities; incorporating the profile-specific measure space yields the observable carrier as an earned structure.

title: Observable Typed Operator id: observable-typed-operator role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Observable Typed Operator

Typed combinators for numeric/observable structures (entropy, free energy, probabilities) tied to the typed judgment system.

Definition

  • Operators: expectation, combination, entropy/free-energy evaluation derived from Observable Algebra.
  • Typing: rules in Typed Judgment System ensuring observables are applied to appropriate section types.

Invariants

  • Type safety: observable operations only applied to observables of correct type.
  • Semantic alignment: results correspond to observable algebra semantics.

Proof

  • Uses: observable algebra and typing rules.
  • Shows that: numeric/probabilistic combinators are type-directed and sound.

title: Observation Kernel id: observation-kernel role: structure status: plan emerges_from: observation-kernel proof_refs:

  • observation-kernel
  • transition-law
  • fixed-invariant-state fields:
  • name: domain type: fixed-invariant-state description: current fixed invariant state.
  • name: codomain type: recognition description: observational distribution after one flow–measure step.
  • name: flow_nucleus type: flow-nucleus-adjunction description: compatibility data ensuring flow/nucleus commute with kernel action.

Observation Kernel

One-step flow–measure kernel assigning to each fixed invariant state an earned observational recognition/distribution. Built from the act Observation Kernel composed with Transition Law over the fixed invariant lattice.

Invariants

  • Flow–measure Markov property: next observation depends only on current fixed invariant state.
  • Lattice compatibility: respects the invariant lattice (order/meet/join up to stabilisation).
  • Stochasticity: preserves total mass; positivity of transition probabilities.
  • Sheaf/flow compatibility: commutes with restriction/glue on the hypertensor site and with flow/nucleus actions via flow_nucleus.

Proof

  • Derived from: Observation Kernel act and Transition Law on fixed invariant states.
  • Shows that: the kernel maps fixed invariant states to observational recognitions while preserving flow/nucleus and sheaf locality properties.

title: Observation Witness id: observation-witness role: structure status: plan derived_from: acts/witness-observation proof_refs: []

Observation Witness

Certificate that observations suffice to witness contextual equivalence in the full profile.

Definition

  • Carrier: witnesses produced by applying Observe through Witness Observation to establish contextual equivalence.
  • Role: evidence that two contexts are indistinguishable using earned observations across the entire index base.

Invariants

  • Observation-closed: witnesses reference only earned Observation carriers.
  • Reindexing-stable: witnesses remain valid under all base morphisms admitted by the flow/nucleus adjunction.

Proof

  • Derived from: Witness Observation, which provides the observation-to-equivalence witness.
  • Shows that: the act yields a carrier of witnesses confined to earned observations, sufficient to decide contextual equivalence in the full profile; this carrier is used directly by witness operators.

title: Observation id: observation role: structure status: plan derived_from: acts/observe proof_refs: []

Observation

Carrier of observations generated by the act Observe.

Definition

  • Carrier: morphisms from the observation act, ranging over the full context geometry (no fixed-profile restriction).
  • Context: domains and codomains sit in the earned context geometry; reindexing along admissible morphisms is governed by the flow/nucleus adjunction.
  • Role: forms the base observational evidence used to decide contextual equivalence at the math layer.

Invariants

  • Geometric closure: closed under composition and identities of the context geometry.
  • Adjunction stability: reindexing observations along flow/nucleus preserves their meaning and admissibility.

Proof

  • Derived from: Observe, which constructs the observation carrier over the context geometry.
  • Shows that: the act already furnishes a well-defined observation carrier with reindexing stability, providing the base evidence for later witness operators.

title: Open Source Communities Program id: open-source-communities-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Open Source Communities Program

This program models open source communities and projects as recognitions so participation and governance are traceable.

Behaviour

  • Represent projects, contributors, issues, commits, and releases as recognitions with provenance.
  • Link governance structures, policies, and decision records to project artefacts.
  • Measure participation, review health, and release cadence.

Intended operators

  • Track contributions and reviews with trace.
  • Run governance processes (proposals, votes) for projects.
  • Surface community health metrics and guide interventions.

Proof
