  • Shows that: ports can be treated as math carriers for IO in the system stack.

title: Preference Profile id: preference-profile role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Preference Profile

Agent’s ranked recognitions represented as stable nucleus-based orderings.

Definition

  • Carrier: ordering over recognitions for an Agent, reflecting desirability/priority.
  • Structure: preorder/partial order consistent with the recognition lattice and agent norms/epistemics.

Invariants

  • Stability: preferences respect agent’s norm regime and epistemic state.
  • Transitivity: rankings form a preorder; nucleus-equivalent recognitions share rank.

Proof

  • Uses: agent data and recognition order to define preferences.
  • Shows that: preferences can be encoded as ordered recognitions.

title: Presheaf Topos id: presheaf-topos role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/presheaf-topos proof_refs: []

Presheaf Topos

The Presheaf Topos is the earned category of presheaves on the relational profile site, with finite limits and exponentials as given by acts/presheaf-topos.

Definition

  • Objects: presheaves over the profile site (contexts ↦ observations).
  • Morphisms: natural transformations between these presheaves.
  • Structure: inherits finite limits and exponentials from the act.

Invariants

  • Finite limits and exponentials exist as earned by the act.
  • Restrictions respect the flow/nucleus compatibility of the profile site.

Proof

Directly derived from acts/presheaf-topos, which constructs the presheaf category and exhibits its topos structure.

title: Profile Kernel Chain id: profile-kernel-chain role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/profile-kernel-chain proof_refs: []

Profile Kernel Chain

The Profile Kernel Chain threads the observation kernel through successive profiles in a profile tower.

Definition

  • Built by acts/profile-kernel-chain from a Profile Tower.
  • Encodes the transition rule at each level as induced by the observation kernel.

Invariants

  • Preserves the profile tower structure at each level.
  • Interacts coherently with the underlying geometry and invariant lattice.

Proof

Directly earned by acts/profile-kernel-chain, which composes profile towers with the observation kernel to form a Stochastic-like chain over profiles.

title: Profile Kernel id: profile-kernel role: structure status: plan emerges_from: profile-kernel-step proof_refs:

  • profile-kernel-step
  • profile-kernel-chain
  • observation-kernel
  • profile-tower fields:
  • name: step type: observation-kernel description: base kernel lifted through the profile tower.
  • name: tower type: profile-tower description: profile tower over which the kernel evolves.
  • name: chain type: profile-kernel-chain description: iterated application of the step along the tower.
  • name: flow_nucleus type: flow-nucleus-adjunction description: compatibility data ensuring flow/nucleus commute with kernel action along the tower.

Profile Kernel

Flow–measure kernel lifted along the profile tower, evolving profiles of recognitions via the observation kernel.

Definition

  • Step: earned by Profile Kernel Step, lifting the Observation Kernel through the Profile Tower to map a profile to its successor.
  • Chain: Profile Kernel Chain composes steps to yield an iterated profile-level stochastic chain.

Invariants

  • Compatibility: each step commutes with tower reconstruction; projecting to any layer matches the observation-kernel step on that layer.
  • Markov property: future profiles depend only on the current profile via the earned kernel.
  • Stationarity: fixed profiles correspond to Fixed Chain states; mixing/ergodicity propagate along the chain when earned.
  • Flow/nucleus stability: kernel action respects flow/nucleus laws along the tower.

Proof

  • Derived from: Profile Kernel Step and Profile Kernel Chain.
  • Uses: Observation Kernel and Profile Tower to define profile evolution.
  • Shows that: lifting the observation kernel along the profile tower yields a well-defined profile stochastic chain compatible with tower projections and admitting fixed/mixing properties.

title: Profile Topos id: profile-topos role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/profile-topos proof_refs: []

Profile Topos

The Profile Topos embeds the fixed profile category of recognitions into the presheaf topos, preserving limits and exponentials.

Definition

  • Objects: fixed-profile recognitions viewed as presheaves.
  • Morphisms: profile morphisms mapped to natural transformations.
  • Embedding: a functor from the profile category into the Presheaf Topos that preserves the earned topos structure.

Invariants

  • Finite limits and exponentials are preserved by the embedding.
  • Image lies inside the sheaf subcategory after sheafification.

Proof

Derived from acts/profile-topos, which constructs the embedding and exhibits preservation of topos structure.

title: Profile Tower id: profile-tower role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/build-profile-tower proof_refs: []

Profile Tower

The Profile Tower is the tower of recognitions built by repeatedly stacking profiles, earned by acts/build-profile-tower.

Definition

  • Cells: recognitions at increasing heights built by the act.
  • Structure: includes face/degeneracy maps as provided by the act.

Invariants

  • Tower composition laws (faces/degeneracies) hold.
  • Each level remains within the earned profile recognitions.

Proof

Direct from build-profile-tower, which constructs the tower and its maps.

title: Program Command id: program-command role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-recognition proof_refs: []

Program Command

Generic command carrier for program-layer actions over the documentation interpreter.

Definition

  • Carrier: refinement of Computer Recognition scoped to program actions (e.g., measure, plan, mutate, snapshot).
  • Fields (concrete at computer layer):
    • command_id (string/UUID)
    • verb (string enum: measure/plan/mutate/snapshot/other domain verbs)
    • targets (list of identifiers for docs/tasks/ports)
    • parameters (object of verb-specific arguments)
    • provenance (actor/origin/trace_ref)
    • policy (policy binding/ref)

Invariants

  • Validated: references resolve to existing program artefacts or declared new ones with correct metadata.
  • Layer-safe: commands do not introduce upward deps; respect policy/trace constraints.
  • Traceable: carries provenance for audit/replay.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Recognition.
  • Shows that: normalising program actions as recognitions yields a reusable command carrier for program-layer operators.

title: Program Session id: program-session role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-evaluation-state proof_refs: []

Program Session

Generic session carrier binding a program workspace to an evaluation context.

Definition

  • Carrier: projection of Computer Evaluation State with ready queue, bindings, and failure slot for program tasks.
  • Fields (concrete at computer layer):
    • session_id (string/UUID)
    • plan_ref (wiring plan id)
    • ready_queue (ordered list of step ids)
    • bindings (port-id → carrier instance)
    • in_flight (current step id + inputs, or null)
    • failure_slot (failure or null)
    • timestamp (ISO-8601 UTC)
    • provenance (actor/origin/trace_ref)
    • integrity_hash (digest over the above)
  • Serialization: JSON Schema/YAML mirroring these fields; integrity hash covers the canonical form of session_id, plan_ref, ready_queue, bindings, in_flight, failure_slot, timestamp, provenance.

Invariants

  • Task ordering respects plan precedence.
  • Failure purity: failures do not mutate program state; recorded in failure slot.
  • Serializable: can be snapshotted and resumed.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Evaluation State.
  • Shows that: specialising evaluation state to program tasks yields a reusable session carrier for program runbooks.

title: Program Snapshot id: program-snapshot role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-snapshot proof_refs: []

Program Snapshot

Program-layer snapshot derived from the computer snapshot, carrying program state/workspace for persistence.

Definition

  • Carrier: Computer Snapshot specialised to program artefacts (workspace, plan, session).
  • Fields (concrete at computer layer):
    • snapshot_id (string/UUID)
    • timestamp (ISO-8601 UTC)
    • state_digest (SHA-256 hex) over program workspace/session bindings
    • plan_ref (optional wiring plan id)
    • workspace_digest (optional SHA-256 hex) of workspace payload
    • session_ref (optional program session id)
    • provenance (actor/origin/trace_ref)
    • integrity_hash (SHA-256 hex) covering header+payload
    • payload (object): serialized workspace/session/plan bindings
  • Serialization: JSON/YAML following the above; integrity hash recomputed over canonical serialization of header+payload.

Invariants

  • Integrity: hashes/digests cover id + payload; must verify on load.
  • Fidelity: save/load round-trips yield equivalent program state.
  • Stability: payload adheres to program schema; no extra state.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Snapshot.
  • Shows that: lifting computer snapshots to program payloads yields a reusable snapshot carrier for program persistence.

title: Program Transcript id: program-transcript role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-snapshot proof_refs: []

Program Transcript

Program-layer transcript capturing ordered commands and outcomes for replay.

Definition

  • Carrier: Computer Snapshot extended with a typed event log over Program Command, Program Wiring Plan, and Program Session.
  • Fields (concrete at computer layer):
    • snapshot_id (string/UUID, required)
    • timestamp (string, ISO-8601 UTC, required)
    • provenance (object {actor, origin, trace_ref}, required)
    • state_digest (string/SHA-256 hex, required): digest of program session/workspace at capture.
    • plan_ref (string, optional): identifier of the plan used during capture.
    • events (array, required): ordered list of event objects:
      • seq (integer, required)
      • command_id (string, required) referencing a program-command
      • command_payload (object, required) — serialized command content
      • result (object, optional) — serialized outcome on success
      • failure (object, optional) — serialized computer failure on failure
      • bindings_digest (string/SHA-256 hex, optional) — digest of bindings after the event
    • integrity_hash (string/SHA-256 hex, required): covers snapshot header + state_digest + events.
    • Suggested schemas:
      • JSON Schema: events as array items with seq, command_id, command_payload, result|failure, bindings_digest.
      • YAML runbook transcript: top-level keys snapshot_id, state_digest, plan_ref, events: with list entries, integrity_hash.

Invariants

  • Order-preserving: events are recorded in evaluation/trace order.
  • Integrity: integrity_hash and state_digest must verify; each event’s bindings_digest matches the bound state after applying the event.
  • Deterministic replay: replaying events from the referenced snapshot yields a program session whose digest equals state_digest.
  • Reference integrity: plan_ref (if present) must exist; command_ids in the log refer to recorded commands.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Snapshot.
  • Uses: Program Command, Program Wiring Plan, and Program Session to type the event log and replay target.
  • Shows that: extending snapshots with typed, hashed event logs yields a replayable transcript consistent with computer storage semantics; replaying the log reproduces the recorded session up to observational equivalence.

title: Program Wiring Plan id: program-wiring-plan role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-wiring-plan proof_refs: []

Program Wiring Plan

Reusable wiring plan for program runbooks over computer operators/carriers.

Definition

  • Carrier: Computer Wiring Plan specialised to program tasks/commands.
  • Fields (inherits computer wiring plan fields; program-specific hints):
    • command_ports: typed mapping from program command verbs to ports.
    • policy_annotations: per-step hints for approvals/traceability.
    • retry/timeout knobs for program-level robustness.
  • Serialization: same JSON/YAML patterns as computer wiring plan; integrity hash covers program-specific annotations too.

Invariants

  • Acyclic, well-typed; respects program command constraints.
  • Failure propagation follows computer evaluation semantics.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Wiring Plan.
  • Shows that: specialising wiring plans to program commands yields a reusable plan carrier for program execution.

title: Projects Coordination Program id: projects-coordination-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Projects Coordination Program

This program models projects, tasks, and coordination as recognitions so work can be tracked and audited.

Behaviour

  • Represent projects, milestones, tasks, dependencies, and assignments as recognitions with provenance.
  • Link projects to resources, policies, and domains; map dependencies and risk.
  • Measure progress, throughput, and blockers.

Intended operators

  • Plan and sequence tasks with dependencies; assign and track execution.
  • Review progress and adjust plans based on measurements.
  • Surface risks and coordination issues for intervention.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Proof Engine Program id: proof-engine-program role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Proof Engine Program

Program-level engine that executes proof scripts and runbooks over the computer kernel, producing proof certificates or explicit failures. It exposes a program-facing API for ingesting proofs, compiling them into Computer Wiring Plans, stepping them deterministically, and persisting checkpoints.

Definition

  • Inputs:
    • Proof scripts/runbooks recognised into Computer Wiring Plans via Recognition.
    • Initial Computer Evaluation State seeded with obligations, environments, and goal queue.
  • Execution (reduction semantics):
    1. Validate the plan for acyclicity, typing, and required witnesses.
    2. Iterate Evaluate Step over the plan, invoking machine-level reductions through computer carriers.
    3. For each goal, require a recognised witness; unfulfilled goals emit Computer Failure tagged with the offending obligation and environment.
  • Persistence:
    • Checkpoint intermediate states and certificates through Computer Storage into Computer Snapshots to allow replay, bisimulation, and audit.
  • Outputs:
    • Updated wiring plans that encode remaining goals or extracted lemmas.
    • Updated evaluation state capturing bindings and ready queues.
    • Success as a proof certificate embedded in the evaluation bindings; failure as Computer Failure without mutating committed bindings.

Invariants

  • Soundness: every accepted step must be justified by a recognised witness; no unchecked state transitions or assumption leaks.
  • Determinism: given an identical wiring plan, evaluation state, and snapshot sequence, execution yields the same bindings and certificate hashes.
  • Failure purity: obligation or typing failures emit Computer Failure and leave prior bindings untouched; recovery must restart from a saved snapshot.
  • Auditability: every step, witness, and binding is available for replay from stored Computer Snapshots so third parties can independently check the proof trace.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Kernel.
  • Shows that: encoding proof scripts as computer wiring plans and stepping them through kernel operators yields a proof engine that preserves machine semantics, surfaces failures explicitly, and remains replayable under snapshot/restore without adding new computational effects.

title: Proof Trace id: proof-trace role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Proof Trace

Captures derivational history of recognitions as path-like witnesses for inference steps.

Definition

  • Carrier: sequences of inference steps/recognitions arranged in a Trace Shuffle Class, annotated with derived recognitions.
  • Structure: supports composition, restriction, and shuffle equivalence.

Invariants

  • Causality: proof steps respect dependency ordering.
  • Stability: nucleus-equivalent proofs yield equivalent proof traces.

Proof

  • Uses: trace structure and recognition annotations to encode derivations.
  • Shows that: inference histories can be treated as traces for analysis and replay.

title: Quantum Physics Simulation Program id: quantum-physics-sim-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Quantum Physics Simulation Program

This program simulates quantum systems as recognitions so states and dynamics are traceable and consistent with earned structures.

Behaviour

  • Represent quantum states, operators, Hamiltonians, and measurements as recognitions with provenance.
  • Evolve states under earned dynamics; record measurements and collapse events with trace.
  • Link simulations to validation data where available.

Intended operators

  • Set up and evolve quantum systems; sample measurements.
  • Compare simulations to observations; adjust parameters with trace.
  • Export slices for analysis or visualization programs.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Quasi-Identity Flow id: quasi-identity-flow role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Quasi-Identity Flow

Set of “almost identity” flows: flow steps that deviate only within nucleus-equivalence.

Definition

  • Elements: flows whose effect on recognitions is nucleus-equivalent to identity, possibly with small deviations.
  • Structure: subset of flow context annotated with deviation witnesses.

Invariants

  • Identity up to nucleus: composition with quasi-identity is equivalent to identity after nucleus collapse.
  • Closed under composition where deviations remain within nucleus bounds.

Proof

  • Uses: flow context and identity paths to define near-identity behaviour.
  • Shows that: quasi-identity flows form a meaningful substructure for perturbative reasoning.

title: Quasicrystalline Constraint id: quasicrystalline-constraint role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • math/structure/hypertensor-sheaf proof_refs: []

Quasicrystalline Constraint

Local rules/aperiodicity constraints imposed on hypertensor sections to earn the quasicrystalline qualifier.

Definition

  • Carrier: predicates on Hypertensor Section over Hypertensor Window enforcing local matching rules and forbidding periodicity.

Invariants

  • Local: rules depend only on finite windows; stable under restriction.
  • Aperiodic: no nontrivial translations leave sections invariant (as earned when acts supply aperiodicity).

Proof

  • Derived from: Hypertensor Sheaf with additional local rule constraints.
  • Shows that: sections satisfying these constraints form the quasicrystalline subclass.

title: Quasicrystalline Hypertensor Program id: quasicrystalline-hypertensor-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/relational-state proof_refs: []

Quasicrystalline Hypertensor Program

This program works with quasicrystalline hypertensor sheaf sections—the earned calculus structure underlying the relational machine state.

Behaviour

  • Represent lattice windows, sites, and attached profile towers as recognitions following the calculus structure.
  • Construct, evolve, and compare finite sections; ensure compatibility with flow/nucleus and dynamics operators.
  • Measure invariants (fixed points, ergodicity, commutations) over sections.

Intended operators

  • Build and persist sections (window, towers) with trace.
  • Apply dynamics and flow/nucleus operations to sections.
  • Analyse fixed/invariant structures and evolution behaviour.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Relational State.
  • Shows that: this program operates over the computer-facing sheaf state without extending machine semantics.

title: Quasicrystalline Hypertensor Sheaf Dynamics Program id: quasicrystalline-hypertensor-sheaf-dynamics-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/relational-state proof_refs: []

Quasicrystalline Hypertensor Sheaf Dynamics Program

This program applies dynamics to quasicrystalline hypertensor sheaf sections, tracking evolution and invariants.

Behaviour

  • Represent dynamics kernels, fixed chains, and evolution steps over sections as recognitions with provenance.
  • Ensure kernels are consistent with earned dynamics theorems (row-stochastic, fixed-chain compatibility).
  • Measure mixing, ergodicity, and invariant structures under dynamics.

Intended operators

  • Apply dynamics to sections and record resulting sections and measurements.
  • Compute fixed points and chains; analyse convergence.
  • Compare dynamics across parameter choices or domains.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Relational State.
  • Shows that: this program evolves the computer-facing sheaf state consistent with machine semantics.

title: Quasicrystalline Hypertensor Sheaf Program id: quasicrystalline-hypertensor-sheaf-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/relational-state proof_refs: []

Quasicrystalline Hypertensor Sheaf Program

This program focuses on the sheaf structure of the quasicrystalline hypertensor manifold used by the relational machine.

Behaviour

  • Represent sheaf components (base space, fibres, sections, restriction maps) as recognitions aligned with the earned calculus structure.
  • Construct and verify sheaf properties over lattice windows and towers.
  • Measure coherence and compatibility of sections across overlaps.

Intended operators

  • Build sheaf data (fibres, restriction) for given windows.
  • Check sheaf axioms and section compatibility.
  • Relate sheaf sections to machine/kernel state for analysis.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Relational State.
  • Shows that: this program builds and analyses sheaf data over the computer-facing state without extending machine semantics.

title: Quasicrystalline Hypertensor Sheaf Section id: quasicrystalline-hypertensor-sheaf-section role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/structure/hypertensor-section proof_refs: []

Quasicrystalline Hypertensor Sheaf Section

Definition

  • Carrier: calculus-level lift of the math Hypertensor Section parameterised by quasicrystalline constraints.
  • Components: window/fiber/tower/section with restrict/glue satisfying sheaf axioms under quasicrystal coverage; flow–nucleus compatibility enforced for earned dynamics.

Invariants

  • Sheaf axioms: restriction/gluing satisfy sheaf condition over earned coverage.
  • Flow–nucleus compatibility: flow and nucleus commute for the lifted section.
  • Uniqueness: section carrier unique up to earned equivalence for given quasicrystal constraints.

Proof

  • Derived from: Hypertensor Section, lifting it with quasicrystalline parameters and earned flow/nucleus dynamics.
  • Shows that: parameterising the math section yields the calculus-level quasicrystalline hypertensor sheaf section with preserved invariants.

title: Quasicrystalline Hypertensor Topos id: quasicrystalline-hypertensor-topos role: structure status: plan emerges_from: presheaf-topos proof_refs:

  • presheaf-topos
  • sheafification
  • hypertensor-site
  • hypertensor-sheaf fields:
  • name: site type: hypertensor-site description: base site of hypertensor windows/covers.
  • name: objects type: hypertensor-sheaf description: hypertensor sheaves over the site.
  • name: classifier type: recognition-classifier description: subobject classifier giving internal logic over recognitions.
  • name: flow_nucleus type: flow-nucleus-adjunction description: flow/nucleus compatibility data lifted to the topos.

Quasicrystalline Hypertensor Topos

Topos of hypertensor sheaves over the hypertensor site with inherited internal logic and classifier structure.

Definition

  • Objects: hypertensor sheaves over Hypertensor Site satisfying Hypertensor Sheaf Condition.
  • Morphisms: natural transformations between such sheaves.
  • Internal logic: from Recognition Classifier and relational logic.

Invariants

  • Topos structure: has limits/colimits, exponentials, subobject classifier inherited via sheafification over the hypertensor site.
  • Internal logic: recognition-classifier provides subobject classifier; flow/nucleus compatibility is preserved by morphisms.
  • Stability: base change, pullback/pushforward stable on the hypertensor site.

Proof

  • Derived from: Presheaf Topos and sheafification over the hypertensor site/sheaves.
  • Uses: hypertensor site and sheaf condition to ensure objects are sheaves.
  • Shows that: hypertensor sheaves form a topos with earned internal logic and flow/nucleus compatibility.

title: Quasicrystalline Relational Hypertensor id: quasicrystalline-relational-hypertensor role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • math/structure/quasicrystalline-hypertensor-sheaf-section proof_refs: []

Quasicrystalline Relational Hypertensor

A Quasicrystalline Relational Hypertensor refers to the earned sheaf-section structure treated as a hypertensor over lattice windows and fibres. It packages the sheaf-section carrier for reuse in calculus-level constructions.

Proof

  • TODO: fill proof.

title: RDF and OWL Alignment Program id: rdf-owl-alignment-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

RDF and OWL Alignment Program

This program aligns RDF/OWL knowledge with the relational calculus so external graphs become earned recognitions.

Behaviour

  • Represent RDF triples, OWL axioms, and vocabularies as recognitions with provenance.
  • Map external ontologies into earned schemas without breaking invariants.
  • Measure alignment quality, coverage, and consistency.

Intended operators

  • Ingest RDF/OWL sources into recognition carriers.
  • Align or map vocabularies; surface conflicts for resolution.
  • Export aligned slices for use in other programs.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Reachability Radius id: reachability-radius role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Reachability Radius

Cost metric: minimal flow distance to reach a target recognition from a source.

Definition

  • Radius: minimal distance (from Relational Metric) over admissible flow paths connecting source to target recognitions.
  • Role: guides scheduling/planning.

Invariants

  • Non-negative; zero when source=target up to nucleus.
  • Triangle inequality inherited from the metric.

Proof

  • Uses: flow context and relational metric to measure path lengths.
  • Shows that: reachability cost is well-defined for admissible flows.

title: Reading and Media Queue Program id: reading-media-queue-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Reading and Media Queue Program

This program manages reading and media queues as recognitions so consumption and attention are traceable.

Behaviour

  • Represent items, queues, priorities, and states (to-read, in-progress, done) as recognitions with provenance.
  • Link items to domains, recommendations, and annotations.
  • Measure completion rates, revisit patterns, and attention allocation.

Intended operators

  • Ingest and prioritise items; schedule consumption.
  • Annotate and summarise items; feed insights to other programs.
  • Review queues and adjust based on measurements and goals.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Recipes and Meal Planning Program id: recipes-meal-planning-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Recipes and Meal Planning Program

This program models recipes and meal plans as recognitions so nutrition and shopping can be coordinated and audited.

Behaviour

  • Represent recipes, ingredients, meals, and plans as recognitions with provenance.
  • Link to inventory, shopping, and nutrition measurements.
  • Measure cost, nutrition, and preparation effort.

Intended operators

  • Plan meals; generate shopping lists; track preparation.
  • Adjust plans based on inventory and measurements.
  • Share or adapt recipes with provenance.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Recognition Catalysis id: recognition-catalysis role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Recognition Catalysis

Recognition whose presence enables flow or collapse that would otherwise be impossible.

Definition

  • Components: catalytic recognition, target process (flow/collapse), and witness that process succeeds only when catalyst present.
  • Role: models enabling effects in relational dynamics.

Invariants

  • Necessity: without catalyst, process fails; with catalyst, succeeds.
  • Stability: catalyst effect persists under admissible flow.

Proof

  • Uses: recognition carrier and flow operations to exhibit catalytic effect.
  • Shows that: catalysts can be identified as enablers of otherwise forbidden transitions.

title: Recognition Classifier id: recognition-classifier role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/structure/subobject-classifier proof_refs: []

Recognition Classifier

Identification of the recognition algebra with the subobject classifier Omega in the earned topos.

Definition

  • Carrier: the recognition algebra viewed as Omega, with truth values represented by recognitions.
  • Map: isomorphism between recognitions and sieves/characteristic maps that classify subobjects.

Invariants

  • Isomorphism: recognition order/operations coincide with the logical structure on Omega.
  • Truth alignment: true corresponds to the maximal recognition; characteristic maps correspond to earned recognitions of subobjects.
  • Stability: classification is natural with respect to pullbacks/transport in the topos.

Proof

  • Uses: Relational Logic Algebra to supply the logical operations; Subobject Classifier to supply classifier structure.
  • Shows that: the recognition algebra satisfies the classifier axioms and thus serves as Omega, aligning truth values with earned recognitions.

title: Recognition Collapse Depth id: recognition-collapse-depth role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Recognition Collapse Depth

Counts nucleus/simplification steps needed to reach recognition normal form.

Definition

  • Metric: number of collapse/simplification iterations from a recognition to its Recognition Normal Form.
  • Role: complexity measure for logical simplification.

Invariants

  • Non-negative integer; zero iff already in normal form.
  • Finite for well-formed recognitions.

Proof

  • Uses: normalisation process to count steps.
  • Shows that: collapse depth is well-defined and finite on admissible inputs.

title: Recognition Endpoint Program id: recognition-endpoint-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Recognition Endpoint Program

This program specifies endpoints for submitting or retrieving recognitions, ensuring intake and traceability are enforced.

Behaviour

  • Represent endpoints, payload schemas, authentication, and rate limits as recognitions with provenance.
  • Bind endpoints to intake runbooks and trace carriers; enforce policy and validation.
  • Measure throughput, latency, and error rates.

Intended operators

  • Configure endpoints for recognition submission/retrieval.
  • Validate and ingest requests into recognition carriers with trace.
  • Monitor endpoint health and adapt policy/rate limits as needed.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Recognition Normal Form id: recognition-normal-form role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Recognition Normal Form

Canonical representative of a recognition obtained by collapsing via nucleus and logical simplification; enables deterministic comparison/hashing.

Definition

  • Representative: element of the Recognition Carrier reduced by nucleus closure and logical simplification rules (idempotent, absorption, De Morgan, implication adjunction).
  • Role: unique (up to earned equivalence) form for equality checks and storage.

Invariants

  • Uniqueness up to logical equivalence/nucleus.
  • Idempotence: normalising twice yields the same representative.
  • Stability: normal forms are closed under nucleus.

Proof

  • Uses: nucleus and logic algebra laws to define normalisation.
  • Shows that: recognitions admit canonical forms for deterministic comparison.

title: Recognition Polytope id: recognition-polytope role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Recognition Polytope

Finite set of recognitions generating others via convex-like closure (join/meet combinations).

Definition

  • Generators: finite subset of the Recognition Carrier.
  • Closure: all joins/meets (and admissible combinations) of generators.

Invariants

  • Finite generation: polytope determined by generators.
  • Stability: closed under allowed joins/meets.

Proof

  • Uses: recognition lattice to form closures.
  • Shows that: recognitions can be finitely generated analogously to convex hulls.

title: Recognition Ring id: recognition-ring role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Recognition Ring

Ring-like algebra of recognitions under merge/addition and convolution multiplication.

Definition

  • Carrier: Recognition Carrier equipped with:
    • Addition/merge: join/meet-based combination of recognitions.
    • Multiplication: Recognition Convolution.
  • Structure: distributivity of convolution over merge where admissible.

Invariants

  • Associativity/commutativity for addition; associativity for convolution up to nucleus; distributivity where flows compose.
  • Identities: neutral recognition for addition; identity recognition if available for convolution.

Proof

  • Uses: recognition lattice for additive structure and convolution operator for multiplicative structure.
  • Shows that: recognitions form a ring-like algebra compatible with flow/nucleus semantics.

title: Recognition Telescope id: recognition-telescope role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Recognition Telescope

Sequence of recognitions linked by progressively coarse nucleus collapses.

Definition

  • Data: chain r0 → r1 → … → rn where each step is a nucleus collapse of the previous recognition, possibly across towers/scales.
  • Purpose: track how recognitions simplify through successive collapses.

Invariants

  • Monotone: each step is at least as coarse as the previous.
  • Termination: may stabilise at a fixed point.

Proof

  • Uses: nucleus operations on the recognition carrier.
  • Shows that: collapse chains can be packaged as telescopes for analysis.

title: Recognition Universe id: recognition-universe role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Recognition Universe

Universe of recognitions of recognitions—type-like carrier for higher-order reasoning.

Definition

  • Carrier: higher-level recognitions whose elements are themselves recognitions from the Recognition Carrier.
  • Structure: supports universe operations (lowering/closing) and self-reference.

Invariants

  • Closure: universe closed under earned logical operations.
  • Hierarchy: may be stratified to avoid paradox; lowering maps back to base recognitions.

Proof

  • Uses: recognition carrier to build a universe object.
  • Shows that: higher-order recognitions can be handled as a universe structure.

title: Recognition id: recognition role: structure status: plan emerges_from: recognize proof_refs:

  • recognize
  • meet-recognition
  • join-recognition
  • negate
  • recognition-collapse-depth
  • hypertensor-section
  • coverage-site
  • flow-nucleus-adjunction fields:
  • name: support type: hypertensor-section description: section of the hypertensor sheaf over which the recognition is asserted.
  • name: window type: hypertensor-window description: window/locality for the recognition.
  • name: predicate type: recognition-ring description: element of the earned recognition lattice/ring.
  • name: evidence type: recognition-telescope description: witnesses/measurements supporting the predicate.
  • name: scope type: coverage-site description: coverage/contexts where the recognition holds.
  • name: provenance type: trace-carrier description: {origin, timestamp, trace} for reproducibility and replay.
  • name: collapse_depth type: recognition-collapse-depth description: measure of how far the recognition has been collapsed/stabilised.
  • name: nucleus type: flow-nucleus-adjunction description: flow/nucleus compatibility data ensuring stability.

Recognition

Base recognition is earned by the act Recognize: a two-place witness formed from (Void SelfNaming) yielding a recognition. Within the quasicrystalline hypertensor topos, that predicate travels with its support (section/window), evidence, scope, provenance, and stability data. This carrier packages all of that so operators can combine and transport recognitions without violating topos/flow/nucleus invariants.

Invariants

  • Locality: predicate refers to a specific support section and window; restriction/glue along the scope coverage preserves the recognition.
  • Lattice compatibility: meet/join/negation earned from recognition acts operate within the recognition-ring component without violating support.
  • Stability: revalidation under the same nucleus yields an equivalent recognition; collapse_depth records stabilisation.
  • Provenance: provenance is sufficient to replay/verify the recognition; explicit failures travel via Failure Carrier, not here.

Proof

  • Uses: earned act Recognize for the predicate, lattice acts (meet/join/negate) for combination, sheaf/coverage acts for locality, and flow/nucleus acts for stability.
  • Shows that: packaging predicate, support, evidence, scope, provenance, and stability yields an earned, portable carrier for recognition operations in the quasicrystalline hypertensor topos.

title: Recoverability Index id: recoverability-index role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Recoverability Index

Measures how much information can be reconstructed from a collapsed recognition.

Definition

  • Data: measure of inverse-nucleus extent—how many distinct preimages map to a given collapsed recognition.
  • Role: quantify information loss/recoverability.

Invariants

  • Higher index = more recoverable; zero = no recovery beyond the collapsed form.
  • Monotone with respect to coarsening: coarser collapses lower recoverability.

Proof

  • Uses: nucleus mapping and preimage analysis.
  • Shows that: recoverability can be formalised as an index over recognitions.

title: Recurrence Rule id: recurrence-rule role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/computer-recognition proof_refs: []

Recurrence Rule

Recognition of recurrence specifications for calendar events.

Definition

  • Fields: frequency (daily/weekly/etc.), interval, byday/bymonth/bysetpos selectors, start/end dates, exceptions, provenance.
  • Carrier: refinement of Computer Recognition scoped to recurrence semantics.

Invariants

  • Rules are finite/terminating or explicitly marked as infinite with bounds.
  • Deterministic expansion given a start instant/timezone.
  • Traceability: retains provenance and constraints.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Recognition.
  • Shows that: encoding recurrence as recognitions yields deterministic expansions usable by scheduling operators.

title: Reducible Environment Carrier id: reducible-environment role: structure status: plan derived_from: acts/build-reducible-environment proof_refs: []

Reducible Environment Carrier

Definition

  • Carrier: logical-relations environments that map variables to reducible terms and support substitution.
  • Components: environments built by the act Build Reducible Environment, pairing contexts with reducible interpretations.

Invariants

  • Closed under substitution: extending the environment with a reducible term preserves reducibility.
  • Compatible with typing: environments respect the typing discipline earned in the reducibility construction.

Proof

  • Derived from: Build Reducible Environment, which constructs the reducibility environment.
  • Shows that: the act’s construction already establishes the carrier; enforcing substitution closure and typing compatibility yields the reducible environment structure.

title: Reduction and Normalization Structure id: reduction-normalization-structure role: Reduction step, strong normalization, and confluence package for relational terms. status: plan inputs: [] outputs:

  • id: reduction-normalization-structure name: normalization proof_refs: []

Reduction and Normalization Structure

The acts Reduce, NormalizeStrongly, and EnsureConfluence, together with the normalization/reducibility theorem, induce a reduction-and-normalization structure on relational terms:

  • Reduce defines the primitive step relation on terms,
  • the reducibility acts show that well-typed/reducible terms are strongly normalising under this step relation, and
  • EnsureConfluence guarantees that, under the same hypotheses, the step relation is confluent.

Consequently, for the class of terms covered by the normalization theorem:

  • every reduction sequence terminates in a normal form, and
  • the normal form is unique up to the earned equivalences.

This structure is the basis for any interpreter or evaluator: it provides the step relation and the guarantee that evaluation is well-behaved on the earned programs.

Proof

  • TODO: fill proof.

title: Reflexive Profile id: reflexive-profile role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Reflexive Profile

Profile that contains recognitions of its own recognitions, enabling self-reference.

Definition

  • Carrier: Profile Topos augmented so recognitions can reference themselves in the Recognition Carrier.
  • Structure: supports self-recognition and reflection via internal homs.

Invariants

  • Closure: self-referential recognitions stay within the profile.
  • Consistency: reflexivity does not trivialise the logic; contradictions are controlled.

Proof

  • Uses: Profile Topos and Recognition Carrier.
  • Shows that: reflexive recognitions can be accommodated, forming the basis for self-witnessing operators.

title: Register, Style, and Sociolect Program id: register-style-sociolect-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Register, Style, and Sociolect Program

This program models register, style, and sociolect as recognitions to track how language varies across contexts and communities.

Behaviour

  • Represent registers, styles, and sociolects as recognitions with provenance and usage contexts.
  • Link to lexical, syntactic, phonological, and pragmatic variations.
  • Measure shifts in style/register across domains, speakers, or time.

Intended operators

  • Annotate text/speech for register/style/sociolect.
  • Adapt generation or translation to target registers.
  • Analyse style shifts and their effects in discourse.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Reiki System Program id: reiki-system-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Reiki System Program

This program models Reiki practices as recognitions so sessions, symbols, and lineages are explicit and traceable.

Behaviour

  • Represent attunements, symbols, sessions, and practitioners as Computer Recognitions with provenance and consent/policy bindings.
  • Link practices to contexts and outcomes; respect privacy and boundaries via policy-regime recognitions.
  • Measure session frequency, reported effects, and lineage integrity; persist via Computer Storage and Computer Snapshot.

Intended operators

  • Record and schedule sessions; track attunements and symbols used using Computer Recognition.
  • Reflect on outcomes and adjust practice with consent; drive updates via Evaluate Step over a Reiki-specific Computer Wiring Plan.
  • Share or teach practices while preserving provenance and policy; save/load via Computer Storage.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics; all state/flow is expressed via computer carriers and operators.

title: Relation Duality Witness id: relation-duality role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/relate
  • acts/not-relate
  • acts/witness proof_refs: []

Relation Duality Witness

Structure capturing the complementary pair Relate / NotRelate and their two-sided witness.

Components

  • Positive relation witness (Relate).
  • Negative relation witness (NotRelate).
  • Composite witness (Witness) testifying to both.

Proof

Sketch: Relate and NotRelate come from reflex recognition; their composite is Witness, providing the duality datum used by later acts.

title: Relational Activity Streams Program id: relational-activity-streams-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

Relational Activity Streams Program

Behaviour

  • Map streams to domains while respecting policy and privacy constraints.
  • Measure activity volume, latency, and propagation.

Intended operators

  • Ingest and normalise activity events.
  • Project streams into domain-specific views with trace.
  • Analyse activity patterns and drive notifications or audits.

Proof

title: Relational Desktop Program id: relational-desktop-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

Relational Desktop Program

This program presents the relational computer as a desktop environment where files, windows, and actions are recognitions.

Behaviour

  • Represent desktop objects (files, views, shortcuts), sessions, and layouts as recognitions with provenance.
  • Bind GUI actions to underlying recognitions and runbooks; avoid hidden state.
  • Measure usage, performance, and workspace health.

Intended operators

  • Render and manipulate desktop objects via recognitions.
  • Persist and restore sessions and layouts with trace.
  • Audit actions and surface gaps to refine the desktop model.

Proof

title: Relational Directed Homotopy id: relational-directed-homotopy role: structure status: plan derived_from: [] proof_refs: []

Relational Directed Homotopy

Relational Directed Homotopy will formalise directed paths and homotopies in the relational setting, respecting flow/ordering constraints rather than symmetry. This stub marks the need to earn directed path/concatenation acts and their homotopies.

Proof

title: Relational Directed Stratified Infinity Site id: relational-directed-stratified-infinity-site role: structure status: plan derived_from: [] proof_refs: []

Relational Directed Stratified Infinity Site

This structure will encode directed, stratified ∞-sites in the relational setting. It awaits earned acts for stratification, direction, and covering families over the relational base.

Proof

  • TODO: fill proof.

title: Relational Equilibrium id: relational-equilibrium role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Relational Equilibrium

Fixed point of social flow across an agent network: no agent can update epistemic/norm profile unilaterally.

Definition

  • Carrier: configurations of Agents and channels such that applying Social Flow yields no change.
  • Condition: each agent’s epistemic/norm state is stable given others’ states and messages.

Invariants

  • Stability: social flow is idempotent at equilibrium.
  • No unilateral improvement: agents cannot unilaterally refine beliefs/norms to gain advantage (given the model’s utilities).

Proof

  • Uses: Social Flow over Agents.
  • Shows that: the configuration is a fixed point for social interaction.

title: Relational Filesystem Program id: relational-filesystem-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Relational Filesystem Program

Program-level idea to model files, directories, and processes as recognitions so the relational computer can reason about and orchestrate its own substrate.

Core recognitions

  • File recognition: paths, content hashes, formats/schemas, provenance (runbooks or processes that produced them).
  • Directory recognition: hierarchical groupings with semantic roles (config, data, cache, logs).
  • Process recognition: command/args/env, I/O files, measurements.
  • Capability recognition: permissions/capabilities tied to identities/policies.

Topology and measurement

  • File/directory tree enriched with semantic edges (dataflow, ownership, dependency, configuration).
  • Processes linked to the files they read/write and to higher-level recognitions (runbooks, jobs, architectures).
  • Measurements: disk usage, IO bandwidth, runtimes, error rates, retention/age.

Runbooks/flows

  • Introspect filesystem: scan tree, create/update file/directory recognitions with hashes/metadata.
  • Trace process: capture process recognitions linked to inputs/outputs and measurements.
  • Manage storage lifecycle: drive backup/archive/deletion/migration using topology and measurement.

Cross-domain connections

  • Grounds storage/replication in OS-level entities.
  • Supports reproducibility by making file/process provenance first-class in the relational graph.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Relational Free Quotient Program id: relational-free-quotient-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Relational Free Quotient Program

This program explores free quotients in the relational setting as recognitions, for reasoning about equivalence under constraints.

Behaviour

  • Represent generators, relations, and quotient constructions as recognitions with provenance.
  • Link to structures/operators that witness the quotient in the earned math.
  • Measure properties of quotients (normal forms, identifications) in use.

Intended operators

  • Construct quotient recognitions from generators/relations.
  • Normalise elements and check equivalences under the quotient.
  • Export quotient structure for other reasoning programs.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Function id: relational-function role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/form-function-term proof_refs: []

Function

A Function is the math-level carrier consisting of function terms and their applications, earned by the function/application acts.

Components

  • FormFunctionTerm — earns the function-term constructor by abstracting over a trace context.
  • FormApplicationTerm — earns the application-term constructor by applying a function term to an argument term along the trace.
  • ProfileExponential — earns the exponential profile A ⇒ B and identifies function terms with that profile.
  • Function and application terms inhabit JudgmentTerm; they are specific constructors induced by the acts above.

Proof

Sketch: the function and application acts introduce the corresponding constructors in JudgmentTerm; the exponential profiling act equates those constructors with A ⇒ B recognitions. Together these acts generate the function structure over the term carrier.

title: Relational Logic Algebra id: relational-logic-algebra role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/structure/logic-algebra proof_refs: []

Relational Logic Algebra

Mathematical algebra of recognitions with logical operations (meet, join, implication) induced from the act of relationlessness.

Definition

  • Carrier: earned Recognition Carrier equipped with a partial order ≤ interpreted as entailment/refinement.
  • Operations:
    • ⊤ / ⊥: top/bottom recognitions.
    • ∧, ∨: meet/join respecting the order.
    • ⇒: implication satisfying residuation: a∧b ≤ c iff a ≤ (b ⇒ c).
  • Morphisms: order-preserving maps that respect the operations.

Invariants

  • Logic algebra laws: commutativity/associativity/absorption/distributivity; bottom and top are identities for join/meet; implication is right adjoint to meet, inherited from Relational Logic Algebra.
  • Monotonicity: operations are monotone in all arguments.
  • Stability: recognitions closed under these operations remain earned recognitions.

Proof

  • Derived from: Relational Logic Algebra.
  • Uses: Recognition Carrier as the carrier.
  • Shows that: the earned logic structure instantiated on recognitions yields the logical substrate for classifiers and truth values.

title: Relational Measurement Semantics id: relational-measurement-semantics role: structure status: plan derived_from: [] proof_refs: []

Relational Measurement Semantics

Relational Measurement Semantics will link measurement acts to semantic interpretations in the earned calculus, keeping units, scales, and observables explicit. It is a stub pending acts that tie measurement carriers to semantic structures.

Proof

  • TODO: fill proof.

title: Relational Metric id: relational-metric role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Relational Metric

Assigns distances to recognitions based on minimal admissible flow paths modulo nucleus equivalence.

Definition

  • Distance: length of shortest sequence of Flow Context steps connecting two recognitions, factoring by nucleus equivalence.
  • Normalisation: may weight steps by neighborhood size or other earned costs.

Invariants

  • Non-negativity and symmetry (if flow is reversible; otherwise quasi-metric).
  • Triangle inequality from path concatenation.
  • Zero distance iff recognitions are nucleus-equivalent.

Proof

  • Uses: Relational Neighborhood to define step transitions and nucleus to collapse equivalences.
  • Shows that: flow-based distances form a metric/quasi-metric on recognitions.

title: Relational Modal Logic id: relational-modal-logic role: structure status: plan derived_from:

  • acts/box-modality
  • acts/diamond-modality proof_refs: []

Relational Modal Logic

Modal logic over the earned recognition algebra with box/diamond modalities.

Definition

  • Carrier: Relational Logic Algebra enriched with modal operators from Modal Structure.
  • Frames/accessibility: implicit in modal acts (box/diamond) over recognitions.

Invariants

  • Modal operators are monotone and respect meets/joins.
  • If adjointness is earned, box is right adjoint to diamond (or vice versa).

Proof

  • Derived from: modal acts (box/diamond).
  • Uses: logic algebra and modal structure to assemble a modal logic over recognitions.
  • Shows that: the earned modalities yield a modal logic consistent with the recognition algebra.

title: Relational Neighborhood id: relational-neighborhood role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Relational Neighborhood

Minimal context expansion needed to stabilise a recognition—an “open ball” in the relational setting.

Definition

  • Carrier: set of recognitions reachable from a given recognition by one step of Flow Context followed by nucleus closure.
  • Basepoint: the seed recognition around which the neighborhood is formed.

Invariants

  • Minimality: contains all flow-successors within one nucleus class; nothing outside that class.
  • Stability: closed under nucleus; remains within admissible flow domain.

Proof

  • Uses: Flow Context to generate successors and Recognition Carrier as ambient space.
  • Shows that: neighborhoods are well-defined relational open sets for metric and topology analogues.

title: Relational Noise Model id: relational-noise-model role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Relational Noise Model

Describes perturbations in flow or context: errors in recognitions or mislabeled observations.

Definition

  • Components: alternative flow branches, observation distortion probabilities, and admissible noise recognitions.
  • Role: specify how noise is injected and how it is collapsed via nucleus.

Invariants

  • Bounded distortion: noise stays within admissible perturbation bounds.
  • Collapse: noisy branches eventually collapse via nucleus/flow to valid recognitions or failures.

Proof

  • Uses: Flow Context and Observation to define perturbations.
  • Shows that: noise can be modelled structurally within the relational system.

title: Relational Notebook Assistant Program id: relational-notebook-assistant-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Relational Notebook Assistant Program

This program assists with note-taking and research by treating notes, links, and queries as recognitions.

Behaviour

  • Represent notes, citations, queries, and summaries as recognitions with provenance.
  • Link to domains and projects; keep trace for recommendations and retrieval.
  • Measure recall, coverage, and usefulness of notes.

Intended operators

  • Capture and organise notes; annotate and link recognitions.
  • Retrieve and summarise relevant notes for a task with traceable steps.
  • Suggest gaps or next readings based on coverage measurements.

Proof

  • Derived from: Documentation Interpreter.
  • Shows that: this program instantiates the documentation interpreter to realise the described behaviour without extending machine semantics.

title: Relational Notebook Program id: relational-notebook-program role: structure status: someday derived_from: computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Relational Notebook Program

Program that models notes, citations, and queries as earned recognitions, enabling traceable knowledge organisation and retrieval.

Definition

  • Inputs: notes/links/citations recognised via computer Recognition into Computer Recognition carriers; initial Computer Evaluation State.
  • Plan: organise notes/links as a Computer Wiring Plan and execute queries/updates via Evaluate Step.
  • Persistence: save/replay via Computer Storage and Computer Snapshot.
  • Outputs: updated wiring plan/state; failures as Computer Failure.

Behaviour

  • Represent notes, links, citations, summaries as recognitions with provenance.
  • Track evolution of concepts across projects/domains as trace-carrier sequences.
  • Measure relevance, influence, and memory decay via profile reach and entropy metrics.

Invariants

  • Traceability: all notes/links carry provenance; queries respect profile restrictions.
  • Determinism: given same inputs and snapshots, retrieval/upserts are stable.
  • Failure purity: invalid links/queries emit computer failures; state unchanged.

Proof

  • Derived from: Computer Kernel.
  • Shows that: note-taking and retrieval emerge from recognition and trace operations via computer carriers/operators without extending machine semantics.

title: Relational Phase Transition id: relational-phase-transition role: structure status: plan proof_refs: []

Relational Phase Transition

Families of recognitions undergoing abrupt topological/structural changes under parameterised flow.

Definition

  • Parameter space: drawn from Parametric Semantics.
  • Transition: critical parameter values where recognition topology changes (e.g., merges/splits).

Invariants

  • Criticality: transitions marked by non-smooth change in invariants.
  • Stability: phases stable away from critical points.

Proof

  • Uses: parametric flow and invariants to define phases and transitions.
  • Shows that: critical behaviour can be captured recognitionally.

  • Plan simulations as Computer Wiring Plans; drive execution via Evaluate Step.
  • Snapshot simulation state via Computer Storage and Computer Snapshot.

title: Relational Quantum Mechanics id: relational-quantum-mechanics role: structure status: plan derived_from: [] proof_refs: []

title: Relational Sheaf Topos id: relational-sheaf-topos role: structure status: plan derived_from: math/structure/presheaf-topos proof_refs: []

Relational Sheaf Topos

Topos of relational sheaves equipped with internal logic and classifier structure.

Definition

  • Base site: relational contexts/recognitions with covers earned from the relational sheaf acts.
  • Objects: relational sheaves (presheaves satisfying gluing/locality).
  • Morphisms: natural transformations.
  • Internal logic: given by the Relational Logic Algebra and Recognition Classifier as subobject classifier.
  • Limits/colimits: inherited from Presheaf Topos; sheaf condition ensures stability under gluing.

Invariants

  • Has finite limits/colimits, exponentials, subobject classifier (Subobject Classifier).
  • Internal logic matches the recognition algebra; truth values as recognitions.
  • Sheaf gluing/locality respected; covers stable under pullback.

Proof

  • Derived from: Presheaf Topos by imposing the relational sheaf condition.
  • Uses: Relational Logic Algebra, Subobject Classifier, Recognition Classifier to supply internal logic.
  • Shows that: the category of relational sheaves forms a topos with earned internal logic/classifier structures.

title: Relational Simply Typed Lambda Calculus id: relational-simply-typed-lambda-calculus role: structure status: plan derived_from: [] proof_refs: []

Relational Simply Typed Lambda Calculus

This structure will earn the simply typed lambda calculus inside the relational framework: types, terms, and typing/evaluation rules as recognitions induced by acts. It is a stub pending explicit earning of types, abstractions, applications, and typing judgments.

Proof

title: Relational Spreadsheet Program id: relational-spreadsheet-program role: structure status: someday derived_from:

  • computer/type/documentation-interpreter proof_refs: []

Relational Spreadsheet Program

This program expresses spreadsheets as recognitions so cells, formulas, and tables are auditable and aligned with the relational calculus.

Behaviour

  • Represent sheets, cells, formulas, and views as recognitions with provenance.
  • Bind formulas to earned operators; avoid hidden imperative state.
  • Measure recalculation cost, dependencies, and data quality.

Intended operators

  • Create and edit sheets; compute formulas with trace.
  • Import/export slices to other programs (finance, inventory, analytics).
  • Audit lineage of cell values and resolve inconsistencies.

Proof
